name: Chia & almond overnight oats
steps: Combine the oats and seeds in a bowl, then stir in the milk and vanilla. Let stand 5-10 minutes so the oats soften.

Set aside 16 raspberries; crush the rest into the oat mixture. Divide between four glasses, layering with yogurt and both batches of berries. Chill, covered, overnight. Just before eating, top each glass with a splash of almond milk and a scattering of almonds.

name: Healthy baked oats

ingredients.raw_text: 100ml  almond milk or milk of your choice

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Whisk together the milk, egg, and oats with 2 tbsp water, cinnamon, and vanilla. Split the mixture across two 165ml ramekins and bake around 10 minutes, until nearly set. Finish each with half the yogurt, a dusting of cinnamon, and the berries.

name: Slow cooker bio yogurt

ingredients.raw_text: 100ml live yogurt, either shop bought or from a previous homemade batch

steps: Warm the milk in the slow cooker on High until it reaches 82C - roughly two hours. Switch off and let it cool to 43C, which takes another 2-3 hours. Blend a cup of the warm milk with the yogurt, stir back into the cooker, then mix thoroughly. Cover, insulate with a thick towel, and leave undisturbed 9-12 hours until set.

Enjoy over cereal, porridge, or fruit, blended into smoothies, or used in marinades. For a thicker result - good for dips - strain through a muslin-lined sieve set over a bowl until it reaches your preferred texture; longer straining gives a thicker yogurt. Keeps refrigerated up to two weeks.

name: Chia & oat breakfast scones with yogurt and berries

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tsp cold pressed rapeseed oil, plus a little for the ramekins

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Line four 185ml ramekins with a parchment disc and oil the sides with the rapeseed oil. Top up the milk with water to 300ml, then stir in the lemon juice, vanilla, and 2 tsp oil. Blend the flour, seeds, and oats in a food processor until fine, then mix in the baking powder.

Add the wet mixture and combine with a knife blade to form a very wet dough. Divide evenly among the ramekins and bake on a tray for about 20 minutes, until risen and firm (color won't be deep golden). Cool briefly, run a knife around each ramekin's edge, then release the scones.
Serve fresh, or cool and store for later - split and top with yogurt and berries when ready.

name: Healthy granola

ingredients.raw_text: 50g (about 7) soft ready-to-eat dried apricots (we used Crazy Jack organic, because they are sulphur-free)
3 large eggs, whites only (see tip to use up the yolks)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a large tray with parchment.
Blend the apricots, oil, and egg whites until smooth. Stir in the oats, cinnamon, and vanilla, then fold in the coconut, almonds, and pumpkin seeds.
Press the mixture into small clusters and spread over the tray in one layer. Bake 15 minutes, turn the larger pieces, then bake a further 10 minutes until golden and crisp.
Let the granola cool fully on the tray before transferring to an airtight container.

name: Slow cooker Turkish breakfast eggs

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red pepper, cored and finely sliced

steps: Oil the slow cooker insert and switch it on if needed. In a heavy frying pan, heat the remaining oil and cook the onions, pepper, and chilli until softened. Transfer to the slow cooker, add the cherry tomatoes and bread, mix, and season.
Beat the eggs with the milk and parsley, then pour evenly over the mixture so everything is covered. Cook 5-6 hours. Serve topped with yogurt.

name: Oat pancakes
steps: For the compote, simmer the orange juice, berries, and apple over medium heat 10-12 minutes until the berries burst and the apple softens. Cool slightly, loosening with a splash of water if needed.

Blend the oats to a coarse flour and transfer to a mixing bowl. Stir in the baking powder, cinnamon, and orange zest. Whisk the oat milk and egg together, then combine with the dry mix to form a thick batter.

Heat a little oil in a non-stick pan and spoon in 2 tbsp batter per pancake. Cook 2 minutes until bubbles appear, flip, and cook 1 minute more; transfer to a plate. Repeat to make six pancakes total. Stack on two plates, spoon over the compote, and finish with Greek yogurt.

name: Porridge with blueberry compote

ingredients.raw_text: just under ½ x 200ml tub 0% fat Greek-style yogurt

steps: Simmer the oats with 400ml water, stirring occasionally, about 2 minutes until thickened. Off the heat, stir in a third of the yogurt.
At the same time, gently poach the blueberries in a pan with 1 tbsp water (and honey, if using) until thawed and tender but still holding shape.
Spoon the porridge into bowls, add the rest of the yogurt, and top with the blueberries.

name: Fruit & nut breakfast bowl

ingredients.raw_text: just under ½ x 200ml tub 0% fat Greek-style yogurt
60g pot raisins, nuts, goji berries and seeds

steps: Simmer the oats with 400ml water, stirring occasionally, about 4 minutes until thickened.
Meanwhile, peel the oranges and slice off both ends near the core, then trim away the pith close to the flesh. Chop the oranges.
Divide the porridge among bowls, spoon over the yogurt, then top with the oranges and the fruit, nut, and seed mix.

name: Slow cooker spiced apples with barley
steps: Switch on the slow cooker if needed. Add the barley and 750ml boiling water. Core the apples (leaving a coin-sized hole) and halve them.
Arrange the apples skin-side down over the barley. Combine the cinnamon, nutmeg, and orange zest and sprinkle over the top.
Cook on Low for 2 hours. Serve with natural yogurt.

name: Green eggs

ingredients.raw_text: 1½ tbsp olive oil, plus a splash extra
pinch of chilli flakes, plus extra to serve

steps: Heat the oil in a large pan and cook the leeks with a pinch of salt until soft. Stir in the garlic, coriander, fennel, and chilli flakes; once the seeds start to pop, add the spinach and lower the heat. Stir until wilted, then push to one side of the pan. Add a little more oil, crack in the eggs, and fry to your liking.
Mix the yogurt into the spinach and season. Divide between two plates, top with the fried eggs, a squeeze of lemon, and a finish of black pepper and chilli flakes.

name: Orange & blueberry Bircher
steps: Combine 70g oats and 2 tbsp golden linseeds with the zest of half an orange. Pour over 300ml boiling water and leave overnight. The next day, stir in three-quarters of a 175g tub of yogurt, spoon into glasses or bowls, and top with 2 peeled and chopped oranges, the rest of the yogurt, and 4 handfuls of blueberries from a 150g pack.

name: Cinnamon porridge with banana & berries

ingredients.raw_text: ½ tsp cinnamon, plus extra to serve

steps: In a saucepan, combine the oats, cinnamon, sugar, milk, and half the sliced bananas. Bring to a boil, stirring occasionally, then lower the heat and cook 4-5 minutes, stirring continuously.
Divide among four bowls and top with the remaining banana, strawberries, a spoonful of yogurt, and a sprinkle of cinnamon.

name: Tofu scramble
steps: Heat the oil over medium heat and cook the onion 8-10 minutes until golden and sticky. Add the garlic, turmeric, cumin, and paprika; cook 1 minute more.

Mash the tofu with a fork, leaving some texture, then add to the pan and fry 3 minutes. Increase the heat, stir in the tomatoes, and cook 5 minutes more until softened. Fold through the parsley. Serve alone or with toasted rye bread.

name: Salsa verde baked eggs

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small bunch of mint, leaves picked

steps: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan and cook the paprika and cumin 30 seconds over medium heat. Add both the canned and fresh tomatoes, bring to a boil, then cover and simmer 5-6 minutes until softened.
Meanwhile, blend the garlic, most of the parsley, basil, mint, capers, mustard, white wine vinegar, 4 tbsp oil, and 3 tbsp cold water into a smooth sauce. Season.

Stir the spinach into the tomato mixture until wilted (cover briefly to help it along). Make four wells in the mixture and crack an egg into each. Cover and cook over medium heat 6-8 minutes until the eggs are just set. Uncover, drizzle with the herb sauce, and scatter with the reserved parsley and chilli flakes. Serve with flatbreads.

name: Chia & oat breakfast scones with yogurt and berries

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tsp cold pressed rapeseed oil, plus a little for the ramekins

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Line four 185ml ramekins with a parchment disc and oil the sides with the rapeseed oil. Top up the milk with water to 300ml, then stir in the lemon juice, vanilla, and 2 tsp oil. Blend the flour, seeds, and oats in a food processor until fine, then mix in the baking powder.

Add the wet mixture and combine with a knife blade to form a very wet dough. Divide evenly among the ramekins and bake on a tray for about 20 minutes, until risen and firm (color won't be deep golden). Cool briefly, run a knife around each ramekin's edge, then release the scones.
Serve fresh, or cool and store for later - split and top with yogurt and berries when ready.

name: Vanilla & cinnamon breakfast rice

ingredients.raw_text: 12 dried apricots split in half widthways (so they still look whole)

steps: Combine the rice with 600ml water, the milk, vanilla, bay leaves, and cinnamon stick in a deep pan. Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer 25-30 minutes until tender, watching toward the end so it doesn't dry out.
Meanwhile, cook the apricots in a separate pan with 300ml water over low-medium heat 10-15 minutes until tender. Set aside.

Discard the bay leaves and cinnamon stick, then stir half the yogurt into the rice. Both components keep chilled separately up to 24 hours. Spoon the rice into bowls and top with the remaining yogurt, the apricots and their liquid, and the walnuts. If made ahead, serve cold or reheat, loosening the rice with a splash of milk.

name: Budget porridge

ingredients.raw_text: ½tsp ground cinnamon, plus extra to serve (optional)
250ml fortified soya milk, plus 4 tbsp to serve

steps: Combine the oats, cinnamon, 150ml water, and the soya milk in a pan over gentle heat. Once simmering, cook 5 minutes, stirring often, until thickened.
Meanwhile, coarsely grate the apples (skin on), discarding the cores.
Top the porridge with the grated apple, raisins, and nuts, plus extra cinnamon if desired. Loosen with the extra soya milk before serving.

name: Slow cooker Turkish breakfast eggs

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red pepper, cored and finely sliced

steps: Oil the slow cooker insert and switch it on if needed. In a heavy frying pan, heat the remaining oil and cook the onions, pepper, and chilli until softened. Transfer to the slow cooker, add the cherry tomatoes and bread, mix, and season.
Beat the eggs with the milk and parsley, then pour evenly over the mixture so everything is covered. Cook 5-6 hours. Serve topped with yogurt.

name: Breakfast muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 100g apple sauce or pureed apples (find with the baby food)
50g rolled oats, plus extra for sprinkling
2 tbsp mixed seed (we used pumpkin, sunflower and flaxseed)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a 12-hole muffin tin. In a jug, whisk together the eggs, yogurt, oil, apple sauce, banana, honey, and vanilla. In a separate large bowl, combine the remaining dry ingredients (except the seeds) with a pinch of salt.

Fold the wet mixture into the dry until just combined - avoid overmixing, or the muffins will turn dense. Divide among the cases, then top with the reserved oats and seeds. Bake 25-30 minutes until risen and golden, and a skewer comes out clean. Cool on a wire rack. Keeps in a sealed container up to 3 days.

name: Fruit & nut breakfast bowl

ingredients.raw_text: just under ½ x 200ml tub 0% fat Greek-style yogurt
60g pot raisins, nuts, goji berries and seeds

steps: Simmer the oats with 400ml water, stirring occasionally, about 4 minutes until thickened.
Meanwhile, peel the oranges and slice off both ends near the core, then trim away the pith close to the flesh. Chop the oranges.
Divide the porridge among bowls, spoon over the yogurt, then top with the oranges and the fruit, nut, and seed mix.

name: Tofu scramble
steps: Heat the oil over medium heat and cook the onion 8-10 minutes until golden and sticky. Add the garlic, turmeric, cumin, and paprika; cook 1 minute more.

Mash the tofu with a fork, leaving some texture, then add to the pan and fry 3 minutes. Increase the heat, stir in the tomatoes, and cook 5 minutes more until softened. Fold through the parsley. Serve alone or with toasted rye bread.

name: Chia & almond overnight oats
steps: Combine the oats and seeds in a bowl, then stir in the milk and vanilla. Let stand 5-10 minutes so the oats soften.

Set aside 16 raspberries; crush the rest into the oat mixture. Divide between four glasses, layering with yogurt and both batches of berries. Chill, covered, overnight. Just before eating, top each glass with a splash of almond milk and a scattering of almonds.

name: Apricot & hazelnut muesli

ingredients.raw_text: 20g apple fruit crisps, or dried apple

steps: Toast the oats in a dry pan over gentle heat, stirring often. Off the heat, mix in the nuts, seeds, and cinnamon.
Transfer to a large bowl, stirring to cool, then add the fruit, breaking the apple crisps into pieces, and toss together.
Store two-thirds in an airtight container for later (keeps up to two weeks). Serve the remainder across two bowls with 100ml milk and 40g berries each.

name: Healthy shakshuka

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red onion, cut into thin wedges
½ small bunch coriander, roughly chopped
½  small bunch dill, roughly chopped

steps: Heat the oil in a large non-stick pan and cook the onion and peppers over medium heat 8-10 minutes until softening. Add the garlic, cumin, coriander, and paprika; cook 1 minute more. Stir in the tomatoes, spinach, and 100ml water; cook until the spinach wilts, then reduce to a simmer and cook uncovered 10 minutes. Season to taste.
Make four wells in the mixture and crack an egg into each. Cover and cook over gentle heat 8-10 minutes until the eggs just set. Uncover, scatter with fresh herbs, and serve.

name: Herb omelette with fried tomatoes
steps: Heat the oil in a small non-stick pan and cook the tomatoes cut-side down until softened and colored. Meanwhile, beat the eggs with the herbs and black pepper.

Move the tomatoes to two plates. Pour the egg mixture into the pan and stir gently with a wooden spoon, letting cooked egg move aside so raw egg reaches the pan's surface. Stop stirring just before it's fully set so it forms an omelette. Cut into quarters and serve alongside the tomatoes.

name: Salsa verde baked eggs

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small bunch of mint, leaves picked

steps: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan and cook the paprika and cumin 30 seconds over medium heat. Add both the canned and fresh tomatoes, bring to a boil, then cover and simmer 5-6 minutes until softened.
Meanwhile, blend the garlic, most of the parsley, basil, mint, capers, mustard, white wine vinegar, 4 tbsp oil, and 3 tbsp cold water into a smooth sauce. Season.

Stir the spinach into the tomato mixture until wilted (cover briefly to help it along). Make four wells in the mixture and crack an egg into each. Cover and cook over medium heat 6-8 minutes until the eggs are just set. Uncover, drizzle with the herb sauce, and scatter with the reserved parsley and chilli flakes. Serve with flatbreads.

name: Smoky mushroom & potato hash with oaty thins

ingredients.raw_text: 2 medium potatoes (275g), unpeeled, cut into slim wedges

steps: Blend the oats and soya milk in a bowl to break down the texture. Leave to soak 10 minutes.

Meanwhile, boil the potatoes 5 minutes and drain. Heat the oil in a large non-stick pan and cook the mushrooms, onion, and paprika until softened. Add the potatoes and cook 10 minutes, turning occasionally. Stir in the halved tomatoes and cook 5 minutes more.

The oat mixture should now be stiff. Knead in the baking powder by hand, then split in two. With damp hands, press one half into a thin disc on a board. Lift carefully with a palette knife and cook in a dry non-stick pan 2 minutes per side. Set aside and repeat with the second half.

While the second disc cooks, push the potato mixture aside in the other pan, crack in the eggs, and cook 2-3 minutes until whites are set and yolks remain runny. Serve the oat discs topped with the hash and eggs.

name: Chia pudding

ingredients.raw_text: 125ml  oat milk, or substitute for any nut milk, rice milk or dairy milk
2 tsp  maple syrup, plus a drizzle to serve
fruit, such as berries, banana, kiwis or mango,  to serve

steps: Stir together the chia seeds, milk, and 2 tsp maple syrup in a jar or bowl. Cover and chill overnight, or at least 8 hours.
Just before serving, top with fruit and a drizzle of maple syrup.

name: Slow cooker Turkish breakfast eggs

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red pepper, cored and finely sliced

steps: Oil the slow cooker insert and switch it on if needed. In a heavy frying pan, heat the remaining oil and cook the onions, pepper, and chilli until softened. Transfer to the slow cooker, add the cherry tomatoes and bread, mix, and season.
Beat the eggs with the milk and parsley, then pour evenly over the mixture so everything is covered. Cook 5-6 hours. Serve topped with yogurt.

name: Apricot & seed overnight chia
steps: Stir together the chia seeds, milk, and half the mixed seeds in a bowl. Cover and refrigerate overnight to soak.

Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Spread the apricots on a tray and toss with the vanilla bean paste. Roast 15-20 minutes until soft and lightly caramelized. Separately, simmer the blueberries with the lemon zest and juice 4-5 minutes until juicy and bursting.
Divide the chia mixture among four bowls, then top with the apricots, blueberry compote, remaining seeds, and a drizzle of honey if desired.

name: Vanilla-almond chia breakfast bowl
steps: Combine all porridge ingredients in a bowl and let soak at least 20 minutes. Once the oats soften, stir in half the blueberries, adding a little water if the mixture is too thick.
Divide between two bowls and top each with the remaining blueberries, almonds, and honey.

name: Pasta arrabbiata with aubergine

ingredients.raw_text: large handful of  basil, plus extra to serve
25g parmesan or vegetarian Italian-style hard cheese, finely grated
ingredients.food_name: Risotto with butter and Parmesan cheese

steps: Heat the oil in a large pan, add the onion, cover, and cook 5 minutes. Uncover and cook 5 minutes more, stirring, until softened. Stir in the garlic, chilli flakes, and paprika briefly, then add the tomatoes and a can of water. Mix in the bouillon and aubergine; bring to a simmer, cover, and cook 20 minutes.

Boil the penne about 12 minutes until al dente. Drain, keeping 60ml of the water. Toss the pasta into the sauce with the basil, adding a little reserved water if needed. Divide between two bowls and top with cheese and extra basil.

name: Kidney bean curry

ingredients.raw_text: thumb-sized piece of ginger, peeled and finely chopped
1 small pack coriander, stalks finely chopped, leaves roughly shredded

steps: Heat the oil over low-medium heat and cook the onion with a pinch of salt slowly until soft and lightly colored. Add the garlic, ginger, and coriander stalks; cook 2 minutes more until fragrant.
Stir in the spices and cook 1 minute until aromatic. Add the chopped tomatoes and kidney beans with their liquid; bring to a boil.
Lower the heat and simmer 15 minutes until thickened. Season to taste and serve with basmati rice and coriander leaves.

name: Fennel-roasted cauliflower with quinoa

ingredients.raw_text: 1 large cauliflower, or 2 small ones, separated into florets
2 peppers (mix of red, yellow or orange), chopped
1 courgette, halved lengthways, cored and chopped

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Boil the cauliflower in salted water 5 minutes, drain, and spread out to dry.
Crush the fennel and coriander seeds and mix with the paprika and seasoning. Toss the cauliflower in a bowl with half the oil and the spice mix until coated.

Spread the florets on a baking tray, spaced apart. On a separate tray, toss the onions, peppers, and courgettes with the remaining oil. Roast both 30-35 minutes, turning halfway, until browned and slightly crisp.
Combine the garlic and lemon juice into the yogurt, thinning with water if needed. Stir the roasted vegetables into the cooked quinoa with the lemon zest and a pinch of salt.
Plate the quinoa salad, top with the cauliflower, and drizzle with the garlic yogurt.

name: Easy Singapore noodles

ingredients.raw_text: 1 pepper, sliced (we used ½ green and ½ orange)

steps: Cover the noodles with boiled water, pressing them under to soften evenly; let sit 5-10 minutes until fully soft. Meanwhile, mix the curry powder, turmeric, sugar, sesame oil, soy sauce, and 1 tbsp water.

Get the wok very hot, then add the oil, onion, and pepper; stir-fry 3-4 minutes until softened and lightly browned. Drain the noodles and add to the wok with the sauce and beansprouts. Stir-fry 3-4 minutes more until hot. Adjust seasoning with extra soy or sugar, and add chilli for more heat if desired.

name: Slow cooker ratatouille

ingredients.raw_text: 3 courgettes, halved and cut into 2cm pieces
small bunch of basil, roughly chopped, plus a few extra leaves to serve

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion 8 minutes until translucent. Add the garlic and cook 1 minute. Raise the heat, add the aubergines, and fry 5 minutes until golden. Stir in the courgettes and peppers; cook 5 minutes more until slightly softened. Add the tomato puree, fresh tomatoes, herbs, canned tomatoes, vinegar, sugar, and 1 tsp salt; bring to a boil.
Transfer to the slow cooker and cook on low 5-6 hours until everything is soft and the sauce has thickened. Season, top with basil, and serve with sourdough.

name: Vegan paella
steps: Combine the saffron, tomato puree, and bouillon powder in a heatproof bowl, then pour over 1 litre boiling water and set aside.

Heat the oil and cook the onion 5 minutes, stirring, until softening. Add the peppers, garlic, and thyme; cook a few minutes more. Stir in the rice and paprika for about a minute. Pour in the saffron stock, cover, and simmer 10 minutes.

Stir in the frozen beans and courgettes; return to a simmer, cover, and cook 10 minutes more until the rice is tender and the stock absorbed. Rest 5 minutes, then stir in the parsley. Serve half right away with lemon wedges; refrigerate the rest up to three days and reheat thoroughly in the microwave when ready.

name: Tomato penne with avocado

ingredients.raw_text: 1 large onion, sliced, plus 1 tbsp finely chopped
1 orange pepper, deseeded and cut into chunks
handful coriander, chopped, plus extra to serve

steps: Boil the pasta 10-12 minutes until al dente. Meanwhile, heat the oil and fry the onion and pepper 10 minutes, stirring, until golden. Add the garlic and spices, then the tomatoes, half a can of water, corn, and bouillon. Cover and simmer 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, toss the avocado with the lime juice, zest, and finely chopped onion.
Drain the penne and stir into the sauce with the coriander. Divide into bowls, top with the avocado, and scatter with coriander leaves.

name: Artichoke & aubergine rice

ingredients.raw_text: small pack parsley, leaves picked, stalks finely chopped
2 lemons 1 juiced, 1 cut into wedges to serve

steps: Heat 2 tbsp oil and fry the aubergine until colored on all sides (add more oil if it starts sticking); set aside. Add another tbsp oil and soften the onion 2-3 minutes. Add the garlic and parsley stalks, cook briefly, then stir in the spices and rice until coated. Heat 2 minutes, add half the stock, and cook uncovered over medium heat 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.

Nestle the aubergine and artichokes into the rice, add the rest of the stock, and cook 20 minutes more until the rice is done. Stir in the chopped parsley leaves and lemon juice; season well. Serve straight from the pan with lemon wedges.

name: Vegan paella
steps: Combine the saffron, tomato puree, and bouillon powder in a heatproof bowl, then pour over 1 litre boiling water and set aside.

Heat the oil and cook the onion 5 minutes, stirring, until softening. Add the peppers, garlic, and thyme; cook a few minutes more. Stir in the rice and paprika for about a minute. Pour in the saffron stock, cover, and simmer 10 minutes.

Stir in the frozen beans and courgettes; return to a simmer, cover, and cook 10 minutes more until the rice is tender and the stock absorbed. Rest 5 minutes, then stir in the parsley. Serve half right away with lemon wedges; refrigerate the rest up to three days and reheat thoroughly in the microwave when ready.

name: Roasted cauli-broc bowl with tahini hummus

ingredients.food_name: Mixed vegetables (carrots, cauliflower and broccoli florets) raw

steps: The night before, set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Roast the cauliflower and broccoli with the oil and a little sea salt 25-30 minutes until browned. Cool completely.

Assemble each bowl with quinoa, then layer beetroot, spinach, cauliflower, broccoli, and walnuts. Combine the tahini, hummus, lemon juice, and 1 tbsp water; drizzle over just before eating. Serve with lemon wedges.

name: Vegan chickpea curry jacket potatoes

ingredients.raw_text: lemon wedges and coriander leaves, to serve

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Prick the sweet potatoes, place on a tray, and roast 45 minutes until tender.
Meanwhile, melt the coconut oil and fry the cumin seeds 1 minute until fragrant. Add the onion and cook 7-10 minutes until soft.

Add the garlic, ginger, and chilli; cook 2-3 minutes. Stir in the spices and tikka masala paste, cook 2 minutes more, then add the tomatoes. Simmer, add the chickpeas, and cook 20 minutes until thickened. Season.
Split the roasted sweet potatoes open on four plates. Spoon over the curry, squeeze over lemon, season, and finish with coriander.

name: Asparagus & lemon spaghetti with peas

ingredients.raw_text: 160g asparagus, ends trimmed and cut into lengths
2 leeks (220g), cut into lengths, then thin strips
1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped
1 lemon, zested and juiced, plus wedges to serve

steps: Boil the spaghetti 12 minutes, adding the asparagus for the final 3. Meanwhile, heat the oil and cook the leeks and chilli 5 minutes. Stir in the garlic, peas, and lemon zest and juice; cook a few minutes more.
Drain the pasta and toss into the pan with a splash of the pasta water until combined. Serve in bowls with lemon wedges.

name: Mint & basil griddled peach salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp finely chopped mint, plus a few whole leaves to serve
1 small red onion, very finely chopped
1 large Little Gem lettuce (165g), roughly chopped

steps: Mix the lime zest and juice with the oil, mint, and basil; toss half with the peaches. Meanwhile, cook the quinoa per pack instructions.
Cook the beans 3-4 minutes until just tender. Griddle the peaches 1 minute per side (or use a hot non-stick pan with a little oil).

Drain the quinoa into shallow bowls. Toss the warm beans and onion in the remaining mint dressing and pile over the quinoa with the lettuce and rocket. Top with avocado and peaches, scatter with mint, and serve warm.

name: Tofu scramble
steps: Heat the oil over medium heat and cook the onion 8-10 minutes until golden and sticky. Add the garlic, turmeric, cumin, and paprika; cook 1 minute more.

Mash the tofu with a fork, leaving some texture, then add to the pan and fry 3 minutes. Increase the heat, stir in the tomatoes, and cook 5 minutes more until softened. Fold through the parsley. Serve alone or with toasted rye bread.

name: Chia & almond overnight oats
steps: Combine the oats and seeds in a bowl, then stir in the milk and vanilla. Let stand 5-10 minutes so the oats soften.

Set aside 16 raspberries; crush the rest into the oat mixture. Divide between four glasses, layering with yogurt and both batches of berries. Chill, covered, overnight. Just before eating, top each glass with a splash of almond milk and a scattering of almonds.

name: Sweet potato & cauliflower lentil bowl

ingredients.raw_text: 1 large sweet potato, skin left on, scrubbed and cut into medium chunks
1 cauliflower, cut into large florets, stalk diced

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss the sweet potato and cauliflower with the garam masala, half the oil, and seasoning. Spread on a tray with the garlic and roast 30-35 minutes until cooked.
Meanwhile, simmer the lentils in 400ml water 20-25 minutes until tender but with bite. Drain.

Mash the roasted garlic and combine with the remaining oil, ginger, mustard, a pinch of sugar, and a third of the lime juice. Whisk in the warm lentils and season. Grate the carrots, shred the cabbage, and chop the coriander; add the remaining lime juice and season to taste.
Split the lentils between four bowls or containers and top each with a portion of the carrot slaw and roasted vegetables.

name: Linguine with avocado, tomato & lime

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped (optional)

steps: Cook the pasta per pack instructions, about 10 minutes. Meanwhile, combine the lime juice and zest with the avocado, tomatoes, coriander, onion, and chilli (if using). Drain the pasta, toss through the mixture, and serve warm or cold.

name: Veggie olive wraps with mustard vinaigrette
steps: Combine all the filling ingredients and toss well.

Lay the tortilla on foil and pile the filling along one side - it will look like a lot but compacts as you roll. Roll tightly from the filling side, tucking in the ends, then wrap the foil around it. Slice in half to eat right away, or leave whole and wrap in parchment to take with you.

name: Chickpea salad
steps: Combine all the ingredients, mashing lightly so the chickpeas break down slightly and absorb the dressing. Can be made a day ahead and refrigerated. Pairs well with slow-cooked Greek lamb and tzatziki.

name: Baked Salmon & Leek parcel

ingredients.food_name: Mascarpone min. 80 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: ½  lemon, grated zest of 1/4, plus a good squeeze of juice

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Lay out two sheets of parchment, large enough to wrap each salmon fillet.
Simmer the leeks in 6 tbsp water, covered, until the liquid is absorbed and they're nearly tender, about 5 minutes. Stir in the mascarpone, 1 tbsp dill, and seasoning.
Spoon half the leek mixture onto each parchment sheet and top with a salmon fillet. Add lemon zest and a squeeze of juice, then scatter with capers and the remaining dill.
Fold the parchment over the fish and crimp the edges to seal each parcel. Place on a baking sheet, spaced apart.
Bake 12-15 minutes depending on preferred doneness, then carefully open. Serve with lemon wedges and wilted spinach.

name: Beef stew

ingredients.raw_text: 2 really big  carrots, halved lengthways then very chunkily sliced
2 thyme sprigs, 1 whole and 1 leaves picked
850g stewing beef (featherblade or brisket works nicely), cut into nice large chunks

steps: Set the oven to 160C (140C fan/gas 3) and boil the kettle.
Combine the celery, onion, carrots, bay leaves, and thyme sprig in a flameproof casserole with the oil and butter.
Cook 10 minutes to soften, then stir in the flour until no longer dusty, followed by the tomato puree, Worcestershire sauce, and stock cubes.
Gradually stir in the hot water, add the beef, and bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover and transfer to the oven for 2.5 hours, then uncover and cook 30-60 minutes more until the meat is tender and the sauce thickened.
Finish with the reserved thyme leaves.

name: Creamy salmon, leek & potato traybake

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp capers, plus extra to serve
1 tbsp chives, plus extra to serve

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Boil the potatoes 8 minutes, drain, and let steam-dry briefly. Toss with half the oil and seasoning on a tray; roast 20 minutes, turning halfway.
Meanwhile, heat the remaining oil and cook the leek 5 minutes until softening. Add the garlic for 1 minute, then the cream, capers, and hot water; bring to a boil. Stir in the chives.
Heat the grill to high. Pour the leek mixture over the potatoes and top with the salmon. Grill 7-8 minutes until just cooked. Serve with extra chives, capers, and a salad.

name: Chinese chicken curry

ingredients.raw_text: 4 skinless chicken breasts, cut into chunks (or use thighs or drumsticks)

steps: Toss the chicken in the cornflour and season.

Fry the onion in half the oil over low-medium heat until softened, about 5-6 minutes. Add the garlic for 1 minute, then the spices and sugar; cook 1 minute more. Add the stock and soy sauce, simmer 20 minutes, then blend until smooth.
Wipe the pan and brown the chicken in the remaining oil. Return the sauce to the pan, bring to a simmer, stir in the peas, and cook 5 minutes, thinning with water if needed. Serve with rice.

name: Ginger & soy salmon en papillote

ingredients.raw_text: thumb-sized piece of ginger, finely grated
1 courgette, ends trimmed and spiralized into thin noodles
1 carrot, peeled, ends trimmed and spiralized into thin noodles
2 bulbs of pak choi (about 200g), leaves separated
1 red chilli, thinly sliced, deseeded if you like

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Combine the soy, vinegar, ginger, garlic, and pepper. Add the salmon, cover, and marinate 10 minutes at room temperature (or up to 2 hours chilled).
Tear two large sheets of parchment big enough to enclose the fish and vegetables.

Divide the vegetables onto the centre of each sheet, top with a salmon fillet and sliced chilli. Fold the parchment up and over, pouring the remaining marinade over each, then seal tightly into parcels.
Roast 20-25 minutes until the salmon flakes easily. Serve in the parcel with cauliflower or regular rice.

name: Swedish meatballs with beetroot & apple salad

ingredients.raw_text: 100g coarsely grated courgette, squeezed of excess juice
2 tsp vegetable bouillon powder, made up to 300ml with boiling water
1 red onion, halved and thinly sliced

steps: Combine the pork with the grated courgette and onion, half the dill, half the garlic, and pepper. Mix in the egg by hand. Shape into about 28 walnut-sized balls (the mixture will be soft).
Fry the meatballs in the oil in two batches until firm and lightly golden, turning occasionally, about 6-8 minutes per batch.

Wipe out the pan and add the remaining garlic and thyme, then the bouillon; bring to a boil. Off the heat, stir in the creme fraiche and remaining dill, then return the meatballs and coat them in the sauce.

For the salad, combine the apple, onion, parsley, and capers with the lemon, then fold in the beetroot. Serve alongside the meatballs. For two servings, eat half now and refrigerate the rest (up to three days), reheating the meatballs thoroughly in the microwave.

name: Home-style chicken curry
steps: Process the onion with 3 tbsp water to a loose paste (or coarsely grate it without water).
Process the garlic and ginger with 4 tbsp water until smooth (or crush the garlic and finely grate the ginger).
Heat the oil in a wok or sturdy pan over medium heat.
Add the cumin and fennel seeds, cinnamon stick, and chilli flakes; swirl about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the onion paste (it will splutter) and fry until the water evaporates and it turns deep golden, 7-8 minutes.
Add the garlic-ginger paste and cook 2 minutes, stirring.
Stir in the garam masala, turmeric, and sugar; cook 20 seconds, then add the chopped tomatoes.
Cook uncovered over medium heat about 10 minutes until the tomatoes reduce and darken.
Add the chicken chunks once the tomato base has thickened.
Cook 5 minutes to coat the chicken and seal in juices, then pour over the hot stock.
Simmer uncovered 8-10 minutes until the chicken is tender and the sauce lightly thickened, adding more stock or water if needed.
Finish with chopped coriander and serve with flatbreads or basmati rice and yogurt.

name: One-pot chicken chasseur

ingredients.raw_text: 200g pack small  button or chestnut mushrooms

steps: Heat the oil and half the butter in a large lidded casserole.
Season the chicken legs and brown 5 minutes per side; remove and set aside.
Melt the remaining butter, add the onion, and cook 5 minutes until soft.
Add the garlic for 1 minute, then the mushrooms for 2 minutes, then the wine.
Stir in the tomato puree, let it bubble and reduce 5 minutes, then add the thyme and stock.
Return the chicken to the pan, cover, and simmer gently about 1 hour until very tender.
Remove the chicken and keep warm. Boil the sauce rapidly 10 minutes until syrupy and concentrated.
Return the chicken to the sauce and serve.

name: One-pan salmon with roast asparagus
steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Toss the potatoes with 1 tbsp oil in an ovenproof dish and roast 20 minutes until browning. Add the asparagus and roast 15 minutes more.

Add the cherry tomatoes and vinegar, nestle the salmon among the vegetables, drizzle with the remaining oil, and roast 10-15 minutes more until the salmon is cooked. Scatter with basil and serve from the dish.

name: Sardine tomato pasta with gremolata

ingredients.raw_text: small handful of parsley, finely chopped

steps: Cook the pasta per pack instructions. Heat 1 tbsp oil from the sardine tin and sizzle the capers and half the garlic 1-2 minutes until fragrant. Add the tomatoes and cook 4-5 minutes until softened and bursting. Stir in the sardines and rocket, breaking up the fish and wilting the leaves. Season.
For the gremolata, combine the lemon zest, parsley, and remaining garlic; season. Drain the pasta and top with the sardine sauce and gremolata.

name: Sesame salmon, purple sprouting broccoli & sweet potato mash

ingredients.raw_text: 2 sweet potatoes, scrubbed and cut into wedges
1 red chilli, thinly sliced (deseeded if you don't like it too hot)

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a tray with parchment. Combine half the sesame oil with the soy, ginger, garlic, and honey. Microwave the sweet potato wedges with the lime wedges, covered, on high 12-14 minutes until fully soft.
Meanwhile, spread the broccoli and salmon on the tray, spoon over the marinade, and season. Roast 10-12 minutes, then sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Discard the lime wedges and mash the sweet potato with a fork. Stir in the remaining sesame oil, chilli, and seasoning. Serve with the salmon and broccoli.

name: Herb & garlic baked cod with romesco sauce & spinach

ingredients.raw_text: 2 x 140g  skinless cod loin or pollock fillets
2 leeks, well washed and thinly sliced
100g baby spinach, wilted in a pan or the microwave

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Arrange the fish snugly in a shallow dish. Combine 1 tbsp oil with the thyme and garlic, spoon over the fish, and grate over the lemon zest. Bake 10-12 minutes until moist and flaky.

Meanwhile, heat the remaining oil and cook the pepper and leeks 5 minutes until softened. Add the almonds and cook 5 minutes more. Stir in the tomato puree, water, bouillon powder, and vinegar; warm through.
Add lemon juice to taste and blend with a stick blender to a thick, pesto-like sauce. Serve with the fish and wilted spinach.

name: Carrot biryani

ingredients.raw_text: 1 green chilli, chopped (deseeded if you don’t like it very hot)

steps: Heat the oil and fry the onion with a pinch of salt until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the chilli and garlic and cook 1 minute more. Stir in the spices with a splash of water, cook briefly, then add the carrots, coating well.

Add the rice, peas, and cashews, breaking up any clumps and mixing through. Cover and cook over high heat 5 minutes (a little catching on the bottom adds texture). Top with coriander and yogurt, and serve straight from the pan.

name: Curried cod

ingredients.raw_text: thumb-sized piece ginger, peeled and finely grated
zest 1 lemon, then cut into wedges

steps: Heat the oil in a lidded pan and cook the onion over high heat a few minutes. Stir in the curry powder, ginger, and garlic; cook 1-2 minutes until fragrant. Add the tomatoes, chickpeas, and seasoning.
Cook 8-10 minutes until slightly thickened, then top with the cod. Cover and cook 5-10 minutes more until cooked through. Finish with lemon zest and coriander, and serve with lemon wedges.

name: Prawn, fennel & rocket risotto

ingredients.raw_text: 1 small fennel bulb, cored and finely chopped

steps: Bring the stock to a simmer. Meanwhile, heat the oil and cook the onion, garlic, and fennel over low heat 10 minutes until softened but not colored. Add the rice and stir 2 minutes until sizzling. Add the stock a ladleful at a time over medium heat, stirring constantly and letting each addition absorb before adding more.

Once the rice is nearly done, add the prawns, lemon zest, and seasoning. Continue adding stock and cooking 3-4 minutes until the prawns are pink and the rice is tender. Off the heat, stir in the rocket and lemon juice. Rest 2 minutes before serving.

name: Scrambled eggs with basil, spinach & tomatoes

ingredients.raw_text: 175g baby spinach, dried well (if it needs washing)

steps: Heat 1 tsp oil and cook the tomatoes cut-side down over medium heat. Meanwhile, beat the eggs with the yogurt, water, pepper, and basil.
Move the tomatoes to plates. Wilt the spinach in the same pan, stirring occasionally.
Heat the remaining oil and pour in the egg mixture, stirring occasionally until scrambled and just set. Plate the spinach and top with the eggs.

name: Tofu scramble
steps: Heat the oil over medium heat and cook the onion 8-10 minutes until golden and sticky. Add the garlic, turmeric, cumin, and paprika; cook 1 minute more.

Mash the tofu with a fork, leaving some texture, then add to the pan and fry 3 minutes. Increase the heat, stir in the tomatoes, and cook 5 minutes more until softened. Fold through the parsley. Serve alone or with toasted rye bread.

name: Cardamom & peach quinoa porridge
steps: Combine the quinoa, oats, and cardamom pods with 250ml water and 100ml almond milk in a pan. Bring to a boil, then simmer gently 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the remaining almond milk and cook 5 minutes more until creamy.
Remove the cardamom pods, spoon into bowls, and top with peaches and maple syrup.

name: Vanilla & cinnamon breakfast rice

ingredients.raw_text: 12 dried apricots split in half widthways (so they still look whole)

steps: Combine the rice with 600ml water, the milk, vanilla, bay leaves, and cinnamon stick in a deep pan. Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer 25-30 minutes until tender, watching toward the end so it doesn't dry out.
Meanwhile, cook the apricots in a separate pan with 300ml water over low-medium heat 10-15 minutes until tender. Set aside.

Discard the bay leaves and cinnamon stick, then stir half the yogurt into the rice. Both components keep chilled separately up to 24 hours. Spoon the rice into bowls and top with the remaining yogurt, the apricots and their liquid, and the walnuts. If made ahead, serve cold or reheat, loosening the rice with a splash of milk.

name: Orange & blueberry Bircher
steps: Combine 70g oats and 2 tbsp golden linseeds with the zest of half an orange. Pour over 300ml boiling water and leave overnight. The next day, stir in three-quarters of a 175g tub of yogurt, spoon into glasses or bowls, and top with 2 peeled and chopped oranges, the rest of the yogurt, and 4 handfuls of blueberries from a 150g pack.

name: Healthy shakshuka

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red onion, cut into thin wedges
½ small bunch coriander, roughly chopped
½  small bunch dill, roughly chopped

steps: Heat the oil in a large non-stick pan and cook the onion and peppers over medium heat 8-10 minutes until softening. Add the garlic, cumin, coriander, and paprika; cook 1 minute more. Stir in the tomatoes, spinach, and 100ml water; cook until the spinach wilts, then reduce to a simmer and cook uncovered 10 minutes. Season to taste.
Make four wells in the mixture and crack an egg into each. Cover and cook over gentle heat 8-10 minutes until the eggs just set. Uncover, scatter with fresh herbs, and serve.

name: Fruit & nut breakfast bowl

ingredients.raw_text: just under ½ x 200ml tub 0% fat Greek-style yogurt
60g pot raisins, nuts, goji berries and seeds

steps: Simmer the oats with 400ml water, stirring occasionally, about 4 minutes until thickened.
Meanwhile, peel the oranges and slice off both ends near the core, then trim away the pith close to the flesh. Chop the oranges.
Divide the porridge among bowls, spoon over the yogurt, then top with the oranges and the fruit, nut, and seed mix.

name: Healthy granola

ingredients.raw_text: 50g (about 7) soft ready-to-eat dried apricots (we used Crazy Jack organic, because they are sulphur-free)
3 large eggs, whites only (see tip to use up the yolks)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a large tray with parchment.
Blend the apricots, oil, and egg whites until smooth. Stir in the oats, cinnamon, and vanilla, then fold in the coconut, almonds, and pumpkin seeds.
Press the mixture into small clusters and spread over the tray in one layer. Bake 15 minutes, turn the larger pieces, then bake a further 10 minutes until golden and crisp.
Let the granola cool fully on the tray before transferring to an airtight container.

name: Chia & oat breakfast scones with yogurt and berries

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tsp cold pressed rapeseed oil, plus a little for the ramekins

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Line four 185ml ramekins with a parchment disc and oil the sides with the rapeseed oil. Top up the milk with water to 300ml, then stir in the lemon juice, vanilla, and 2 tsp oil. Blend the flour, seeds, and oats in a food processor until fine, then mix in the baking powder.

Add the wet mixture and combine with a knife blade to form a very wet dough. Divide evenly among the ramekins and bake on a tray for about 20 minutes, until risen and firm (color won't be deep golden). Cool briefly, run a knife around each ramekin's edge, then release the scones.
Serve fresh, or cool and store for later - split and top with yogurt and berries when ready.

name: Warming chocolate & banana porridge
steps: Combine the oats, cocoa, and vanilla with 800ml-1L cold water (to taste) in a bowl. Cover and soak overnight.
The next morning, transfer to a pan with the chopped banana and cook over medium heat 15 minutes, stirring often, until done.
Refrigerate half for the next day. Divide the rest between two bowls, swirl in yogurt, and top with sliced banana. Reheat with a splash of milk when serving the second portion.

name: Chia & almond overnight oats
steps: Combine the oats and seeds in a bowl, then stir in the milk and vanilla. Let stand 5-10 minutes so the oats soften.

Set aside 16 raspberries; crush the rest into the oat mixture. Divide between four glasses, layering with yogurt and both batches of berries. Chill, covered, overnight. Just before eating, top each glass with a splash of almond milk and a scattering of almonds.

name: Porridge with blueberry compote

ingredients.raw_text: just under ½ x 200ml tub 0% fat Greek-style yogurt

steps: Simmer the oats with 400ml water, stirring occasionally, about 2 minutes until thickened. Off the heat, stir in a third of the yogurt.
At the same time, gently poach the blueberries in a pan with 1 tbsp water (and honey, if using) until thawed and tender but still holding shape.
Spoon the porridge into bowls, add the rest of the yogurt, and top with the blueberries.

name: Salsa verde baked eggs

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small bunch of mint, leaves picked

steps: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan and cook the paprika and cumin 30 seconds over medium heat. Add both the canned and fresh tomatoes, bring to a boil, then cover and simmer 5-6 minutes until softened.
Meanwhile, blend the garlic, most of the parsley, basil, mint, capers, mustard, white wine vinegar, 4 tbsp oil, and 3 tbsp cold water into a smooth sauce. Season.

Stir the spinach into the tomato mixture until wilted (cover briefly to help it along). Make four wells in the mixture and crack an egg into each. Cover and cook over medium heat 6-8 minutes until the eggs are just set. Uncover, drizzle with the herb sauce, and scatter with the reserved parsley and chilli flakes. Serve with flatbreads.

name: Herb omelette with fried tomatoes
steps: Heat the oil in a small non-stick pan and cook the tomatoes cut-side down until softened and colored. Meanwhile, beat the eggs with the herbs and black pepper.

Move the tomatoes to two plates. Pour the egg mixture into the pan and stir gently with a wooden spoon, letting cooked egg move aside so raw egg reaches the pan's surface. Stop stirring just before it's fully set so it forms an omelette. Cut into quarters and serve alongside the tomatoes.

name: Chicken satay salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 skinless chicken breast fillets (or use turkey breast)
1 tbsp crunchy peanut butter (choose a sugar-free version with no palm oil, if possible)
sunflower oil, for wiping the pan
2 Little Gem lettuce hearts, cut into wedges
1 banana shallot, halved and thinly sliced

steps: Combine the tamari, curry powder, cumin, garlic, and honey. Slice the chicken breasts horizontally into 4 fillets, add to the marinade, and coat well. Chill at least 1 hour, or overnight.

Meanwhile, mix the peanut butter with the chilli sauce, lime juice, and water into a spoonable sauce. Cook the marinated chicken in an oiled pan, covered, 5-6 minutes over medium heat, flipping near the end, until cooked but still moist. Rest, covered, a few minutes.

While resting, toss the lettuce with the cucumber, shallot, coriander, and pomegranate; divide onto plates and spoon over a little sauce. Slice the chicken over the salad and finish with the remaining sauce. Serve warm.

name: Linguine with avocado, tomato & lime

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped (optional)

steps: Cook the pasta per pack instructions, about 10 minutes. Meanwhile, combine the lime juice and zest with the avocado, tomatoes, coriander, onion, and chilli (if using). Drain the pasta, toss through the mixture, and serve warm or cold.

name: Chicken with crushed harissa chickpeas

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small bunch of parsley, finely chopped

steps: Heat the oil and fry the onions and peppers 7 minutes until softened and golden.
Meanwhile, flatten the chicken between parchment to about 2cm thick. Combine the remaining oil with the za'atar and rub over the chicken; season.
Heat the grill to high and cook the chicken on a foil-lined tray 3-4 minutes per side until cooked through.

Warm the chickpeas with the harissa paste and water, then mash roughly. Wilt the spinach separately. Stir the pepper-onion mixture, spinach, and parsley into the chickpeas. Serve with the sliced chicken and lemon wedges.

name: Prawn, fennel & rocket risotto

ingredients.raw_text: 1 small fennel bulb, cored and finely chopped

steps: Bring the stock to a simmer. Meanwhile, heat the oil and cook the onion, garlic, and fennel over low heat 10 minutes until softened but not colored. Add the rice and stir 2 minutes until sizzling. Add the stock a ladleful at a time over medium heat, stirring constantly and letting each addition absorb before adding more.

Once the rice is nearly done, add the prawns, lemon zest, and seasoning. Continue adding stock and cooking 3-4 minutes until the prawns are pink and the rice is tender. Off the heat, stir in the rocket and lemon juice. Rest 2 minutes before serving.

name: Masala frittata with avocado salsa

ingredients.raw_text: 3 onions, 2½ thinly sliced, ½ finely chopped
1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped

steps: Heat the oil in an ovenproof pan and cook the onions over medium heat about 10 minutes until soft and golden. Add the Madras paste for 1 minute, then half the tomatoes and half the chilli; cook until thick and the tomatoes have broken down.

Heat the grill to high. Mix half the coriander into the eggs and season, then pour over the onion mixture. Stir gently once or twice, then cook over low heat 8-10 minutes until nearly set. Finish under the grill 3-5 minutes until set.
For the salsa, combine the avocado, remaining chilli and tomatoes, chopped onion, remaining coriander, and lemon juice. Season and serve alongside the frittata.

name: Scrambled egg & feta hash

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Heat the oil over low-medium heat and cook the tomatoes and spring onions 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the feta for 1 minute to warm through, then pour in the eggs and chives. Season and stir to scramble. Plate some spinach and top with the eggs, serving the rest of the spinach on the side.

name: Miso roast salmon, lentil & pomegranate salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 carrots, cut into fine strips with a julienne peeler or knife

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Boil the lentils 20 minutes until tender. Meanwhile, combine the miso paste with half the ginger, garlic, half the lime juice, oil, and sesame seeds. Spread some over the salmon on a foil-lined tray and roast 10-12 minutes until cooked.

Combine the remaining miso mixture with the rest of the ginger and lime juice, plus the lime zest, vinegar, carrots, pomegranate, onions, and coriander. Drain the lentils and toss through. Divide between plates and top with the salmon.

name: Chicken satay salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 skinless chicken breast fillets (or use turkey breast)
1 tbsp crunchy peanut butter (choose a sugar-free version with no palm oil, if possible)
sunflower oil, for wiping the pan
2 Little Gem lettuce hearts, cut into wedges
1 banana shallot, halved and thinly sliced

steps: Combine the tamari, curry powder, cumin, garlic, and honey. Slice the chicken breasts horizontally into 4 fillets, add to the marinade, and coat well. Chill at least 1 hour, or overnight.

Meanwhile, mix the peanut butter with the chilli sauce, lime juice, and water into a spoonable sauce. Cook the marinated chicken in an oiled pan, covered, 5-6 minutes over medium heat, flipping near the end, until cooked but still moist. Rest, covered, a few minutes.

While resting, toss the lettuce with the cucumber, shallot, coriander, and pomegranate; divide onto plates and spoon over a little sauce. Slice the chicken over the salad and finish with the remaining sauce. Serve warm.

name: Tuna Niçoise protein pot
steps: Boil the egg 8-10 minutes to your preferred yolk, steaming the green beans above the pan for the last 6 minutes until tender. Cool both under running water, then peel and quarter the egg.
Add the beans to a lunch container, top with the tomato, tuna, and quartered egg, and spoon over the dressing. Seal until ready to eat.

name: Moroccan-style chickpea soup

ingredients.raw_text: 400g can chopped plum tomatoes  with garlic
large handful coriander or parsley and flatbread, to serve

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion and celery gently 10 minutes, stirring, until softened. Add the cumin and cook 1 minute more.

Raise the heat and add the stock, tomatoes, chickpeas, and pepper. Simmer 8 minutes. Stir in the broad beans and lemon juice, cook 2 minutes more, and season. Top with lemon zest and herbs; serve with flatbread.

name: Spinach & pepper frittata

ingredients.food_name: Cottage cheese < 10 % fat in dry matter
Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 225g frozen leaf spinach, thawed, squeezed and finely chopped
2 roasted red peppers (not in oil), torn into strips

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Line a 20cm tin with parchment if it has a loose base.

Beat the eggs with the cottage cheese, garlic, half the Parmesan, spinach, peppers, nutmeg, and pepper. Pour into the tin, top with tomatoes, and sprinkle with the remaining Parmesan. Bake 40 minutes until set through and slightly puffed. Slice and serve hot or cold; keeps refrigerated 3-4 days.

name: Wild salmon veggie bowl

ingredients.raw_text: ⅓ small pack dill, chopped, plus some extra fronts (optional)

steps: Spiralize or julienne the carrots and courgette into long strips and divide between two plates.
Combine the beetroot, balsamic vinegar, dill, and red onion; spoon over the vegetables. Flake the salmon on top and scatter with capers and extra dill.

name: Easy turkey burgers
steps: Heat the oil and soften the onion 5 minutes. Add the garlic for 1 minute, then the oats for 2 minutes more. Transfer to a bowl to cool.
Mix in the remaining ingredients by hand, season, and shape into 8 patties.

Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Sear the patties in the remaining oil until well colored, 3-4 minutes per side, then transfer to a tray and bake 10-15 minutes. Serve in rolls with the chutney and cucumber.

For the chutney, cook the onion in oil 5 minutes until soft. Add the garlic for 1 minute, then the sundried tomato paste, chopped tomatoes, and sugar. Simmer gently 20-25 minutes until thick. Season and cool before serving.

name: Cod & tarragon fishcakes with minty peas

ingredients.raw_text: 900g potatoes (we used Maris Piper), peeled and cut into medium chunks
2 x 280g packs skinless cod loin, cut into cubes

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and oil a baking sheet. Boil the potatoes 15-20 minutes until tender, drain, and mash with the mustard, anchovies, spring onions, capers, and tarragon. Fold in the raw cod.
Shape into 8 cakes and lightly coat in flour. Dip in beaten egg, sprinkle with almonds, and place on the tray. Bake 20 minutes, turning halfway, until golden.

Meanwhile, boil the peas and leeks 10 minutes, drain, and stir in the mint. Divide half the pea mixture between two plates with 2 fishcakes each. Toss half the rocket with half the lemon juice and oil and pile on top. Chill the remaining fishcakes and pea mixture separately for up to two days; reheat the fishcakes in the oven and the peas in the microwave, dressing the rocket fresh.

name: Quinoa salad with avocado mayo
steps: Simmer the quinoa in boiling water about 18 minutes until the grains burst. Drain and rinse under cold water.
Meanwhile, blend the avocado with the garlic, mustard, and lemon juice until smooth, thinning with a little water if needed.
Stir the lemon zest, corn, salad vegetables, mint, and pumpkin seeds into the quinoa, adjusting with more lemon juice. Divide onto plates, top with chicken if using, and spoon over the avocado mayo.

name: Salmon risotto

ingredients.raw_text: 1 garlic clove, crushed or finely grated
50g parmesan, grated, plus extra to serve
ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Simmer the salmon in the stock about 6 minutes until just cooked. Remove, keeping the stock warm, and cool the salmon a few minutes before removing the skin.
Heat the oil and half the butter and cook the onion over medium-low heat 10-12 minutes until softened but not browned. Add the garlic for 1 minute, then the rice for 2 minutes until coated.

Add the wine and cook over medium heat 4-5 minutes until evaporated. Add the stock a quarter at a time, stirring until absorbed before each addition, about 4-5 minutes per batch. With the final addition, stir in the peas and flaked salmon; season generously and cook 3-4 minutes until the rice is tender and everything is warmed through, adding water if needed.
Off the heat, stir in the remaining butter, lemon juice, and Parmesan. Top with lemon zest if desired and serve immediately.

name: Cod with cucumber, avocado & mango salsa salad

ingredients.raw_text: 1 small mango, peeled, stoned and chopped (or 2 peaches, stoned and chopped)
1 small avocado, stoned, peeled and sliced

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Place the fish in a dish, pour over half the lime juice and some zest, and grind over pepper. Bake 8 minutes until flaky but still moist.
Meanwhile, combine the remaining ingredients with the rest of the lime juice and zest. Plate and top with the cod, spooning over any pan juices.

name: Chicken meatballs with quinoa & curried cauliflower
steps: Combine all the meatball ingredients with seasoning, shape into six balls, and chill 20 minutes.
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Simmer the rinsed quinoa in 100ml water, then reduce to a gentle simmer and cook 10-15 minutes until doubled and tender. Drain and cool.
Toss the cauliflower and sweet potato with the oil and curry powder on a tray. Roast alongside the meatballs (in a separate tray) for 15 minutes until cooked through.
Combine the quinoa with the roasted vegetables, pistachios, and sultanas, squeeze over the lime juice, and serve with the meatballs.

name: Spanish chicken stew

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red pepper, cut into chunky pieces
large handful black olives (such as couchillo)
cooked rice or crusty bread, to serve

steps: Heat 1 tbsp oil and brown the seasoned chicken 7-8 minutes per side; set aside. Add the remaining oil with the pepper, onion, and garlic; fry 8 minutes until lightly golden. Add the vinegar and cook 1 minute.
Return the chicken to the pan with the paprika and stock. Bring to a boil, then simmer 25 minutes until the chicken is cooked through. Stir in the olives and almonds; serve with rice or crusty bread.

name: Tandoori trout

ingredients.raw_text: yogurt, coriander leaves and mango chutney, to serve

steps: Coat the trout in the curry paste. Boil the potatoes in salted water 15-20 minutes until tender but holding shape. Drain and steam-dry.
Heat the grill and cook the trout on a foil-lined tray until tender, about 6-8 minutes.

Meanwhile, heat the oil and cook the garlic and spices a few minutes until fragrant. Add the potatoes and fry 3 minutes until crisp-edged, then add the frozen peas and cook 2-3 minutes more until warmed through. Season and serve with the trout, coriander, yogurt, and chutney.

name: Sesame tuna steaks with slaw

ingredients.raw_text: 1 lime, 1/2 juiced, 1/2 cut into wedges to serve
100g beansprouts (make sure they are the ready-to-eat variety)

steps: Combine the sesame oil and lime juice with seasoning, then toss with the red cabbage, chilli, yellow pepper, beansprouts, and peanuts.

Get a non-stick pan searing hot. Season the tuna and press both sides into the sesame seeds to coat. Fry 3 minutes per side until browned with a sesame crust but still pink in the middle. Rest briefly, then serve with the slaw and lime wedges.

name: Sesame salmon, purple sprouting broccoli & sweet potato mash

ingredients.raw_text: 2 sweet potatoes, scrubbed and cut into wedges
1 red chilli, thinly sliced (deseeded if you don't like it too hot)

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a tray with parchment. Combine half the sesame oil with the soy, ginger, garlic, and honey. Microwave the sweet potato wedges with the lime wedges, covered, on high 12-14 minutes until fully soft.
Meanwhile, spread the broccoli and salmon on the tray, spoon over the marinade, and season. Roast 10-12 minutes, then sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Discard the lime wedges and mash the sweet potato with a fork. Stir in the remaining sesame oil, chilli, and seasoning. Serve with the salmon and broccoli.

name: Tofu scramble
steps: Heat the oil over medium heat and cook the onion 8-10 minutes until golden and sticky. Add the garlic, turmeric, cumin, and paprika; cook 1 minute more.

Mash the tofu with a fork, leaving some texture, then add to the pan and fry 3 minutes. Increase the heat, stir in the tomatoes, and cook 5 minutes more until softened. Fold through the parsley. Serve alone or with toasted rye bread.

name: Refried beans

ingredients.raw_text: 400g can black, pinto or kidney beans

steps: Drain the beans lightly, keeping about half the tin liquid (don't rinse). Mash roughly or blend smooth, depending on preferred texture; set aside.

Heat the oil and cook the onions 8-10 minutes until soft and golden. Add the garlic, chilli, and cumin seeds; sizzle 2 minutes more. Stir in the beans, oregano, paprika, and a pinch of salt. Cook 5 minutes until heated through, loosening with more reserved bean liquid if needed. Serve right away with coriander, or cool and refrigerate up to three days (or freeze up to a month), reheating with a splash of water.

name: Vegetable stew

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp olive oil, or rapeseed oil
2 x 400g cans tomatoes, or peeled cherry tomatoes
1 vegetable stock cube, made up to 250ml
250g cooked puy lentils (we used Merchant Gourmet ready-to-eat puy lentils)

steps: Heat the oil in a large lidded pan over medium heat. Lower the heat slightly and cook the onion 5-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened.
Add the garlic, smoked paprika, cumin, thyme, carrots, celery, and peppers; cook 5 minutes.
Add the tomatoes, stock, courgettes, and thyme sprigs. Stir, cover, and cook 20-25 minutes.
Discard the thyme sprigs. Stir in the cooked lentils and simmer 5 minutes more until warmed through. Serve with rice, mash, or quinoa.

name: Vegan chickpea curry jacket potatoes

ingredients.raw_text: lemon wedges and coriander leaves, to serve

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Prick the sweet potatoes, place on a tray, and roast 45 minutes until tender.
Meanwhile, melt the coconut oil and fry the cumin seeds 1 minute until fragrant. Add the onion and cook 7-10 minutes until soft.

Add the garlic, ginger, and chilli; cook 2-3 minutes. Stir in the spices and tikka masala paste, cook 2 minutes more, then add the tomatoes. Simmer, add the chickpeas, and cook 20 minutes until thickened. Season.
Split the roasted sweet potatoes open on four plates. Spoon over the curry, squeeze over lemon, season, and finish with coriander.

name: Thirty-minute courgettes with dukkah sprinkle
steps: Heat the oil and cook the onions 5 minutes, stirring, until starting to color. Stir in the coriander and paprika, then add the tomatoes and a can of water. Add the bouillon powder and courgettes, cover, and cook 6 minutes.
Meanwhile, make the dukkah: warm the whole spices, sesame seeds, and almonds in a dry pan, stirring, until fragrant, then remove from the heat.

Add the butter beans, tomatoes, and peas to the courgettes, cover, and cook 5 minutes more. Stir in the coriander and spoon into bowls. Crush the toasted spice mix and scatter on top. For two servings, jar half the seed mix and refrigerate half the vegetable mixture for another day.

name: Brazil nut refried beans
steps: Crush the Brazil nuts to a coarse paste. Transfer to a saucepan with the kidney beans and their liquid, plus a half-can of water. Stir in the remaining ingredients except the lime juice.

Heat gently 15 minutes, then mash with a potato masher to a loose, porridge-like texture. Continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until soft but not runny. Finish with the lime juice and seasoning to taste.

name: Vegetable & bean chilli

ingredients.raw_text: 400g can kidney beans in water, drained

steps: Heat the oil and cook the garlic, ginger, onion, courgettes, and peppers about 5 minutes until softening. Add the chilli powder and cook 1 minute more.
Stir in the lentils, tomato puree, tomatoes, and water. Bring to a boil and cook 15-20 minutes.
Add the sweetcorn and beans and cook 10 minutes more.

name: Omelette roll-up

ingredients.raw_text: a little rapeseed or olive oil for frying

steps: Beat the egg with a splash of water. Heat the oil in a non-stick pan, pour in the egg, and swirl to coat the base like a pancake. Cook until set — no need to flip.
Slide the pancake onto a board, spread with the salsa, sprinkle with coriander, and roll up. Serve warm or cold; keeps refrigerated up to 2 days.

name: Tuna Niçoise protein pot
steps: Boil the egg 8-10 minutes to your preferred yolk, steaming the green beans above the pan for the last 6 minutes until tender. Cool both under running water, then peel and quarter the egg.
Add the beans to a lunch container, top with the tomato, tuna, and quartered egg, and spoon over the dressing. Seal until ready to eat.

name: Masala omelette muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 2 large or 4 small garlic cloves, finely grated
1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7) and lightly oil four ramekins. Grate the courgettes and squeeze out as much liquid as possible. Combine with the remaining ingredients except the feta.
Divide between the ramekins, top with feta, and bake on a tray 20-25 minutes until risen and set. Serve hot or cold with salad, slaw, or vegetables.

name: Basil & olive eggs
steps: Boil the eggs 8 minutes, then cool in cold water. Peel, halve, and scoop out the yolks.
Blend the basil, olives, oil, vinegar, and pepper, then mash in the yolks. Spoon back into the egg whites and chill.

name: Spiced tortilla

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tsp curry spice  (we used coriander, cumin and turmeric)
bunch coriander, stalks finely chopped, leaves roughly chopped

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion and half the chilli 5 minutes until softened. Add the spices for 1 minute, then the tomatoes, potatoes, and coriander stalks. Pour the seasoned eggs over the vegetables and cook gently 8-10 minutes until nearly set.
Finish under a hot grill 1-2 minutes until set on top. Scatter with coriander leaves and remaining chilli, slice, and serve with a green salad.

name: Healthy chocolate milk
steps: Blend all the ingredients together until smooth and pour into a glass to serve.

name: Scrambled eggs with basil, spinach & tomatoes

ingredients.raw_text: 175g baby spinach, dried well (if it needs washing)

steps: Heat 1 tsp oil and cook the tomatoes cut-side down over medium heat. Meanwhile, beat the eggs with the yogurt, water, pepper, and basil.
Move the tomatoes to plates. Wilt the spinach in the same pan, stirring occasionally.
Heat the remaining oil and pour in the egg mixture, stirring occasionally until scrambled and just set. Plate the spinach and top with the eggs.

name: Masala frittata with avocado salsa

ingredients.raw_text: 3 onions, 2½ thinly sliced, ½ finely chopped
1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped

steps: Heat the oil in an ovenproof pan and cook the onions over medium heat about 10 minutes until soft and golden. Add the Madras paste for 1 minute, then half the tomatoes and half the chilli; cook until thick and the tomatoes have broken down.

Heat the grill to high. Mix half the coriander into the eggs and season, then pour over the onion mixture. Stir gently once or twice, then cook over low heat 8-10 minutes until nearly set. Finish under the grill 3-5 minutes until set.
For the salsa, combine the avocado, remaining chilli and tomatoes, chopped onion, remaining coriander, and lemon juice. Season and serve alongside the frittata.

name: Thai broccoli rice

ingredients.raw_text: 1 head of broccoli, cut into florets and the stem cut in half
1 medium red chilli, deseeded and finely diced
1 red pepper, deseeded and sliced into strips

steps: Toast the peanuts in a dry pan, shaking often, then set aside. Pulse the broccoli in a food processor until it resembles rice; transfer to a bowl.

Heat the oil and cook the onion, garlic, ginger, and chilli until soft and fragrant. Add the broccoli rice, coat well, and saute 3-4 minutes until al dente. Combine with the red cabbage, red pepper, half the coriander, and half the peanuts.
Whisk the lime zest and juice, tamari, sugar, and oil into a dressing and toss through. Garnish with the remaining coriander and peanuts.

name: Omelette roll-up

ingredients.raw_text: a little rapeseed or olive oil for frying

steps: Beat the egg with a splash of water. Heat the oil in a non-stick pan, pour in the egg, and swirl to coat the base like a pancake. Cook until set — no need to flip.
Slide the pancake onto a board, spread with the salsa, sprinkle with coriander, and roll up. Serve warm or cold; keeps refrigerated up to 2 days.

name: Masala omelette muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 2 large or 4 small garlic cloves, finely grated
1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7) and lightly oil four ramekins. Grate the courgettes and squeeze out as much liquid as possible. Combine with the remaining ingredients except the feta.
Divide between the ramekins, top with feta, and bake on a tray 20-25 minutes until risen and set. Serve hot or cold with salad, slaw, or vegetables.

name: Roast aubergines with yogurt & harissa
steps: Set the oven to 200C (190C fan/gas 6). Pierce the aubergines, brush with oil, and roast 40-45 minutes until fully tender.

Melt the butter and stir in the harissa. Halve the aubergines and open them up. Season, spoon over yogurt mixed with garlic, drizzle with the spiced butter, and finish with coriander and sesame seeds.

name: Green eggs

ingredients.raw_text: 1½ tbsp olive oil, plus a splash extra
pinch of chilli flakes, plus extra to serve

steps: Heat the oil in a large pan and cook the leeks with a pinch of salt until soft. Stir in the garlic, coriander, fennel, and chilli flakes; once the seeds start to pop, add the spinach and lower the heat. Stir until wilted, then push to one side of the pan. Add a little more oil, crack in the eggs, and fry to your liking.
Mix the yogurt into the spinach and season. Divide between two plates, top with the fried eggs, a squeeze of lemon, and a finish of black pepper and chilli flakes.

name: Roasted cauliflower steaks
steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7) and line a tray with parchment. Cut two thick steaks from the center of the cauliflower (save the rest). Rub with paprika and half the oil, season, and roast 15-20 minutes until cooked through.
Meanwhile, chop the pepper, olives, parsley, and capers; combine with the remaining oil and vinegar, and season. Spoon over the cooked steaks and top with flaked almonds.

name: Grilled courgette & halloumi salad with caper & lemon dressing
steps: Combine all the dressing ingredients with a splash of water and a pinch of salt; set aside.
Toss the courgettes with oil and a pinch of salt.
Griddle the courgettes cut-side down (in batches) 4-5 minutes until char marks form, flip, and cook 3 minutes more. Transfer to a bowl and cover to keep warm.
Meanwhile, cook the halloumi in a separate hot pan a few minutes until golden on both sides.
Arrange the courgettes and halloumi on a platter and spoon over the dressing.

name: Greek salad

ingredients.raw_text: 4 large  vine tomatoes, cut into irregular wedges
1 cucumber, peeled, deseeded, then roughly chopped
½ a red onion thinly sliced
85g feta cheese, cut into chunks (barrel matured feta is the best)
ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 4 tbsp Greek extra virgin olive oil

steps: Combine the tomato wedges, cucumber, red onion, olives, oregano, feta, and olive oil in a large bowl.
Season lightly and serve with crusty bread.

name: Courgette & halloumi skewers
steps: Combine the chilli, half the mint, lemon zest and juice, oil, courgettes, and halloumi; marinate 30 minutes. Soak wooden skewers in water 20 minutes.
Thread the courgettes and halloumi onto the skewers. Grill or barbecue 7-8 minutes, turning and basting with the remaining marinade. Finish with the remaining mint.

name: Basil & olive eggs
steps: Boil the eggs 8 minutes, then cool in cold water. Peel, halve, and scoop out the yolks.
Blend the basil, olives, oil, vinegar, and pepper, then mash in the yolks. Spoon back into the egg whites and chill.

name: Herb omelette with fried tomatoes
steps: Heat the oil in a small non-stick pan and cook the tomatoes cut-side down until softened and colored. Meanwhile, beat the eggs with the herbs and black pepper.

Move the tomatoes to two plates. Pour the egg mixture into the pan and stir gently with a wooden spoon, letting cooked egg move aside so raw egg reaches the pan's surface. Stop stirring just before it's fully set so it forms an omelette. Cut into quarters and serve alongside the tomatoes.

name: Omelette roll-up

ingredients.raw_text: a little rapeseed or olive oil for frying

steps: Beat the egg with a splash of water. Heat the oil in a non-stick pan, pour in the egg, and swirl to coat the base like a pancake. Cook until set — no need to flip.
Slide the pancake onto a board, spread with the salsa, sprinkle with coriander, and roll up. Serve warm or cold; keeps refrigerated up to 2 days.

name: Basil & olive eggs
steps: Boil the eggs 8 minutes, then cool in cold water. Peel, halve, and scoop out the yolks.
Blend the basil, olives, oil, vinegar, and pepper, then mash in the yolks. Spoon back into the egg whites and chill.

name: Chilli avocado
steps: Sprinkle the avocado with chilli flakes, lime juice, and black pepper; eat with a spoon.

name: Roasted cauliflower steaks
steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7) and line a tray with parchment. Cut two thick steaks from the center of the cauliflower (save the rest). Rub with paprika and half the oil, season, and roast 15-20 minutes until cooked through.
Meanwhile, chop the pepper, olives, parsley, and capers; combine with the remaining oil and vinegar, and season. Spoon over the cooked steaks and top with flaked almonds.

name: Soy & butter salmon parcels
steps: Heat the barbecue (or a griddle pan indoors) until the coals turn white or the pan is hot. Cut four sheets of foil for wrapping. Spread a little butter in the center of each to prevent sticking, then add the salmon. Combine the soy and honey and spoon over each fillet, topping with any remaining butter. Fold the foil tightly to seal each parcel.
Cook the parcels on the barbecue or griddle 5-10 minutes (check one carefully — it will be hot). Open and scatter with sesame seeds and spring onion.
Toss the cucumber with a few drops of sesame oil and a little salt. Serve the salmon with the cucumber salad.

name: Thai broccoli rice

ingredients.raw_text: 1 head of broccoli, cut into florets and the stem cut in half
1 medium red chilli, deseeded and finely diced
1 red pepper, deseeded and sliced into strips

steps: Toast the peanuts in a dry pan, shaking often, then set aside. Pulse the broccoli in a food processor until it resembles rice; transfer to a bowl.

Heat the oil and cook the onion, garlic, ginger, and chilli until soft and fragrant. Add the broccoli rice, coat well, and saute 3-4 minutes until al dente. Combine with the red cabbage, red pepper, half the coriander, and half the peanuts.
Whisk the lime zest and juice, tamari, sugar, and oil into a dressing and toss through. Garnish with the remaining coriander and peanuts.

name: Crisp-skin fish with asparagus
steps: Trim the woody ends from the asparagus. Halve the tomatoes, remove the seeds, and finely dice the flesh; set both aside.
Heat 1 tbsp oil and cook the seasoned fish skin-side down over medium heat 3-4 minutes, pressing gently, until crisp and golden. Flip and cook 3-4 minutes more depending on thickness.

Meanwhile, simmer the asparagus in salted water 3-4 minutes and drain. For the dressing, gently warm the remaining oil with the diced tomatoes, olives, capers, and parsley. Toss the asparagus in a little dressing, plate with the fish on top, and spoon the warm tomato dressing around.

name: Baked Salmon & Leek parcel

ingredients.food_name: Mascarpone min. 80 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: ½  lemon, grated zest of 1/4, plus a good squeeze of juice

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Lay out two sheets of parchment, large enough to wrap each salmon fillet.
