Simmer the leeks in 6 tbsp water, covered, until the liquid is absorbed and they're nearly tender, about 5 minutes. Stir in the mascarpone, 1 tbsp dill, and seasoning.
Spoon half the leek mixture onto each parchment sheet and top with a salmon fillet. Add lemon zest and a squeeze of juice, then scatter with capers and the remaining dill.
Fold the parchment over the fish and crimp the edges to seal each parcel. Place on a baking sheet, spaced apart.
Bake 12-15 minutes depending on preferred doneness, then carefully open. Serve with lemon wedges and wilted spinach.

name: Ginger & soy salmon en papillote

ingredients.raw_text: thumb-sized piece of ginger, finely grated
1 courgette, ends trimmed and spiralized into thin noodles
1 carrot, peeled, ends trimmed and spiralized into thin noodles
2 bulbs of pak choi (about 200g), leaves separated
1 red chilli, thinly sliced, deseeded if you like

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Combine the soy, vinegar, ginger, garlic, and pepper. Add the salmon, cover, and marinate 10 minutes at room temperature (or up to 2 hours chilled).
Tear two large sheets of parchment big enough to enclose the fish and vegetables.

Divide the vegetables onto the centre of each sheet, top with a salmon fillet and sliced chilli. Fold the parchment up and over, pouring the remaining marinade over each, then seal tightly into parcels.
Roast 20-25 minutes until the salmon flakes easily. Serve in the parcel with cauliflower or regular rice.

name: Cauliflower rice
steps: Trim the core and stalks from the cauliflower and pulse the rest in a food processor to a rice-like texture. Transfer to a covered, microwave-safe bowl, pierce the covering, and microwave on high 7 minutes (no water needed). Stir in the coriander; add toasted cumin seeds for extra spice.

name: Courgette frittatas
steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7) with an empty muffin tin inside. Grate the courgette and slice the spring onions. Heat the oil and cook the spring onions 3 minutes. Add the garlic and grated courgette, cook 1 minute more, and cool.

Beat the eggs with the dill and yogurt; season and stir in the courgette mixture. Line the hot tin with eight muffin cases, divide the mixture between them, and bake 15-18 minutes until set and golden. Serve hot or cold with salad.

name: Masala frittata with avocado salsa

ingredients.raw_text: 3 onions, 2½ thinly sliced, ½ finely chopped
1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped

steps: Heat the oil in an ovenproof pan and cook the onions over medium heat about 10 minutes until soft and golden. Add the Madras paste for 1 minute, then half the tomatoes and half the chilli; cook until thick and the tomatoes have broken down.

Heat the grill to high. Mix half the coriander into the eggs and season, then pour over the onion mixture. Stir gently once or twice, then cook over low heat 8-10 minutes until nearly set. Finish under the grill 3-5 minutes until set.
For the salsa, combine the avocado, remaining chilli and tomatoes, chopped onion, remaining coriander, and lemon juice. Season and serve alongside the frittata.

name: Baked salmon

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp olive oil or melted butter
chopped herbs, lemon slices and steamed long-stem broccoli, to serve (optional)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Brush the salmon with oil or butter and season.
Place in an ovenproof dish, covering if you prefer tender fish or leaving uncovered for a slightly roasted texture.
Roast 10-15 minutes (about 4 minutes per cm of thickness) until opaque and flaking easily. Serve with chopped herbs, lemon slices, and steamed broccoli.

name: Roasted cauliflower steaks
steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7) and line a tray with parchment. Cut two thick steaks from the center of the cauliflower (save the rest). Rub with paprika and half the oil, season, and roast 15-20 minutes until cooked through.
Meanwhile, chop the pepper, olives, parsley, and capers; combine with the remaining oil and vinegar, and season. Spoon over the cooked steaks and top with flaked almonds.

name: Masala omelette muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 2 large or 4 small garlic cloves, finely grated
1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7) and lightly oil four ramekins. Grate the courgettes and squeeze out as much liquid as possible. Combine with the remaining ingredients except the feta.
Divide between the ramekins, top with feta, and bake on a tray 20-25 minutes until risen and set. Serve hot or cold with salad, slaw, or vegetables.

name: Gluten-free banana pancakes

ingredients.raw_text: pinch of baking powder (gluten-free if coeliac)

steps: Mash the banana to a thick puree in a bowl.
Stir in the beaten eggs, baking powder, and vanilla.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat with a little oil.
Spoon in half the batter as two pancakes, cook 1-2 minutes per side, then transfer to a plate. Repeat with the remaining oil and batter.
Top with chopped pecans and raspberries.

name: Cardamom & peach quinoa porridge
steps: Combine the quinoa, oats, and cardamom pods with 250ml water and 100ml almond milk in a pan. Bring to a boil, then simmer gently 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the remaining almond milk and cook 5 minutes more until creamy.
Remove the cardamom pods, spoon into bowls, and top with peaches and maple syrup.

name: Kale, tomato & poached egg on toast

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Bring a pan of water to a boil. Heat the oil and cook the kale, garlic, and chilli flakes 4 minutes, stirring, until crisping and wilted to half its size; set aside.
Poach the eggs in the boiling water 2 minutes. Meanwhile, toast the bread.
Lift out the eggs with a slotted spoon and top each toast with kale, a poached egg, cherry tomatoes, and feta.

name: Porridge with blueberry compote

ingredients.raw_text: just under ½ x 200ml tub 0% fat Greek-style yogurt

steps: Simmer the oats with 400ml water, stirring occasionally, about 2 minutes until thickened. Off the heat, stir in a third of the yogurt.
At the same time, gently poach the blueberries in a pan with 1 tbsp water (and honey, if using) until thawed and tender but still holding shape.
Spoon the porridge into bowls, add the rest of the yogurt, and top with the blueberries.

name: Pistachio nut & spiced apple Bircher muesli

ingredients.raw_text: 3 tbsp pomegranate seeds or mixed berries

steps: Combine the base ingredients (except yogurt) with water and soak at least 20 minutes, or overnight. Once softened, stir in the yogurt, divide between two bowls, and top with the topping.

name: Eggy spelt bread with orange cheese & raspberries

ingredients.food_name: Cottage cheese < 10 % fat in dry matter

steps: Beat the eggs and orange juice in a shallow bowl. Soak the bread in the mixture 2 minutes, turning halfway.
Meanwhile, mix the cheese and orange zest. Heat the oil in a non-stick pan; when hot, cook the soaked bread undisturbed a couple of minutes, then flip and cook 1-2 minutes more.
Plate the bread, top with the cheese mixture, raspberries, and honey if desired.

name: Honey nut crunch pears
steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Halve the pears lengthways, remove the core, and top with a little butter and mixed spice. Arrange in a baking dish and roast 5 minutes until softening. Meanwhile, melt the honey with more butter in the microwave 30 seconds and toss with the cornflakes and nuts.
Top the pears with the cornflake mixture and bake 5 minutes more until deep golden. Cool briefly (they crisp as they cool) and serve warm with ice cream.

name: Hash browns with mustard & smoked salmon

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp wholegrain mustard or horseradish sauce
soured cream or crème fraîche, to serve

steps: Grate the unpeeled potato onto a tea towel, wrap, and squeeze out excess water. Transfer to a bowl with the flour and mustard or horseradish; season and mix.
Shape into 8 flattened patties. Heat the butter and oil in a pan and cook 2-3 minutes per side over medium heat until golden.
Stack on plates and top with smoked salmon, soured cream or creme fraiche, and chives.

name: Breakfast smoothie

ingredients.raw_text: about 140g blackberries, blueberries, raspberries or strawberries  (or use a mix), plus extra to serve
apple juice  or mineral water, optional

steps: Blend the banana and berries until smooth. With the blender running, add juice or water to reach your preferred consistency. Top with extra fruit, a drizzle of honey, and serve.

name: Perfect porridge

ingredients.raw_text: 350ml milk  or water, or a mixture of the two
Greek yogurt, thinned with a little milk and clear honey, to serve

steps: Combine the oats, milk or water, and a pinch of salt in a saucepan. Bring to a boil and simmer 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally, watching it doesn't stick.
Alternatively, microwave the same mixture in a large bowl on high 5 minutes, stirring halfway, then rest 2 minutes.
Serve topped with Greek yogurt thinned with milk and a drizzle of honey.

name: Chicken satay salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 skinless chicken breast fillets (or use turkey breast)
1 tbsp crunchy peanut butter (choose a sugar-free version with no palm oil, if possible)
sunflower oil, for wiping the pan
2 Little Gem lettuce hearts, cut into wedges
1 banana shallot, halved and thinly sliced

steps: Combine the tamari, curry powder, cumin, garlic, and honey. Slice the chicken breasts horizontally into 4 fillets, add to the marinade, and coat well. Chill at least 1 hour, or overnight.

Meanwhile, mix the peanut butter with the chilli sauce, lime juice, and water into a spoonable sauce. Cook the marinated chicken in an oiled pan, covered, 5-6 minutes over medium heat, flipping near the end, until cooked but still moist. Rest, covered, a few minutes.

While resting, toss the lettuce with the cucumber, shallot, coriander, and pomegranate; divide onto plates and spoon over a little sauce. Slice the chicken over the salad and finish with the remaining sauce. Serve warm.

name: Sweet potato toasts with curried chickpeas

ingredients.raw_text: 3 large sweet potatoes (about 350g), trimmed and thinly sliced lengthways into 12
⅓ x 30g pack of coriander, chopped

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Brush the sweet potato slices with oil, arrange on a tray, and bake 20-25 minutes, checking to avoid uneven browning.

For the chickpeas, heat the oil and cook the celery 5 minutes, stirring. Add the garlic, curry powder, and cumin seeds briefly, then the tomatoes, tomato puree, and bouillon. Stir in the chickpeas and frozen spinach, cover, and simmer 15 minutes. Finish with coriander.

Top six sweet potato slices with half the chickpea mixture for serving (three per person). Cool and chill the rest, reheating gently with a splash of water and warming the toast in a dry pan before serving; keeps refrigerated up to three days.

name: Halloumi, carrot & orange salad

ingredients.raw_text: 3 tbsp rapeseed or olive oil, plus extra for frying

steps: Peel and segment the oranges over a bowl to catch the juice, squeezing extra juice from the pith. Whisk in the mustard, honey, vinegar, oil, and seasoning.

Peel carrot ribbons into the dressing and toss gently. Cook the halloumi in a little oil until golden on both sides. Toss the watercress through the dressed carrots, plate, and top with halloumi and orange segments.

name: Healthy carrot soup

ingredients.raw_text: a pinch of cayenne pepper, plus extra to serve
1 tbsp soured cream, plus extra to serve

steps: Blend the carrots with the ginger, turmeric, cayenne, bread, soured cream, and stock until smooth. Heat through and serve with a swirl of soured cream or extra cayenne.

name: Vegan chickpea curry jacket potatoes

ingredients.raw_text: lemon wedges and coriander leaves, to serve

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Prick the sweet potatoes, place on a tray, and roast 45 minutes until tender.
Meanwhile, melt the coconut oil and fry the cumin seeds 1 minute until fragrant. Add the onion and cook 7-10 minutes until soft.

Add the garlic, ginger, and chilli; cook 2-3 minutes. Stir in the spices and tikka masala paste, cook 2 minutes more, then add the tomatoes. Simmer, add the chickpeas, and cook 20 minutes until thickened. Season.
Split the roasted sweet potatoes open on four plates. Spoon over the curry, squeeze over lemon, season, and finish with coriander.

name: Beetroot soup

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tsp onion seeds (nigella), plus extra to serve

steps: Blend the beetroot, lentils, apple, garlic, onion seeds, stock, and seasoning until smooth. Heat through and top with extra onion seeds if desired.

name: Vegan leek & potato soup

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp rapeseed oil, plus a drizzle to serve (optional)
500ml vegan vegetable stock, made with 1½ tsp bouillon powder
chopped chives and bread, to serve

steps: Heat the oil and cook the garlic and leeks, stirring, until softening. Add the potatoes and stock, cover, and simmer 15 minutes until soft.

Stir in the almond milk, remove from the heat, and blend to your preferred texture — chunky or smooth. Reheat gently if needed, then serve topped with chives, a drizzle of oil, and bread. Freezes up to three months.

name: Griddled salad jar

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: ¼ cucumber, deseeded and roughly chopped

steps: For the dressing, gently heat the onion with the coriander seeds, vinegar, and water 15 minutes until soft and bright pink; cool.
Heat a griddle pan until smoking. Brush the aubergine and courgette with oil and griddle 3-4 minutes per side until char marks form. Cool and season.

Whisk the lemon juice and oil into the cooled onion mixture; season. Layer into a jar: tomatoes, feta, chickpeas, olives, then sundried tomatoes with their oil, the griddled vegetables, cucumber, pepper, and herbs. Seal and chill until ready.
To serve, tip out onto a platter so the dressing coats everything.

name: Red lentil & sweet potato pâté

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp olive oil, plus extra for drizzling
1 tsp smoked paprika, plus a little extra
1 small sweet potato, peeled and diced
3 thyme sprigs, leaves chopped, plus a little extra to decorate (optional)
500ml low-sodium vegetable stock (choose a vegan brand, if desired)
1 tsp red wine vinegar (choose a vegan brand, if desired)
pitta bread and vegetable sticks, to serve

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion slowly until soft and golden. Add the paprika for 2 minutes, then the sweet potato, lentils, thyme, and stock. Simmer 20 minutes until tender.
Add the vinegar and seasoning, then mash to your preferred texture. Chill 1 hour, then drizzle with oil, dust with extra paprika, and top with thyme. Serve with pitta and vegetable sticks.

name: Carrot & hummus roll-ups

ingredients.food_name: Wrap filled with salad and fried chicken breast fillet

steps: Spread the hummus over the wraps, top with grated carrot and a handful of rocket, and season. Roll up and eat.

name: Favourite pasta salad
steps: Cook the pasta, adding the peas for the final 2 minutes. Drain, rinse under cold water, and drain again. Combine with the herbs, lemon zest and juice, oil, and seasoning. Cover and chill, portioning out as needed; after a few days, stir in a little extra oil or something creamy to loosen it.

name: Prawn sweet chilli noodle salad

ingredients.raw_text: 200g cooked king prawns, defrosted if frozen

steps: Boil the noodles 4 minutes, drain, and cool under running water. Drain again and cut into shorter lengths.
Halve and deseed the cucumber, slice into half-moons, and add to the noodles with the onions, tomatoes, chilli, and prawns.
Whisk the lime zest, juice, and chilli sauce into a dressing and toss through. Plate over a handful of spinach and top with the cashews.

name: Sardine tomato pasta with gremolata

ingredients.raw_text: small handful of parsley, finely chopped

steps: Cook the pasta per pack instructions. Heat 1 tbsp oil from the sardine tin and sizzle the capers and half the garlic 1-2 minutes until fragrant. Add the tomatoes and cook 4-5 minutes until softened and bursting. Stir in the sardines and rocket, breaking up the fish and wilting the leaves. Season.
For the gremolata, combine the lemon zest, parsley, and remaining garlic; season. Drain the pasta and top with the sardine sauce and gremolata.

name: Gingery broccoli-fry with cashews

ingredients.raw_text: 320g head of  broccoli, stalks and florets separated
1 red pepper, deseeded and cut into thin strips
1 large carrot (160g), cut into thin strips
1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped, plus extra sliced, to serve
7g chopped coriander, plus extra to serve

steps: Pulse the broccoli stalks in a food processor until finely chopped, then add the florets and pulse to a rice-like texture.
Toast the cashews in a dry pan and set aside. Heat the oil and stir-fry the ginger, onion, pepper, carrot, garlic, and chilli 2-3 minutes until browning, then cover and cook 2 minutes more.

Add the broccoli rice and water; stir-fry 3 minutes until tender. Add the tamari, lime juice and zest, and coriander, then pour in the eggs and stir-fry briefly to just set. Serve topped with the cashews, extra coriander, and chilli.

name: Poached salmon with tarragon

ingredients.raw_text: small pack tarragon, leaves only, chopped
1 garlic clove, crushed to a paste
cooked baby new potatoes, to serve

steps: Zest and wedge the lemon. Combine the creme fraiche, tarragon, garlic, lemon zest, and a squeeze of juice; season and set aside.
Simmer the green beans in salted water 3 minutes until just done. Drain, cool under running water, and toss with the creme fraiche mixture.
Return the pan to a gentle simmer and poach the salmon 8-10 minutes to your liking. Drain on kitchen paper and serve with the green beans and baby new potatoes.

name: Prawn, fennel & rocket risotto

ingredients.raw_text: 1 small fennel bulb, cored and finely chopped

steps: Bring the stock to a simmer. Meanwhile, heat the oil and cook the onion, garlic, and fennel over low heat 10 minutes until softened but not colored. Add the rice and stir 2 minutes until sizzling. Add the stock a ladleful at a time over medium heat, stirring constantly and letting each addition absorb before adding more.

Once the rice is nearly done, add the prawns, lemon zest, and seasoning. Continue adding stock and cooking 3-4 minutes until the prawns are pink and the rice is tender. Off the heat, stir in the rocket and lemon juice. Rest 2 minutes before serving.

name: Mediterranean vegetables with lamb

ingredients.raw_text: 250g lean lamb fillet, trimmed of any fat and thinly sliced
½ tsp each ground cumin, paprika and ground coriander
1 red, 1 orange and 1 green pepper, cut into chunks

steps: Heat the oil and cook the lamb and shallots over high heat 2-3 minutes until golden. Add the courgettes and stir-fry 3-4 minutes until softening.
Add the spices, then the peppers and garlic. Lower the heat and cook 4-5 minutes until softening.
Add the stock, then the tomatoes; season, cover, and simmer 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender. Finish with coriander.

name: Steamed fish & pak choi parcels

ingredients.raw_text: 4 plaice, haddock or other MSC-certified white fish fillets

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Place each fish fillet on a large square of foil, top with the pak choi, spring onions, chilli, and ginger, and pull up the edges.
Combine the soy sauce, lime juice, and water; spoon over each fillet. Crimp the foil tightly to seal, leaving no gaps.
Bake the parcels 10-15 minutes until the fish is cooked through. Open and drizzle with sesame oil. Serve with rice.

name: Pork & bulgur-stuffed peppers
steps: Microwave the peppers cut-side down on high 4 minutes until cooked but still holding shape, adding more time in 1-minute increments if needed.
Brown the pork in a dry pan, breaking up any lumps. Add the garlic and spices for 1 minute, then the bulgur and stock. Cover and simmer 10 minutes until the bulgur is soft.
Heat the grill. Stir half the parsley into the bulgur, stuff into the peppers, and grill to crisp. Finish with the remaining parsley and serve with yogurt mixed with parsley.

name: Instant frozen berry yogurt
steps: Blend the berries, yogurt, and honey or agave 20 seconds until it reaches an ice-cream-like texture. Scoop into bowls and serve.

name: Almond, raisin & popcorn trail mix
steps: Heat the oil, add the corn, cover, and cook 2-3 minutes until popped. Cool, then mix in the raisins, almonds, and puffed wheat. Store in an airtight container.

name: Avocado & strawberry ices

ingredients.raw_text: 1 avocado, stoned, peeled and roughly chopped

steps: Blend the strawberries (reserving a few), avocado, vinegar, and vanilla until as smooth as possible. Add maple syrup only if needed for sweetness.
Divide into containers, add a reserved strawberry to each, cover, and freeze. Let soften 5-10 minutes before eating.

name: Watermelon salsa
steps: Finely chop the watermelon, shallots, and coriander. Combine with the lime juice and oil, season, and serve as a dip or over nachos.

name: Smoky chipotle pepper salsa
steps: Heat the grill to high. Char the peppers skin-side up until blackened, then cover in a bowl until cool enough to peel.
Deseed the tomatoes and blend with the garlic, lime juice, oil, and chipotle paste. Add the peeled peppers and pulse to a chunky texture. Stir through the onion and coriander before serving.

name: Beetroot & mint dip

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tsp chopped mint, plus a few leaves for sprinkling

steps: Blend the beetroot and cumin with seasoning until smooth. Transfer to a bowl, stir in the mint and lemon juice, then gently swirl in the creme fraiche for a marbled effect. Top with mint leaves and nigella seeds.

name: Fresh orange lollies
steps: Zest one orange, then peel and chop the flesh of all the oranges. Blend with the zest and pears until smooth. Pour into lolly molds, insert sticks, and freeze solid.

name: Spicy chickpeas
steps: Toss the chickpeas with the oil and chilli powder until coated. Spread on a baking tray and cook 25 minutes. Cool, then sprinkle with sea salt before serving.

name: Chicken with Spanish-style butter beans

ingredients.raw_text: 400g can of butter beans, drained and rinsed

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Whisk the roasted garlic, oil, lemon zest and juice, smoked paprika, and seasoning together. Toss with the butter beans, peppers, tomatoes, and rocket. Divide into containers, top with the chicken, and crumble over the feta.

name: Giant couscous salad with charred veg & tangy pesto

ingredients.raw_text: 2 green or orange peppers, deseeded and cubed

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss the beetroot, onions, and peppers with oil, spread on a lined tray, and roast 35 minutes. Add the cherry tomatoes and roast 10 minutes more until softened and tender.

Meanwhile, cook the couscous per pack instructions, rinse, and drain. For the pesto, blend the coriander, half the parsley, garlic, chilli, cumin, vinegar, oil, most of the pine nuts, and water until smooth.
Toss the roasted vegetables and remaining parsley through the couscous, top with the pesto, and scatter with the reserved pine nuts. For two servings, eat half now and chill the rest for another day.

name: Vegan kale pesto pasta
steps: Blanch the kale 30 seconds, then plunge into ice water 5 minutes. Drain and pat dry.
Blend the basil, garlic, seeds, oil, nutritional yeast, lemon juice and zest, and kale until smooth; season, thinning with water if too thick.
Cook the pasta per pack instructions and toss with the pesto to serve.

name: Lentil salad with tahini dressing

ingredients.raw_text: 2 large carrots, cut into thin sticks (320g)
2 medium red onions, halved and sliced
good handful of mint, roughly chopped
a little smoked paprika, to serve

steps: Heat the oil in a wok and fry the sweet potato 5 minutes, stirring, until softening (cover if browning too fast). Add the carrot, courgette, onion, cumin, and ginger; cook over high heat, stirring, until tender and lightly charred. Stir in the seeds for the last couple of minutes. Off the heat, add the lentils, bouillon powder, lemon zest, mint, and parsley.
Meanwhile, combine the tahini, garlic, yogurt, and water into a dressing. Spoon the salad into bowls, top with the dressing and paprika if using. For meal prep, portion into containers and chill.

name: Herby quinoa, feta & pomegranate salad

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 200g pomegranate seeds from tub or fruit
85g toasted pine nuts or toasted flaked almonds
small pack each coriander, flat leaf parsley and mint, roughly chopped

steps: Cook the quinoa per pack instructions until tender with a little bite. Drain and spread on a platter to cool and steam-dry quickly.
Once just cool, stir through the remaining ingredients with plenty of seasoning.

name: Quick chicken hummus bowl

ingredients.raw_text: 1cooked chicken breast, sliced at an angle

steps: Combine 2 tbsp hummus with half the lemon juice, the zest, and enough water for a drizzly dressing. Separate the grain pouch, divide between bowls, and toss with the dressing. Top each with a handful of spinach.

Squeeze the remaining lemon juice over the avocado halves and add one to each bowl. Divide the chicken, pomegranate, onion, almonds, and remaining hummus between the bowls, mixing gently just before eating.

name: Bombay potato frittata

ingredients.raw_text: ½ green chilli, deseeded and finely chopped
1 small bunch of coriander, finely chopped

steps: Boil the potatoes 6 minutes until tender, drain, and steam-dry. Meanwhile, microwave the spinach with a little water, covered, 3 minutes until wilted.

Heat the oil and cook the onion over medium heat 10 minutes until golden and sticky. Add the garlic, coriander, cumin, mustard seeds, and turmeric; cook 1 minute more. Add the tomatoes and wilted spinach, cook 3 minutes, then add the potatoes.
Heat the grill to medium. Beat the eggs with the chilli and most of the coriander and pour over the potato mixture. Grill 4-5 minutes until golden and just set with a slight wobble.
Cool slightly and slice into wedges. Mix the mango chutney, yogurt, and remaining coriander, and serve alongside.

name: Veggie olive wraps with mustard vinaigrette
steps: Combine all the filling ingredients and toss well.

Lay the tortilla on foil and pile the filling along one side - it will look like a lot but compacts as you roll. Roll tightly from the filling side, tucking in the ends, then wrap the foil around it. Slice in half to eat right away, or leave whole and wrap in parchment to take with you.

name: Sesame chicken noodles

ingredients.raw_text: ½ tsp chilli flakes, plus extra to serve
½ cucumber, seeds removed and cut into half moons
½ small pack mint, roughly chopped

steps: Whisk the tahini, lime juice, soy sauce, roasted garlic flesh, sesame oil, and chilli flakes, adding water for a creamy dressing.
Toss the noodles, leftover chicken, aubergine, and carrot with the dressing. Gently fold in the cucumber and mint. Divide into containers and top with extra chilli flakes.

name: Bulgur & quinoa lunch bowls

ingredients.raw_text: 150g bulgur and quinoa (this comes ready mixed)

steps: Combine the onion and bulgur mix in a pan with water, thyme, and bouillon. Cover and cook over low heat 15 minutes, then rest 10 minutes until the liquid is absorbed. Cool, remove the thyme, and divide between containers.
For the avocado topping, combine all ingredients except the rocket and pile onto two portions of bulgur, then top with rocket.

For the beetroot topping, layer the chickpeas onto the remaining portions, then toss the beetroot with tomato, mint, cumin, cinnamon, oil, and vinegar. Add the orange, pile on top, and finish with pine nuts and extra cinnamon. Chill until needed.

name: Lemon & spinach rice with feta

ingredients.raw_text: 700ml hot vegetable stock, made with 1 tsp bouillon powder
1 lemon, zested, 1/2 juiced, 1/2 cut into 4 wedges

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onions and garlic 10 minutes, stirring often, until softened.
Add the rice, stock, frozen spinach, and half each of the dill and lemon zest. Cover, lower the heat, and cook 25 minutes until the rice is tender.

Stir in the remaining dill, lemon zest, nuts, and feta along with the lemon juice. Serve half right away with lemon wedges; cool and chill the rest up to three days, reheating thoroughly before serving with more lemon wedges.

name: Sweet potato toasts with curried chickpeas

ingredients.raw_text: 3 large sweet potatoes (about 350g), trimmed and thinly sliced lengthways into 12
⅓ x 30g pack of coriander, chopped

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Brush the sweet potato slices with oil, arrange on a tray, and bake 20-25 minutes, checking to avoid uneven browning.

For the chickpeas, heat the oil and cook the celery 5 minutes, stirring. Add the garlic, curry powder, and cumin seeds briefly, then the tomatoes, tomato puree, and bouillon. Stir in the chickpeas and frozen spinach, cover, and simmer 15 minutes. Finish with coriander.

Top six sweet potato slices with half the chickpea mixture for serving (three per person). Cool and chill the rest, reheating gently with a splash of water and warming the toast in a dry pan before serving; keeps refrigerated up to three days.

name: Sweet potato & cauliflower lentil bowl

ingredients.raw_text: 1 large sweet potato, skin left on, scrubbed and cut into medium chunks
1 cauliflower, cut into large florets, stalk diced

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss the sweet potato and cauliflower with the garam masala, half the oil, and seasoning. Spread on a tray with the garlic and roast 30-35 minutes until cooked.
Meanwhile, simmer the lentils in 400ml water 20-25 minutes until tender but with bite. Drain.

Mash the roasted garlic and combine with the remaining oil, ginger, mustard, a pinch of sugar, and a third of the lime juice. Whisk in the warm lentils and season. Grate the carrots, shred the cabbage, and chop the coriander; add the remaining lime juice and season to taste.
Split the lentils between four bowls or containers and top each with a portion of the carrot slaw and roasted vegetables.

name: Aubergine dhal with tomato & onion raita

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red chilli, seeded and thinly sliced
⅓ of a 100g pack coriander, chopped
small handful fresh mint, chopped, plus a few small leaves

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion, garlic, and ginger 8 minutes until softened. Add the turmeric, coriander, cumin, and chilli; cook 20 seconds, stirring.

Add the boiling water, bouillon, lentils, and aubergine. Cover and simmer 15 minutes until the lentils break down. Stir in the chickpeas with their liquid if more liquid is needed, and cook 5 minutes more.
Meanwhile, combine the red onion, coriander, mint, and tomatoes with the yogurt for the raita. Spoon the dhal into bowls, top with raita, and finish with mint leaves.

name: Cumin roast veg with tahini dressing
steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss all the vegetables with the cumin seeds, oil, and vinegar in a roasting tin. Roast 45-50 minutes until tender and starting to char.
Meanwhile, combine the tahini and peanut butter with the lemon juice, coriander, garlic, and water to make a dressing.
Cool the roasted vegetables slightly, then toss with the mint, coriander, lemon zest, and chickpeas.
For meal prep, serve two portions now with eggs, some dressing, and half the spinach; save the rest for another day without the eggs.

name: Brown rice tabbouleh with eggs & parsley
steps: Simmer the rice with the thyme and celery 20 minutes until tender. Meanwhile, boil the eggs 7 minutes, cool in cold water, and peel.
Drain the rice and combine with the bouillon, lemon zest and juice, and red onion. Scatter with parsley and pomegranate. Plate or box up, then halve or quarter the eggs on top.

name: Balsamic feta & mint puy lentils

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small bunch of mint, finely chopped, plus extra leaves to serve

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Whisk 1 tbsp oil with 1 tbsp balsamic vinegar and half the mint; stir in the feta and set aside.
Defrost the peas by covering with boiling water in a bowl, then draining. Warm the lentils in a sieve under boiling water, then drain and combine with the peas.
Add the radishes, remaining oil, vinegar, and mint; toss together. Divide into bowls and top with the marinated feta and black pepper.

name: Moroccan-style chickpea soup

ingredients.raw_text: 400g can chopped plum tomatoes  with garlic
large handful coriander or parsley and flatbread, to serve

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion and celery gently 10 minutes, stirring, until softened. Add the cumin and cook 1 minute more.

Raise the heat and add the stock, tomatoes, chickpeas, and pepper. Simmer 8 minutes. Stir in the broad beans and lemon juice, cook 2 minutes more, and season. Top with lemon zest and herbs; serve with flatbread.

name: Roasted cauli-broc bowl with tahini hummus

ingredients.food_name: Mixed vegetables (carrots, cauliflower and broccoli florets) raw

steps: The night before, set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Roast the cauliflower and broccoli with the oil and a little sea salt 25-30 minutes until browned. Cool completely.

Assemble each bowl with quinoa, then layer beetroot, spinach, cauliflower, broccoli, and walnuts. Combine the tahini, hummus, lemon juice, and 1 tbsp water; drizzle over just before eating. Serve with lemon wedges.

name: Cumin-spiced halloumi with corn & tomato slaw

ingredients.raw_text: 100g fine green beans, blanched, trimmed and halved
1 red onion, halved and finely sliced

steps: Combine the lime zest and juice with the oil, thyme, turmeric, cumin, coriander, and garlic. Add the halloumi and turn gently to coat (it breaks easily).
For the slaw, mix the lime juice and zest, yogurt, coriander, and chilli, then stir in the corn, red pepper, beans, tomatoes, onion, and cabbage.
Fry the halloumi in batches in a hot non-stick or griddle pan 1 minute per side. Serve over the slaw. For two servings, halve the portions and chill the rest for another day.

name: Vegan mushroom & potato hash

ingredients.raw_text: 2 medium potatoes (275g), no need to peel, cut into slim wedges

steps: Blend the oats and soya milk in a bowl to break down the texture. Leave to soak 10 minutes.

Meanwhile, boil the potatoes 5 minutes and drain. Heat the oil and cook the mushrooms, onion, and paprika until softened. Add the potatoes and cook 10 minutes, turning occasionally. Stir in the halved tomatoes and cook 5 minutes more.

The oat mixture should now be stiff. Knead in the baking powder by hand and split in two. With damp hands, press each half into a thin disc and cook in a dry non-stick pan 2 minutes per side. Plate and top with the hash to serve.

name: Vegan chickpea curry jacket potatoes

ingredients.raw_text: lemon wedges and coriander leaves, to serve

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Prick the sweet potatoes, place on a tray, and roast 45 minutes until tender.
Meanwhile, melt the coconut oil and fry the cumin seeds 1 minute until fragrant. Add the onion and cook 7-10 minutes until soft.

Add the garlic, ginger, and chilli; cook 2-3 minutes. Stir in the spices and tikka masala paste, cook 2 minutes more, then add the tomatoes. Simmer, add the chickpeas, and cook 20 minutes until thickened. Season.
Split the roasted sweet potatoes open on four plates. Spoon over the curry, squeeze over lemon, season, and finish with coriander.

name: Vegan leek & potato soup

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp rapeseed oil, plus a drizzle to serve (optional)
500ml vegan vegetable stock, made with 1½ tsp bouillon powder
chopped chives and bread, to serve

steps: Heat the oil and cook the garlic and leeks, stirring, until softening. Add the potatoes and stock, cover, and simmer 15 minutes until soft.

Stir in the almond milk, remove from the heat, and blend to your preferred texture — chunky or smooth. Reheat gently if needed, then serve topped with chives, a drizzle of oil, and bread. Freezes up to three months.

name: Roasted cauli-broc bowl with tahini hummus

ingredients.food_name: Mixed vegetables (carrots, cauliflower and broccoli florets) raw

steps: The night before, set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Roast the cauliflower and broccoli with the oil and a little sea salt 25-30 minutes until browned. Cool completely.

Assemble each bowl with quinoa, then layer beetroot, spinach, cauliflower, broccoli, and walnuts. Combine the tahini, hummus, lemon juice, and 1 tbsp water; drizzle over just before eating. Serve with lemon wedges.

name: Sesame parsnip & wild rice tabbouleh

ingredients.raw_text: 500g (5 medium) parsnips, peeled and cut into thumb-sized pieces
2 ½ tbsp cold pressed rapeseed oil
1 small pack mint, leaves roughly chopped

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss the parsnips with most of the oil, the turmeric, coriander, and seasoning, then coat in sesame seeds. Roast 30 minutes until tender.

Meanwhile, cook the wild rice per pack instructions. Heat the remaining oil and cook the onion with water 10-15 minutes, stirring, until fully soft. Raise the heat, add half the vinegar, and cook until bright pink.
Whisk the tahini with the remaining vinegar and warm water into a creamy dressing; season.
Drain the rice and stir in the onions and most of the herbs. Divide onto plates, top with the parsnips, pomegranate seeds, and remaining herbs, and drizzle with the tahini dressing.

name: Sweet potato & cauliflower lentil bowl

ingredients.raw_text: 1 large sweet potato, skin left on, scrubbed and cut into medium chunks
1 cauliflower, cut into large florets, stalk diced

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss the sweet potato and cauliflower with the garam masala, half the oil, and seasoning. Spread on a tray with the garlic and roast 30-35 minutes until cooked.
Meanwhile, simmer the lentils in 400ml water 20-25 minutes until tender but with bite. Drain.

Mash the roasted garlic and combine with the remaining oil, ginger, mustard, a pinch of sugar, and a third of the lime juice. Whisk in the warm lentils and season. Grate the carrots, shred the cabbage, and chop the coriander; add the remaining lime juice and season to taste.
Split the lentils between four bowls or containers and top each with a portion of the carrot slaw and roasted vegetables.

name: Sweet potato toasts with curried chickpeas

ingredients.raw_text: 3 large sweet potatoes (about 350g), trimmed and thinly sliced lengthways into 12
⅓ x 30g pack of coriander, chopped

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Brush the sweet potato slices with oil, arrange on a tray, and bake 20-25 minutes, checking to avoid uneven browning.

For the chickpeas, heat the oil and cook the celery 5 minutes, stirring. Add the garlic, curry powder, and cumin seeds briefly, then the tomatoes, tomato puree, and bouillon. Stir in the chickpeas and frozen spinach, cover, and simmer 15 minutes. Finish with coriander.

Top six sweet potato slices with half the chickpea mixture for serving (three per person). Cool and chill the rest, reheating gently with a splash of water and warming the toast in a dry pan before serving; keeps refrigerated up to three days.

name: Fennel, roast lemon & tomato salad

ingredients.raw_text: 500g mixed tomatoes (I used cherry tomatoes and some larger ones)

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a tray with parchment. Slice one lemon thinly, spread on the tray, drizzle with oil, and sprinkle with sugar. Roast 20-25 minutes until shriveled and caramelized (can be done ahead and kept at room temperature).
Meanwhile, chop the tomatoes and thinly slice the fennel, reserving the fronds. Combine with the remaining oil, juice from the second lemon, and pomegranate seeds; season.
Just before serving, chop the herbs and toss through with the roasted lemon and reserved fennel fronds.

name: Spaghetti puttanesca with red beans & spinach
steps: Boil the spaghetti 10-12 minutes until al dente. Meanwhile, cook the onion in the oil, covered, until tender and golden. Stir in the chilli, garlic, and cherry tomatoes.
Add the vinegar, capers, olives, and paprika with a ladle of pasta water. Stir in the beans and warm through.
Wilt the spinach in the pasta water, then drain well. Toss with the tomato-bean mixture, parsley, and basil, and serve.

name: Avocado hummus & crudités

ingredients.raw_text: pinch chilli flakes, plus extra to serve

steps: Blend the avocado, chickpeas, garlic, chilli flakes, and lime juice until smooth; season. Top with coriander and extra chilli flakes, and serve with carrot, pepper, and sugar snap crudites. Can be made the night before.

name: Linguine with avocado, tomato & lime

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped (optional)

steps: Cook the pasta per pack instructions, about 10 minutes. Meanwhile, combine the lime juice and zest with the avocado, tomatoes, coriander, onion, and chilli (if using). Drain the pasta, toss through the mixture, and serve warm or cold.

name: Next-level chicken caesar salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 chicken breasts  with skin on (if you can get it) for chicken crackling
100ml olive oil, plus about 4 tbsp for the dressing

ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Remove the chicken skin and reserve. Flatten the chicken slightly under cling film and marinate in a bowl with half the lemon juice, one garlic clove, oil, and seasoning.
For optional crackling, lay the skin on a lined tray, salt it, cover with another sheet of parchment, and weigh down with a tray. Roast 25-30 minutes until crisp and golden.

Meanwhile, tear the crustless sourdough into pieces, toss on a tray with the remaining oil and grated parmesan, and bake 15-25 minutes alongside the crackling, tossing occasionally, until golden and crunchy. Both can be made a day ahead and stored airtight.

For the dressing, crush the remaining garlic and mash in the anchovies. Combine with grated parmesan, the remaining lemon juice, mayo, and mustard, then whisk in the oil gradually. Can be made up to three hours ahead.
Heat a griddle pan until smoking and cook the chicken 4-5 minutes per side until just done. Trim the lettuce into bite-sized pieces, slice the chicken, and shave the remaining parmesan.
Combine the lettuce, croutons, chicken, and cheese in a large bowl, toss with most of the dressing, then finish with more dressing and the crackling.

name: Chicken satay salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 skinless chicken breast fillets (or use turkey breast)
1 tbsp crunchy peanut butter (choose a sugar-free version with no palm oil, if possible)
sunflower oil, for wiping the pan
2 Little Gem lettuce hearts, cut into wedges
1 banana shallot, halved and thinly sliced

steps: Combine the tamari, curry powder, cumin, garlic, and honey. Slice the chicken breasts horizontally into 4 fillets, add to the marinade, and coat well. Chill at least 1 hour, or overnight.

Meanwhile, mix the peanut butter with the chilli sauce, lime juice, and water into a spoonable sauce. Cook the marinated chicken in an oiled pan, covered, 5-6 minutes over medium heat, flipping near the end, until cooked but still moist. Rest, covered, a few minutes.

While resting, toss the lettuce with the cucumber, shallot, coriander, and pomegranate; divide onto plates and spoon over a little sauce. Slice the chicken over the salad and finish with the remaining sauce. Serve warm.

name: Minty griddled chicken & peach salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp mint, finely chopped, plus a few leaves to serve
2 peaches (200g), each cut into 8 thick wedges
1 large Little Gem lettuce (165g), roughly shredded

steps: Combine the lime zest and juice with the oil and mint; mix half with the garlic. Slice the chicken and toss in the garlic mixture with black pepper.

Cook the beans 3-4 minutes until just tender. Meanwhile, griddle the chicken and onion a few minutes per side until cooked; set aside. Quickly griddle the peaches (or use a hot non-stick pan with a little oil).

Toss the warm beans and onion in the remaining mint mixture, and pile onto a platter with the lettuce and rocket. Top with avocado, peaches, and chicken, and scatter with mint. Serve warm with the potatoes.

name: Chicken fattoush

ingredients.raw_text: 200g pack cooked chicken pieces (or leftover cooked chicken)

steps: Whisk the lemon juice while slowly adding the oil until thickened; season.
Add the lettuce, tomatoes, parsley, cucumber, chicken, and spring onions, and toss to coat.
Toast the pittas until crisp, chop into chunks, and scatter over the salad with the sumac. Serve right away.

name: Chopped green salad with herby chilli dressing
steps: Blend all the dressing ingredients together and season to taste.
Combine the salad ingredients in a bowl, drizzle with the dressing, and toss.

name: Chilli green salad

ingredients.raw_text: 145g bag of spinach watercress  and rocket salad

steps: Combine the sweet chilli sauce, soy sauce, lime zest, and juice.
Toss with the spinach, rocket, and watercress and serve immediately.

name: Green bean salad
steps: Boil the beans in salted water 5-6 minutes until just tender, then refresh under cold water and pat dry (can be done a day ahead and refrigerated).
Heat 1 tbsp oil and fry the garlic quickly until crisp and lightly golden. Cool in a bowl with the oil (can also be done a day ahead).
Whisk the remaining oil, vinegar, mint, and seasoning; pour over the beans and mix well. Transfer to a serving bowl and scatter with the garlic and its oil.

name: Shredded greens salad

ingredients.raw_text: 1 cucumber, deseeded and cut into matchsticks

steps: Zest and juice two limes into a salad bowl. Segment the remaining limes over the bowl to catch the juice, then chop. Add the apples, fennel, cucumber, celery, spring onions, and oil. Cover and chill until serving.

name: Shaved fennel & rocket salad
steps: Trim the fennel fronds and set aside. Thinly slice the bulb and scrunch with salt, fennel seeds (if using), and lemon juice. Rest at room temperature at least 10 minutes, or chill up to a few hours.
Drizzle the fennel with oil, toss through the rocket and reserved fronds, and serve.

name: Feta, beetroot & pomegranate salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 cooked beetroot, cut into thin wedges

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: ½ romaine lettuce, torn into bite-sized pieces

steps: Whisk the oil, pomegranate molasses, cinnamon, cumin seeds, and lemon juice; loosen with a splash of water and season.
Toss with the herbs, beetroot, feta, rocket, and lettuce. Finish with pomegranate seeds.

name: Courgette salad
steps: Coarsely grate the courgettes.
Toss with the oil, citrus juice, honey, poppy seeds, and crushed garlic; season. Serve promptly (it turns watery if left) alongside grilled chicken, lamb, kebabs, or burgers.

name: Lettuce & fennel salad with orange & mustard dressing

ingredients.food_name: Sweet peppers stewed (with fat and salt)

ingredients.raw_text: 2  Cos lettuces  or 8 Little Gems, torn into bite-size pieces

steps: Quarter, core, and finely shred the fennel using a mandolin or food processor.
Whisk the orange juice and Dijon mustard with seasoning, then whisk in the walnut oil. Pour over the fennel and marinate up to 24 hours.
Tear the lettuce into pieces, combine with the marinated fennel and dressing, and toss to serve.

name: Puy lentil, spiced roast carrot & feta salad

ingredients.raw_text: 500g carrots, peeled, halved and cut into batons
250g pouch cooked Puy lentils  (or canned, drained and rinsed)
large handful mint  leaves, roughly chopped

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss half the oil with the cumin, carrots, and seasoning in a roasting tin. Roast 25 minutes, turning halfway. Drizzle with honey and roast 5 minutes more.
Meanwhile, warm the lentils with the onion, lemon juice, remaining oil, and seasoning; cool slightly.
Toss the dressed lentils with mint and lamb's lettuce. Top with the warm carrots and feta.

name: Halloumi with lemony lentils, chickpeas & beets

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small pack parsley, roughly chopped

steps: Boil the lentils 20-25 minutes until just done. Meanwhile, squeeze the juice of one lemon over the onion with a pinch of salt to lightly pickle; set aside.

Zest the second lemon and reserve for the halloumi. Whisk the juice with the oil, seasoning, and capers. Drain the lentils and combine with the chickpeas and dressing, then fold in the beets, parsley, and pickled onion with its liquid.
Fry the halloumi 1-2 minutes per side until golden. Toss with the reserved lemon zest and place on top of the salad.

name: Salmon & puy lentil salad with olive dressing

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp chopped basil (or a dash of pesto)
3-4 eggs (depending on how much salmon you have)
4 large tomatoes, deseeded and roughly chopped
175g-200g cooked leftover salmon, flaked into large chunks

steps: Chop half the olives (reserve the rest) and combine with the remaining dressing ingredients.
Boil the green beans 5 minutes and refresh under cold water. Boil the eggs 5 minutes, cool, shell, and halve or quarter.

Microwave the lentils per pack instructions. Toss with the tomatoes, most of the dressing, whole olives, and green beans. Top with the eggs and salmon, scatter with rocket, and finish with the remaining dressing. Serve with crusty bread.

name: Miso roast salmon, lentil & pomegranate salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 carrots, cut into fine strips with a julienne peeler or knife

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Boil the lentils 20 minutes until tender. Meanwhile, combine the miso paste with half the ginger, garlic, half the lime juice, oil, and sesame seeds. Spread some over the salmon on a foil-lined tray and roast 10-12 minutes until cooked.

Combine the remaining miso mixture with the rest of the ginger and lime juice, plus the lime zest, vinegar, carrots, pomegranate, onions, and coriander. Drain the lentils and toss through. Divide between plates and top with the salmon.

name: Roasted carrot, rocket & lentil salad

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss the carrots with 1 tbsp oil, cumin seeds, and seasoning. Roast on a tray 30-35 minutes until soft and lightly charred.
Meanwhile, whisk the remaining oil with the lemon zest and juice, honey, and mustard. Once the carrots are done, combine with the remaining ingredients and toss through the dressing to serve.

name: Mustardy beetroot & lentil salad

ingredients.raw_text: 200g puy lentils (or use 2 x 250g packs pre-cooked lentils)
1 ½ tbsp extra virgin olive oil
300g pack cooked beetroot (not in vinegar), sliced

steps: If not using pre-cooked lentils, cook per pack instructions, drain, and cool. Meanwhile, combine the mustard, oil, and seasoning for a dressing.
Toss the lentils with the dressing, then stir in the beetroot, tarragon, and seasoning. Serve.

name: Smoked salmon with lentil salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp dill, chopped, plus extra fronds to garnish

steps: Rinse the lentils and simmer in water 25-30 minutes until tender. Drain and cool. Combine the shallot with the mustard, vinegar, and seasoning, then whisk in the oil.
Boil the eggs 3 minutes, cool under running water, and peel; keep in cold water until ready.
Stir the dill into the dressing and toss with the lentils. Divide onto plates, halve the eggs on top, and add two slices of smoked salmon per plate. Finish with black pepper and dill fronds.

name: Build your own pesto pasta salad

ingredients.raw_text: 5 tbsp pesto (see recipe, right and below, if you want to make your own)

ingredients.food_name: Vegetarian cold cuts alternative, with egg

ingredients.raw_text: halved cherry tomatoes, drained sweetcorn, peas, rocket

steps: Cook the pasta per pack instructions, drain, and toss with 1 tbsp oil. Cool, tossing occasionally to prevent sticking.

Toast the pine nuts in a dry pan over low heat 4-5 minutes until browned. Once the pasta is cool, toss with the pesto, toasted pine nuts, remaining oil, basil, lemon zest and juice, and any of the tomatoes, sweetcorn, peas, or rocket. Season and finish with parmesan.

name: Spiced whole cauliflower & warm chickpea salad

ingredients.raw_text: 400g can chickpeas, drained and patted dry with kitchen paper
2 ripe tomatoes, deseeded and roughly chopped
small pack coriander, leaves roughly chopped (reserve the stems for the marinade)
extra virgin olive oil, to serve (optional)
2 tbsp finely chopped coriander stems (from the pack above)

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Combine the marinade ingredients with salt and pepper. Coat the cauliflower fully, place in an oiled roasting tin, and spoon over any remaining marinade. Roast 30 minutes until the topping sets.
Add water to the tin, cover loosely with foil, and steam in the oven 30 minutes more.

Meanwhile, toss the chickpeas, onion, spices, and seasoning together. Uncover the cauliflower, scatter the chickpea mixture around it, and roast 20 minutes more until the onion is tender, the chickpeas are crisping, and a skewer passes easily through the cauliflower.
Combine the yogurt and coconut cream for a dressing; season. Fold the tomatoes, coriander, and lemon juice through the chickpeas and serve with the dressing and a drizzle of oil.

name: Grilled courgette & halloumi salad with caper & lemon dressing
steps: Combine all the dressing ingredients with a splash of water and a pinch of salt; set aside.
Toss the courgettes with oil and a pinch of salt.
Griddle the courgettes cut-side down (in batches) 4-5 minutes until char marks form, flip, and cook 3 minutes more. Transfer to a bowl and cover to keep warm.
Meanwhile, cook the halloumi in a separate hot pan a few minutes until golden on both sides.
Arrange the courgettes and halloumi on a platter and spoon over the dressing.

name: Warm lemony courgette salad

ingredients.raw_text: 1 lemon, zested, plus a squeeze of juice
¼ small pack of basil, roughly torn

steps: Peel the courgettes into wide strips, discarding the seedy core. Heat the oil, add the lemon zest and garlic, and cook 1 minute. Add the courgette strips and cook, stirring, 1-2 minutes until slightly softened. Finish with lemon juice and toss through the basil.

name: Warm pearl barley & roasted carrot salad with dill vinaigrette

ingredients.raw_text: 1 large bunch of carrots, peeled and cut into diagonal slices (approx. 500g/1lb 2oz)
½ small pack dill, roughly chopped

ingredients.food_name: Blue cheese min. 45 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: a few fennel tops or dill, to serve (optional)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Combine the carrots, shallots, garlic, and fennel seeds in a tin with the oil; season. Roast 35-40 minutes until caramelized and tender.
Meanwhile, boil the pearl barley in salted water about 30 minutes until al dente. Whisk together the dressing ingredients with seasoning.

Drain the barley and toss with the dressing, roasted carrots, dill, parsley, and half the cheese. Finish with fennel tops and the remaining cheese. Serve warm or cold, with buttered soda bread if desired.

name: Warm Thai chicken & noodle salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 good handfuls of greens, such as  Chinese leaf, finely shredded
1 red pepper, seeded and finely sliced
a handful of fresh coriander  leaves
1 red chilli, seeded and finely chopped

steps: Heat the grill to high and cook the chicken on a tray 10-12 minutes without turning, until cooked through.
Meanwhile, cook the noodles per pack instructions, drain, and rinse under cold water.
Combine the vegetables in a bowl. Slice the chicken and add with the noodles and coriander leaves.
Whisk the dressing ingredients with water, pour over the salad, and toss. Serve right away.

name: Avocado, roasted broccoli & sesame rice salad

ingredients.raw_text: 1 large head broccoli, cut into florets
1 large red onion, peeled and cut into chunky wedges
250g pouch microwavable brown rice, cooked following pack instructions and cooled a little
1 small avocado, stoned, peeled and sliced
½ small bunch coriander, roughly chopped

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Toss the broccoli and red onion with 1 tbsp sesame oil on a tray and roast 20 minutes until tender. Cool slightly.

Whisk the remaining sesame oil with the soy, rice wine vinegar, and honey. Combine with the roasted vegetables, rice, and dressing, then fold in the avocado and coriander. Divide into bowls and top with sesame seeds and chilli.

name: Strawberry green goddess smoothie

ingredients.raw_text: 1 small avocado, halved and the flesh scooped out
2 small oranges, juiced, plus ½ tsp finely grated zest

steps: Blend all the ingredients until completely smooth, thinning with a splash of cold water if too thick. Pour into glasses and serve right away.

name: Kale smoothie
steps: Blend all the ingredients with a splash of water, adding more water to reach your preferred consistency.

name: Green goddess smoothie bowl

ingredients.raw_text: 1 ripe avocado, stoned, peeled and chopped into chunks
1 small ripe mango, stoned, peeled and chopped into chunks
250ml milk (unsweetened almond or coconut milk works well)
1 tbsp unsweetened almond or peanut butter
1 tbsp clear honey, agave or maple syrup (optional)
2 tbsp clear honey, agave or maple syrup
175g mixed fresh fruit, chopped (we used banana, mango, raspberries and blueberries)

steps: Slice the bananas onto a lined tray and freeze 2 hours until solid (can then be bagged and frozen up to 3 months).

For the seed mix, set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Toss the seeds, coconut, and almonds with the cinnamon and honey or syrup, spread on a lined tray, and bake 10-15 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes, until lightly toasted. Cool; keeps airtight up to a month.

Blend the avocado, mango, spinach, milk, nut butter, frozen banana, and honey (if using) to a thick consistency, scraping down as needed. Divide into bowls, top with fruit, and finish with 1-2 tbsp of the seed mix per bowl.

name: Kiwi fruit smoothie
steps: Blend all the ingredients until smooth and pour into two glasses.

name: Spinach smoothie

ingredients.raw_text: 2 chunks frozen spinach (or 2 handfuls fresh baby spinach)

steps: Blend the frozen spinach with the avocado, mango, banana, and milk until smooth. Freeze any leftovers in lolly molds for a frozen treat.

name: Spinach smoothie

ingredients.raw_text: 2 chunks frozen spinach (or 2 handfuls fresh baby spinach)

steps: Blend the frozen spinach with the avocado, mango, banana, and milk until smooth. Freeze any leftovers in lolly molds for a frozen treat.

name: Strawberry green goddess smoothie

ingredients.raw_text: 1 small avocado, halved and the flesh scooped out
2 small oranges, juiced, plus ½ tsp finely grated zest

steps: Blend all the ingredients until completely smooth, thinning with a splash of cold water if too thick. Pour into glasses and serve right away.

name: Green goddess smoothie bowl

ingredients.raw_text: 1 ripe avocado, stoned, peeled and chopped into chunks
1 small ripe mango, stoned, peeled and chopped into chunks
250ml milk (unsweetened almond or coconut milk works well)
1 tbsp unsweetened almond or peanut butter
1 tbsp clear honey, agave or maple syrup (optional)
2 tbsp clear honey, agave or maple syrup
175g mixed fresh fruit, chopped (we used banana, mango, raspberries and blueberries)

steps: Slice the bananas onto a lined tray and freeze 2 hours until solid (can then be bagged and frozen up to 3 months).

For the seed mix, set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Toss the seeds, coconut, and almonds with the cinnamon and honey or syrup, spread on a lined tray, and bake 10-15 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes, until lightly toasted. Cool; keeps airtight up to a month.

Blend the avocado, mango, spinach, milk, nut butter, frozen banana, and honey (if using) to a thick consistency, scraping down as needed. Divide into bowls, top with fruit, and finish with 1-2 tbsp of the seed mix per bowl.

name: Raspberry and apple smoothie
steps: Blend all the ingredients until smooth, adding water or milk if too thick.

name: Breakfast smoothie

ingredients.raw_text: about 140g blackberries, blueberries, raspberries or strawberries  (or use a mix), plus extra to serve
apple juice  or mineral water, optional

steps: Blend the banana and berries until smooth. With the blender running, add juice or water to reach your preferred consistency. Top with extra fruit, a drizzle of honey, and serve.

name: Strawberry green goddess smoothie

ingredients.raw_text: 1 small avocado, halved and the flesh scooped out
2 small oranges, juiced, plus ½ tsp finely grated zest

steps: Blend all the ingredients until completely smooth, thinning with a splash of cold water if too thick. Pour into glasses and serve right away.

name: Two-minute breakfast smoothie

ingredients.raw_text: 80g soft fruit (whatever you have – strawberries, blueberries, and mango all work well)
ingredients.food_name: Carbonated soft drink with higher fruit juice content

steps: Blend all the ingredients 1 minute until smooth.
Pour into two glasses to serve.

name: Sunshine smoothie
steps: Blend the ingredients until smooth. Drink immediately or bottle for later; keeps refrigerated up to a day.

name: Kale smoothie
steps: Blend all the ingredients with a splash of water, adding more water to reach your preferred consistency.

name: Green goddess smoothie bowl

ingredients.raw_text: 1 ripe avocado, stoned, peeled and chopped into chunks
1 small ripe mango, stoned, peeled and chopped into chunks
250ml milk (unsweetened almond or coconut milk works well)
1 tbsp unsweetened almond or peanut butter
1 tbsp clear honey, agave or maple syrup (optional)
2 tbsp clear honey, agave or maple syrup
175g mixed fresh fruit, chopped (we used banana, mango, raspberries and blueberries)

steps: Slice the bananas onto a lined tray and freeze 2 hours until solid (can then be bagged and frozen up to 3 months).

For the seed mix, set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Toss the seeds, coconut, and almonds with the cinnamon and honey or syrup, spread on a lined tray, and bake 10-15 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes, until lightly toasted. Cool; keeps airtight up to a month.

Blend the avocado, mango, spinach, milk, nut butter, frozen banana, and honey (if using) to a thick consistency, scraping down as needed. Divide into bowls, top with fruit, and finish with 1-2 tbsp of the seed mix per bowl.

name: Strawberry & banana almond smoothie
steps: Blend the sliced banana, strawberries, yogurt, milk, and ground almonds until completely smooth. Pour into a glass and enjoy.

name: Avocado & strawberry smoothie

ingredients.raw_text: ½ avocado, stoned, peeled and cut into chunks
lemon or  lime juice, to taste

steps: Blend all the ingredients until smooth, thinning with a little water if too thick.

name: Kiwi fruit smoothie
steps: Blend all the ingredients until smooth and pour into two glasses.

name: Peach Melba smoothie

ingredients.raw_text: 100g frozen raspberry, plus a few for garnish
150ml fresh custard, plus a spoonful for garnish

steps: Blend the drained peaches with the raspberries, orange juice, and custard until smooth.
Pour over ice, top with a spoonful of custard and a few raspberries, and serve chilled.

name: Minty pineapple smoothie

ingredients.raw_text: 200g pineapple, peeled, cored and cut into chunks

steps: Blend all the ingredients with water until smooth, adding more water (up to 400ml) to reach the right consistency.

name: Avocado & strawberry smoothie

ingredients.raw_text: ½ avocado, stoned, peeled and cut into chunks
lemon or  lime juice, to taste

steps: Blend all the ingredients until smooth, thinning with a little water if too thick.

name: Smoked salmon, quinoa & dill lunch pot

ingredients.raw_text: 250g pouch ready-to-eat quinoa (we used Merchant Gourmet)

steps: Combine the soured cream and lemon juice with most of the dill, reserving a quarter.
Combine the quinoa with the cucumber and radishes, then stir through half the dressing; season and top with the salmon and remaining dill.
Add the rest of the dressing to a small pot to drizzle over just before eating.

name: Air fryer chicken breasts

ingredients.raw_text: steamed rice  and greens such as broccoli or green beans or a green salad, to serve (optional)

steps: Coat the chicken in oil. Combine the salt, garlic, paprika, and mixed herbs with black pepper on a plate.

Roll each chicken breast in the seasoning and place in the air fryer basket. Cook at 180C 18-20 minutes, turning after 10, until the juices run clear or a thermometer reads 70C. Serve with rice and greens or salad.

name: Bulgur & quinoa lunch bowls

ingredients.raw_text: 150g bulgur and quinoa (this comes ready mixed)

steps: Combine the onion and bulgur mix in a pan with water, thyme, and bouillon. Cover and cook over low heat 15 minutes, then rest 10 minutes until the liquid is absorbed. Cool, remove the thyme, and divide between containers.
For the avocado topping, combine all ingredients except the rocket and pile onto two portions of bulgur, then top with rocket.

For the beetroot topping, layer the chickpeas onto the remaining portions, then toss the beetroot with tomato, mint, cumin, cinnamon, oil, and vinegar. Add the orange, pile on top, and finish with pine nuts and extra cinnamon. Chill until needed.

name: Falafel burgers

ingredients.raw_text: handful of flat-leaf parsley  or curly parsley
½ tsp harissa paste  or chilli powder

ingredients.food_name: Green asparagus salad (cooked) with vinegar marinade

steps: Pat the drained chickpeas dry. Blend with the onion, garlic, parsley, cumin, coriander, harissa, flour, and salt until fairly smooth. Shape into four patties.
Heat the oil and fry the patties 3 minutes per side until lightly golden. Serve with toasted pitta, tomato salsa, and green salad.

name: Prawn & mango salad

ingredients.raw_text: ½ avocado, peeled and cut into cubes, see tip, below left
1 mango cheek, peeled and cut into cubes
couple of mint leaves, very finely shredded

steps: Toss the avocado with the lemon juice, then combine with the prawns, mango, tomatoes, cucumber, spinach, and mint. Pack into a container, drizzle with sweet chilli sauce, and chill until ready to eat.

name: Egg & rocket pizzas

ingredients.food_name: Wrap filled with salad and fried chicken breast fillet

ingredients.raw_text: a little olive oil, for brushing
1 roasted red pepper, from a jar
½ red onion, very thinly sliced

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Lightly brush the tortillas with oil and bake 3 minutes. Meanwhile, combine the chopped pepper and tomatoes with the tomato puree, seasoning, and herbs. Flip the tortillas and spread with the sauce, leaving the center clear.
Crack an egg into the center of each and bake 10 minutes more until set and the edges are crispy. Top with rocket and onion to serve.

name: Vegan chickpea curry jacket potatoes

ingredients.raw_text: lemon wedges and coriander leaves, to serve

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Prick the sweet potatoes, place on a tray, and roast 45 minutes until tender.
Meanwhile, melt the coconut oil and fry the cumin seeds 1 minute until fragrant. Add the onion and cook 7-10 minutes until soft.

Add the garlic, ginger, and chilli; cook 2-3 minutes. Stir in the spices and tikka masala paste, cook 2 minutes more, then add the tomatoes. Simmer, add the chickpeas, and cook 20 minutes until thickened. Season.
Split the roasted sweet potatoes open on four plates. Spoon over the curry, squeeze over lemon, season, and finish with coriander.

name: Lemon & spinach rice with feta

ingredients.raw_text: 700ml hot vegetable stock, made with 1 tsp bouillon powder
1 lemon, zested, 1/2 juiced, 1/2 cut into 4 wedges

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onions and garlic 10 minutes, stirring often, until softened.
Add the rice, stock, frozen spinach, and half each of the dill and lemon zest. Cover, lower the heat, and cook 25 minutes until the rice is tender.

Stir in the remaining dill, lemon zest, nuts, and feta along with the lemon juice. Serve half right away with lemon wedges; cool and chill the rest up to three days, reheating thoroughly before serving with more lemon wedges.

name: Chicken satay salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 skinless chicken breast fillets (or use turkey breast)
1 tbsp crunchy peanut butter (choose a sugar-free version with no palm oil, if possible)
sunflower oil, for wiping the pan
2 Little Gem lettuce hearts, cut into wedges
1 banana shallot, halved and thinly sliced

steps: Combine the tamari, curry powder, cumin, garlic, and honey. Slice the chicken breasts horizontally into 4 fillets, add to the marinade, and coat well. Chill at least 1 hour, or overnight.

Meanwhile, mix the peanut butter with the chilli sauce, lime juice, and water into a spoonable sauce. Cook the marinated chicken in an oiled pan, covered, 5-6 minutes over medium heat, flipping near the end, until cooked but still moist. Rest, covered, a few minutes.

While resting, toss the lettuce with the cucumber, shallot, coriander, and pomegranate; divide onto plates and spoon over a little sauce. Slice the chicken over the salad and finish with the remaining sauce. Serve warm.

name: Veggie olive wraps with mustard vinaigrette
steps: Combine all the filling ingredients and toss well.

Lay the tortilla on foil and pile the filling along one side - it will look like a lot but compacts as you roll. Roll tightly from the filling side, tucking in the ends, then wrap the foil around it. Slice in half to eat right away, or leave whole and wrap in parchment to take with you.

name: Bulgur & aubergine pilaf

ingredients.raw_text: 2 onions, halved and thinly sliced (about 280g), scrunched to separate the pieces
1 aubergine (about 260g), thinly sliced, or halved and sliced if it’s a thicker one

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Heat 2 tsp oil and cook the onions and ginger 10 minutes, stirring, until softened and golden; set aside.

Meanwhile, combine the bulgur, bouillon, and cumin with 400ml water in a pan. Bring to a boil, cover, and cook over low heat 10-15 minutes until the liquid is absorbed. Stir in the chickpeas, cover, and set aside.
In the same pan used for the onions, heat the remaining oil and fry the aubergine slices 8-10 minutes in a single layer, flipping halfway, then cover and cook a few minutes more until soft.
Toss the aubergine into the bulgur with the onions and coriander. Pile onto a platter and finish with feta and almonds.

name: Sweet potato toasts with curried chickpeas

ingredients.raw_text: 3 large sweet potatoes (about 350g), trimmed and thinly sliced lengthways into 12
⅓ x 30g pack of coriander, chopped

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Brush the sweet potato slices with oil, arrange on a tray, and bake 20-25 minutes, checking to avoid uneven browning.

For the chickpeas, heat the oil and cook the celery 5 minutes, stirring. Add the garlic, curry powder, and cumin seeds briefly, then the tomatoes, tomato puree, and bouillon. Stir in the chickpeas and frozen spinach, cover, and simmer 15 minutes. Finish with coriander.

Top six sweet potato slices with half the chickpea mixture for serving (three per person). Cool and chill the rest, reheating gently with a splash of water and warming the toast in a dry pan before serving; keeps refrigerated up to three days.

name: Quick chicken hummus bowl

ingredients.raw_text: 1cooked chicken breast, sliced at an angle

steps: Combine 2 tbsp hummus with half the lemon juice, the zest, and enough water for a drizzly dressing. Separate the grain pouch, divide between bowls, and toss with the dressing. Top each with a handful of spinach.

Squeeze the remaining lemon juice over the avocado halves and add one to each bowl. Divide the chicken, pomegranate, onion, almonds, and remaining hummus between the bowls, mixing gently just before eating.

name: Bombay potato frittata

ingredients.raw_text: ½ green chilli, deseeded and finely chopped
1 small bunch of coriander, finely chopped

steps: Boil the potatoes 6 minutes until tender, drain, and steam-dry. Meanwhile, microwave the spinach with a little water, covered, 3 minutes until wilted.

Heat the oil and cook the onion over medium heat 10 minutes until golden and sticky. Add the garlic, coriander, cumin, mustard seeds, and turmeric; cook 1 minute more. Add the tomatoes and wilted spinach, cook 3 minutes, then add the potatoes.
Heat the grill to medium. Beat the eggs with the chilli and most of the coriander and pour over the potato mixture. Grill 4-5 minutes until golden and just set with a slight wobble.
Cool slightly and slice into wedges. Mix the mango chutney, yogurt, and remaining coriander, and serve alongside.

name: Roasted cauli-broc bowl with tahini hummus

ingredients.food_name: Mixed vegetables (carrots, cauliflower and broccoli florets) raw

steps: The night before, set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Roast the cauliflower and broccoli with the oil and a little sea salt 25-30 minutes until browned. Cool completely.

Assemble each bowl with quinoa, then layer beetroot, spinach, cauliflower, broccoli, and walnuts. Combine the tahini, hummus, lemon juice, and 1 tbsp water; drizzle over just before eating. Serve with lemon wedges.

name: Feta & kale loaded sweet potato

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Prick the sweet potatoes and roast 40 minutes. Add the chickpeas to the tray and roast 10 minutes more until the potatoes are tender and the chickpeas crisp slightly.
Meanwhile, quick-pickle the onion with the vinegar, sugar, and salt. Separately, marinate the feta in oil and chilli flakes.

Wilt the kale in a little water 3 minutes and season. Halve the potatoes, plate, and top with the kale, chickpeas, pickled onion (reserving the vinegar), marinated feta, and pumpkin seeds. Toss the rocket in the reserved vinegar and serve alongside.

name: Healthy tomato soup

ingredients.raw_text: 1 large  sweet potato, peeled and chopped
1 low-salt veg or  chicken stock cube
3 rosemary sprigs, leaves picked and chopped

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion 8-10 minutes until softened and lightly colored. Add the carrots, garlic, peppers, and sweet potato; cook 10 minutes more, stirring, taking care not to burn the garlic. Add the tomatoes, stock, herbs, and water; simmer, covered, 20 minutes until the sweet potato is soft.
Blend until smooth, stir in the cream, and season to taste. Keeps refrigerated three days or frozen up to three months.

name: Cumin roast veg with tahini dressing
steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss all the vegetables with the cumin seeds, oil, and vinegar in a roasting tin. Roast 45-50 minutes until tender and starting to char.
Meanwhile, combine the tahini and peanut butter with the lemon juice, coriander, garlic, and water to make a dressing.
Cool the roasted vegetables slightly, then toss with the mint, coriander, lemon zest, and chickpeas.
For meal prep, serve two portions now with eggs, some dressing, and half the spinach; save the rest for another day without the eggs.

name: Puff pastry pizzas

ingredients.food_name: Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: sweetcorn, olives, peppers, red onion, cherry tomatoes, spinach, basil

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Cut the pastry into six squares on lined trays and score a border around each. Bake 15 minutes until puffed but not fully cooked.
Meanwhile, combine the tomato puree, ketchup, oregano, and water for the sauce. Grate the cheese and prepare your toppings.
Press down the center of each pastry square, spread with sauce to the puffed edges, sprinkle with cheese, and add toppings. Bake 5-8 minutes more and serve.

name: Falafel burgers

ingredients.raw_text: handful of flat-leaf parsley  or curly parsley
½ tsp harissa paste  or chilli powder

ingredients.food_name: Green asparagus salad (cooked) with vinegar marinade

steps: Pat the drained chickpeas dry. Blend with the onion, garlic, parsley, cumin, coriander, harissa, flour, and salt until fairly smooth. Shape into four patties.
Heat the oil and fry the patties 3 minutes per side until lightly golden. Serve with toasted pitta, tomato salsa, and green salad.

name: Easy egg muffins

ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 4) and oil an 8-hole muffin tin. Heat the remaining oil and cook the broccoli, pepper, and spring onions 5 minutes; cool.

Whisk the eggs with the milk, smoked paprika, and half the cheese. Stir in the cooked vegetables, divide between the muffin holes, and top with the remaining cheese and chives. Bake 15-17 minutes until golden and set.

name: Healthy tomato soup

ingredients.raw_text: 1 large  sweet potato, peeled and chopped
1 low-salt veg or  chicken stock cube
3 rosemary sprigs, leaves picked and chopped

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion 8-10 minutes until softened and lightly colored. Add the carrots, garlic, peppers, and sweet potato; cook 10 minutes more, stirring, taking care not to burn the garlic. Add the tomatoes, stock, herbs, and water; simmer, covered, 20 minutes until the sweet potato is soft.
Blend until smooth, stir in the cream, and season to taste. Keeps refrigerated three days or frozen up to three months.

name: Layered rainbow salad pots

ingredients.raw_text: 350g pasta shapes (De Cecco is a good brand that stays nice and firm)
200g green beans, trimmed and chopped into short lengths
160g can tuna in olive oil, drained
1 orange pepper, cut into little cubes 195g can sweetcorn, drained

steps: Cook the pasta 2 minutes less than pack instructions for a firmer bite, then drain well. Boil the green beans 2 minutes, rinse in cold water, and drain. Combine the tuna with the mayonnaise, yogurt, and chives.
Layer the pasta into glasses or jars, top with the tuna dressing, then the green beans, cherry tomatoes, and finally pepper and sweetcorn. Cover and chill until ready to eat.

name: Sausage & pesto pizza

ingredients.food_name: Yeast dough for pizza/pizza dough, raw

ingredients.raw_text: 6 tbsp fresh pesto, or vegetarian alternative

ingredients.food_name: Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Thaw the pizza dough 1-2 hours before cooking. Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7) with a tray inside to heat.
Simmer the chopped tomatoes uncovered 15 minutes until reduced; season.
Squeeze the sausage from its skins into 10 small balls. Fry in the oil over medium heat 5 minutes until golden.

Roll the dough into two oval pizzas about 20cm long. Top with the tomato sauce, swirl in the pesto, tear over the mozzarella, and scatter with the sausage balls. Slide onto the hot tray and bake 18-20 minutes until crisp and charred at the edges. Finish with basil if desired.

name: Easy cheesy frittata

ingredients.raw_text: 1 courgette, halved with the ends cut off

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Snip the spring onions into a bowl and add the peas.
Grate the courgette and add to the bowl.
