ingredients.raw_text: squeeze or two of lemon juice

steps: Beat the quark and sugar with a little lemon juice until smooth and creamy (can be done ahead).
Fold in the raspberries until they break up slightly and streak the mixture pink; adjust with more lemon juice if desired. Spoon into glasses and serve.

name: Tarte tatin

ingredients.raw_text: 6 dessert apples (about 900g/2lb), such as Braeburn, Cox’s Orange Pippin or Adam’s Pearmain
85g unsalted butter (60g/21/4oz chilled and diced, 25g/1oz melted)
crème fraîche (full-fat please!) or high-quality vanilla ice cream, to serve

steps: Roll the pastry to a 24cm circle, prick with a fork, and freeze on a tray while preparing the apples.

Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Peel, quarter, and core the apples. Cook the sugar in an ovenproof pan over medium-high heat 5-7 minutes to a dark amber caramel, then off the heat stir in the diced cold butter.
Arrange the apple quarters tightly around the edge of the pan, then fill the center, pressing to avoid gaps. Brush with melted butter.
Bake 30 minutes, then top with the frozen pastry disc, tucking the edges down and piercing a few steam holes. Bake 40-45 minutes more until golden and crisp.
Cool 1 hour, run a knife around the edge, and invert onto a deep serving plate. Serve with creme fraiche or vanilla ice cream.

name: Spiced apple & blackberry hand pies

ingredients.raw_text: 2 cooking apples, peeled, cored and cut into small pieces

steps: Cook the apples and sugar with water, covered, 6 minutes until soft. Cool slightly, stir in the blackberries. Combine the cornflour with the honey and cinnamon and stir into the fruit.

Cut 12 circles from the pastry. Spoon filling onto half, leaving a border, brush with egg, and top with plain discs, pressing an apple-shaped dent top and bottom. Seal edges with a fork and brush with egg. Add pastry leaf and stalk cutouts, and poke a steam hole. Chill 30 minutes to 24 hours (freezes unbaked up to 2 months).
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Bake 20 minutes (add 5 minutes if from frozen) until golden and bubbling. Sprinkle with sugar and cool 20 minutes before serving with custard.

name: The best apple crumble

ingredients.raw_text: 575g Bramley apple (3 medium apples), peeled, cored and sliced to 1cm thick
double cream, clotted cream or custard, to serve

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5).
Toss the sliced apples with sugar and press into a deep baking dish.
Combine the flour, sugar, and salt in a bowl.
Rub in the cold butter until it resembles breadcrumbs (or pulse in a processor).
Pile the crumb mixture over the apples and level with a fork.
Press the surface lightly with the back of the fork for texture.
Sprinkle with oats and demerara sugar if desired.
Bake on a tray 35-40 minutes until golden and the apples are soft. Cool 10 minutes before serving.

name: Apple crisp

ingredients.raw_text: vanilla ice cream or custard, to serve
8 Cox's or Russet apples, peeled, cored and sliced into 5mm-thick rounds

steps: Combine the topping ingredients with a pinch of salt and rub together to a crumb texture (or use a processor). Chill until needed, up to two days.
Toss the apples with sugar and lemon zest and juice, spread into a deep dish, flatten, and top with the crumb mixture (can be covered and chilled a day ahead).
Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Bake on a tray 40 minutes until deep golden and bubbling at the edges. Cool 10 minutes before serving with ice cream or custard.

name: French apple tart

ingredients.raw_text: 200g plain flour, plus extra for dusting
1 ¾ kg (about 12) eating apples, like Cox’s, Russet or Granny Smith

steps: For the pastry, rub the butter into the flour, sugar, and salt until crumbly. Mix in the egg to form a dough, shape into a disc, and chill at least 30 minutes (keeps two days).

Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Roll the pastry to line a 23cm tart tin, leaving overhang. Blind bake with parchment and baking beans 15 minutes, then remove and bake 10-15 minutes more until biscuity. Trim the edges and cool.

Meanwhile, set aside four apples; peel, core, and chop the rest. Simmer with water, most of the sugar, and alcohol (if using), covered, 25-30 minutes until collapsed into a puree, adjusting sweetness to taste.

Raise the oven to 210C (190C fan/gas 8). Slice the reserved apples and arrange over the puree in concentric circles. Brush with butter, sprinkle with the reserved sugar, and bake 20-25 minutes until golden.
Warm the jam with hot water and brush generously over the finished tart. Cool slightly and serve warm or cold, dusted with icing sugar if desired.

name: Spiced roasted apples & blackberries

ingredients.raw_text: finely grated zest and juice of 1 large orange

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Core the apples, leaving a coin-sized hole, and score around the middle.
Stand in a baking dish. Combine the honey, cinnamon, and orange zest and spoon into each cavity. Pour the orange juice into the dish.

Roast about 40 minutes, basting occasionally. Add the blackberries around and over the apples near the end and return to the oven 10 minutes until the juices run. Spoon the fruit and juices over to serve.

name: Toffee apple turnover puff pie

ingredients.raw_text: 6 eating apples (about 800g), peeled, cored and quartered

steps: Chop the apples. Melt the butter until sizzling, add most of the sugar, and bubble until dissolved and sticky. Add the apples, season with salt, and cook 5-6 minutes until softened and caramelized with the juices evaporated. Cool completely (keeps chilled a day).

Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) with a tray inside. Spoon the apple filling onto one side of the pastry, leaving a border; brush the border with egg yolk. Fold the other side over to enclose, seal the edge, score triangles on top with slits, brush with remaining yolk, and sprinkle with the reserved sugar.
Bake on the hot tray 25-30 minutes until puffed and deep golden. Cool 10 minutes, then cut into wedges along the scored lines.

name: Apple flapjack crumble
steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Slice the apples and combine with the jam and orange juice. Spread in a buttered dish.

Combine the oats, flour, and cinnamon. Rub in the butter, then the sugar. Drizzle with syrup and mix with a knife into small clumps. Scatter over the apples and bake 30-35 minutes until the juices bubble up. Cool 10 minutes before serving with custard, cream, or ice cream.

name: Flat apple & vanilla tart

ingredients.raw_text: 5 large eating apples  - Cox's, russets or Elstar
3 tsp vanilla sugar or 1 tsp vanilla extract

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Roll the pastry to a 35cm round and transfer to a lined tray.
Toss the sliced apples in lemon juice and spread over the pastry, leaving a 2cm border, curling the edges up slightly.
Dot with butter and sprinkle with vanilla and sugar. Bake 15-20 minutes until the apples are tender and pastry crisp.
Brush the warmed conserve over the apples and pastry edge. Serve hot with vanilla ice cream or creme fraiche.

name: Eve's pudding
steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Slice the apples into a buttered dish and scatter with raisins.
Beat the remaining butter and sugar 10 minutes until pale, then beat in the eggs and fold in the flour. Spread over the apples and bake 45 minutes until golden. Serve with custard.

name: Sticky toffee apple pudding

ingredients.raw_text: 2 Bramley apples  (or other cooking) apples, peeled, cored and sliced

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and butter a large ovenproof dish. Combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt. Whisk the milk, butter, egg, and vanilla into a smooth batter. Arrange the apples in the dish and spoon the batter over.

For the topping, dissolve the sugar in boiling water and pour over the batter. Scatter with pecans and bake about 40 minutes until risen and golden. Serve with the caramel sauce that forms underneath, alongside cream, custard, or ice cream.

name: Cinnamon apple pecan pudding

ingredients.raw_text: 1 Bramley apple (about 280g), peeled, cored, a quarter grated, the rest diced
Greek yogurt or cream, to serve

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and lightly grease a dish. Combine the butter, xylitol, flour, oats, cinnamon, and baking powder with the eggs and milk; beat until smooth. Stir in the apple, spread in the dish, and top with pecans.
Bake 35-45 minutes until risen and a skewer comes out clean. Serve with Greek yogurt or cream.

name: Apple Charlotte

ingredients.raw_text: 100g  unsalted butter, melted, plus extra for the basin
custard or vanilla ice cream, to serve
125g eating apples (we used Braeburn), peeled, cored and cut into 1cm chunks
1 tsp ground  orange, zested and juiced

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Butter and sugar a pudding basin, then line with a strip of parchment.
Trim the crusts from the bread and flatten. Dip in melted butter and use to line the basin, overlapping to avoid gaps. Chill while preparing the filling.

Caramelize the sugar in a pan without stirring, then stir in the butter carefully. Add the apples, orange juice, zest, and cinnamon, and cook over high heat 5-8 minutes until softened and slightly reduced.
Pour the filling into the lined basin and top with the remaining bread, pressing the edges to seal.
Bake on a tray 35-40 minutes until golden. Cool 5 minutes, then invert onto a plate to unmold. Serve with custard or ice cream.

name: Easy chocolate fudge cake

ingredients.raw_text: 150ml sunflower oil, plus extra for the tin
2½ tbsp milk (a little more if needed)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two sandwich tins. Sift the flour, cocoa, and bicarbonate of soda together with the sugar.
Make a well and add the golden syrup, eggs, oil, and milk; beat until smooth.
Divide between the tins and bake 25-30 minutes until risen and firm. Cool 10 minutes before turning out.
For the icing, beat the butter until soft, gradually beat in the icing sugar and cocoa, then add milk until fluffy and spreadable.
Sandwich the cakes together and cover the top and sides with the remaining icing.

name: Chocolate orange tart

ingredients.raw_text: 140g raw cacao powder (find it in health food shops or online), plus extra for dusting

steps: Soak the dates in boiling water 20 minutes. Meanwhile, blend the crust ingredients until combined and press into a tart tin. Chill.

Drain the dates and blend with the orange zest and juice, coconut oil, honey, and cacao until mostly smooth. Spread into the chilled crust and chill at least 1 hour. Sprinkle with extra orange zest and cacao before serving.

name: Squidgy chocolate pear pudding

ingredients.raw_text: 410g can pear halves in juice,  drained
cream or ice cream, to serve

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5) and grease a shallow dish. Melt the butter, remove from heat, and stir in the sugar.
Whisk the eggs and gradually beat into the butter mixture. Sift in the flour and cocoa and beat until combined.
Pour into the dish, nestle in the pears, and scatter with chunks of chocolate and almonds if desired (can be frozen at this stage).
Bake 30 minutes until crusty on top but still gooey inside — don't overcook. Serve warm with cream or ice cream.

name: Chocolate torta della nonna

ingredients.raw_text: 175g cold unsalted butter, cut into cubes
75g icing sugar, plus extra for dusting

ingredients.food_name: Ricotta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: For the pastry, pulse the flour, butter, and icing sugar to crumbs, then add the egg yolks (and a little water if too dry). Knead briefly, flatten into a disc, wrap, and chill 20 minutes.
For the filling, heat the milk with the vanilla seeds and pod until just bubbling; cool slightly.
Whisk the egg yolks, sugar, and flour 5-10 minutes until pale and thick. Remove the vanilla pod and whisk the warm milk into the eggs.
Return to a clean pan and cook over medium-low heat, whisking, about 10 minutes until very thick. Push through a sieve, cover the surface with cling film, and cool.
Roll the pastry to line a tart tin, chill 30 minutes. Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Blind bake with parchment and beans 15 minutes, then 5-10 minutes more until golden. Cool.

Reduce the oven to 160C (140C fan/gas 3). Combine the custard with the ricotta, orange zest, and grated chocolate, spread into the tart shell, top with pine nuts, and bake 35-40 minutes (still wobbly). Chill at least 3 hours or overnight, then dust with icing sugar.

name: Chocolate, pistachio & nougat semifreddo

ingredients.raw_text: 140g hard  nougat or torrone, chopped into 0.5cm chunks

steps: Line a loaf tin with cling film. Whisk the sugar and eggs over simmering water until pale, thick, and doubled, then cool over cold water while continuing to whisk.
Melt the chocolate and fold into the egg mixture. Whip the cream to soft peaks and fold in with the nougat and pistachios.
Pour into the tin, smooth, cover, and freeze until firm (can be made up to 2 weeks ahead). To serve, unmold and soften in the fridge 15 minutes before slicing.

name: Double-the-love chocolate cake

ingredients.raw_text: 2-3 x 80g bags white chocolate buttons, flowers and ribbons, optional

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two round tins (20cm and 15cm).

Melt half the butter and chocolate together. Combine half the flour, sugar, cocoa, and bicarbonate of soda with salt. Whisk 2 eggs with half the buttermilk, combine with the melted chocolate, then mix into the dry ingredients with boiling water until smooth. Divide between the tins and bake 40-45 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tins.
Repeat to make a second batch, for two of each size cake total.

Melt the white chocolate over simmering water. Beat the butter and icing sugar, then beat in the white chocolate. Split each cooled cake in half and sandwich all four layers of each size with icing. Stack the smaller cake on the larger one, coat fully in icing, and decorate with chocolate buttons.
For edible flower decoration, wrap the stems in damp paper and cling film before adding to the finished cake.

name: Chocolate macarons

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tsp skimmed milk, warmed a little

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a tray. Sift the icing sugar and cocoa, stir in the ground almonds. Whisk the egg whites to stiff peaks and fold into the dry mixture.
Pipe 24 small blobs onto the tray, spaced apart. Smooth with a wet finger and let dry 15 minutes. Bake 15-20 minutes until firm and easily peeled off. Cool, then store airtight up to a week.
For the filling, melt the chocolate over simmering water, stir in the warm milk until smooth, and cool until thickened. Use to sandwich the macarons.

name: No-bake chocolate tart

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp icing sugar, plus extra for dusting

steps: Crush the biscuits in a bag with a rolling pin. Melt the butter with the syrup or honey and stir in the biscuits. Press into a tart tin and chill.

Melt the chocolate bars in the microwave, stirring, then mix in the vanilla and sift in the icing sugar. Whip the cream to soft peaks and fold into the chocolate. Pour into the base and chill at least 2 hours, up to 2 days.
To serve, loosen the edges, slide onto a plate, and let sit at room temperature 30 minutes for easier release. Top with creme fraiche and raspberries, dust with icing sugar, and slice thinly.

name: Chocolate ganache & salted caramel brittle

ingredients.raw_text: 200g dark chocolate (or gluten-free alternative), broken into pieces

steps: For the ganache, pour hot cream over the chocolate, rest 5 minutes, then stir smooth. Cool and chill.
For the caramel sauce, melt the sugar and butter together, add the cream, boil briefly, then cool.
For the brittle, cook the sugar and water without stirring until golden, stir in the nuts, and pour thinly onto lined parchment. Sprinkle with salt and let set.
To serve, drizzle plates with caramel sauce, scoop warmed ganache with a heated spoon onto each plate, and top with shards of brittle and a pinch of salt.

name: Zesty haddock with crushed potatoes & peas

ingredients.raw_text: 600g floury potato, unpeeled, cut into chunks
4 haddock or other chunky white fish fillets, about 120g each (or use 2 small per person)

steps: Boil the potatoes 10 minutes until tender, adding the peas for the last minute. Drain, crush roughly with oil and seasoning, and keep warm.
Meanwhile, combine oil, lemon juice and zest, capers, and chives with seasoning for the dressing.
Dust the fish in seasoned flour. Fry in the remaining oil 2-3 minutes per side until cooked, then add the dressing and warm through. Serve with the crushed potatoes and broccoli.

name: Baked haddock & cabbage risotto

ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Heat the oil and soften the onion 5 minutes. Add the rice and cook 2 minutes. Pour in the stock, bring to a boil, add the cabbage, cover, and bake 20 minutes.
Stir the rice, place the fish on top, re-cover, and bake 5 minutes more.
Flake the fish and stir into the rice with the creme fraiche and half the Parmesan. Season and finish with the remaining Parmesan.

name: Tuna pie

ingredients.raw_text: 4  x 145g cans tuna chunks in sunflower oil
1 tbsp paprika, or 1⁄2 tbsp paprika mixed with 1⁄2 tbsp smoked paprika
500g plain flour, or an equal mix of plain and wholemeal flours, plus extra for dusting
100g lard (or use butter or margarine), melted

steps: For the pastry, pulse the flour and lard to crumbs. Dissolve salt in boiled water, add, and pulse to a dough. Wrap and chill 1 hour (add vinegar for a make-ahead version that keeps two days).
Heat the oil and cook the onions and peppers until soft, about 8-10 minutes.
Add the drained tuna and reserved oil to the pan with the paprika and salt; cook, stirring, 5 minutes. Cool slightly (keeps chilled up to a day).

Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Roll the pastry into two discs. Place one on a lined tray, spoon in the filling leaving a border, top with the second disc, and crimp to seal. Brush with egg and bake 25-30 minutes until golden. Serve hot or cold; keeps chilled up to two days.

name: Smoked trout fish pies

ingredients.raw_text: 2 x 175g packs smoked trout fillets, any skin and bones removed, flaked into big chunks

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Melt the butter, stir in the flour, then gradually add the milk and horseradish (if using). Simmer to thicken, then stir in the dill, most of the spring onions, lemon zest, fish, and seasoning. Divide into pie dishes.
Combine the mash with the remaining spring onion, spoon over the filling, and bake 15-20 minutes until golden and bubbling.

name: Quick fish pie

ingredients.raw_text: 400g can chopped tomato  with garlic and herbs

ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Heat the grill to high. Heat the oil and soften the onion 5 minutes. Add the tomatoes and stock, boil, then simmer 5 minutes; season.
Flake in the smoked mackerel and stir, then transfer to a shallow dish. Sprinkle with breadcrumbs and parmesan and grill 5 minutes until bubbling and golden.

name: Vegan paella
steps: Combine the saffron, tomato puree, and bouillon powder in a heatproof bowl, then pour over 1 litre boiling water and set aside.

Heat the oil and cook the onion 5 minutes, stirring, until softening. Add the peppers, garlic, and thyme; cook a few minutes more. Stir in the rice and paprika for about a minute. Pour in the saffron stock, cover, and simmer 10 minutes.

Stir in the frozen beans and courgettes; return to a simmer, cover, and cook 10 minutes more until the rice is tender and the stock absorbed. Rest 5 minutes, then stir in the parsley. Serve half right away with lemon wedges; refrigerate the rest up to three days and reheat thoroughly in the microwave when ready.

name: Sardine tomato pasta with gremolata

ingredients.raw_text: small handful of parsley, finely chopped

steps: Cook the pasta per pack instructions. Heat 1 tbsp oil from the sardine tin and sizzle the capers and half the garlic 1-2 minutes until fragrant. Add the tomatoes and cook 4-5 minutes until softened and bursting. Stir in the sardines and rocket, breaking up the fish and wilting the leaves. Season.
For the gremolata, combine the lemon zest, parsley, and remaining garlic; season. Drain the pasta and top with the sardine sauce and gremolata.

name: Zesty salmon with roasted beets & spinach

ingredients.raw_text: 2 ½ small oranges, zest of 1 and juice of half

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Trim the beetroot stems, reserving tender leaves. Quarter the beetroot, toss with oil, coriander seeds, and seasoning, wrap in foil, and bake 45 minutes until tender. Top with salmon and half the orange zest, and roast 15 minutes more. Toast the pumpkin seeds in the oven 10 minutes if desired.
Meanwhile, segment the oranges over a bowl to catch the juice. Grate the garlic and let sit 10 minutes, then combine with the orange juice and remaining oil to make a dressing.

Lift the salmon from the parcel. Toss the beetroot with the red onion, remaining orange zest, pumpkin seeds, and spinach. Gently fold in the orange segments and avocado with any beet leaves. Plate, top with the warm salmon, and drizzle with the dressing.

name: Cod & olive tagine with brown rice

ingredients.raw_text: 3 strips of lemon peel, finely chopped
300ml vegetable stock, made with 1½ tsp bouillon powder

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onions, swede, carrots, lemon peel, and garlic 10 minutes, stirring often. Add the spices briefly.
Stir in the tomatoes, tomato puree, stock, and olives. Cover and cook 25 minutes.

Cook the rice per pack instructions. Stir the tagine, nestle in the frozen cod, cover, and cook 20 minutes until the fish is done. Finish with coriander. Serve half with the rice; cool and chill the rest up to three days, reheating the tagine on the hob and the rice in the microwave.

name: Cod & smashed celeriac

ingredients.raw_text: 4 spring onions, each cut into 3 on the diagonal
1 lemon, ½ juiced and ½ cut into wedges, to serve
1 tbsp chopped parsley leaves, to serve

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss the celeriac with oil and fennel seeds in a roasting tin, season, and roast 45 minutes, stirring halfway, until lightly charred.

Meanwhile, char the spring onions in a dry pan 3-4 minutes. Divide between two foil sheets, top with grated courgette, garlic, and fish, season, and drizzle with lemon juice and remaining oil. Seal into parcels and bake 10-12 minutes.
Mash the celeriac and season. Plate with the celeriac, then carefully open the parcels and slide the fish alongside. Garnish with parsley and serve with rocket and lemon wedges.

name: Roast side of salmon with chermoula

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small pack mint, leaves picked

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Line a roasting tin with parchment. Brush the salmon skin with oil, season, and roast skin-side down 15-20 minutes until just cooked.

Meanwhile, toast the coriander and cumin seeds, then blend with the remaining sauce ingredients and enough water for a drizzling consistency; season (can be made ahead and chilled). Serve the salmon with some sauce drizzled over and the rest on the side.

name: Cod puttanesca with spinach & spaghetti

ingredients.raw_text: small handful chopped parsley, to serve

steps: Boil the spaghetti 10 minutes until al dente, adding the spinach for the last 2 minutes. Meanwhile, cook the onion in the oil, covered, until golden. Stir in the chilli and garlic, then the tomatoes.

Add the vinegar, capers, olives, and paprika with a ladle of pasta water. Top with the cod fillets, cover, and cook 5-7 minutes until the fish flakes. Drain the pasta and spinach, plate, and top with the fish and sauce. Finish with parsley.

name: Budget kedgeree

ingredients.raw_text: 3 x 120g cans sardines in sunflower oil (boned if you prefer), 2 tbsp oil reserved
650ml hot vegetable stock made with 1 tsp vegetable bouillon powder

steps: Boil the eggs 8 minutes, then cool enough to peel. Meanwhile, heat the reserved sardine oil and cook the onions, ginger, and chilli 10 minutes until soft and golden.

Add the rice, curry powder, and cumin seeds, then the stock. Space the frozen spinach blocks on top without stirring, cover, and cook over low heat 15 minutes. Add the sardines, cover, and cook 5 minutes more until the rice is tender.

Gently toss everything with the coriander. Cut the eggs into wedges and serve on top with lemon wedges. For two servings, cool and chill the rest up to three days, reheating without the lemon (best served cold).

name: Salmon & purple sprouting broccoli grain bowl

ingredients.raw_text: 200g purple sprouting broccoli, each stem cut into three pieces

steps: Combine most of the oil with the honey, mustard, lemon juice, and seasoning. Boil the broccoli 3-4 minutes until tender with a bite; drain.

Heat the remaining oil, sizzle the garlic briefly, then add the grain pouch, separating the grains. Add the broccoli, dressing, herbs, and radishes; mix, season, and gently stir in the salmon. Serve warm or cold.

name: Quick and easy pasta salad

ingredients.food_name: Tomato dried, in oil, canned, drained
Pizza prosciutto (with tomato sauce, boiled ham, mozzarella)

steps: Boil the pasta 8 minutes, add the peas, and cook 2 minutes more until tender. Drain and cool under cold water.
Meanwhile, blend the chopped tomato with half the sundried tomatoes, oil, vinegar, garlic, and basil until smooth; season and pour into a large bowl.
Add the pasta and peas to the dressing. Slice the remaining sundried tomatoes and add with the rest of the basil. Tear in the prosciutto or salami and toss together.

name: Build your own pesto pasta salad

ingredients.raw_text: 5 tbsp pesto (see recipe, right and below, if you want to make your own)

ingredients.food_name: Vegetarian cold cuts alternative, with egg

ingredients.raw_text: halved cherry tomatoes, drained sweetcorn, peas, rocket

steps: Cook the pasta per pack instructions, drain, and toss with 1 tbsp oil. Cool, tossing occasionally to prevent sticking.

Toast the pine nuts in a dry pan over low heat 4-5 minutes until browned. Once the pasta is cool, toss with the pesto, toasted pine nuts, remaining oil, basil, lemon zest and juice, and any of the tomatoes, sweetcorn, peas, or rocket. Season and finish with parmesan.

name: Beetroot & feta pasta

ingredients.raw_text: 2 large beetroots, peeled and roughly chopped into chunks
4 celery sticks, finely chopped, celery tops kept separate

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Boil the beetroot in salted water 5 minutes, then lift out with a slotted spoon; reserve the cooking water.
Meanwhile, heat the oil and cook the onions, celery, and carrots 8 minutes until softened. Chop the basil stalks and celery tops and add with the garlic; fry 30 seconds more.
Cook the pasta in the reserved beetroot water 13-15 minutes until just tender. Drain, reserving some pasta water, and return the pasta to the pan.
Blend the beetroot with half the fried vegetables, most of the basil, and the creme fraiche until smooth, thinning with reserved pasta water to a thick sauce.
Stir the sauce through the pasta with the remaining vegetables and seasoning. Divide into bowls, crumble over feta, and finish with basil and black pepper.

name: Creamy pesto & chicken pasta salad
steps: Cook the pasta per pack instructions. Meanwhile, combine the red pepper, cherry tomatoes, and chicken. Mix the pesto and creme fraiche. Drain the pasta and toss with everything to combine; serve immediately or pack for lunch.

name: Chicken pasta salad

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red pepper, deseeded and thickly sliced
2 tbsp each chopped thyme and oregano

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Toss the pepper and onion with a little oil and roast 20 minutes.
Cook the pasta per pack instructions, drain, and set aside.
Meanwhile, flatten the chicken breasts between cling film. Combine the remaining oil, herbs, chilli, and garlic and rub over the chicken. Grill or griddle 3-4 minutes per side.
Slice the chicken and toss into the pasta with any juices, the roasted vegetables, cherry tomatoes, rocket, vinegar, and seasoning. Serve warm or cold.

name: Favourite pasta salad
steps: Cook the pasta, adding the peas for the final 2 minutes. Drain, rinse under cold water, and drain again. Combine with the herbs, lemon zest and juice, oil, and seasoning. Cover and chill, portioning out as needed; after a few days, stir in a little extra oil or something creamy to loosen it.

name: Chicken pasta bake

ingredients.food_name: Mascarpone min. 80 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 4 skinless chicken breasts, sliced into strips

ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter
Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small bunch of parsley, finely chopped

steps: Heat 2 tbsp oil and cook the onion gently 10-12 minutes. Add the garlic and chilli flakes for 1 minute. Add the tomatoes and sugar; season and simmer uncovered 20 minutes until thickened, then stir in the mascarpone.
Heat 1 tbsp oil and cook the seasoned chicken 5-7 minutes until cooked through.

Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Cook the penne per pack instructions, drain, and toss with the remaining oil. Combine with the chicken in an ovenproof dish, pour over the sauce, and top with cheddar, mozzarella, and parsley. Bake 20 minutes until golden and bubbling.

name: Rigatoni sausage bake

ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Squeeze the sausage meat from the skins and chop small. Heat the oil and cook the onion 5 minutes until soft. Add the sausage and brown lightly. Stir in the carrot, wine, stock, and tomato puree; season.
Bring to a boil and simmer uncovered about 15 minutes until thickened; adjust seasoning.
Whisk the butter, flour, and milk over heat until thickened and smooth. Season with nutmeg and simmer 2 minutes.
Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Boil the pasta 10-12 minutes until tender. Stir in the spinach off the heat until wilted, then drain well.

Layer half the pasta in a baking dish, spoon over the sausage sauce, top with the remaining pasta, pour over the white sauce, and sprinkle with cheddar. Bake 20-25 minutes until golden. Rest 5 minutes before serving.

name: Cheesy broccoli pasta bake

ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Cook the pasta, adding the broccoli for the final 4-5 minutes until tender. Drain and heat the grill.
Melt the butter, stir in the flour, and cook 1 minute. Gradually whisk in the milk, bring to a boil, and simmer 2 minutes. Stir in the mustard, half the cheese, and seasoning.
Combine the pasta and broccoli with the sauce in an ovenproof dish. Top with the remaining cheese and grill 3-4 minutes until golden and bubbling.

name: Tuna & broccoli pasta bake

ingredients.raw_text: 2 x 185g cans tuna in olive oil, drained well, 1 tbsp oil reserved
2 x 400g cans chopped tomato with garlic and herbs

ingredients.food_name: Soft cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter
Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Heat the reserved tuna oil with the tomatoes and water, and simmer while cooking the pasta per pack instructions, adding the broccoli for the last 3 minutes. Drain, reserving some cooking water.
Heat the grill. Melt the soft cheese into the tomato sauce, then combine with the pasta, broccoli, and tuna, loosening with reserved water if needed; season.
Transfer to an ovenproof dish, top with cheddar and breadcrumbs, and grill 4-6 minutes until golden and bubbling. Serve with a side salad.

name: Sausage pasta bake

ingredients.food_name: Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 50g parmesan, grated, plus extra to serve
ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: For the ragu, squeeze the sausage meat into small balls directly into hot oil in a pan; brown 10 minutes. Add the garlic for 1 minute, then the chilli flakes, tomato puree, and vinegar. Add the tomatoes, bring to a simmer, and cook on low 30 minutes.
For the white sauce, melt the butter, stir in the flour, and cook briefly, then gradually whisk in the milk. Simmer gently 10 minutes, season, and whisk in the parmesan.

Boil the pasta 1 minute less than pack instructions, drain, and return to the pan. Stir in most of the white sauce, then most of the ragu and mozzarella. Transfer to a baking dish, top with the remaining white sauce, ragu, and mozzarella, and sprinkle with parmesan. Cool completely, then chill up to a day or freeze up to two months (defrost 24 hours before baking).
Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Bake 25-30 minutes until crisp at the edges and the cheese melted. Rest 5-10 minutes before serving.

name: Creamy courgette lasagne

ingredients.food_name: Ricotta min. 45 % fat in dry matter
Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Boil the lasagne sheets about 5 minutes until softened but not fully cooked. Rinse in cold water and toss with a little oil to prevent sticking.

Heat the oil and fry the onion 3 minutes, then add the courgettes and garlic and cook until softened and bright green. Stir in two-thirds each of the ricotta and cheddar; season. Warm the tomato sauce in the microwave 2 minutes.

Layer a baking dish with half the courgette mixture, then pasta, then tomato sauce; repeat. Top with the remaining ricotta and cheddar. Bake on the top shelf about 10 minutes until the pasta is tender and the cheese golden.

name: Cheesy broccoli pasta bake

ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Cook the pasta, adding the broccoli for the final 4-5 minutes until tender. Drain and heat the grill.
Melt the butter, stir in the flour, and cook 1 minute. Gradually whisk in the milk, bring to a boil, and simmer 2 minutes. Stir in the mustard, half the cheese, and seasoning.
Combine the pasta and broccoli with the sauce in an ovenproof dish. Top with the remaining cheese and grill 3-4 minutes until golden and bubbling.

name: Creamy courgette lasagne

ingredients.food_name: Ricotta min. 45 % fat in dry matter
Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Boil the lasagne sheets about 5 minutes until softened but not fully cooked. Rinse in cold water and toss with a little oil to prevent sticking.

Heat the oil and fry the onion 3 minutes, then add the courgettes and garlic and cook until softened and bright green. Stir in two-thirds each of the ricotta and cheddar; season. Warm the tomato sauce in the microwave 2 minutes.

Layer a baking dish with half the courgette mixture, then pasta, then tomato sauce; repeat. Top with the remaining ricotta and cheddar. Bake on the top shelf about 10 minutes until the pasta is tender and the cheese golden.

name: Lemon & greens pesto pasta

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small head of broccoli, cut into florets
1 large handful of  spinach wilted in boiling water, cooled and excess water squeezed out
large handful of frozen peas, set aside in boiled water until heated through
3 tbsp grated parmesan or vegetarian hard cheese
ingredients.food_name: Risotto with butter and Parmesan cheese
Soft cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Cook the pasta per pack instructions. Meanwhile, boil the broccoli 4 minutes, drain, and blend with the basil, spinach, and pine nuts.
Stir in the garlic, lemon zest and juice, oil, peas, parmesan, and soft cheese; season.
Drain the pasta, reserving some water, and toss with the pesto, loosening with the reserved water. Check seasoning and serve.

name: Sausage, leek & fennel pasta

ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Squeeze the sausage meat into the hot oil, breaking into small pieces, and cook 8-10 minutes until crispy in places. Remove and set aside.

Cook the leeks in the same pan over low heat 10-12 minutes until softened and starting to caramelize, seasoning as you go. Meanwhile, cook the pasta per pack instructions in boiling salted water; drain, reserving some water.

Add the garlic and fennel seeds to the leeks for 2 minutes. Stir in the mustard and sausage, add a splash of pasta water, and bubble 1 minute. Toss in the pasta with the lemon zest, adding more pasta water if needed. Serve topped with Parmesan and black pepper.

name: Quick and easy pasta salad

ingredients.food_name: Tomato dried, in oil, canned, drained
Pizza prosciutto (with tomato sauce, boiled ham, mozzarella)

steps: Boil the pasta 8 minutes, add the peas, and cook 2 minutes more until tender. Drain and cool under cold water.
Meanwhile, blend the chopped tomato with half the sundried tomatoes, oil, vinegar, garlic, and basil until smooth; season and pour into a large bowl.
Add the pasta and peas to the dressing. Slice the remaining sundried tomatoes and add with the rest of the basil. Tear in the prosciutto or salami and toss together.

name: Spaghetti puttanesca with red beans & spinach
steps: Boil the spaghetti 10-12 minutes until al dente. Meanwhile, cook the onion in the oil, covered, until tender and golden. Stir in the chilli, garlic, and cherry tomatoes.
Add the vinegar, capers, olives, and paprika with a ladle of pasta water. Stir in the beans and warm through.
Wilt the spinach in the pasta water, then drain well. Toss with the tomato-bean mixture, parsley, and basil, and serve.

name: Asparagus, chilli & feta farfalle

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp olive oil, plus extra to serve
250g asparagus, woody ends trimmed, cut into small pieces

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Boil the pasta 1 minute less than pack instructions.
Meanwhile, heat the oil and cook the garlic, chilli, and salt 1 minute until fragrant. Add the asparagus and lemon zest, cooking 4-5 minutes until just tender.
Drain the pasta into the asparagus pan. Add the lemon juice, basil, and black pepper, and divide into bowls. Top with pine nuts, feta, and a drizzle of oil.

name: Creamy linguine with ham, lemon & basil

ingredients.food_name: Pizza prosciutto (with tomato sauce, boiled ham, mozzarella)

ingredients.raw_text: large handful grated parmesan, plus extra to serve, if you like
ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Cook the linguine per pack instructions. Meanwhile, tear the ham and fry in the oil until golden and crisp.

Drain the pasta, reserving some water, and return to the pan with the ham. Whisk the lemon juice, egg yolks, and creme fraiche, then stir through the pasta with the basil and Parmesan, loosening with a little pasta water to make a creamy coating sauce. Finish with extra Parmesan if desired.

name: Cod puttanesca with spinach & spaghetti

ingredients.raw_text: small handful chopped parsley, to serve

steps: Boil the spaghetti 10 minutes until al dente, adding the spinach for the last 2 minutes. Meanwhile, cook the onion in the oil, covered, until golden. Stir in the chilli and garlic, then the tomatoes.

Add the vinegar, capers, olives, and paprika with a ladle of pasta water. Top with the cod fillets, cover, and cook 5-7 minutes until the fish flakes. Drain the pasta and spinach, plate, and top with the fish and sauce. Finish with parsley.

name: Green spaghetti & meatballs

ingredients.raw_text: 250g-300g wholemeal spaghetti (for children, use white spaghetti as wholemeal can be too filling)
200g baby spinach, plus extra to serve
1 ripe avocado, stoned and peeled, plus extra to serve
25g parmesan, grated, plus extra to serve
ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Combine the mince, apple, and fennel seeds; shape into 24 small balls. Heat the oil and cook the meatballs until golden and cooked through. Cook the pasta per pack instructions.

Meanwhile, reserve a handful of spinach, some peas, and half the avocado for later. Defrost the remaining peas with hot water, then drain. Blend the defrosted peas, spinach, avocado, basil, and parmesan with pasta water to make a sauce; season.

Add the reserved peas to the pasta for the last minute. Drain, reserving some water, and combine the pasta with the meatballs and sauce, cooking briefly until hot, adjusting seasoning or water as needed. Serve with a spinach-avocado salad and extra parmesan.

name: Pepper & lemon spaghetti with basil & pine nuts

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp finely grated parmesan or vegetarian alternative (optional)
ingredients.food_name: Vegetarian cold cuts alternative, with egg

steps: Heat the oil and cook the pepper 5 minutes. Meanwhile, boil the pasta 10-12 minutes until tender.
Add the courgette and garlic to the pepper and cook, stirring often, 10-15 minutes until very soft.
Stir in the lemon zest and juice, basil, and drained spaghetti, tossing with a little reserved pasta water until coated. Add the pine nuts, then serve topped with parmesan if using.

name: Spaghetti Genovese

ingredients.raw_text: 200g trimmed  green bean, cut in half

steps: Boil the potatoes and spaghetti together with salt about 10 minutes until nearly tender. Add the green beans and cook 5 minutes more.
Drain, reserving some cooking liquid, and return everything to the pan. Stir in the pesto and reserved liquid; season, plate, and drizzle with olive oil.

name: Spaghetti puttanesca with red beans & spinach
steps: Boil the spaghetti 10-12 minutes until al dente. Meanwhile, cook the onion in the oil, covered, until tender and golden. Stir in the chilli, garlic, and cherry tomatoes.
Add the vinegar, capers, olives, and paprika with a ladle of pasta water. Stir in the beans and warm through.
Wilt the spinach in the pasta water, then drain well. Toss with the tomato-bean mixture, parsley, and basil, and serve.

name: Classic spaghetti

ingredients.raw_text: 400g dried spaghetti  (roughly three quarters of a 500g pack)

ingredients.food_name: Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Finely dice the onion.

Heat the oil and cook the onion 5 minutes until softening. Add the garlic and cook 2 minutes more until golden. Add the tomatoes and half the basil; simmer gently 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thick and pulpy.
Boil the spaghetti in well-salted water per pack instructions, about 10 minutes, until just soft. Reserve a cup of the cooking water before draining.

Return the drained pasta to its pan and pour over the sauce, adding reserved pasta water if it's dry, or reducing over low heat if watery. Tear in the mozzarella and stir through with the remaining basil. Serve right away.

name: Lemon spaghetti with tuna & broccoli

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp olive oil, plus extra for drizzling

steps: Boil the spaghetti in salted water 6 minutes, add the broccoli, and boil 4 minutes more until both are tender.

Meanwhile, combine the shallots, olives, capers, tuna, and lemon zest and juice in a large bowl. Drain the pasta and broccoli, add to the bowl, and toss well with the olive oil and black pepper. Drizzle with extra oil to serve.

name: Tomato & mozzarella spaghetti bake

ingredients.raw_text: handful basil leaves, plus a few extra

ingredients.food_name: Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter
Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Heat the oil and cook the garlic 1 minute. Add the tomatoes, chilli, and seasoning; simmer 10 minutes.

Meanwhile, cook and drain the spaghetti. Combine with the tomato sauce and basil, spoon half into a baking dish, layer with half the mozzarella and Parmesan, then repeat. Bake 15-20 minutes until lightly browned. Finish with basil and serve with a green salad.

name: Harissa-spiced chicken with bulgur wheat

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp harissa paste  (we used Belazu Rose Harissa)
1 tbsp vegetable oil  or sunflower oil

steps: Rub the harissa over the chicken. Fry in the oil about 3 minutes per side until golden (not yet cooked through); set aside.
Cook the onions gently 5 minutes until soft. Add the pine nuts and toast a few minutes more. Stir in the apricots, bulgur, and stock; season, cover, and cook 10 minutes until nearly absorbed.
Return the chicken to the pan, re-cover, and cook 5 minutes over low heat until the chicken is cooked through and the liquid absorbed. Fluff the bulgur and finish with coriander.

name: Chicken stroganoff

ingredients.raw_text: 4 skinless chicken breasts, cut into chunks
a large handful of parsley, chopped
rice mash or pasta, to serve

steps: Heat half the oil and brown the chicken over medium-high heat, stirring; season and set aside.

Heat the remaining oil over medium heat and cook the onion 6-8 minutes until softened. Add the garlic and paprika briefly, then the stock, Dijon, and Worcestershire sauce. Return the chicken with its juices and simmer 5-6 minutes until reduced and the chicken is cooked.
Off the heat (or very low), stir in the soured cream until just combined and gently simmering. Finish with parsley and serve with rice, mash, or pasta.

name: Chicken pasta bake

ingredients.food_name: Mascarpone min. 80 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 4 skinless chicken breasts, sliced into strips

ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter
Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small bunch of parsley, finely chopped

steps: Heat 2 tbsp oil and cook the onion gently 10-12 minutes. Add the garlic and chilli flakes for 1 minute. Add the tomatoes and sugar; season and simmer uncovered 20 minutes until thickened, then stir in the mascarpone.
Heat 1 tbsp oil and cook the seasoned chicken 5-7 minutes until cooked through.

Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Cook the penne per pack instructions, drain, and toss with the remaining oil. Combine with the chicken in an ovenproof dish, pour over the sauce, and top with cheddar, mozzarella, and parsley. Bake 20 minutes until golden and bubbling.

name: Chicken Milanese

ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Halve each chicken breast lengthways to make four fillets, then flatten between parchment with a rolling pin.

Set up three dishes: seasoned flour, beaten egg, and breadcrumbs mixed with parmesan. Coat each fillet in flour, egg, then the crumb mixture. Keeps chilled a day or frozen (stacked with parchment) up to three months.
Fry in the oil 3-4 minutes per side (add 1 minute per side if frozen) until golden and crisp. Drain on paper and serve with lemon wedges.

name: Air fryer chicken breasts

ingredients.raw_text: steamed rice  and greens such as broccoli or green beans or a green salad, to serve (optional)

steps: Coat the chicken in oil. Combine the salt, garlic, paprika, and mixed herbs with black pepper on a plate.

Roll each chicken breast in the seasoning and place in the air fryer basket. Cook at 180C 18-20 minutes, turning after 10, until the juices run clear or a thermometer reads 70C. Serve with rice and greens or salad.

name: Chinese chicken curry

ingredients.raw_text: 4 skinless chicken breasts, cut into chunks (or use thighs or drumsticks)

steps: Toss the chicken in the cornflour and season.

Fry the onion in half the oil over low-medium heat until softened, about 5-6 minutes. Add the garlic for 1 minute, then the spices and sugar; cook 1 minute more. Add the stock and soy sauce, simmer 20 minutes, then blend until smooth.
Wipe the pan and brown the chicken in the remaining oil. Return the sauce to the pan, bring to a simmer, stir in the peas, and cook 5 minutes, thinning with water if needed. Serve with rice.

name: Healthy chicken stir-fry

ingredients.raw_text: 15g ginger, peeled and cut into thin matchsticks
2 small red onions (160g), cut into wedges
160g broccoli, broken into florets, stem finely chopped
2 carrots (160g), halved lengthways, then cut into diagonal slices
200g chicken breast, cut into thin strips

steps: Cook the rice per pack instructions and drain. Heat the oil and stir-fry the ginger and red onions 2 minutes. Add the broccoli stem, carrots, and chilli (if using); cook 1 minute.
Add the chicken and cumin, stir-fry briefly, then add the broccoli florets and water. Cover and steam 3-4 minutes until the broccoli is tender and the chicken cooked through.
Combine the peanut butter with the tamari and vinegar, stir into the pan, and serve over the rice.

name: Classic chicken parmigiana

ingredients.raw_text: 15g parmesan, finely grated, plus extra to serve
ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter
Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 2 x 400g cans chopped tomatoes or passata

steps: Halve each chicken breast lengthways to make four fillets and flatten between parchment.
Set up flour, beaten egg, and breadcrumb-parmesan mixture in separate dishes. Coat each fillet in flour, egg, then crumbs. Keeps frozen up to three months, layered with parchment.

For the sauce, heat the oil and sizzle the garlic 2 minutes. Add sugar and a splash of vinegar, then the tomatoes or passata and oregano; season and simmer 10-15 minutes until thickened. Freezes up to three months; defrost overnight and reheat.

Fry the breadcrumbed fillets in oil 3 minutes per side (fresh or frozen) until golden. Arrange on a tray, top each with sauce and a slice of mozzarella. Heat the grill to high. Cook the spaghetti per pack instructions, drain, and toss with the remaining sauce. Grill the fillets 3-4 minutes (1-2 more if frozen) until the cheese melts, then serve.

name: Honey mustard chicken pot with parsnips
steps: Heat half the oil in a lidded pan and brown the chicken; set aside. Heat the remaining oil and cook the onions 5 minutes until softened.

Return the chicken to the pan with the parsnips. Combine the stock with the mustard and honey and pour in. Scatter with thyme, bring to a simmer, cover, and cook 30 minutes (or longer if desired) until the chicken is tender. Season and serve with steamed greens.

name: One-pot chicken chasseur

ingredients.raw_text: 200g pack small  button or chestnut mushrooms

steps: Heat the oil and half the butter in a large lidded casserole.
Season the chicken legs and brown 5 minutes per side; remove and set aside.
Melt the remaining butter, add the onion, and cook 5 minutes until soft.
Add the garlic for 1 minute, then the mushrooms for 2 minutes, then the wine.
Stir in the tomato puree, let it bubble and reduce 5 minutes, then add the thyme and stock.
Return the chicken to the pan, cover, and simmer gently about 1 hour until very tender.
Remove the chicken and keep warm. Boil the sauce rapidly 10 minutes until syrupy and concentrated.
Return the chicken to the sauce and serve.

name: Chicken tagine with spiced brussels sprouts & feta

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small bunch coriander, roughly chopped

steps: Heat some of the coconut oil in a lidded casserole. Cook the onion, pepper, and garlic 3-4 minutes until softening. Raise the heat, add the chicken thighs, and fry together about 3 minutes.

Add the spices and tomato puree, frying 1 minute. Add the stock, bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer, partially covered. After 30 minutes, add the apricots and chickpeas and simmer 10 minutes more.
Meanwhile, heat the remaining coconut oil, toast the cumin seeds 10 seconds, then add the shredded sprouts and fry over high heat, stirring often, 5 minutes until softened and browned in places.
Serve the tagine topped with the fried sprouts, crumbled feta, and coriander.

name: Summer chicken stew

ingredients.raw_text: 500g new potatoes, larger ones quartered, smaller ones halved

steps: Heat the oil and cook the leeks, garlic, and thyme, covered, over gentle heat 10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Season the chicken and add to the dish with the potatoes.
Raise the heat, add the stock, and bring to a simmer. Reduce heat and cook covered 35 minutes. Add the green beans and peas for the final 10 minutes. Season and serve with a squeeze of lemon.

name: Lemony chicken stew with giant couscous

ingredients.raw_text: 500g skinless boneless chicken thighs, each cut into 2-3 chunks
3 tbsp tagine paste or 2 tbsp ras el hanout
2 x 400g cans tomato with chopped mixed olives
small handful fresh oregano, leaves picked and chopped
2 preserved lemons, flesh removed, skin rinsed and finely chopped

steps: Heat the oil in a lidded casserole. Cook the onions 10 minutes until starting to caramelize. Push aside and brown the chicken over high heat 5 minutes.

Add the tagine paste, tomatoes, oregano, preserved lemons, honey, and crumbled stock cube, plus a half-can of water. Season, stir, cover, and simmer gently 40 minutes (or up to 4 hours on very low heat if serving staggered).
Stir in the couscous 10 minutes before serving, cover, and simmer 10 minutes until cooked (for staggered servings, cook 50g couscous per portion separately). Finish with parsley.

name: Spicy meatballs

ingredients.raw_text: 500g minced  chicken, turkey, lamb, beef or pork
2 tsp mild or medium curry powder
½ tsp paprika  or cayenne pepper

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4).
Combine the mince with the onions, garlic, curry powder, cumin, garam masala, paprika or cayenne, and coriander until well mixed.
Add the beaten egg and breadcrumbs and mix again.
Shape into 15-18 walnut-sized balls, washing hands thoroughly after handling the raw meat.
Fry the meatballs in the oil 5 minutes, turning until golden, then transfer to a baking tray.
Bake 15-20 minutes until cooked through.
Cool slightly and serve with a crisp green salad, pitta bread, and tomato salsa.

name: Indian turkey with spinach & new potatoes
steps: Boil the new potatoes until tender; drain. Heat the oil and cook the onion 5-6 minutes. Add the garlic and turkey mince, cooking 4-5 minutes. Stir in the curry paste for 1 minute, then add the tomatoes and stock. Cover and cook 20 minutes. Stir in the potatoes and spinach, heating through 3-4 minutes.

name: Honey & mustard chicken thighs with spring veg

ingredients.raw_text: 300g new potatoes, unpeeled, smaller left whole, bigger halved

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Combine the honey, mustard, garlic, and lemon zest and juice. Coat the chicken thighs and season.
Arrange the chicken skin-side up on a tray with the new potatoes dotted between, drizzle the potatoes with oil, and sprinkle with salt. Roast 35 minutes until the skin caramelizes and chars.
Add the spinach and peas to the tray and roast 2-3 minutes more until the spinach wilts and the peas are hot and coated in sauce.

name: Air fryer chicken thighs
steps: Combine the paprika, herbs, garlic granules (if using), salt, and pepper on a plate. Rub the chicken thighs with oil and coat in the spice mix.

Place skin-side down in the air fryer and cook 10 minutes at 180C. Flip and cook 10-15 minutes more until cooked through and the skin is crisp, checking the juices run clear. Remove immediately to keep the skin crisp.

name: Slow cooker honey mustard chicken thighs

ingredients.raw_text: 8 chicken thighs (or fillets if you prefer)
2 tbsp double cream or crème fraîche

steps: Switch on the slow cooker. Brown the chicken thighs in butter, getting good color on the skin, season, and transfer to the slow cooker. Brown the spring onions and add as well. Add the stock, mustard, and honey and cook on low 4 hours.
Stir in the cream and peas, then cook uncovered 15 minutes more. Re-crisp the skin under the grill if desired.

name: Moroccan-style chicken with sour cherries & olives

ingredients.raw_text: 3 tbsp olive oil, plus a drizzle
thumb-sized piece of ginger, peeled and finely grated
½ preserved lemon,  finely chopped, or 1 strip of lemon peel
100g dried sour cherries or any other dried fruit (like cranberries, apricots, raisins or sultanas)
chopped coriander and cooked couscous (see tips, below), to serve

steps: Season the chicken and coat with ras el hanout (can be done the night before). Brown in the oil over medium heat 15-20 minutes in batches; set aside.

Cook the onions and carrots 8-10 minutes until soft and golden. Add the garlic and ginger for 2 minutes, then the harissa and preserved lemon; cook 1 minute until sticky. Add the cinnamon, bay leaves, and tomatoes; simmer.
Cook a few minutes until reduced to a thick paste. Return the chicken and its juices, add the stock, olives, and cherries, and simmer. Cover and cook gently 45 minutes to 1 hour until tender.
Finish with coriander and serve with couscous. Cool completely before chilling up to three days or freezing up to three months; defrost overnight and reheat gently.

name: Honey & soy chicken with sesame broccoli

ingredients.raw_text: 1 large head broccoli, cut into florets

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Combine the soy, honey, some oil, and garlic-ginger paste. Coat the chicken thighs and marinate briefly if time allows. Transfer to a roasting tin skin-side up and roast 45 minutes until sticky and crisp.

With 15 minutes left, cook the rice per pack instructions. Heat the remaining oil, sizzle the remaining garlic-ginger paste 1 minute, then add the broccoli and a splash of water. Cover and cook 10 minutes until tender, then uncover, add sesame seeds, and toss. Season and serve with the chicken and pan juices.

name: Roast chicken thighs with brown rice & salsa verde

ingredients.raw_text: 3 skinless boneless chicken thighs, cut in half

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Season the chicken, rub with oil, and roast with the garlic in a tin 25-30 minutes.
Meanwhile, blend the herbs, anchovy, capers, lemon juice, and remaining oil to a fine salsa verde; season and set aside.

Once the chicken is cooked, squeeze the garlic from its skins into the tin. Stir in the rice, breaking it up, then add the spinach and lemon zest, tossing well. Return to the oven 5 minutes. Divide into bowls and top with salsa verde.

name: Beef stew

ingredients.raw_text: 2 really big  carrots, halved lengthways then very chunkily sliced
2 thyme sprigs, 1 whole and 1 leaves picked
850g stewing beef (featherblade or brisket works nicely), cut into nice large chunks

steps: Set the oven to 160C (140C fan/gas 3) and boil the kettle.
Combine the celery, onion, carrots, bay leaves, and thyme sprig in a flameproof casserole with the oil and butter.
Cook 10 minutes to soften, then stir in the flour until no longer dusty, followed by the tomato puree, Worcestershire sauce, and stock cubes.
Gradually stir in the hot water, add the beef, and bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover and transfer to the oven for 2.5 hours, then uncover and cook 30-60 minutes more until the meat is tender and the sauce thickened.
Finish with the reserved thyme leaves.

name: Beef & Guinness stew with carrots

ingredients.raw_text: 1kg stewing beef, cut into large chunks
Creamy parsnip mash, to serve (see recipe below)

steps: Set the oven to 160C (140C fan/gas 3). Heat the oil in a lidded casserole and brown the meat in batches; set aside. Brown the onion and carrots in the same pot, scatter over the flour, and stir. Return the meat and juices, pour in the stout, crumble in the stock cube, and season with salt, pepper, and a pinch of sugar. Add the herbs and bring to a simmer.
Cover and bake about 2.5 hours until very tender. Freezes up to 3 months. Rest briefly before serving with parsnip mash.

name: Beef massaman curry

ingredients.raw_text: 600g stewing beef steak, cut into large chunks
4 kaffir lime leaves (available from Thai shops or dried from supermarkets)
1 tbsp palm or soft light brown sugar

ingredients.food_name: Velvety fish sauce from white basic sauce

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red chilli, deseeded and finely sliced, to serve

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and roast the peanuts 5 minutes until golden; cool and chop. Reduce the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4).
Heat some coconut milk (using the cream if present) in a lidded casserole. Fry the curry paste 1 minute, then coat the beef and seal.

Add the remaining coconut milk, half a can of water, potatoes, onion, lime leaves, cinnamon, tamarind, sugar, fish sauce, and most of the peanuts. Simmer, cover, and bake 2 hours until the beef is tender.
Finish with sliced chilli and remaining peanuts, and serve straight from the dish with jasmine rice.

name: Beef pie with crisp potato crust

ingredients.raw_text: 500g beef  braising steak, cut into chunks
250g pack oyster mushrooms, sliced if large
200ml hot  beef stock, from a cube

steps: Heat the oil and brown the beef 10 minutes; remove. Cook the onion 7 minutes until softened, add the mushrooms, and cook 3 minutes more. Stir in the tomato paste and flour, cooking 1 minute. Add the wine and stock, bring to a simmer, and return the beef. Cook uncovered over gentle heat about 2 hours until very tender, topping up with water as needed. Pour into a baking dish.

Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Boil the potatoes 10 minutes until softening, cool under running water, and coarsely grate. Scatter over the beef, dot with butter, and bake 30 minutes until crisp on top.

name: Healthy chilli con carne

ingredients.raw_text: 1-2 tbsp olive oil, plus extra if needed
1-2 tbsp chipotle paste (or gluten-free alternative), depending on how spicy you like it
400g can kidney bean in chilli sauce
1 lime, zested and cut into wedges
¼ small pack coriander, leaves only

steps: Heat the oil and brown the beef in batches; set aside.

Cook the onion in the same pan until softened, adding more oil if needed. Add the garlic, cumin, and chipotle paste; cook 1 minute. Drain the kidney beans, reserving the liquid. Add the reserved liquid, chopped tomatoes, and a can of water to the pan, then return the meat. Simmer, covered, 2 hours until tender (or bake at 160C/140C fan/gas 3 for 3 hours).
Stir in the reserved kidney beans and lime zest; season and warm through. Serve with coriander, lime wedges, and rice.

name: Beef stew in red wine sauce recipe

ingredients.raw_text: 600g piece beef skirt or slices of shin, cut into large chunks
250g mushrooms, halved (we used small Portobello mushrooms)

steps: Set the oven to 150C (130C fan/gas 2). Melt the butter in a lidded casserole and cook the onions and garlic 10 minutes until starting to brown; set aside.
Toss the beef in seasoned flour in batches to coat.
Heat the oil in the same pot and brown the beef with the bay leaves. Add the wine, return the onions, then the tomato puree and stock; bring to a simmer, cover, and bake 1 hour.
Add the mushrooms and bake 1 hour more, checking tenderness and cooking up to 30 minutes further if needed. Finish with parsley.

name: Slow-cooked beetroot & beef curry (Chukandar Gosht)

ingredients.raw_text: 3-4 tbsp corn oil or  sunflower oil
1 black  cardamom pod (preferable) or 3 green cardamom pods
naan bread or cooked basmati rice

steps: Heat the oil and sizzle the cardamom, cinnamon, cumin, and coriander seeds. Add the onions and stir-fry until light golden.
Add the garlic and ginger, cooking 1 minute (add water if sticking), then the tomatoes and cook until soft.
Add salt, chilli powder, and the beef; stir-fry over high heat to seal. Reduce heat, cover loosely, and cook about 10 minutes.
Raise the heat, add the grated beetroot, stir-fry briefly, then cover, reduce heat, and cook 1 hour 15 minutes until the meat is tender, stirring occasionally and adding water if it dries out.
Finish with coriander leaves, green chilli, and a squeeze of lime; serve with naan or basmati rice.

name: Slow cooker beef curry

ingredients.raw_text: 750g beef for braising (braising steak, chuck, flank etc) cut into chunks

steps: Switch on the slow cooker if needed. Brown the beef in batches in oil and transfer to the slow cooker. Soften the onion in the same pan over low heat, then add the garlic and ginger for 1 minute.
Add the spices and fry 1 minute until fragrant. Add the coconut milk, bring to a simmer, then tip everything into the slow cooker.
Cook 4 hours on high or 8 hours on low. Finish with coriander and serve.

name: Griddled lamb with spiced new potatoes

ingredients.raw_text: 1 ½kg new potatoes, halved (or quartered depending on size)
2 preserved lemons (we used Belazu), flesh removed and skin finely chopped

steps: Boil the potatoes 10-15 minutes until tender; drain, reserving half for another day. Toss the remaining potatoes with oil, garlic, preserved lemon, and cumin; keep warm.
Rub the lamb with oil and season. Griddle 3 minutes per side to your liking. Season the potatoes, stir in the mint, and serve with the lamb and peas or salad.

name: Sizzled masala lamb with chopped salad

ingredients.raw_text: ¼ cucumber, peeled, deseeded and finely diced
½ small red onion, very finely sliced
small handful mint, roughly chopped, plus a few leaves to garnish
small handful coriander, roughly chopped, plus a few leaves to garnish

steps: Heat the grill. Rub the chops with oil and curry paste, season, and grill 4-5 minutes per side for medium (adjust to taste). Rest a couple of minutes.
Meanwhile, combine the remaining salad ingredients (except garnish) and season. Plate the salad, top with the chops, and scatter with remaining herbs.

name: Chilli ginger lamb chops
steps: Blend the garlic with the ginger, lemon juice, oil, spices, and seasoning to a smooth paste. Coat the lamb chops and marinate in the fridge a couple of hours or overnight.
Grill over hot coals 3 minutes per side until cooked but still pink and juicy in the center.

name: Spiced lamb with lemon & herb quinoa

ingredients.raw_text: ¼ tsp red chilli or pinch of chilli flakes

steps: Crush the cumin seeds with the garlic, chilli, and seasoning to a paste. Stir in the lemon juice and oil, and marinate the lamb chops with half; reserve the rest.
Rinse the quinoa and boil in double the volume of water 10 minutes. Off the heat, stir in the reserved dressing, cover, and let sit 10 minutes more.
Griddle or grill the lamb 3-5 minutes per side to your liking. Stir the spring onions and coriander into the quinoa and serve with the lamb and steamed broccoli drizzled with oil.

name: Rosemary roast lamb chops
steps: Set the oven to 200C (220C fan/gas 7). Brown the lamb in half the oil, 2 minutes per side; set aside. Fry the potatoes in the remaining oil 4-5 minutes until browning. Add the rosemary and garlic, then return the lamb to the pan.
Roast 20 minutes, then add the tomatoes and balsamic vinegar. Roast 5 minutes more until the tomatoes just split. Serve straight from the dish.

name: Imam bayildi with BBQ lamb & tzatziki

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp olive oil, plus extra for brushing
½ cucumber, seeds scooped out, grated

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Halve the aubergines, score the flesh, brush with oil, and roast 20 minutes until soft enough to scoop.

Cook the onion until soft, add the garlic and cinnamon for 1 minute. Scoop and chop the aubergine flesh and add to the onions. Deseed and chop the tomatoes, add to the pan, and cook 10 minutes until soft. Stir in most of the parsley.
Fill the aubergine shells with the tomato mixture in a baking dish, pour over any reserved tomato juice, drizzle with oil, and bake 30 minutes until collapsed.
Meanwhile, combine the tzatziki ingredients in a small bowl.
Season the lamb and griddle or grill 3 minutes per side until browned. Squeeze lemon over. Scatter the aubergines with parsley and serve with the lamb and tzatziki.

name: Grilled lamb with hummus mash

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Heat the grill. Toss the chops and courgettes with oil and seasoning on a tray. Grill 3-5 minutes, then turn and cook 5 minutes more until the lamb is done.
Meanwhile, marinate thinly sliced onion in oil and lemon juice.

Warm the chickpeas with boiling water, then blend with the remaining oil and half the feta, loosening with water; season. Serve with the lamb, courgettes, and onions, topped with mint and remaining feta.

name: Lamb chops with fruity couscous & mint

ingredients.raw_text: 4 lamb chops or leg steaks, trimmed
20g pack each mint and flat-leaf parsley, roughly chopped

steps: Combine the lamb with the paprika, half the onion, vinegar, and oil; season and set aside.
For the couscous, combine with the pine nuts, dates, lemon zest, and half the juice. Cover with boiling water, cover the bowl, and let stand 10 minutes.

Heat the grill. Grill the marinated lamb 2-3 minutes per side, then rest wrapped in foil 10 minutes. Stir half the herbs and seasoning into the couscous, adding the remaining lemon juice to taste. Combine the remaining herbs, red onion, vinegar, and oil for a vinaigrette. Serve the lamb and couscous with the vinaigrette.

name: Minty lamb with warm veg salad

ingredients.raw_text: 3 medium parsnips, peeled, cut into sticks
2 red onions, peeled, cut into wedges
3 large cooked beetroot, cut into wedges

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Toss the carrots, parsnips, and onions with oil and roast 15 minutes. Add the beetroot and balsamic vinegar and roast 15 minutes more until just tender.

Meanwhile, fry the chops 6-8 minutes, turning halfway, to your liking. Blend half the mint with the remaining vinegar and oil for a sauce, seasoning and thinning with water if needed. Toss the remaining mint with the roasted vegetables and serve alongside the chops, drizzled with mint sauce.

name: Pork chops with rhubarb & grains

ingredients.raw_text: 3 garlic cloves, bashed with skin on

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss the rhubarb, onion, and garlic with balsamic vinegar, oil, and seasoning in a tin. Roast 20 minutes.

Meanwhile, melt the butter and fry the rosemary. Brown the pork 2 minutes per side; remove. Add the grains and stock to the roasted vegetables, mix, then top with the pork and rosemary. Roast 10-12 minutes more until the pork is cooked. Serve each chop with grains, scattered with parsley.

name: Oven-baked pork chops
steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss the potatoes with oil, garlic, and thyme on a tray; season and roast 25 minutes until nearly tender.

Whisk the honey and mustard together. Season the pork and brush with half the glaze. Nestle among the potatoes and bake 10 minutes, then turn and spoon over the remaining glaze; bake 10 minutes more until cooked through.

name: Pork & parsnip traybake

ingredients.raw_text: 4 large parsnips (about 500g/1lb 3oz), peeled and cut lengthways into 6
2 red onions, each cut into 8 wedges through the root

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Toss the vegetables with oil and mustard in a tin, season, and roast 20 minutes. Meanwhile, sear the seasoned pork chops in a hot pan 30 seconds to 1 minute per side, including the fat edges.

Stir the vegetables, top with the chops, and rub with the remaining mustard. Roast 15 minutes more. Drizzle with honey, scatter with sage, and roast 5 minutes more until cooked through. Serve with the pan juices.

name: Cumin pork chops with honey carrots
steps: Whisk half the oil, half the honey, the lemon zest and juice, and cumin seeds together. Coat the pork chops well. Cook in a hot pan with the glaze 5-7 minutes per side, basting occasionally. Wrap in foil to rest.

Meanwhile, boil the carrots 10 minutes until tender. Combine the remaining oil, honey, and cinnamon; add to the same pan with the carrots and toss to coat and heat through. Serve alongside the chops with yogurt.

name: Mustard chops & chips

ingredients.raw_text: 700g floury potato, cut into thin chips, patted dry
1 large fennel bulb, halved and very thinly sliced

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Toss the potatoes with oil and seasoning on a tray and bake 20 minutes. Add the fennel seeds and paprika, turn, and bake 20 minutes more until golden.
Meanwhile, boil the green beans 5-6 minutes, cool under cold water, and drain. Combine oil, vinegar, shallot, sugar, mustard, and seasoning; toss with the beans and fennel.
Rub the chops with oil, season, and pan-fry about 7 minutes per side, browning the fat edge too. Smear with mustard and rest. Serve with the potatoes and bean salad, drizzled with resting juices.

name: Fennel-crusted pork chops with winter celeriac slaw

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tsp each fennel seed and dried oregano
½ small celeriac, cut into matchsticks or shredded
1 large  carrot, cut into matchsticks or shredded

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Combine the oil, fennel seeds, oregano, chilli, and seasoning on a plate and coat the chops. Roast on a tray 40 minutes.
Meanwhile, combine the mayonnaise, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, mustard, sugar, and seasoning; toss with the vegetables for the slaw.
Set aside two chops and a third of the slaw for another meal. Divide the remaining slaw among plates with a chop each.

name: Herb & lemon pork chops

ingredients.food_name: Pork loin chop raw cured, smoked, boiled

ingredients.raw_text: ½ tsp fennel seeds, roughly chopped
zest and juice 1 lemon, plus 1 lemon, quartered

steps: Combine the garlic, parsley, fennel seeds, lemon zest and juice, oil, and bay; rub over the chops. Marinate at least an hour, up to a day.
Season the chops and grill or griddle 5-8 minutes per side until cooked through (wrap cooked ones in foil if cooking in batches). Char the lemon quarters for the last minute and serve alongside.

name: Pork schnitzel
steps: Combine the sage and breadcrumbs with seasoning on a plate. Beat the eggs on another plate. Pat the pork dry, dip in egg, then coat in the crumbs. Freeze or chill until ready to cook.

Defrost if frozen. Place on a greased tray, brush with butter, and grill 7-10 minutes until browned, then flip, brush again, and grill 7 minutes more until crisp and cooked through. Serve with green beans, peas, and cheesy mash.

name: One-pot roast pork chops with fennel & potatoes

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red pepper, halved, deseeded and cut into 8 wedges

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Combine the potatoes, fennel, pepper, thyme, and garlic in a roasting tin. Mix the tomato paste with stock and pour in. Cover tightly with foil and cook 30 minutes. Raise the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7).
Uncover, nestle the pork among the vegetables, season, and roast 15-20 minutes more until golden and cooked through. Serve with the pan juices drizzled over.

name: Pork chops with aubergine
steps: Heat the oil and fry the aubergine 5 minutes until soft and golden. Add the vinegar, then once it settles, stir in the sugar, tomatoes, basil, and seasoning. Cook over high heat 5 minutes until thickened.
Meanwhile, heat the grill to medium, season the pork, and grill 15 minutes, turning halfway, until golden and cooked through. Serve with the aubergine sauce.

name: Balsamic pork with olives

ingredients.raw_text: 2 handfuls green olives, pitted and halved

steps: Combine the oil, vinegar, mustard, and garlic. Score the pork, season, and marinate in the mixture 5 minutes.

Griddle the pork (scraping off excess garlic) 4 minutes per side; keep warm. Add the marinade and olives to the pan, cook 2 minutes, then stir in the basil. Pour any resting juices from the pork into the sauce, drizzle over, and serve with pasta.

name: Cheesy chops & chips

ingredients.raw_text: splash of cider, wine, water or stock
2 tbsp oil, olive if you've got it

ingredients.food_name: Hard cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 230C (210C fan/gas 8). Toss the potatoes, onion, liquid, and oil together; season. Transfer to a sauté pan and bake 20-30 minutes until browning. Add the chops on top and cook 10 minutes more.
Combine the cheese, mustard, and milk. Spread over the chops, switch the oven to grill, and grill about 5 minutes until the cheese bubbles and the potatoes are golden. Serve straight from the pan.

name: Slow-cooker beef pot roast

ingredients.food_name: Beef brisket/centre cut, raw cured, smoked

steps: Heat the oil and dust the beef with seasoned flour.
Place the chopped carrots, celery, parsnips, onion, and mushrooms in the slow cooker on low.
Sear the beef all over in the hot pan, then place on the vegetables.
Add the bay leaves, garlic, and mustard, pour over the wine and stock, cover, and cook 7 hours.
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Transfer the beef to a tray and roast 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, reduce the cooking liquid rapidly in a pan to a rich gravy.
Slice the beef and serve with roast potatoes, the softened vegetables, gravy, and wilted greens.

name: Slow cooker beef curry

ingredients.raw_text: 750g beef for braising (braising steak, chuck, flank etc) cut into chunks

steps: Switch on the slow cooker if needed. Brown the beef in batches in oil and transfer to the slow cooker. Soften the onion in the same pan over low heat, then add the garlic and ginger for 1 minute.
Add the spices and fry 1 minute until fragrant. Add the coconut milk, bring to a simmer, then tip everything into the slow cooker.
Cook 4 hours on high or 8 hours on low. Finish with coriander and serve.

name: Easy chilli con carne

ingredients.raw_text: 2 beef stock cubes (we like Just Bouillon)
2 large red peppers, deseeded and cut into chunks
3 x 400g cans red kidney beans, drained

steps: Set the oven to 150C (130C fan/gas 3). Heat the oil and fry the onions 8 minutes. Add the garlic, spices, and oregano; cook 1 minute. Add the mince gradually, browning it, then stir in the tomatoes, half a can of water, and crumbled stock cube; season.
Cover and bake 30 minutes. Stir in the peppers and sundried tomatoes, then bake 30 minutes more until the peppers are tender. Stir in the beans.
Reheat on the hob until bubbling before serving with avocado or salad, rice or tortilla chips, and soured cream.

For a slow cooker: fry the onions 8 minutes, add the garlic, spices, and oregano for 1 minute, then brown the mince. Transfer everything to the slow cooker with the tomatoes, peppers, sundried tomatoes, beans, and crumbled stock cubes; season. Cook on low 8-10 hours, then serve as above.

name: Easy Singapore noodles

ingredients.raw_text: 1 pepper, sliced (we used ½ green and ½ orange)

steps: Cover the noodles with boiled water, pressing them under to soften evenly; let sit 5-10 minutes until fully soft. Meanwhile, mix the curry powder, turmeric, sugar, sesame oil, soy sauce, and 1 tbsp water.

Get the wok very hot, then add the oil, onion, and pepper; stir-fry 3-4 minutes until softened and lightly browned. Drain the noodles and add to the wok with the sauce and beansprouts. Stir-fry 3-4 minutes more until hot. Adjust seasoning with extra soy or sugar, and add chilli for more heat if desired.

name: Vegan katsu curry

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp rapeseed oil or vegetable oil
¼ large  cucumber, peeled into ribbons
handful of mint leaves or coriander leaves, or both
1 tbsp rapeseed oil or vegetable oil
2cm piece ginger, peeled and grated or finely chopped
½ tbsp curry powder, mild or medium
2 tsp maple syrup (or honey if not cooking for vegans)
200g dried breadcrumbs, we used panko (gluten-free if necessary)

steps: Cook the onion and carrot in the oil 8 minutes until soft and caramelizing. Add the garlic and ginger for 1 minute, then the curry powder and turmeric. Add the coconut milk, maple syrup, and water; season, cover, and simmer 20 minutes, thinning with water as needed.

Meanwhile, halve the tofu and cut into triangles. Combine cornflour with water and seasoning in one bowl and breadcrumbs in another. Coat each tofu piece in the cornflour mixture then the crumbs, pressing firmly (thicken the cornflour mix if it isn't sticking).
