Once the carrot is soft, blend the sauce smooth, thinning with water if needed, and adjust seasoning with salt, maple syrup, or lime juice. Keep warm.
Fry the tofu 4-5 minutes per side until golden. Serve over rice with the curry sauce, crispy tofu, cucumber and carrot ribbons, herbs, and lime wedges.

name: Stuffed pumpkin
steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Cut the top off the pumpkin and scoop out the seeds (save for another use). Rub with oil inside and out, season, and roast with the lid on the side 45 minutes until tender.
Meanwhile, rinse and cook the wild rice per pack instructions, then spread out to cool. Thinly slice the fennel and apple, tossing with half the lemon juice to prevent browning.

Heat the remaining oil and fry the fennel seeds and chilli flakes; once popping, add half the garlic and the fennel, cooking 5 minutes until softened. Mix in the apple, pecans, and lemon zest. Combine with the cooked rice and parsley, and season.

Fill the pumpkin with the mixture and return to the oven 10-15 minutes until hot through. Whisk the remaining lemon juice with the tahini, remaining garlic, and water for a dressing. Serve the pumpkin topped with pomegranate seeds and the dressing.

name: Giant couscous salad with charred veg & tangy pesto

ingredients.raw_text: 2 green or orange peppers, deseeded and cubed

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss the beetroot, onions, and peppers with oil, spread on a lined tray, and roast 35 minutes. Add the cherry tomatoes and roast 10 minutes more until softened and tender.

Meanwhile, cook the couscous per pack instructions, rinse, and drain. For the pesto, blend the coriander, half the parsley, garlic, chilli, cumin, vinegar, oil, most of the pine nuts, and water until smooth.
Toss the roasted vegetables and remaining parsley through the couscous, top with the pesto, and scatter with the reserved pine nuts. For two servings, eat half now and chill the rest for another day.

name: Thirty-minute courgettes with dukkah sprinkle
steps: Heat the oil and cook the onions 5 minutes, stirring, until starting to color. Stir in the coriander and paprika, then add the tomatoes and a can of water. Add the bouillon powder and courgettes, cover, and cook 6 minutes.
Meanwhile, make the dukkah: warm the whole spices, sesame seeds, and almonds in a dry pan, stirring, until fragrant, then remove from the heat.

Add the butter beans, tomatoes, and peas to the courgettes, cover, and cook 5 minutes more. Stir in the coriander and spoon into bowls. Crush the toasted spice mix and scatter on top. For two servings, jar half the seed mix and refrigerate half the vegetable mixture for another day.

name: Spiced aubergine bake
steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Brush the aubergine slices with oil and arrange in a single layer on trays. Cook 10 minutes, flip, and cook 5-10 minutes more until golden. Reduce the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4).

Heat the coconut oil and sweat the onions, covered, 5 minutes until softened. Add the garlic, mustard seeds, fenugreek, garam masala, chilli powder, cinnamon stick, cumin, and coriander; cook briefly until fragrant.
Add the chopped tomatoes and coconut milk to the spiced onions; season to taste.

Layer a third of the sauce in a baking dish, then half the aubergine, another third of sauce, the remaining aubergine, and the rest of the sauce. Top with flaked almonds and coriander, and bake 25-30 minutes. Garnish with more coriander.

name: Falafel burgers

ingredients.raw_text: handful of flat-leaf parsley  or curly parsley
½ tsp harissa paste  or chilli powder

ingredients.food_name: Green asparagus salad (cooked) with vinegar marinade

steps: Pat the drained chickpeas dry. Blend with the onion, garlic, parsley, cumin, coriander, harissa, flour, and salt until fairly smooth. Shape into four patties.
Heat the oil and fry the patties 3 minutes per side until lightly golden. Serve with toasted pitta, tomato salsa, and green salad.

name: Vegan brownies

ingredients.raw_text: 80g vegan margarine, plus extra for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 170C (150C fan/gas 3.5) and line a square tin. Combine the flaxseed with water and set aside at least 5 minutes.
Melt most of the chocolate with the coffee and margarine and water over low heat; cool slightly.

Combine the flour, almonds, cocoa, baking powder, and salt. Whisk the sugar into the melted chocolate mixture until smooth and glossy. Stir in the flaxseed mixture, vanilla, and remaining chocolate, then the flour mixture. Spoon into the tin.
Bake 35-45 minutes until a skewer comes out with moist crumbs. Cool completely in the tin before cutting; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Vegan leek & potato soup

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp rapeseed oil, plus a drizzle to serve (optional)
500ml vegan vegetable stock, made with 1½ tsp bouillon powder
chopped chives and bread, to serve

steps: Heat the oil and cook the garlic and leeks, stirring, until softening. Add the potatoes and stock, cover, and simmer 15 minutes until soft.

Stir in the almond milk, remove from the heat, and blend to your preferred texture — chunky or smooth. Reheat gently if needed, then serve topped with chives, a drizzle of oil, and bread. Freezes up to three months.

name: Vegan banana bread

ingredients.raw_text: 75ml vegetable oil or sunflower oil, plus extra for the tin
225g plain flour (or use self-raising flour and reduce the baking powder to 2 heaped tsp)

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Mash the bananas and combine with the oil and sugar.
Add the flour, baking powder, and cinnamon or mixed spice, combining well. Fold in dried fruit or nuts if using.
Bake in a lined loaf tin 20 minutes, covering with foil if browning too fast.
Bake 20 minutes more until a skewer comes out clean.
Cool briefly before slicing; even better the day after baking.

name: Carrot & coriander soup

ingredients.raw_text: handful coriander (about ½ a supermarket packet)

steps: Heat the oil and fry the onion 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the ground coriander and potato, cooking 1 minute.
Add the carrots and stock, bring to a boil, then reduce the heat.
Cover and cook 20 minutes until the carrots are tender.
Blend with fresh coriander until smooth (in batches if needed). Return to the pan, season, and reheat to serve.

name: Mushroom & potato curry

ingredients.raw_text: 1 large potato, chopped into small chunks
1 aubergine, trimmed and chopped into chunks
2-4 tbsp curry paste (depending on how hot you like it)

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion and potato, covered, over low heat 5 minutes until softening. Add the aubergine and mushrooms, cooking a few minutes more.
Stir in the curry paste, then add the stock and coconut milk. Bring to a boil and simmer 10 minutes until the potato is tender. Finish with coriander and serve with rice or naan.

name: Kidney bean curry

ingredients.raw_text: thumb-sized piece of ginger, peeled and finely chopped
1 small pack coriander, stalks finely chopped, leaves roughly shredded

steps: Heat the oil over low-medium heat and cook the onion with a pinch of salt slowly until soft and lightly colored. Add the garlic, ginger, and coriander stalks; cook 2 minutes more until fragrant.
Stir in the spices and cook 1 minute until aromatic. Add the chopped tomatoes and kidney beans with their liquid; bring to a boil.
Lower the heat and simmer 15 minutes until thickened. Season to taste and serve with basmati rice and coriander leaves.

name: Vegan tagine

ingredients.raw_text: ½ tsp each  ground cinnamon, coriander and cumin

steps: Heat the oil and fry the onions 5 minutes until soft. Stir in the spices. Add the courgettes, tomatoes, chickpeas, raisins, and stock, and bring to a boil. Cover and simmer 10 minutes. Stir in the peas and cook 5 minutes more. Finish with coriander.

name: Vegan sponge cake

ingredients.raw_text: 150g dairy-free spread, plus extra for the tins
ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

ingredients.raw_text: 300ml dairy-free milk, we used oat milk

ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

ingredients.raw_text: 200g icing sugar, plus extra for dusting

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Line two sandwich tins with parchment and grease with dairy-free spread.

Combine the dairy-free milk with the vinegar and let sit a few minutes until slightly curdled. Combine half the vanilla seeds with the remaining cake ingredients in a bowl, then add the milk mixture and beat until smooth.
Divide between the tins and bake 30-35 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tins, then turn out onto racks.
For the buttercream, beat the dairy-free spread, icing sugar, and remaining vanilla seeds until pale and fluffy (mix by hand if the spread is very soft, or add extra milk if too firm).
Spread jam on one sponge, top with buttercream, and sandwich with the second sponge. Dust with icing sugar before slicing.

name: Coconut & banana pancakes
steps: Combine the flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt. Whisk the coconut milk to recombine, then measure out and stir into the dry mixture (or blend everything together) to a smooth batter.

Heat an oiled pan and cook 2 tbsp batter per pancake, two at a time. Press in banana pieces and cook until bubbles appear and edges look dry; flip gently (they're delicate) and cook 1 minute more. Repeat to make 8-10 pancakes.
Simmer the remaining coconut milk and sugar with a pinch of salt until it thickens like cream. Serve over the pancakes with passion fruit seeds.

name: Avocado hummus & crudités

ingredients.raw_text: pinch chilli flakes, plus extra to serve

steps: Blend the avocado, chickpeas, garlic, chilli flakes, and lime juice until smooth; season. Top with coriander and extra chilli flakes, and serve with carrot, pepper, and sugar snap crudites. Can be made the night before.

name: Vegan leek & potato soup

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp rapeseed oil, plus a drizzle to serve (optional)
500ml vegan vegetable stock, made with 1½ tsp bouillon powder
chopped chives and bread, to serve

steps: Heat the oil and cook the garlic and leeks, stirring, until softening. Add the potatoes and stock, cover, and simmer 15 minutes until soft.

Stir in the almond milk, remove from the heat, and blend to your preferred texture — chunky or smooth. Reheat gently if needed, then serve topped with chives, a drizzle of oil, and bread. Freezes up to three months.

name: Carrot & coriander soup

ingredients.raw_text: handful coriander (about ½ a supermarket packet)

steps: Heat the oil and fry the onion 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in the ground coriander and potato, cooking 1 minute.
Add the carrots and stock, bring to a boil, then reduce the heat.
Cover and cook 20 minutes until the carrots are tender.
Blend with fresh coriander until smooth (in batches if needed). Return to the pan, season, and reheat to serve.

name: Healthy pumpkin soup

ingredients.raw_text: 1 pumpkin (around 1.5kg), peeled and chopped, seeds reserved

steps: Rinse and dry the pumpkin seeds. Toast most of them in a pan with the oats and half the coriander seeds and chilli flakes until fragrant. Stir in the tamari and maple syrup, simmering briefly until sticky. Set aside.
Heat the oil and cook the onion, celery, carrots, garlic, and pumpkin with the remaining spices and seasoning 10 minutes. Add the stock, bring to a simmer, cover, and cook 20-25 minutes.
Blend until smooth and serve topped with the savory granola.

name: Soup maker tomato soup

ingredients.raw_text: 500g ripe tomatoes, off the vine and quartered or halved

steps: Add all the ingredients to the soup maker (without exceeding the max fill line) and run the smooth soup function.
Once done, season well, adjusting sweetness with a little sugar, salt, or tomato puree if needed.

name: Indian chickpea & vegetable soup

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tsp finely grated fresh root ginger
2 large carrots, quartered lengthways and chopped

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion, ginger, and garlic 2 minutes. Add the garam masala for 1 minute, then the stock and carrots. Simmer 10 minutes, add the chickpeas, and blend briefly with a stick blender for some texture. Stir in the beans and simmer 3 minutes. Pack for lunch or serve with naan.

name: Minty pea & potato soup

ingredients.raw_text: 800g potato, peeled and cut into small chunks

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion 5 minutes until softened. Add the potatoes and stock, and bring to a boil. Cover and simmer 10-15 minutes until tender, adding the peas 2 minutes before the end.
Remove a quarter of the vegetables with a slotted spoon and set aside. Blend the rest smooth, then stir back in the reserved vegetables, chopped mint, and seasoning. Serve with bread.

name: Moroccan spiced cauliflower & almond soup

ingredients.raw_text: ½ tsp each ground cinnamon, cumin and  coriander
2 tbsp harissa paste, plus extra drizzle
50g toasted flaked almond, plus extra to serve

steps: Cut the cauliflower into florets. Fry the oil, cinnamon, cumin, coriander, and harissa 2 minutes. Add the cauliflower, stock, and almonds. Cover and cook 20 minutes until tender. Blend until smooth and serve with extra harissa and toasted almonds.

name: Jackfruit bolognese with vegan parmesan

ingredients.raw_text: 410g can jackfruit in water, drained and finely chopped
4 tsp vegetable bouillon powder (check it’s vegan)

steps: Heat the oil and cook the carrots, celery, and garlic 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Meanwhile, blend the cashews, nutritional yeast, and garlic granules to fine crumbs for the vegan parmesan, seasoning with salt if needed.
Add the jackfruit, lentils, water, tomato puree, bouillon powder, and thyme to the vegetables; cover and cook 25 minutes, adding water if needed for a pulpy consistency.
Meanwhile, cook the spaghetti per pack instructions. Toss with the sauce and top with the vegan parmesan and parsley. The sauce keeps refrigerated up to 3 days or frozen up to 3 months.

name: Spaghetti puttanesca with red beans & spinach
steps: Boil the spaghetti 10-12 minutes until al dente. Meanwhile, cook the onion in the oil, covered, until tender and golden. Stir in the chilli, garlic, and cherry tomatoes.
Add the vinegar, capers, olives, and paprika with a ladle of pasta water. Stir in the beans and warm through.
Wilt the spinach in the pasta water, then drain well. Toss with the tomato-bean mixture, parsley, and basil, and serve.

name: Tomato & aubergine pasta
steps: Boil the pasta per pack instructions. Meanwhile, fry the aubergine in oil 5 minutes; season. Add garlic and sugar, frying 2 minutes more until softened. Add the tomatoes, capers, and parsley, warming through, then toss with the drained pasta.

name: Asparagus & lemon spaghetti with peas

ingredients.raw_text: 160g asparagus, ends trimmed and cut into lengths
2 leeks (220g), cut into lengths, then thin strips
1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped
1 lemon, zested and juiced, plus wedges to serve

steps: Boil the spaghetti 12 minutes, adding the asparagus for the final 3. Meanwhile, heat the oil and cook the leeks and chilli 5 minutes. Stir in the garlic, peas, and lemon zest and juice; cook a few minutes more.
Drain the pasta and toss into the pan with a splash of the pasta water until combined. Serve in bowls with lemon wedges.

name: Linguine with avocado, tomato & lime

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped (optional)

steps: Cook the pasta per pack instructions, about 10 minutes. Meanwhile, combine the lime juice and zest with the avocado, tomatoes, coriander, onion, and chilli (if using). Drain the pasta, toss through the mixture, and serve warm or cold.

name: Vegan kale pesto pasta
steps: Blanch the kale 30 seconds, then plunge into ice water 5 minutes. Drain and pat dry.
Blend the basil, garlic, seeds, oil, nutritional yeast, lemon juice and zest, and kale until smooth; season, thinning with water if too thick.
Cook the pasta per pack instructions and toss with the pesto to serve.

name: Favourite pasta salad
steps: Cook the pasta, adding the peas for the final 2 minutes. Drain, rinse under cold water, and drain again. Combine with the herbs, lemon zest and juice, oil, and seasoning. Cover and chill, portioning out as needed; after a few days, stir in a little extra oil or something creamy to loosen it.

name: Burnt aubergine veggie chilli

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp olive oil or rapeseed oil
70g puy lentils or green lentils, rinsed
tortilla chips, mashed avocado, yogurt or soured cream, grated cheddar, roughly chopped coriander (optional)

steps: Char the aubergine directly over a gas flame (or under a very hot grill), turning until blackened all over. Cool, peel off the skin, discard the stem, and roughly chop the flesh.
Heat the oil and cook the onion and carrots with salt over low-medium heat 15-20 minutes until the carrots soften.

Add the aubergine, both lentils, kidney beans with their liquid, soy sauce, tomatoes, chocolate, chilli powder, oregano, and spices. Add the stock, bring to a boil, then reduce to very low heat. Cover and cook 1.5 hours, stirring every 15-20 minutes.
Uncover and simmer over low-medium heat, stirring occasionally, about 15 minutes until thickened. Stir in the lime juice and adjust seasoning. Serve hot over rice.

name: Sweet potato & peanut curry

ingredients.raw_text: 3 tbsp Thai red curry paste (check the label to make sure it’s vegetarian/ vegan)
500g sweet potato, peeled and cut into chunks

steps: Melt the coconut oil and soften the onion 5 minutes. Add the garlic and ginger, cooking 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the curry paste, peanut butter, and sweet potato, then add the coconut milk and water.
Bring to a boil, then simmer uncovered 25-30 minutes until the sweet potato is soft.
Stir in the spinach and lime juice, season, and serve with rice and a scattering of peanuts if desired.

name: Gingery broccoli-fry with cashews

ingredients.raw_text: 320g head of  broccoli, stalks and florets separated
1 red pepper, deseeded and cut into thin strips
1 large carrot (160g), cut into thin strips
1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped, plus extra sliced, to serve
7g chopped coriander, plus extra to serve

steps: Pulse the broccoli stalks in a food processor until finely chopped, then add the florets and pulse to a rice-like texture.
Toast the cashews in a dry pan and set aside. Heat the oil and stir-fry the ginger, onion, pepper, carrot, garlic, and chilli 2-3 minutes until browning, then cover and cook 2 minutes more.

Add the broccoli rice and water; stir-fry 3 minutes until tender. Add the tamari, lime juice and zest, and coriander, then pour in the eggs and stir-fry briefly to just set. Serve topped with the cashews, extra coriander, and chilli.

name: Tofu stir-fry
steps: Combine the soy sauce, honey, and vinegar; set aside.

Dry and season the tofu. Fry in half the oil over high heat 5 minutes until golden, stirring occasionally. Add the garlic and ginger for 1 minute. Toss with half the soy dressing in a bowl; set aside.
Add the remaining oil to the pan and cook the vegetables 5 minutes until starting to color, then add a splash of water and cook 3-5 minutes more until tender.

Combine the cornflour with water and stir into the remaining dressing, then add to the pan and bubble 30 seconds, coating everything. Finish with spring onion, sesame seeds, and coriander. Serve with rice.

name: Sweet potato & peanut curry

ingredients.raw_text: 3 tbsp Thai red curry paste (check the label to make sure it’s vegetarian/ vegan)
500g sweet potato, peeled and cut into chunks

steps: Melt the coconut oil and soften the onion 5 minutes. Add the garlic and ginger, cooking 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the curry paste, peanut butter, and sweet potato, then add the coconut milk and water.
Bring to a boil, then simmer uncovered 25-30 minutes until the sweet potato is soft.
Stir in the spinach and lime juice, season, and serve with rice and a scattering of peanuts if desired.

name: Tomato & mascarpone risotto

ingredients.food_name: Vegetarian cold cuts alternative, with egg
Mascarpone min. 80 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small bunch of basil, chopped

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion with salt 10 minutes until softening. Add the garlic for 1 minute, then the rice, cooking 2 minutes.
Add the tomatoes and simmer. Add half the stock, stirring until absorbed, then the rest a ladle at a time, cooking about 20 minutes until the rice is al dente.
Stir in the parmesan, mascarpone, and basil; season and serve.

name: Pea & leek open lasagne

ingredients.raw_text: 2 leeks, washed and sliced into half moons

steps: Heat the oil and cook the leeks, garlic, and salt until soft. Meanwhile, boil water for the pasta.

Add the peas and kale with a splash of water; cook until the kale wilts and peas defrost. Lower the heat and stir in the mustard, creme fraiche, and most of the lemon zest, adding water to loosen; season.

Cook the lasagne sheets per pack instructions and drain. Plate one sheet, top with half the leek-pea mixture, layer the second sheet, and top with the remaining greens. Finish with the remaining lemon zest and black pepper.

name: Air fryer pasta chips
steps: Cook the pasta per pack instructions, drain, and transfer to a bowl.
Toss with the oil, spices, and herbs, seasoning well.
Air fry at 180C for 15-20 minutes, shaking every 5 minutes, until crisp and golden. Serve alone or with a dip.

name: Linguine with avocado, tomato & lime

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped (optional)

steps: Cook the pasta per pack instructions, about 10 minutes. Meanwhile, combine the lime juice and zest with the avocado, tomatoes, coriander, onion, and chilli (if using). Drain the pasta, toss through the mixture, and serve warm or cold.

name: Tomato penne with avocado

ingredients.raw_text: 1 large onion, sliced, plus 1 tbsp finely chopped
1 orange pepper, deseeded and cut into chunks
handful coriander, chopped, plus extra to serve

steps: Boil the pasta 10-12 minutes until al dente. Meanwhile, heat the oil and fry the onion and pepper 10 minutes, stirring, until golden. Add the garlic and spices, then the tomatoes, half a can of water, corn, and bouillon. Cover and simmer 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, toss the avocado with the lime juice, zest, and finely chopped onion.
Drain the penne and stir into the sauce with the coriander. Divide into bowls, top with the avocado, and scatter with coriander leaves.

name: Creamy mushroom pasta

ingredients.raw_text: 200g parmesan (or vegetarian alternative), grated, plus extra to serve
ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small bunch parsley, finely chopped

steps: Heat the oil and butter and cook the onion over low heat 10 minutes until soft and translucent.
Add the mushrooms and cook 10 minutes over medium heat. Add the garlic for 2 minutes, then the wine; simmer until reduced by half.
Add the cream and simmer, then stir in the lemon zest and parmesan; season generously.
Meanwhile, cook the pasta per pack instructions, reserving some cooking water. Toss the pasta into the sauce, loosening with the reserved water. Finish with parsley and extra cheese.

name: Pasta arrabbiata with aubergine

ingredients.raw_text: large handful of  basil, plus extra to serve
25g parmesan or vegetarian Italian-style hard cheese, finely grated
ingredients.food_name: Risotto with butter and Parmesan cheese

steps: Heat the oil in a large pan, add the onion, cover, and cook 5 minutes. Uncover and cook 5 minutes more, stirring, until softened. Stir in the garlic, chilli flakes, and paprika briefly, then add the tomatoes and a can of water. Mix in the bouillon and aubergine; bring to a simmer, cover, and cook 20 minutes.

Boil the penne about 12 minutes until al dente. Drain, keeping 60ml of the water. Toss the pasta into the sauce with the basil, adding a little reserved water if needed. Divide between two bowls and top with cheese and extra basil.

name: Vegetarian lasagne

ingredients.raw_text: 3 red peppers, cut into large chunks
2 aubergines, cut into ½ cm thick slices
8 tbsp olive oil, plus extra for the dish

ingredients.food_name: Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: For the tomato sauce, heat the oil and cook the onions, garlic, and carrot 5-7 minutes until softened. Stir in the tomato puree, cook 1 minute, add the wine, and reduce by two-thirds. Add the tomatoes and basil, bring to a boil, and simmer 20 minutes. Cool and blend smooth. Keeps refrigerated three days or frozen three months.

For the white sauce, melt the butter, stir in the flour, and cook 2 minutes. Gradually whisk in the milk, bring to a boil, and cook until thickened enough to coat a spoon. Keeps refrigerated three days or frozen three months.
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss the peppers and aubergines with oil, season, and roast 25 minutes until lightly browned.

Reduce the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Layer the roasted vegetables in an oiled dish with a third of the tomato sauce, then lasagne sheets, then a quarter of the white sauce. Repeat for three pasta layers.
Top with the remaining white sauce, mozzarella, and cherry tomatoes. Bake 45 minutes until bubbling and golden.

name: Pepper & lemon spaghetti with basil & pine nuts

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp finely grated parmesan or vegetarian alternative (optional)
ingredients.food_name: Vegetarian cold cuts alternative, with egg

steps: Heat the oil and cook the pepper 5 minutes. Meanwhile, boil the pasta 10-12 minutes until tender.
Add the courgette and garlic to the pepper and cook, stirring often, 10-15 minutes until very soft.
Stir in the lemon zest and juice, basil, and drained spaghetti, tossing with a little reserved pasta water until coated. Add the pine nuts, then serve topped with parmesan if using.

name: Spinach & courgette lasagne

ingredients.food_name: Mascarpone min. 80 % fat in dry matter
Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Wilt the spinach with boiling water, cool, and squeeze dry. Heat the oil and soften the garlic 1 minute. Add the nutmeg for 1 minute, then the mascarpone, spinach, half the cream, and half the parmesan; season generously and set aside.

Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Layer a third of the filling in a baking dish, top with lasagne sheets, then courgettes; repeat twice more. Pour the remaining cream over the top and sprinkle with remaining parmesan. Bake 40-45 minutes until bubbling and the pasta is tender. Rest 5 minutes before serving.

name: Slow cooker ratatouille

ingredients.raw_text: 3 courgettes, halved and cut into 2cm pieces
small bunch of basil, roughly chopped, plus a few extra leaves to serve

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion 8 minutes until translucent. Add the garlic and cook 1 minute. Raise the heat, add the aubergines, and fry 5 minutes until golden. Stir in the courgettes and peppers; cook 5 minutes more until slightly softened. Add the tomato puree, fresh tomatoes, herbs, canned tomatoes, vinegar, sugar, and 1 tsp salt; bring to a boil.
Transfer to the slow cooker and cook on low 5-6 hours until everything is soft and the sauce has thickened. Season, top with basil, and serve with sourdough.

name: Slow cooker spiced root & lentil casserole

ingredients.raw_text: 3 carrots, peeled and cut into 3cm slices
500g (about 5 medium) parsnips, peeled and cut into 3cm slices

steps: Switch on the slow cooker to low. Cook the onion in oil 10 minutes until soft. Add the carrots and parsnips, frying 8-10 minutes until golden. Stir in the garlic and spices, frying 4-5 minutes until fragrant, adding water if needed. Transfer to the slow cooker with the lentils, stock, bay, and seasoning.
Cover and cook on low 5-6 hours until the vegetables are tender and the sauce thickened. Adjust seasoning and add lemon juice to taste. Serve with bread, rice, or potatoes.

name: Slow cooker spiced apples with barley
steps: Switch on the slow cooker if needed. Add the barley and 750ml boiling water. Core the apples (leaving a coin-sized hole) and halve them.
Arrange the apples skin-side down over the barley. Combine the cinnamon, nutmeg, and orange zest and sprinkle over the top.
Cook on Low for 2 hours. Serve with natural yogurt.

name: Slow cooker bio yogurt

ingredients.raw_text: 100ml live yogurt, either shop bought or from a previous homemade batch

steps: Warm the milk in the slow cooker on High until it reaches 82C - roughly two hours. Switch off and let it cool to 43C, which takes another 2-3 hours. Blend a cup of the warm milk with the yogurt, stir back into the cooker, then mix thoroughly. Cover, insulate with a thick towel, and leave undisturbed 9-12 hours until set.

Enjoy over cereal, porridge, or fruit, blended into smoothies, or used in marinades. For a thicker result - good for dips - strain through a muslin-lined sieve set over a bowl until it reaches your preferred texture; longer straining gives a thicker yogurt. Keeps refrigerated up to two weeks.

name: Slow-cooked summer blush

ingredients.raw_text: 2 heads of  garlic, broken down into individual unpeeled cloves

steps: Set the oven to 160C (140C fan/gas 3). Toss everything except the basil together in a roasting pan; season.
Roast undisturbed 2 hours. Cool, then either drain and serve the juices on the side, or stir through the basil and adjust seasoning.
Can be roasted up to 2 days ahead; chill covered, but bring to room temperature at least 4 hours before serving.

name: Slow cooker Turkish breakfast eggs

ingredients.raw_text: 1 red pepper, cored and finely sliced

steps: Oil the slow cooker insert and switch it on if needed. In a heavy frying pan, heat the remaining oil and cook the onions, pepper, and chilli until softened. Transfer to the slow cooker, add the cherry tomatoes and bread, mix, and season.
Beat the eggs with the milk and parsley, then pour evenly over the mixture so everything is covered. Cook 5-6 hours. Serve topped with yogurt.

name: 5-a-day tagine

ingredients.raw_text: 4 small parsnips, or 3 large, cut into chunks
2 red peppers, deseeded and cut into chunks
1 tsp each ground cumin, paprika, cinnamon and mild chilli powder

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss the vegetables with half the oil on trays, season, and roast 30 minutes until tender and browning.

Meanwhile, fry the spices in the remaining oil 1 minute until fragrant. Add the tomatoes, apricots, honey, and water; simmer 5 minutes until slightly reduced and the apricots plump. Stir in the roasted vegetables and season. Serve with couscous or jacket potatoes.

name: Gluten-free lemon drizzle cake

ingredients.raw_text: 175g ground almond (switch for polenta or wheat-free flour to make this recipe nut-free)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Line and grease a cake tin. Beat the sugar and butter until light and fluffy, then beat in the egg gradually. Fold in the almonds, cold mashed potato, lemon zest, and baking powder.

Transfer to the tin, level, and bake 40-45 minutes until golden and a skewer comes out clean. Cool 10 minutes, then turn out onto a rack. Combine the granulated sugar and lemon juice and spoon over the warm cake, letting it drip down the sides. Cool completely before slicing.

name: Flourless chocolate & pear cake

ingredients.raw_text: 85g butter, plus 1 tbsp extra for tin
85g golden caster sugar, plus extra for tin
85g gluten-free dark chocolate, broken into pieces
85g hazelnut, toasted and ground in a food processor
4 very ripe pears, peeled, halved and cored

steps: Line a tin base with parchment. Brush with melted butter, then coat with sugar, tipping out any excess.

Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Melt the chocolate and butter over hot water, remove from heat, stir in the brandy, and cool. Whisk the egg yolks and sugar until pale and thick, then fold into the chocolate with the hazelnuts.

Whisk the egg whites to soft peaks. Fold a spoonful into the chocolate mixture, then carefully fold in the rest in two additions. Spoon into the tin, level, and arrange the pears cut-side down on top. Bake 40 minutes until the pears are soft and the cake is cooked through. Cool slightly in the tin before releasing, then cool completely on a rack. Dust with icing sugar and serve with creme fraiche.

name: Easy chocolate fudge cake

ingredients.raw_text: 150ml sunflower oil, plus extra for the tin
2½ tbsp milk (a little more if needed)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two sandwich tins. Sift the flour, cocoa, and bicarbonate of soda together with the sugar.
Make a well and add the golden syrup, eggs, oil, and milk; beat until smooth.
Divide between the tins and bake 25-30 minutes until risen and firm. Cool 10 minutes before turning out.
For the icing, beat the butter until soft, gradually beat in the icing sugar and cocoa, then add milk until fluffy and spreadable.
Sandwich the cakes together and cover the top and sides with the remaining icing.

name: Rhubarb gin

ingredients.raw_text: 400g caster sugar (don't use golden - it muddies the colour)

steps: Trim the rhubarb and cut into short lengths. Combine with the sugar in a large jar, shake, seal, and leave overnight to draw out the juice.
The next day, add the gin, seal, and shake. Leave about 4 weeks before drinking, straining if desired or serving straight from the jar over time.

name: Vodka martini

ingredients.raw_text: olive or lemon peel, to garnish

steps: Stir the vodka and dry vermouth with ice, or shake in a cocktail shaker.
Strain into a chilled glass and serve with an olive or a twist of lemon peel.

name: Vodka martini

ingredients.raw_text: olive or lemon peel, to garnish

steps: Stir the vodka and dry vermouth with ice, or shake in a cocktail shaker.
Strain into a chilled glass and serve with an olive or a twist of lemon peel.

name: Martini

ingredients.raw_text: olive or lemon peel, to garnish

steps: Stir the gin or vodka with the dry vermouth and ice, or shake in a cocktail shaker.
Strain into a chilled glass and serve with an olive or a twist of lemon peel.

name: Halloumi, carrot & orange salad

ingredients.raw_text: 3 tbsp rapeseed or olive oil, plus extra for frying

steps: Peel and segment the oranges over a bowl to catch the juice, squeezing extra juice from the pith. Whisk in the mustard, honey, vinegar, oil, and seasoning.

Peel carrot ribbons into the dressing and toss gently. Cook the halloumi in a little oil until golden on both sides. Toss the watercress through the dressed carrots, plate, and top with halloumi and orange segments.

name: Zingy grapefruit, prawn, avocado & tamarind salad

ingredients.raw_text: 3cm piece of ginger, peeled and finely grated

ingredients.food_name: Velvety fish sauce from white basic sauce

ingredients.raw_text: 2 ripe avocados, stoned, peeled and thinly sliced
2 red grapefruits, peeled, pith removed, sliced into rounds
½ small bunch of  coriander, leaves picked and torn

steps: Combine the sesame oil, tamarind, garlic, ginger, fish sauce, and lime juice; adjust with more fish sauce and season.
Arrange the avocado, grapefruit, radishes, and prawns on a platter. Drizzle with the dressing and scatter with sesame seeds and coriander.

name: Smoked salmon, leek & dill tart
steps: Cook the leeks in the oil or butter with a pinch of salt 10 minutes, stirring, until softened. Cool a few minutes.
Meanwhile, whisk the creme fraiche, eggs, black pepper, lemon zest, salt, and half the dill.
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Unroll the pastry into a tin so it comes up the sides, trimming excess if desired.
Spread the cooled leeks over the pastry, top with the smoked salmon, and pour over the egg mixture. Season and bake 25-30 minutes until set and golden.
Cool 10 minutes before serving, scattered with the remaining dill. Keeps chilled up to two days; serve hot or cold.

name: Caprese salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 balls of mozzarella (or vegetarian alternative), sliced
ingredients.food_name: Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Arrange alternating slices of tomato and mozzarella on a platter, scatter with basil, season generously, and drizzle with olive oil and vinegar. Serve right away.

name: Smoked salmon with prawns, horseradish cream & lime vinaigrette

ingredients.raw_text: 10 large cooked prawns, peeled but tails left on
juice 1 lime, finely grated zest of ½
½ tsp finely grated fresh root ginger

steps: Combine the creme fraiche with the horseradish and seasoning. For the dressing, whisk the lime juice and zest with the honey, ginger, and seasoning, then whisk in the oil.
Arrange the smoked salmon and prawns on plates, topped with the horseradish cream. Toss the salad with most of the dressing and pile on top. Drizzle with remaining dressing to serve.

name: Grilled lobster tails with lemon & herb butter

ingredients.raw_text: handful parsley leaves, finely chopped, plus extra to serve

steps: Combine the butter ingredients, season, and set aside (can be made two days ahead; soften before using).

Cut along the top of each lobster shell, flip and crack the ribs, then loosen the meat, keeping it attached at the base, and pull it half out. Slit along the top without cutting through and remove any vein. Sit the tails in a tray and dot with butter (can be prepped a few hours ahead and chilled).
Heat the grill to high and grill 10 minutes until cooked through. Plate and drizzle with the pan butter, or serve the butter alongside for dipping, with lemon wedges and extra parsley.

name: One-pot chicken chasseur

ingredients.raw_text: 200g pack small  button or chestnut mushrooms

steps: Heat the oil and half the butter in a large lidded casserole.
Season the chicken legs and brown 5 minutes per side; remove and set aside.
Melt the remaining butter, add the onion, and cook 5 minutes until soft.
Add the garlic for 1 minute, then the mushrooms for 2 minutes, then the wine.
Stir in the tomato puree, let it bubble and reduce 5 minutes, then add the thyme and stock.
Return the chicken to the pan, cover, and simmer gently about 1 hour until very tender.
Remove the chicken and keep warm. Boil the sauce rapidly 10 minutes until syrupy and concentrated.
Return the chicken to the sauce and serve.

name: Roast cauliflower with caper dressing

ingredients.raw_text: 1 medium cauliflower, leaves trimmed and reserved

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Scatter the cauliflower leaves in a small tin and place the cauliflower in the middle. Rub with butter, sprinkle with salt, and roast 20 minutes.

Reduce to 160C (140C fan/gas 3) and cook 20 minutes more, basting with butter. Add the oil, capers, parsley, and lemon zest and juice to the hot butter, then spoon over the cauliflower a few times before serving so it soaks up the flavor. Cut into wedges and serve with the crisp leaves.

name: Lobster thermidor

ingredients.raw_text: 2 cooked lobsters (about 500g each), defrosted if frozen, reserve the shell and scraps for the sauce (below)
chips or boiled baby potatoes and a green salad, to serve
food_name: Green asparagus salad (cooked) with vinegar marinade

ingredients.raw_text: 2 banana shallots or 3 small shallots, finely chopped
handful of parsley leaves chopped (or use a mix of parsley, chervil, chives and tarragon)

ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Snap the claws and legs from each lobster. Halve the body lengthways, scoop the head contents into a bowl, and remove the vein from the tail. Chop the tail meat into another bowl. Set the empty shells cut-side up on a tray.

Crack the claws and knuckles and pick out the meat, adding to the tail meat bowl; put broken shell pieces with the head meat. Push meat from the legs similarly with a rolling pin. Can be prepped a day ahead and chilled.

For the sauce, cook the shallots in butter 5-6 minutes until soft and golden. Add the broken shells and head meat, raise the heat, and cook 2 minutes. Add the wine and reduce 2 minutes. Add the stock and cream, bring to a boil, and cook 10-12 minutes until reduced and thickened. Cool slightly, then strain through a fine sieve, pressing to extract all the liquid. Stir in the mustard and lemon juice, and season. Can be made a day ahead and chilled.

Heat the grill to high. Fill the reserved shells with the claw and tail meat. Reheat the sauce if needed and stir in the herbs. Spoon over the lobster meat, sprinkle with parmesan, and grill 4-5 minutes until bubbling and lightly golden. Serve with chips or boiled potatoes and a green salad.

name: Spiced aubergine bake
steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Brush the aubergine slices with oil and arrange in a single layer on trays. Cook 10 minutes, flip, and cook 5-10 minutes more until golden. Reduce the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4).

Heat the coconut oil and sweat the onions, covered, 5 minutes until softened. Add the garlic, mustard seeds, fenugreek, garam masala, chilli powder, cinnamon stick, cumin, and coriander; cook briefly until fragrant.
Add the chopped tomatoes and coconut milk to the spiced onions; season to taste.

Layer a third of the sauce in a baking dish, then half the aubergine, another third of sauce, the remaining aubergine, and the rest of the sauce. Top with flaked almonds and coriander, and bake 25-30 minutes. Garnish with more coriander.

name: Roasted vanilla & honey crème brûlée
steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Scrape the vanilla seeds and set aside; roast the pod on a tray about 15 minutes until dried and brittle. Cool.

Gently heat the honey until it starts to caramelize. Add the cream and vanilla seeds, return to a boil, and whisk to dissolve. Remove from heat and infuse 20 minutes. Crush the roasted pod to a powder and stir into the mixture.

Return to a boil, then gradually whisk into the egg. Return to the pan and cook over low heat, stirring, until thickened to 86C or a smooth custard. Sieve, cool slightly, then blend 30 seconds until smooth. Strain into a dish and chill overnight.
Sprinkle with sugar and caramelize with a blowtorch. Serve with shortbread.

name: No-bake chocolate tart

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp icing sugar, plus extra for dusting

steps: Crush the biscuits in a bag with a rolling pin. Melt the butter with the syrup or honey and stir in the biscuits. Press into a tart tin and chill.

Melt the chocolate bars in the microwave, stirring, then mix in the vanilla and sift in the icing sugar. Whip the cream to soft peaks and fold into the chocolate. Pour into the base and chill at least 2 hours, up to 2 days.
To serve, loosen the edges, slide onto a plate, and let sit at room temperature 30 minutes for easier release. Top with creme fraiche and raspberries, dust with icing sugar, and slice thinly.

name: Chocolate hazelnut ice cream cheesecake
steps: Combine the cornflakes with half the chocolate hazelnut spread and press into the base of a springform tin.
Beat the cream cheese smooth, fold in the remaining spread, and spread over the base. Wrap tightly and freeze overnight.
Soften 30 minutes before serving. Slice and top with hazelnuts; keeps frozen up to a month.

name: Roast mushroom gnocchi

ingredients.raw_text: 3 tbsp olive oil, plus extra for drizzling

ingredients.food_name: Blue cheese min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Combine the sliced mushrooms and gnocchi with oil in a roasting tin. Roast 25-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the gnocchi are golden.
Stir in half the spinach to wilt, crumble the blue cheese on top, and return to the oven briefly to melt. Serve with the remaining spinach and a drizzle of oil.

name: Ratatouille tart with flaky cheddar & thyme pastry

ingredients.raw_text: 5 medium tomatoes (a mixture of different colours looks nice)

ingredients.food_name: Vegetarian cold cuts alternative, with egg

ingredients.raw_text: 300g plain flour, plus extra for dusting

ingredients.food_name: Polenta (Maize semolina prepared with water, fat and salt) boiled, baked
Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: small bunch of thyme, leaves picked
1 egg yolk, beaten (freeze the white for another recipe)

steps: Freeze the pastry butter wrapped in foil overnight for easy grating. Combine the flour, half the polenta, cheese, thyme, and salt. Grate in the frozen butter, stirring with a knife as you go. Mix in cold water, bring together gently by hand, wrap, and chill 30 minutes.
Slice the tomatoes; reserve half for the top and roughly chop the rest.
Char the aubergine under a hot grill or over a flame, turning, 8-10 minutes until soft and collapsing. Cool slightly.
Cook the onions in half the oil with salt 10 minutes until caramelizing. Meanwhile, scrape the skin off the aubergine and chop the flesh.

Add the garlic and aubergine to the onions and cook 5 minutes until any liquid evaporates. Add the chopped tomatoes and sugar, season, and cook 5-10 minutes until thickened. Cool and chill (can be made a day ahead).
Slice the courgettes, toss with salt, and rest 10 minutes.
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) with a tray inside. Roll the chilled pastry to a 35cm circle on parchment.
Pat the courgettes dry. Sprinkle the remaining polenta over the pastry, top with the chilled tomato-aubergine mixture, leaving a border.
Arrange the reserved tomato slices and courgettes in circles on top. Brush with oil, season, sprinkle with parmesan, fold the pastry edge over, and brush with egg yolk.
Slide onto the hot tray and bake 45 minutes until golden. Scatter with extra thyme and serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Homity pie

ingredients.raw_text: 700g floury potatoes (such as Maris Piper or King Edward), quartered and thickly sliced

ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 200g plain flour, plus extra for dusting

steps: For the pastry, combine the flours, salt, and cayenne. Rub in the butter to a crumb texture, then mix in the egg and water. Bring together, chill 30 minutes, then roll out to line a deep pie dish. Trim and chill.

For the filling, cook the onions, leeks, and thyme in butter with seasoning 12-15 minutes until soft and sticky. Stir in the potatoes, cover, and cook 10-15 minutes until they break down when pressed. Add the cream, stir briefly, and cool slightly.

Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) with a tray inside. Stir half the cheddar and most of the spring onions into the filling. Scatter half the remaining cheddar over the pastry base, add the filling, and top with the rest of the cheese and spring onions. Bake on the hot tray 40-45 minutes until deep golden and bubbling. Rest at least 10 minutes before serving hot or warm.

name: Tomato galette

ingredients.raw_text: 500g tomatoes (use a mixture of sizes and colours), cut into ½cm pieces
50g gruyère or vegetarian alternative, coarsely grated
ingredients.food_name: Vegetarian cold cuts alternative, with egg

ingredients.raw_text: 225g plain flour, plus extra for dusting
2 eggs, plus extra beaten egg for brushing

steps: Salt the tomatoes in a colander over a bowl and let sit 1 hour to draw out moisture. Meanwhile, blend the flour, butter, and salt to fine crumbs in a processor. Add beaten egg and pulse to a soft dough, adding water if dry. Shape into a disc, wrap, and chill 20 minutes.
Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Combine the drained tomatoes with the cheese, garlic, herbs, and pepper.
Roll the pastry to a 30cm circle. Pile the tomato mixture in the center, leaving a border. Brush the border with egg and fold up over the filling, overlapping loosely.
Brush with more egg and bake 40-50 minutes until golden and the tomatoes are tender.

name: Vegan lemon cake

ingredients.raw_text: 100ml vegetable oil, plus extra for the tin

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a loaf tin. Combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, and lemon zest. Add the oil, lemon juice, and water, mixing until smooth.
Pour into the tin and bake 30 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tin 10 minutes, then transfer to a rack to cool fully.
For the icing, sift the icing sugar and mix in just enough lemon juice for a thick pourable icing, then drizzle over the loaf.

name: Blueberry & coconut cake

ingredients.raw_text: 250ml rice bran oil, plus extra for the tin
140g fresh or frozen blueberries, plus extra to serve

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and grease a ring tin. Whisk the oil, eggs, sugar, and vanilla. Combine the flour and coconut, then alternately fold this and the soya milk into the wet mixture, starting and ending with the flour.
Spoon a quarter of the batter into the tin. Fold the blueberries into the rest and spoon in. Bake 1-1.25 hours until a skewer comes out clean, covering with foil if browning too fast.
Cool 10 minutes in the tin, then turn out to cool completely. Fill the center with extra blueberries and dust with icing sugar.

name: Vegan sponge cake

ingredients.raw_text: 150g dairy-free spread, plus extra for the tins
ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

ingredients.raw_text: 300ml dairy-free milk, we used oat milk

ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

ingredients.raw_text: 200g icing sugar, plus extra for dusting

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Line two sandwich tins with parchment and grease with dairy-free spread.

Combine the dairy-free milk with the vinegar and let sit a few minutes until slightly curdled. Combine half the vanilla seeds with the remaining cake ingredients in a bowl, then add the milk mixture and beat until smooth.
Divide between the tins and bake 30-35 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tins, then turn out onto racks.
For the buttercream, beat the dairy-free spread, icing sugar, and remaining vanilla seeds until pale and fluffy (mix by hand if the spread is very soft, or add extra milk if too firm).
Spread jam on one sponge, top with buttercream, and sandwich with the second sponge. Dust with icing sugar before slicing.

name: Vegan cupcakes with banana & peanut butter

ingredients.raw_text: 2 large or 3 small ripe bananas, mashed

steps: Set the oven to 170C (150C fan/gas 3.5) and line muffin tins. Combine the flour, sugar, salt, and bicarbonate of soda. Separately combine the mayonnaise, mashed banana, and vanilla. Fold the wet into the dry until just combined. Divide into cases and bake 20 minutes.
Sprinkle chocolate chips over the warm cupcakes without touching, so they melt and reset.
Beat the margarine and icing sugar, add the vegan milk, and mix until smooth, then beat 3 minutes more. Stir in the peanut butter. Pipe or spread over the cooled cakes; keeps airtight up to 2 days.

name: Vegan mug cake

ingredients.raw_text: 3 tbsp dairy-free milk, we used oat milk
coconut cream or dairy-free ice cream

steps: Combine the milk with the lemon zest and juice in a mug and let sit 2-3 minutes until it looks slightly curdled. Stir in the oil, flour, sugar, and bicarbonate of soda until smooth.
Drop in the raspberries and microwave on high 1.5 minutes until puffed and cooked through.
Serve with coconut cream or dairy-free ice cream.

name: Better beetroot brownies

ingredients.raw_text: 250g good-quality  dark chocolate (70% cocoa, dairy-free if you want, chopped)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a tin. Boil the beetroots 15-20 minutes until tender, cool, and peel. Cube a third and blend the rest to a paste, straining off a little juice for the icing. Mix the oil into the puree.

Melt the chocolate and cool slightly. Whisk the eggs, sugar, and vanilla until tripled in volume, then fold into the beetroot mixture, followed by the melted chocolate. Fold in the flour, cocoa, and baking powder, then add walnuts and the cubed beetroot.
Pour into the tin and bake 20-25 minutes, keeping the center slightly gooey. Cool. Mix the reserved beetroot juice with icing sugar for a runny icing, drizzle over, and cut into squares.

name: Cupcake recipe

ingredients.raw_text: food colouring paste of your choice (optional)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a 12-hole tray with cases.
Beat the butter and sugar until pale and fluffy, then whisk in the eggs one at a time, scraping down the bowl.
Add the vanilla, flour, and salt, whisking until just combined, then spoon into the cases.
Bake 15 minutes until golden and a skewer comes out clean. Cool completely on a rack.
For the buttercream, whisk the butter until very soft, then add the icing sugar, vanilla, and salt.
Whisk until smooth, then beat in the milk.
Add food coloring if desired, then pipe or spread onto the cooled cupcakes.

name: Chocolate rice crispy cakes

ingredients.raw_text: sprinkles, mini marshmallows, nuts, Smarties, dried fruit or white chocolate buttons

steps: Melt the chocolate with the butter and golden syrup in the microwave or over simmering water until smooth. Off the heat, stir in the puffed rice until fully coated.
Divide between cupcake cases (set in a muffin tin to hold shape) and let set, or chill 1 hour to speed it up.
Drizzle with extra melted chocolate and decorate with sweets or nuts while still tacky. Keeps airtight up to five days.

name: Turmeric latte

ingredients.raw_text: 350ml almond milk (or any milk of your choice)

steps: Whisk all the ingredients together in a saucepan over gentle heat (a milk frother helps) until hot. Pour into mugs and sprinkle with extra cinnamon.

name: Unicorn hot chocolate

ingredients.raw_text: your choice of coloured sweets and/or hundreds and thousands

steps: Whip the cream (if using) to the consistency of creme fraiche. If desired, streak the inside of a piping bag with gel food coloring using a skewer.
Heat the milk until simmering, remove from heat, and stir in the white chocolate until melted. Add food coloring for a pastel hue and pour into mugs.
Fill the prepared piping bag with the whipped cream and pipe mounds on top. Scatter with sprinkles and sweets if desired.

name: Homemade protein shake
steps: Blend all the ingredients until smooth and serve immediately.

name: Kiwi fruit smoothie
steps: Blend all the ingredients until smooth and pour into two glasses.

name: Watermelon & strawberry slushie

ingredients.raw_text: 225g punnet of ripe strawberries, tops cut off

steps: The day before, cube the watermelon (discarding rind and seeds) and freeze half in a bag for a few hours.
Blend the frozen watermelon with the remaining fresh watermelon, strawberries, and lime juice until smooth and slushy. Serve with straws.

name: Layered rainbow salad pots

ingredients.raw_text: 350g pasta shapes (De Cecco is a good brand that stays nice and firm)
200g green beans, trimmed and chopped into short lengths
160g can tuna in olive oil, drained
1 orange pepper, cut into little cubes 195g can sweetcorn, drained

steps: Cook the pasta 2 minutes less than pack instructions for a firmer bite, then drain well. Boil the green beans 2 minutes, rinse in cold water, and drain. Combine the tuna with the mayonnaise, yogurt, and chives.
Layer the pasta into glasses or jars, top with the tuna dressing, then the green beans, cherry tomatoes, and finally pepper and sweetcorn. Cover and chill until ready to eat.

name: Olive, cherry tomato & feta focaccia

ingredients.raw_text: 500g strong bread flour, plus extra for dusting
5 tbsp olive oil, plus extra for the tin
200g cherry tomatoes, halved (a mixture of colours looks nice)
small bunch of oregano, leaves picked, or use 1 tsp dried oregano

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Combine the flour, yeast, and salt. Add some oil and lukewarm water, mixing to a slightly sticky dough. Knead 5-10 minutes until soft and smooth, then prove covered in a bowl 1 hour until doubled.
Oil a tin and stretch the dough to fill it. Cover and prove 35-45 minutes more. Meanwhile, toss the tomatoes with seasoning, oregano, and a little oil.

Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Press the tomatoes and olives into the dough, crumble over the feta, drizzle with oil, sprinkle with salt, and bake 25-30 minutes until golden and puffed. Drizzle with remaining oil while hot. Cool in the tin before slicing; keeps up to two days.

name: Puff pastry pizzas

ingredients.food_name: Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: sweetcorn, olives, peppers, red onion, cherry tomatoes, spinach, basil

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Cut the pastry into six squares on lined trays and score a border around each. Bake 15 minutes until puffed but not fully cooked.
Meanwhile, combine the tomato puree, ketchup, oregano, and water for the sauce. Grate the cheese and prepare your toppings.
Press down the center of each pastry square, spread with sauce to the puffed edges, sprinkle with cheese, and add toppings. Bake 5-8 minutes more and serve.

name: Build your own pesto pasta salad

ingredients.raw_text: 5 tbsp pesto (see recipe, right and below, if you want to make your own)

ingredients.food_name: Vegetarian cold cuts alternative, with egg

ingredients.raw_text: halved cherry tomatoes, drained sweetcorn, peas, rocket

steps: Cook the pasta per pack instructions, drain, and toss with 1 tbsp oil. Cool, tossing occasionally to prevent sticking.

Toast the pine nuts in a dry pan over low heat 4-5 minutes until browned. Once the pasta is cool, toss with the pesto, toasted pine nuts, remaining oil, basil, lemon zest and juice, and any of the tomatoes, sweetcorn, peas, or rocket. Season and finish with parmesan.

name: Carrot cake fridge flapjacks

ingredients.raw_text: 170g butter, chopped, plus extra for the tin

ingredients.food_name: Soft cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 1 small  orange, zested, plus 1-2 tbsp orange juice

steps: Set the oven to 170C (150C fan/gas 3.5) and line a tin. Soak the dates in boiling water 10 minutes, then blend smooth.

Melt the butter and honey together. Combine the carrots, oats, cranberries, apricots, walnuts, seeds, spices, and salt in a bowl. Stir in the butter mixture and date puree, press into the tin, and bake 45-50 minutes, covering with foil if browning too fast. Cool in the tin, then chill at least 3 hours.
Whisk the icing ingredients smooth, drizzle over, and cut into 12 bars; keeps chilled three days.

name: Cheese & onion pasties

ingredients.raw_text: 150g mature cheddar (or a mixture of strong hard cheeses), grated
ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 250g plain flour, plus extra for dusting

steps: For the pastry, grate the butter into the flour, add the cayenne, mustard powder, half the egg, and salt, and bring together by hand, adding water if dry. Knead briefly, flatten into a disc, wrap, and chill.
Cook the onions and thyme in oil 8-10 minutes until softened and caramelizing; cool. Mix in the cheese, mustard, and spring onions.
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Roll the pastry thin and cut into circles, re-rolling scraps for about 10-12 total.
Spoon filling into the center of each circle, brush the edges with egg, fold into a half-moon, and crimp to seal. Brush with egg; freezes up to two months.
Bake 20-25 minutes (or 40-45 from frozen) until deep golden. Cool at least 10 minutes before serving warm, or cool completely for a picnic.

name: Minty griddled chicken & peach salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp mint, finely chopped, plus a few leaves to serve
2 peaches (200g), each cut into 8 thick wedges
1 large Little Gem lettuce (165g), roughly shredded

steps: Combine the lime zest and juice with the oil and mint; mix half with the garlic. Slice the chicken and toss in the garlic mixture with black pepper.

Cook the beans 3-4 minutes until just tender. Meanwhile, griddle the chicken and onion a few minutes per side until cooked; set aside. Quickly griddle the peaches (or use a hot non-stick pan with a little oil).

Toss the warm beans and onion in the remaining mint mixture, and pile onto a platter with the lettuce and rocket. Top with avocado, peaches, and chicken, and scatter with mint. Serve warm with the potatoes.

name: Easy vanilla cupcakes

ingredients.raw_text: 1-2 tbsp milk, plus a little extra for the icing

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a tin with cupcake cases. Cream the butter and sugar until fluffy. Beat the eggs and vanilla, then gradually beat into the butter mixture.
Fold in the flour and a little milk to a dropping consistency. Divide between the cases, filling halfway.
Bake 12-15 minutes until firm and lightly golden. Cool 5 minutes in the tin, then transfer to cool completely.
For the icing, beat the butter until very soft, then gradually beat in the icing sugar and a splash of milk until pipeable. Pipe onto the cooled cakes.

name: Olive, cherry tomato & feta focaccia

ingredients.raw_text: 500g strong bread flour, plus extra for dusting
5 tbsp olive oil, plus extra for the tin
200g cherry tomatoes, halved (a mixture of colours looks nice)
small bunch of oregano, leaves picked, or use 1 tsp dried oregano

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Combine the flour, yeast, and salt. Add some oil and lukewarm water, mixing to a slightly sticky dough. Knead 5-10 minutes until soft and smooth, then prove covered in a bowl 1 hour until doubled.
Oil a tin and stretch the dough to fill it. Cover and prove 35-45 minutes more. Meanwhile, toss the tomatoes with seasoning, oregano, and a little oil.

Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Press the tomatoes and olives into the dough, crumble over the feta, drizzle with oil, sprinkle with salt, and bake 25-30 minutes until golden and puffed. Drizzle with remaining oil while hot. Cool in the tin before slicing; keeps up to two days.

name: Cheese & onion pasties

ingredients.raw_text: 150g mature cheddar (or a mixture of strong hard cheeses), grated
ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 250g plain flour, plus extra for dusting

steps: For the pastry, grate the butter into the flour, add the cayenne, mustard powder, half the egg, and salt, and bring together by hand, adding water if dry. Knead briefly, flatten into a disc, wrap, and chill.
Cook the onions and thyme in oil 8-10 minutes until softened and caramelizing; cool. Mix in the cheese, mustard, and spring onions.
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Roll the pastry thin and cut into circles, re-rolling scraps for about 10-12 total.
Spoon filling into the center of each circle, brush the edges with egg, fold into a half-moon, and crimp to seal. Brush with egg; freezes up to two months.
Bake 20-25 minutes (or 40-45 from frozen) until deep golden. Cool at least 10 minutes before serving warm, or cool completely for a picnic.

name: Carrot cake fridge flapjacks

ingredients.raw_text: 170g butter, chopped, plus extra for the tin

ingredients.food_name: Soft cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 1 small  orange, zested, plus 1-2 tbsp orange juice

steps: Set the oven to 170C (150C fan/gas 3.5) and line a tin. Soak the dates in boiling water 10 minutes, then blend smooth.

Melt the butter and honey together. Combine the carrots, oats, cranberries, apricots, walnuts, seeds, spices, and salt in a bowl. Stir in the butter mixture and date puree, press into the tin, and bake 45-50 minutes, covering with foil if browning too fast. Cool in the tin, then chill at least 3 hours.
Whisk the icing ingredients smooth, drizzle over, and cut into 12 bars; keeps chilled three days.

name: Easy honey flapjacks

ingredients.raw_text: 225g butter, plus extra for the tin

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a tin. Melt the butter, sugar, and honey together, stirring until smooth.
Combine with the oats until coated. Press into the tin and bake 10-15 minutes until lightly golden. Cool in the tin before cutting into squares.

name: Caramelised onion & thyme sausage rolls
steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Squeeze the sausage meat into a bowl and combine with the chutney, mustard, and thyme. Roll the pastry out and arrange the meat down the center. Brush the pastry edge with egg, roll to enclose, seal with a fork, and cut into eight. Brush with more egg, sprinkle with poppy seeds, and bake on a lined tray 25 minutes until golden.

name: Coronation chicken scones

ingredients.raw_text: 225g self-raising flour, plus extra for dusting
140g cold butter, chopped into small pieces
3 cooked chicken breasts, finely chopped or shredded

steps: For the scones, set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7) and line a tray. Combine the flour, baking powder, and salt, then rub in the butter to fine crumbs. Add the milk and nigella seeds, mixing to a soft dough with a knife.
Knead briefly, roll to about 1.5cm thick, and cut 12 rounds (re-rolling scraps as needed). Brush with egg and bake 10-12 minutes until golden. Cool.
Combine the chicken, chutney, curry powder, yogurt, mayo, herbs, lemon juice, and seasoning; chill until ready to use.
Split the scones and fill with the coronation chicken, cucumber, and red onion. Secure with a skewer if desired.

name: Chocolate-dipped strawberries

ingredients.raw_text: 30g white and milk  chocolate, roughy chopped (optional)

steps: Melt the dark chocolate over simmering water or in short microwave bursts, stirring until smooth. Transfer to a deep bowl.
Dip the strawberries by their tops into the chocolate, tap off excess, and set on parchment to firm up. Save any extra melted chocolate on parchment to reuse.
For decoration, melt the white and milk chocolate and drizzle lightly over the dipped strawberries.

name: Mini milkshakes

ingredients.raw_text: 200ml coconut milk for drinking (or coconut kefir)

steps: Blend the banana with half the milk and yogurt until smooth. Pour into bottles and add blueberry skewers as stirrers.
Blend the strawberries and most of the raspberries with the remaining milk and yogurt. Pour into bottles with raspberry skewer stirrers.
Blend the mango and coconut milk with a squeeze of lime. Pour into bottles and serve with lime wedges.

name: Easy vanilla cupcakes

ingredients.raw_text: 1-2 tbsp milk, plus a little extra for the icing

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a tin with cupcake cases. Cream the butter and sugar until fluffy. Beat the eggs and vanilla, then gradually beat into the butter mixture.
Fold in the flour and a little milk to a dropping consistency. Divide between the cases, filling halfway.
Bake 12-15 minutes until firm and lightly golden. Cool 5 minutes in the tin, then transfer to cool completely.
For the icing, beat the butter until very soft, then gradually beat in the icing sugar and a splash of milk until pipeable. Pipe onto the cooled cakes.

name: Lemon & yogurt chicken flatbreads

ingredients.raw_text: 2 skinless chicken breasts, cut into strips

steps: Combine the lemon zest and half the juice with the oregano, garlic, cinnamon, and oil in a bowl. Add the chicken, coat well, cover, and chill 1 hour (no longer, as the lemon starts to cure it).
Heat the barbecue or a griddle. Thread the chicken onto skewers if needed and grill a couple of minutes per side until charred and cooked through.
Warm the flatbreads briefly. Spread each with yogurt, top with the chicken, remaining yogurt, pepper, and lettuce. Fold or roll to eat.

name: Cod & pea fritters

ingredients.raw_text: 500g leftover mashed potato (you can use ready-made, but make sure it’s a firm variety)
cherry tomatoes and rocket, to serve

steps: Cook the cod chunks gently in butter until flaking, about 5 minutes. Flake into a bowl and season. Mash in the potato, then add the peas, mint, and mayo or yogurt, mixing gently. Shape into eight patties with floured hands, dust with flour, and chill at least 30 minutes.
Fry in batches in oil 3-4 minutes per side until golden and crisp. Serve warm with cherry tomatoes and rocket, or cool and pack for lunch.

name: Chocolate rice crispy cakes

ingredients.raw_text: sprinkles, mini marshmallows, nuts, Smarties, dried fruit or white chocolate buttons

steps: Melt the chocolate with the butter and golden syrup in the microwave or over simmering water until smooth. Off the heat, stir in the puffed rice until fully coated.
Divide between cupcake cases (set in a muffin tin to hold shape) and let set, or chill 1 hour to speed it up.
Drizzle with extra melted chocolate and decorate with sweets or nuts while still tacky. Keeps airtight up to five days.

name: Pitta pocket
steps: Fill the pitta half with chicken, cucumber, and cherry tomatoes.

name: Griddled salad jar

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: ¼ cucumber, deseeded and roughly chopped

steps: For the dressing, gently heat the onion with the coriander seeds, vinegar, and water 15 minutes until soft and bright pink; cool.
Heat a griddle pan until smoking. Brush the aubergine and courgette with oil and griddle 3-4 minutes per side until char marks form. Cool and season.

Whisk the lemon juice and oil into the cooled onion mixture; season. Layer into a jar: tomatoes, feta, chickpeas, olives, then sundried tomatoes with their oil, the griddled vegetables, cucumber, pepper, and herbs. Seal and chill until ready.
To serve, tip out onto a platter so the dressing coats everything.

name: Carrot cake cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 350g plain flour, plus extra for dusting your hands

steps: Combine the cream cheese, icing sugar, and vanilla; freeze 30 minutes to firm up.
Combine the flour, baking powder, and spices. Beat the butter and sugar until creamy, then beat in the egg and carrot. Mix in the dry ingredients to a dough.
Divide into 14 balls on a lined tray, flatten each, add a teaspoon of the cream cheese filling to the center, and wrap the dough around it, sealing well.
Flatten slightly and chill 30 minutes. Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6).
Bake 20 minutes until golden. Cool on the tray 10-15 minutes, then transfer to a rack.
Mix icing sugar with orange juice to a drizzling consistency, drizzle over the cookies, and sprinkle with walnuts and orange zest. Best eaten fresh; keeps chilled up to 2 days.

name: PBJ bakewell tart

ingredients.raw_text: 80g roasted unsalted peanuts, plus 2 tbsp to serve
1 egg, plus 1 egg white (use the yolk in the pastry)
50g raspberry jam, plus 2 tsp to serve
130g cold butter, chopped into small pieces
2 tbsp icing sugar, plus 5 tbsp for the drizzle

steps: For the pastry, blend the flour, cocoa, butter, and salt to crumbs. Add the egg yolk, icing sugar, and water, blending to a dough. Knead briefly, flatten, wrap, and chill 30 minutes.
Blend the remaining filling ingredients (except jam and peanuts for topping) until smooth. Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4).
Roll the pastry to line a tart tin, trimming the edge with a small overhang. Blind bake with parchment and beans 15 minutes.
Remove the beans and bake 5 minutes more until biscuity. Trim the pastry edge level with the tin.
Spread the jam over the base, top with the peanut filling, and bake 45 minutes until set with a clean skewer. Cool 15 minutes, then transfer to a rack.
Mash the remaining icing sugar with jam for a smooth icing, drizzle on top, and scatter with chopped peanuts. Keeps airtight three days.

name: Healthy potato salad
steps: Blanch the basil 30 seconds in boiling salted water, then set aside to cool. Add the potatoes to the same water and cook until tender.
Squeeze the basil dry and blend with the oil, vinegar, garlic, and seasoning to a vibrant green oil.
Drain and steam-dry the potatoes. Toss with the chopped parsley and basil oil in a serving bowl, season, and serve; keeps two days.

name: Veggie olive wraps with mustard vinaigrette
steps: Combine all the filling ingredients and toss well.

Lay the tortilla on foil and pile the filling along one side - it will look like a lot but compacts as you roll. Roll tightly from the filling side, tucking in the ends, then wrap the foil around it. Slice in half to eat right away, or leave whole and wrap in parchment to take with you.

name: Lemon & yogurt chicken flatbreads

ingredients.raw_text: 2 skinless chicken breasts, cut into strips

steps: Combine the lemon zest and half the juice with the oregano, garlic, cinnamon, and oil in a bowl. Add the chicken, coat well, cover, and chill 1 hour (no longer, as the lemon starts to cure it).
Heat the barbecue or a griddle. Thread the chicken onto skewers if needed and grill a couple of minutes per side until charred and cooked through.
Warm the flatbreads briefly. Spread each with yogurt, top with the chicken, remaining yogurt, pepper, and lettuce. Fold or roll to eat.

name: Minty griddled chicken & peach salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp mint, finely chopped, plus a few leaves to serve
2 peaches (200g), each cut into 8 thick wedges
1 large Little Gem lettuce (165g), roughly shredded

steps: Combine the lime zest and juice with the oil and mint; mix half with the garlic. Slice the chicken and toss in the garlic mixture with black pepper.

Cook the beans 3-4 minutes until just tender. Meanwhile, griddle the chicken and onion a few minutes per side until cooked; set aside. Quickly griddle the peaches (or use a hot non-stick pan with a little oil).

Toss the warm beans and onion in the remaining mint mixture, and pile onto a platter with the lettuce and rocket. Top with avocado, peaches, and chicken, and scatter with mint. Serve warm with the potatoes.

name: Easy vegan coleslaw

ingredients.raw_text: 4 tbsp extra virgin olive oil or rapeseed oil

steps: Whisk or shake together the dressing ingredients, then pour over the vegetables and mix thoroughly.

name: Fennel, roast lemon & tomato salad

ingredients.raw_text: 500g mixed tomatoes (I used cherry tomatoes and some larger ones)

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a tray with parchment. Slice one lemon thinly, spread on the tray, drizzle with oil, and sprinkle with sugar. Roast 20-25 minutes until shriveled and caramelized (can be done ahead and kept at room temperature).
Meanwhile, chop the tomatoes and thinly slice the fennel, reserving the fronds. Combine with the remaining oil, juice from the second lemon, and pomegranate seeds; season.
Just before serving, chop the herbs and toss through with the roasted lemon and reserved fennel fronds.

name: Easy tzatziki

ingredients.raw_text: 1 large cucumber, peeled, halved and deseeded
1 small garlic clove, crushed or finely chopped
small handful of mint leaves or dill sprigs, finely chopped

steps: Grate the cucumber lengthways into a sieve, salt lightly, and set aside 10 minutes. Combine the yogurt, garlic, vinegar, and oil, and season with pepper.
Squeeze the cucumber dry and stir into the yogurt mixture with the mint. Adjust seasoning and serve; can be made a day ahead and chilled, stirred before serving.

name: Spiced chicken kebabs with chopped salad & flatbreads

ingredients.raw_text: 2 green cardamom pods, pods bashed and seeds finely ground (optional)
pinch of chilli flakes, or to taste
1 cucumber, peeled, ½ coarsely grated, ½ finely chopped

steps: Combine half the yogurt with the spices, half the garlic, lemon zest, and salt. Coat the chicken, cover, and marinate at least 30 minutes or overnight.
Combine the grated cucumber and remaining garlic into the remaining yogurt; season and chill.
Combine the finely chopped cucumber and lettuce, squeeze over lemon, and season. Cut the remaining lemon half into quarters. Heat the grill or a hot griddle.
Thread the chicken onto skewers and grill 10-12 minutes, turning often, until charred and cooked through. Warm the flatbreads.
Serve the chicken in the flatbreads with the chopped salad and cucumber yogurt, drizzled with hot sauce if desired.

name: Halloumi kebabs with thyme & lemon baste

ingredients.raw_text: 250g low fat  halloumi cheese, cut into 16 chunks (we used Cypressa Halloumi Lite)
2 tsp fresh thyme  leaves (preferably lemon thyme)

steps: Halve and thickly slice the courgettes. Cut the onion into wedges and separate. Thread the halloumi, tomatoes, courgettes, and onion onto skewers. Chill until ready to cook (up to half a day ahead).
Combine the oil, lemon juice, thyme, mustard, and seasoning for the baste (can be made ahead).
Grill the kebabs 4-5 minutes, turning often and brushing with the baste, until the cheese golds and vegetables are tender. Serve hot with warm pitta.

name: Tangy tomato relish

ingredients.raw_text: 4 ripe tomatoes, chopped into small chunks

steps: Combine all the ingredients except the coriander with a pinch of salt (can be made up to three days ahead and chilled). Stir in the coriander just before serving.

name: Pineapple & pork skewers

ingredients.food_name: Velvety fish sauce from white basic sauce

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small pineapple, peeled, cored and cut into chunks (or use ready prepped fresh pineapple, drained well)
1 green pepper, deseeded and cut into squares (optional)
4 spring onions, trimmed and cut into 4 equal lengths
cooked rice or pitta, to serve

steps: Cube the pork. Heat the sugar and vinegar until dissolved, add the fish sauce, and cool. Toss the pork in the mixture to coat.
Heat the barbecue or a griddle. Thread the pork and pineapple onto skewers, alternating with pepper and spring onion.
Grill or griddle 3-4 minutes per side (longer if griddling). Sprinkle with coriander and serve with rice or in pittas.

name: Lemon & yogurt chicken flatbreads

ingredients.raw_text: 2 skinless chicken breasts, cut into strips

steps: Combine the lemon zest and half the juice with the oregano, garlic, cinnamon, and oil in a bowl. Add the chicken, coat well, cover, and chill 1 hour (no longer, as the lemon starts to cure it).
Heat the barbecue or a griddle. Thread the chicken onto skewers if needed and grill a couple of minutes per side until charred and cooked through.
Warm the flatbreads briefly. Spread each with yogurt, top with the chicken, remaining yogurt, pepper, and lettuce. Fold or roll to eat.

name: Spiced chicken kebabs with chopped salad & flatbreads

ingredients.raw_text: 2 green cardamom pods, pods bashed and seeds finely ground (optional)
pinch of chilli flakes, or to taste
1 cucumber, peeled, ½ coarsely grated, ½ finely chopped

steps: Combine half the yogurt with the spices, half the garlic, lemon zest, and salt. Coat the chicken, cover, and marinate at least 30 minutes or overnight.
Combine the grated cucumber and remaining garlic into the remaining yogurt; season and chill.
Combine the finely chopped cucumber and lettuce, squeeze over lemon, and season. Cut the remaining lemon half into quarters. Heat the grill or a hot griddle.
Thread the chicken onto skewers and grill 10-12 minutes, turning often, until charred and cooked through. Warm the flatbreads.
Serve the chicken in the flatbreads with the chopped salad and cucumber yogurt, drizzled with hot sauce if desired.

name: Classic homemade coleslaw

ingredients.raw_text: 1 small white  cabbage or ½ large
½ small bunch  dill, chives, parsley or coriander, finely chopped (optional)
50g  yogurt, soured cream or more mayo

steps: Trim and core the cabbage, then shred finely by hand, mandoline, or food processor into a bowl.
Coarsely grate the carrots and finely slice the onion; add to the bowl with any herbs.
Whisk the mustard, mayo, yogurt, and vinegar together, seasoning and adjusting to taste.
Toss the dressing through the vegetables to coat evenly. Sprinkle with paprika and serve; keeps chilled up to 3 days, stirred before serving.

name: Soy & butter salmon parcels
