steps: Heat the barbecue (or a griddle pan indoors) until the coals turn white or the pan is hot. Cut four sheets of foil for wrapping. Spread a little butter in the center of each to prevent sticking, then add the salmon. Combine the soy and honey and spoon over each fillet, topping with any remaining butter. Fold the foil tightly to seal each parcel.
Cook the parcels on the barbecue or griddle 5-10 minutes (check one carefully — it will be hot). Open and scatter with sesame seeds and spring onion.
Toss the cucumber with a few drops of sesame oil and a little salt. Serve the salmon with the cucumber salad.

name: Watermelon & strawberry slushie

ingredients.raw_text: 225g punnet of ripe strawberries, tops cut off

steps: The day before, cube the watermelon (discarding rind and seeds) and freeze half in a bag for a few hours.
Blend the frozen watermelon with the remaining fresh watermelon, strawberries, and lime juice until smooth and slushy. Serve with straws.

name: Spiced chicken kebabs with chopped salad & flatbreads

ingredients.raw_text: 2 green cardamom pods, pods bashed and seeds finely ground (optional)
pinch of chilli flakes, or to taste
1 cucumber, peeled, ½ coarsely grated, ½ finely chopped

steps: Combine half the yogurt with the spices, half the garlic, lemon zest, and salt. Coat the chicken, cover, and marinate at least 30 minutes or overnight.
Combine the grated cucumber and remaining garlic into the remaining yogurt; season and chill.
Combine the finely chopped cucumber and lettuce, squeeze over lemon, and season. Cut the remaining lemon half into quarters. Heat the grill or a hot griddle.
Thread the chicken onto skewers and grill 10-12 minutes, turning often, until charred and cooked through. Warm the flatbreads.
Serve the chicken in the flatbreads with the chopped salad and cucumber yogurt, drizzled with hot sauce if desired.

name: Pineapple & pork skewers

ingredients.food_name: Velvety fish sauce from white basic sauce

ingredients.raw_text: ½ small pineapple, peeled, cored and cut into chunks (or use ready prepped fresh pineapple, drained well)
1 green pepper, deseeded and cut into squares (optional)
4 spring onions, trimmed and cut into 4 equal lengths
cooked rice or pitta, to serve

steps: Cube the pork. Heat the sugar and vinegar until dissolved, add the fish sauce, and cool. Toss the pork in the mixture to coat.
Heat the barbecue or a griddle. Thread the pork and pineapple onto skewers, alternating with pepper and spring onion.
Grill or griddle 3-4 minutes per side (longer if griddling). Sprinkle with coriander and serve with rice or in pittas.

name: Foil-wrapped baby potatoes
steps: Light the barbecue if needed and let the ash turn gray. Boil the potatoes 5 minutes until slightly tender.
Drain and steam-dry 2 minutes. Toss with oil and seasoning on foil, arranging flat, then wrap tightly. Grill 10 minutes.
Check with a skewer, then unwrap and serve.

name: Sticky chicken
steps: Slash the drumsticks a few times. Combine the soy, honey, oil, tomato puree, and mustard, coat the chicken thoroughly, and marinate 30 minutes at room temperature or overnight chilled. Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6).
Transfer to a roasting tray and cook 35 minutes, turning occasionally, until tender and glazed.

name: Barbecue baked sweet potatoes
steps: Rub each potato with oil and salt, then wrap in a double layer of foil.
Place directly on hot barbecue coals and cook 15 minutes, turn, and cook 15 minutes more. Check one is cooked through.
Unwrap, split open, and top with yogurt and spring onion.

name: Cheese & chilli melts

ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 1 green or red chilli, deseeded and finely chopped
½ large bunch coriander, leaves roughly chopped

steps: Combine the cheese, tomatoes, chilli, coriander, and seasoning.
Warm the tortillas to make them pliable. Spoon the filling onto one half of each, fold over, and press to seal into half-moons.
Brush with oil and grill oil-side down on the barbecue a couple of minutes until crisp, then brush the other side, flip, and grill briefly more. Slice into wedges and serve.

name: Cajun chicken
steps: Pat the chicken dry and slash diagonally on the smooth side. Combine the spices with seasoning on a plate.
Brush the chicken with oil and coat in the spice mix. Grill or griddle 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through. Serve with guacamole.

name: Barbecue sesame sweet potatoes

ingredients.raw_text: 6 sweet potatoes, washed and cut into wedges
1 tbsp sesame seeds (black if you have them)
½ bunch of spring onions, washed and chopped

steps: Light the barbecue or set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Toss the sweet potatoes with oil and seasoning on a tray. Cook on the barbecue or in the oven 25 minutes until charred and softened.

Whisk the remaining oils, ginger, garlic, soy, and lime juice. Baste the potatoes and cook 30-40 minutes more, basting as they go, until sticky and glazed. Sprinkle with sesame seeds and peanuts, cool slightly, then toss with chilli and spring onions in a bowl to serve.

name: Classic homemade coleslaw

ingredients.raw_text: 1 small white  cabbage or ½ large
½ small bunch  dill, chives, parsley or coriander, finely chopped (optional)
50g  yogurt, soured cream or more mayo

steps: Trim and core the cabbage, then shred finely by hand, mandoline, or food processor into a bowl.
Coarsely grate the carrots and finely slice the onion; add to the bowl with any herbs.
Whisk the mustard, mayo, yogurt, and vinegar together, seasoning and adjusting to taste.
Toss the dressing through the vegetables to coat evenly. Sprinkle with paprika and serve; keeps chilled up to 3 days, stirred before serving.

name: Braised butter beans & leeks

ingredients.raw_text: 3 tbsp dry vermouth or white wine (optional)
400g can butter beans, drained and rinsed
2 tbsp flat-leaf parsley, chopped, to serve

steps: Cook the leeks in butter 5 minutes until lightly browned.

Add the vermouth (if using) and simmer 1 minute, then stir in the stock, butter beans, and thyme. Simmer covered 5 minutes, then uncover and cook 8-10 minutes until the leeks are tender and most stock evaporated. Off the heat, stir in the creme fraiche and finish with parsley.

name: Easy tzatziki

ingredients.raw_text: 1 large cucumber, peeled, halved and deseeded
1 small garlic clove, crushed or finely chopped
small handful of mint leaves or dill sprigs, finely chopped

steps: Grate the cucumber lengthways into a sieve, salt lightly, and set aside 10 minutes. Combine the yogurt, garlic, vinegar, and oil, and season with pepper.
Squeeze the cucumber dry and stir into the yogurt mixture with the mint. Adjust seasoning and serve; can be made a day ahead and chilled, stirred before serving.

name: Allotment salad
steps: Combine the dressing ingredients and season.
Blanch the asparagus 2 minutes, beans 4 minutes, broccoli 2.5 minutes, and peas 1 minute in boiling salted water, transferring each to iced water afterward. Drain and pat dry.
Combine with the radishes, cabbage, spring onions, and mint. Serve with the dressing on the side.

name: Lime, chilli & feta butter

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Mash the softened butter with the crumbled feta, lime zest, chilli, and coriander, adding a little salt. Chill until needed.

name: Tear-and-share cheese & garlic rolls

ingredients.raw_text: rapeseed oil, for the bowl and tray

ingredients.food_name: Polenta (Maize semolina prepared with water, fat and salt) boiled, baked
Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter
Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Warm the water and stir in the butter. Combine the flour, yeast, sugar, and salt, then add the liquid and mix to a soft dough. Knead 10 minutes by hand (or 5 in a mixer) until smooth and stretchy. Prove covered in an oiled bowl 1.5-2 hours until doubled.
Oil a tray and scatter with polenta. Knock back the dough, pinch off walnut-sized pieces, roll into balls, and arrange on the tray with space between.

Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Cover and prove 30-60 minutes until doubled and touching. Combine the remaining butter with garlic, brush over the rolls, and scatter with cheese. Bake 25-30 minutes until cooked through. Cool 5 minutes before serving.

name: Curried rice & egg salad

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp mild curry paste (we used korma paste)
small bunch of coriander, leaves picked

steps: Cook the onion in oil 8-10 minutes until golden. Stir in the curry paste, turmeric, rice, and raisins. Crumble in the stock cube and add enough boiling water to cover by half a centimeter; season. Simmer gently, cover, reduce to the lowest heat, and cook 2 minutes more. Remove from heat, keeping covered, and steam 15 minutes for fluffy rice.
Meanwhile, boil the eggs 7 minutes, cool in cold water, then drain and halve.
Chill the rice at least 1 hour, up to two days. To serve, scatter with coriander and almonds, and top with grated carrot, cucumber, and eggs.

name: Courgette, potato & cheddar soup

ingredients.raw_text: bunch spring onion, sliced - save 1 for serving, if eating straight away
100g extra-mature cheddar or vegetarian alternative, grated, plus a little extra to serve
ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: good grating fresh nutmeg, plus extra to serve

steps: Combine the potatoes with water to cover and crumbled stock cubes; boil, cover, and cook 5 minutes. Add the courgettes and cook 5 minutes more, then the spring onions for a final 5 minutes.

Off the heat, stir in the cheese, nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Blend to a thick soup, thinning with hot water as needed. Serve topped with extra cheddar, spring onions, and nutmeg or pepper; freezes up to 3 months.

name: Spinach soup

ingredients.raw_text: 1l stock (made with two chicken or vegetable stock cubes)

steps: Melt the butter and sweat the spring onions, leek, celery, and potato, covered, 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the stock and cook 10-15 minutes until the potato is soft.
Add the spinach and cook a couple of minutes until wilted. Blend smooth.
Stir in the creme fraiche, reheat, and serve.

name: Lentil soup

ingredients.raw_text: small handful of chopped  parsley, to serve

steps: Bring the stock to a boil, add the lentils, and let soften a few minutes.

Add the carrots and leeks, season (skip salt if using ham stock), bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer 45 minutes to 1 hour until the lentils break down. Finish with parsley and serve with buttered bread.

name: Nettle soup

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp olive oil, plus extra for drizzling
1 large floury potato (Maris Piper or similar), thinly sliced
400g stinging or Dead nettles, washed, leaves picked (see tips below)

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion, carrot, leek, and potato 10 minutes until softening. Add the stock and cook 10-15 minutes more until the potato is soft.
Add the nettle leaves, simmer 1 minute to wilt, then blend smooth. Season, stir in the butter and cream, and serve drizzled with extra oil.

name: Leek, kale & potato soup topped with shoestring fries

ingredients.raw_text: 4 large potatoes (around 500g), 3 peeled and cubed, 1 left whole with skin on
5 leeks (around 500g), washed and sliced into half moons

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7) and line a tray. Cut the potato into matchsticks, pat dry, toss with oil and seasoning, spread on the tray, and roast 15-18 minutes.

Melt the butter and cook the leeks, chopped potatoes, and salt gently 10 minutes until the leeks soften. Add the garlic for 1 minute, then the stock. Simmer 10-12 minutes until the potatoes are soft, then add the kale and cook 2-3 minutes to wilt.
Stir in the creme fraiche, blend smooth, and season. Divide into bowls and top with the shoestring fries.

name: Creamy spinach soup

ingredients.raw_text: 1 medium potato, peeled and chopped into chunks
450g fresh spinach, washed if necessary and roughly chopped
finely grated zest of half a  lemon

steps: Melt the butter and cook the onion and garlic gently 5-6 minutes until softening. Stir in the potato for 1 minute. Add the stock and simmer 8-10 minutes until the potato starts to cook. Add the milk, bring to a simmer, then stir in half the spinach and the lemon zest. Cover and simmer 15 minutes until the spinach fully wilts. Cool 5 minutes.

Blend with the remaining spinach until silky smooth (in batches if needed; keeps frozen up to a month). Return to the pan, reheat, and season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg, diluting with extra stock if too thick. Serve swirled with cream.

name: Minty pea & potato soup

ingredients.raw_text: 800g potato, peeled and cut into small chunks

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion 5 minutes until softened. Add the potatoes and stock, and bring to a boil. Cover and simmer 10-15 minutes until tender, adding the peas 2 minutes before the end.
Remove a quarter of the vegetables with a slotted spoon and set aside. Blend the rest smooth, then stir back in the reserved vegetables, chopped mint, and seasoning. Serve with bread.

name: Minted pea soup
steps: Slice the spring onions, reserving some green tops for garnish. Soften in butter 1 minute. Add the peas and stock, and bring to a boil.
Simmer 5 minutes, then blend half the soup and return to the pan with the creme fraiche. Reheat gently, season, and serve garnished with the reserved spring onion tops.

name: Thai green chicken soup

ingredients.raw_text: 500g pack skinless chicken thigh fillets, finely chopped

ingredients.food_name: Velvety fish sauce from white basic sauce

ingredients.raw_text: 1 bunch spring onions, the white sliced on the diagonal and the green finely chopped
280g pack fine green bean, trimmed and halved
juice 2 limes, plus wedges to serve

steps: Heat the oil and soften the onion 3 minutes. Add the chicken and garlic, cooking until the chicken changes color.

Add the curry paste, coconut milk, stock, lime leaves, and fish sauce; simmer 12 minutes. Add the spring onion tops, green beans, and bamboo shoots, and cook 4-6 minutes until the beans are just tender.
Blend the lime juice and basil to a smooth green paste, stir into the soup with the sliced spring onion, and heat through. Serve with lime wedges.

name: Spring roast chicken

ingredients.raw_text: 8 chicken thighs, on the bone, skin on
1 lemon, zested and cut into wedges
2 courgettes, thickly sliced on the diagonal

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Combine the chicken thighs with oil, lemon zest and wedges, shallots, rosemary, and tarragon in a tin, seasoning well. Arrange skin-side up and roast 40-50 minutes until crisp and golden.
Meanwhile, boil the asparagus 3 minutes until tender, adding the courgettes and peas for the final minute.
Drain the vegetables and toss with the chicken and its juices. Crumble over feta and serve.

name: Easy lemon layer cake

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tsp vanilla bean paste or extract
3 lemons, zest of 2 pared into strips, juiced (you’ll need 60ml)
1 tsp vanilla bean paste or extract

ingredients.food_name: Soft cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two sandwich tins. Beat the butter and sugar 3 minutes until fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well. Fold in the flour and baking powder, then the yogurt, vanilla, and lemon zest. Divide between the tins and bake 30-35 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
For the drizzle, dissolve the sugar in lemon juice and water over heat, add the zest, and simmer 2-3 minutes until softened and syrupy. Lift out the zest and set the syrup aside.
Cool the sponges 10 minutes in the tins, then pour over the warm drizzle syrup. Cool completely.
For the icing, beat the butter and icing sugar 4-5 minutes until smooth. Add the vanilla and soft cheese, beating 4 minutes more until thick (it will loosen then thicken as you beat).

Pipe icing around the edge of one sponge, fill the center, and chill 45 minutes to 1 hour until set. Top with the second sponge, pipe blobs of icing around the edge, and fill the gaps with the candied lemon zest.

name: Rhubarb and ricotta bread & butter pudding

ingredients.raw_text: about 250g bread (soft white rolls, sliced bread, or brioche)

ingredients.food_name: Ricotta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 25g icing sugar, plus extra for dusting
crème fraîche, or cream and Greek yogurt, to serve

steps: Trim the rhubarb into pieces. Simmer with water and some of the sugar, add the rhubarb, and cook 1.5 minutes, then lift out with a slotted spoon onto a tray in a single layer to stop it overcooking.

Bring the milk and cream to a boil with salt and vanilla. Beat the eggs, extra yolk, and remaining sugar, then pour the hot milk mixture over, stirring constantly. Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4).

Butter the bread. Drain and sweeten the ricotta with icing sugar, then spread thickly on the buttered bread. Sprinkle with citrus zest and layer with the rhubarb in a baking dish. Strain the egg-cream mixture over and let sit 30 minutes.

Place the dish in a roasting tin with boiling water halfway up the sides. Bake 40-45 minutes until puffed, set, and golden. Cool 10 minutes, dust with icing sugar, and serve with creme fraiche or cream mixed with yogurt.

name: Carrot cake cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 350g plain flour, plus extra for dusting your hands

steps: Combine the cream cheese, icing sugar, and vanilla; freeze 30 minutes to firm up.
Combine the flour, baking powder, and spices. Beat the butter and sugar until creamy, then beat in the egg and carrot. Mix in the dry ingredients to a dough.
Divide into 14 balls on a lined tray, flatten each, add a teaspoon of the cream cheese filling to the center, and wrap the dough around it, sealing well.
Flatten slightly and chill 30 minutes. Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6).
Bake 20 minutes until golden. Cool on the tray 10-15 minutes, then transfer to a rack.
Mix icing sugar with orange juice to a drizzling consistency, drizzle over the cookies, and sprinkle with walnuts and orange zest. Best eaten fresh; keeps chilled up to 2 days.

name: Giant Yorkshire pudding Sunday lunch

ingredients.raw_text: 2 large eggs (these should make 100ml in a jug)
rapeseed or  sunflower oil, for the tin
1 fat steak (rather than a long, thin one)
2 carrots, peeled and cut into batons, or 8-10 baby carrots

steps: Whisk the eggs with the flour until smooth. Gradually whisk in the milk until lump-free. Season, pour into a jug, cover, and chill 30 minutes to 24 hours.

Brown the oiled, seasoned steak in a dry hot pan on each side, then cool completely (can be wrapped and chilled up to 24 hours). Quarter the peeled potatoes, boil 2 minutes, drain, dry, and chill until needed.

Set the oven to 230C (210C fan/gas 8). Heat oiled pans in the oven — one for the Yorkshire batter, one for the potatoes with carrots and steak. Pour the batter into its hot pan and return everything to the oven; cook the Yorkshire 25 minutes.

Check the Yorkshire is deep golden and firm; remove and set aside. Take out the steak to rest, wrapped in foil. Turn the potatoes and carrots, add the broccoli, and cook 10 minutes more, removing vegetables as they finish while returning potatoes to the oven. Heat the gravy.
Warm the Yorkshire through again. Slice the steak, adding any juices to the gravy. Fill the Yorkshire with the meat, vegetables, and potatoes, and pour over the gravy.

name: Father's Day cupcakes

ingredients.raw_text: 140g self-raising flour  (swap 1 tbsp of flour for cocoa powder)

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5) and line a muffin tin. Combine the yogurt, eggs, and vanilla in a jug. Combine the dry ingredients and salt in a bowl and make a well.

Add the yogurt mixture and melted butter, folding quickly without overworking. Divide into the cases and bake 18-20 minutes until golden and springy. Cool briefly, then transfer to a rack; keeps airtight up to 3 days or freezes well.

For the frosting, melt the chocolate and cool. Beat the butter and icing sugar until creamy, then beat in the chocolate. Chill up to a month; bring to room temperature before spreading over the cakes and topping with chocolate buttons.

name: Tomato galette

ingredients.raw_text: 500g tomatoes (use a mixture of sizes and colours), cut into ½cm pieces
50g gruyère or vegetarian alternative, coarsely grated
ingredients.food_name: Vegetarian cold cuts alternative, with egg

ingredients.raw_text: 225g plain flour, plus extra for dusting
2 eggs, plus extra beaten egg for brushing

steps: Salt the tomatoes in a colander over a bowl and let sit 1 hour to draw out moisture. Meanwhile, blend the flour, butter, and salt to fine crumbs in a processor. Add beaten egg and pulse to a soft dough, adding water if dry. Shape into a disc, wrap, and chill 20 minutes.
Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Combine the drained tomatoes with the cheese, garlic, herbs, and pepper.
Roll the pastry to a 30cm circle. Pile the tomato mixture in the center, leaving a border. Brush the border with egg and fold up over the filling, overlapping loosely.
Brush with more egg and bake 40-50 minutes until golden and the tomatoes are tender.

name: Father's Day cupcakes

ingredients.raw_text: 140g self-raising flour  (swap 1 tbsp of flour for cocoa powder)

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5) and line a muffin tin. Combine the yogurt, eggs, and vanilla in a jug. Combine the dry ingredients and salt in a bowl and make a well.

Add the yogurt mixture and melted butter, folding quickly without overworking. Divide into the cases and bake 18-20 minutes until golden and springy. Cool briefly, then transfer to a rack; keeps airtight up to 3 days or freezes well.

For the frosting, melt the chocolate and cool. Beat the butter and icing sugar until creamy, then beat in the chocolate. Chill up to a month; bring to room temperature before spreading over the cakes and topping with chocolate buttons.

name: Sticky upside-down banana cake

ingredients.raw_text: 80g unsalted butter, plus extra for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a deep round tin.
Melt the butter and sugar together for the topping, then stir in the vanilla. Pour into the tin, and arrange the halved bananas cut-side down on top.

Beat all the batter ingredients with salt until smooth. Pour over the bananas and level. Bake 55 minutes to 1 hour until a skewer comes out clean, covering with foil after 45 minutes if browning too fast. Cool 10 minutes in the tin before turning out. Best warm, but good at room temperature; eat within 2 days.

name: Giant cookie cake

ingredients.food_name: Chocolate white, filled with coconut chips

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and grease a springform tin. Beat the butter and sugar until pale and fluffy, then mix in the egg, extra yolk, and vanilla.
Combine the flour, cornflour, and baking powder, and fold in until just combined (the mixture will be thick). Stir in the chocolate chips.
Spoon into the tin and bake 20-25 minutes until light golden. Cool 5 minutes and serve warm with ice cream.

name: Raspberry cake

ingredients.raw_text: 125ml sunflower oil, plus extra for the tin

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a deep cake tin. Combine the flour and sugar in a bowl.

Combine the oil, yogurt, eggs, vanilla, and lemon zest in another bowl, then fold into the flour mixture until smooth. Fold in the raspberries and bake 30-40 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool briefly in the tin, then transfer to a rack. Dust with icing sugar and top with extra raspberries; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Polish chocolate & walnut cake

ingredients.raw_text: 250g walnuts,  chopped, plus a handful to decorate

steps: Set the oven to 170C (150C fan/gas 3.5) and line a shallow tin. Combine the flour and cocoa. Whisk the egg whites to stiff peaks, adding the sugar gradually, then whisk in the yolks one at a time. Fold in the flour mixture. Pour into the tin and bake 35 minutes. Cool 5 minutes, remove, and re-line the tin.

For the meringue, whisk the egg whites to stiff peaks, gradually whisking in the sugar. Fold in the chopped walnuts, flour, and starch. Spread into the tin and bake 40 minutes at the same temperature. Cool.
For the glaze, heat the cream, remove from heat, and stir in the chopped chocolate until melted and glossy.

Cut the cooled sponge and meringue each into two layers. Layer meringue on the bottom sponge, sprinkle with walnuts, add another meringue layer, then the top sponge. Cover with the chocolate glaze and decorate with walnuts. Chill 1-2 hours before serving.

name: Pecan toffee cake

ingredients.raw_text: 200g butter, softened, plus extra for greasing

steps: Grind most of the pecans in a processor. Boil the dates in water until very soft, drain, and blend smooth; cool.
Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a cake tin. Beat the butter, sugar, and spice until creamy, then mix in the dates, ground pecans, eggs, and salt until smooth.

Fold in the flour, spoon into the tin, and level. Scatter the remaining pecans on top without pressing in, and bake 40 minutes until risen and a skewer comes out clean. Serve warm with maple syrup and ice cream.

name: Apricot & pistachio frangipane blondies

ingredients.raw_text: 100g unsalted butter, melted, plus a little extra for the tin
6 large ripe apricots, halved and stoned

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a rectangular tin. For the frangipane, beat the sugar and butter until fluffy, then add the egg and pistachios.
For the blondie batter, combine the muscovado sugar, fennel seeds, melted butter, and salt. Add the egg, then fold in the flour and half the pistachios until no streaks remain. Loosen with milk.

Spoon two-thirds of the batter into the tin, top with the apricot halves pressed in, and dot the frangipane around. Top with the remaining batter, partially covering the fruit, and scatter with the rest of the pistachios. Bake 25-30 minutes until a skewer comes out with moist crumbs. Cool completely in the tin before cutting into 12 squares.

name: Vegan burger

ingredients.raw_text: 1 stick of celery, or ¼ red pepper, chopped
400g can  chickpeas, drained and left to dry a little
1 tbsp polenta, couscous, or dried breadcrumbs
ingredients.food_name: Polenta (Maize semolina prepared with water, fat and salt) boiled, baked

ingredients.raw_text: burger buns or pitta bread, lettuce, tomato, and sauces to serve

steps: Blend the shallot, celery, parsley, and most of the chickpeas to a coarse paste (rougher than hummus). Mash the remaining chickpeas and stir into the paste with the garam masala, tomato puree, flour, and polenta; season well.
Shape into four patties and rest at least 30 minutes, or overnight chilled, to let the polenta absorb moisture.
Fry gently in oil until browned and crisp on each side, handling carefully as they're soft when warm. Serve in buns or pitta with your favorite toppings.

name: Lemon & ginger tea
steps: Halve the lemon; juice one half and slice the rest. Divide the juice and slices between two mugs with the sliced ginger.
Top up with boiling water and steep 3 minutes, or until cool enough to sip. Sweeten with honey if desired.

name: Easy bread rolls

ingredients.raw_text: 500g strong white bread flour, plus extra for dusting
1 tsp sunflower oil, plus extra for the work surface and bowl

steps: Combine the flour, yeast, sugar, salt, and oil with warm water, mixing to a shaggy dough. Cover and rest 10 minutes.
Knead on an oiled surface at least 10 minutes until springy (or 5 minutes with a dough hook). Shape into a ball, place in an oiled bowl, and prove 1 hour until doubled.
Shape into a log, halve, then divide into eight pieces. Roll into tight balls, space apart on a tray, and prove covered 40 minutes to 1 hour until nearly doubled.
Set the oven to 230C (210C fan/gas 8). Dust with flour (glaze with milk or egg and top with seeds if desired). Bake 25-30 minutes until golden and hollow-sounding. Cool on a rack.

name: Pizza rolls

ingredients.raw_text: 2 balls mozzarella, sliced (we used Sainsbury's Basics)
ingredients.food_name: Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Cut the tops off the rolls and hollow out. Spread with tomato puree, then fill with ham, tomatoes, and mozzarella. Scatter with oregano and top with an olive if desired.
Bake on a tray 15 minutes until crusty and the cheese is bubbling. Rest briefly and serve hot with a side salad.

name: Tear-and-share cheese & garlic rolls

ingredients.raw_text: rapeseed oil, for the bowl and tray

ingredients.food_name: Polenta (Maize semolina prepared with water, fat and salt) boiled, baked
Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter
Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Warm the water and stir in the butter. Combine the flour, yeast, sugar, and salt, then add the liquid and mix to a soft dough. Knead 10 minutes by hand (or 5 in a mixer) until smooth and stretchy. Prove covered in an oiled bowl 1.5-2 hours until doubled.
Oil a tray and scatter with polenta. Knock back the dough, pinch off walnut-sized pieces, roll into balls, and arrange on the tray with space between.

Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Cover and prove 30-60 minutes until doubled and touching. Combine the remaining butter with garlic, brush over the rolls, and scatter with cheese. Bake 25-30 minutes until cooked through. Cool 5 minutes before serving.

name: Cheese & pesto whirls

ingredients.raw_text: 450g strong white bread flour, plus a little for dusting
2 tbsp olive oil, plus a drizzle
240g tub semi-dried tomatoes, drained and roughly chopped
100g grated mozzarella (ready-grated is best for this, as it is drier than fresh)
ingredients.food_name: Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter
Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Combine the flour, yeast, sugar, and salt. Add warm water and oil, mixing to a dough. Knead 10 minutes by hand (or 5 in a mixer) until soft and elastic. Prove in an oiled, covered bowl 1-3 hours until doubled.
Line a tray. Knock back the dough and roll to a large rectangle. Spread with pesto, scatter with tomatoes, both cheeses, and basil, then roll up into a log from a long side.

Cut into 12 pieces and arrange cut-side up on the tray in a grid, tucking the loose ends toward the center so they don't unravel. Cover loosely and prove 30 minutes to 1 hour until doubled. Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6).
Bake 35-40 minutes until golden and cooked through. Cool at least 10 minutes before serving.

name: Cheese roll-ups

ingredients.raw_text: 200g self-raising flour, plus extra for dusting

ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Rub the flour and butter together, then stir in the paprika.
Add the milk and mix to a soft dough, adding more milk if dry.
Roll out on a floured surface to about 0.5cm thick, keeping a rectangular shape.
Sprinkle with cheese and roll up like a log along the long side. Cut into 12 rounds.
Arrange cut-side down on a lined tray, almost touching, so the spiral shows.
Bake 20-25 minutes until golden and melty. Cool; keeps airtight up to 3 days.

name: Simit bread

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp pomegranate molasses or molasses syrup

steps: Combine the flour, salt, and sugar. Add the yeast and warm water, mix, and knead a few minutes to bring together, then knead 10-12 minutes more until stretchy. Prove covered in a bowl 1-2 hours until doubled.
Meanwhile, toast the sesame seeds until golden. Combine the molasses (or pomegranate molasses) with water in a shallow bowl.

Divide the dough into four pieces and roll each into a 45cm rope. Fold back and twist the strands, then loop into a ring, sealing the ends. Dip each in the molasses water, then the sesame seeds, coating well. Transfer to a tray, cover, and prove 45 minutes to 1 hour. Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6).
Bake 15-18 minutes until golden. Cool, then wrap to keep fresh; can be made a day ahead and warmed gently.

name: Tear-and-share stuffed deli rolls

ingredients.raw_text: 100g cooked artichoke in olive oil, drained and chopped (reserve a little of the oil)
3 roasted  peppers (from a jar), drained and chopped

ingredients.food_name: Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Make the bread dough per pack instructions, substituting pesto for the oil. Let rise until doubled.
Brush a springform tin with oil, reserving some pesto and chopped peppers for filling. Combine the remaining filling ingredients.

Divide the dough into 12 pieces, flatten each into a disc, fill with the pesto-vegetable mixture, and reshape into balls, sealing well. Arrange seam-side down in the tin, cover, and prove until doubled.

Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Brush with the reserved pesto and scatter with remaining peppers. Bake 50 minutes until golden and cooked through. Cool 10 minutes in the tin, then cool completely on a rack before packing.

name: Garlic bread pizzas

ingredients.raw_text: 500g strong white flour, plus extra for rolling

ingredients.food_name: Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Combine the base ingredients with warm water, stirring until it comes together.
Knead briefly on a floured surface into a ball. Divide into 8 pieces, keeping the rest covered. Roll each thinly into a 15cm round and place on a baking sheet.
Combine the butter and crushed garlic and spread a little over each pizza. Tear the mozzarella over. Repeat for all pieces, fitting two per sheet.
Set the oven to 240C (220C fan/gas 9). Bake 12-15 minutes until crisp. Meanwhile, combine and season the remaining ingredients. Top the baked pizzas with the tomato mixture and serve immediately.

name: Smoked haddock & sweetcorn chowder with herby garlic bread

ingredients.food_name: Pork streaky bacon, raw cured, smoked

ingredients.raw_text: 300g smoked haddock, skinned and cut into 1cm pieces
small pack  flat-leaf parsley, finely chopped

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Combine the butter, garlic, and half the parsley, and spread between diagonal cuts in the baguette. Wrap in foil.
Heat the oil and cook the onion and bacon 5 minutes until softened. Add the potato and cook 5 minutes more. Bake the garlic bread 15 minutes, opening the foil for the last 5 to brown.

Stir the flour into the bacon and potato, cook briefly, then gradually add the milk and stock. Bring to a boil, then simmer gently 10-15 minutes until the potato is nearly cooked. Add the haddock and sweetcorn, and simmer 5 minutes more. Finish with the remaining parsley and serve with the garlic bread.

name: Sweet potato & rosemary soup with garlic toasts

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tsp olive oil, plus extra for brushing

steps: Heat the oil and cook the onion until soft. Add crushed garlic for 1 minute more. Stir in the sweet potato, cover with stock, and bring to a boil. Add rosemary leaves from one sprig and simmer 10 minutes until the potato is soft.

Meanwhile, set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Brush the bread with oil, rub with the cut side of the remaining garlic clove and the second rosemary sprig's leaves, season, and bake 10 minutes until crisp.
Blend the soup roughly, thinning with water if too thick; season and serve with the toasts.

name: Wholemeal flatbreads

ingredients.raw_text: 350g wholemeal flour, plus extra for dusting

steps: Rub the oil into the flour, then stir in warm water and knead until smooth and elastic.
Divide into eight balls and roll each thinly to about 22cm across, flouring as needed (freeze unbaked if making ahead).
Cook one at a time in a hot dry pan 30 seconds per side, pressing to encourage puffing, until lightly browned in patches. Keep warm wrapped in a tea towel.

name: Rye bread

ingredients.raw_text: 200g rye flour, plus extra for dusting

steps: Combine the flours, yeast, and salt. Mix the honey with warm water and stir in, adding more water if too dry. Knead 10 minutes until smooth (rye dough is less springy than white).
Prove in an oiled, covered bowl 1-2 hours until doubled. Dust a loaf tin with flour.
Knock back the dough, working in caraway seeds if using. Shape into an oval, place in the tin, cover, and prove 1-1.5 hours until doubled.
Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Dust with rye flour, slash the top, and bake 30 minutes until dark and hollow-sounding. Cool at least 20 minutes on a rack.

name: Stout & apple wheaten bread

ingredients.raw_text: 60g butter, cut into small cubes, plus more for the tins
1 large apple (or 2 small ones), peeled, cored and diced
oat flakes, pinhead oatmeal or sesame seeds (or a mixture of all three) to sprinkle over the top

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and butter three loaf tins. Toss the apple with some flour. Combine the remaining flours and oatmeal with salt and bicarbonate of soda. Rub in the butter, then stir in the sugar.
Make a well and gradually mix in the stout, treacle, and buttermilk with a knife, working in the floured apple quickly without overmixing.
Divide between the tins, sprinkle with oats or seeds, and bake 35-40 minutes. Test by tapping the base for a hollow sound. Cool on a rack.

name: Seeded flatbreads

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp kalonji seeds (also called black onion seeds or nigella seeds)

steps: Combine the yeast with warm water and sugar, and let sit a few minutes. Combine the flours and salt, make a well, add the yeast mixture and warm water, and mix to a dough. Knead 5-10 minutes until smooth. Prove covered 1 hour until doubled.
Knock back the dough and knead in the seeds. Divide into 12 pieces and roll each thinly. Cook in a hot dry pan 2 minutes per side until bubbly. Wrap in foil; keeps up to a day, reheat in a warm oven.

name: Pitta bread

ingredients.raw_text: 500g strong white bread flour, plus extra for dusting

steps: Combine the yeast with warm water and let sit 5 minutes until bubbly. Add the flour, salt, and oil, bringing together into a soft dough.
Knead 5-10 minutes with minimal extra flour until smooth and elastic. Prove in an oiled, covered bowl about 1 hour until doubled.
Set the oven as high as possible with a tray inside to heat. Divide the dough into eight, flatten each, then roll into ovals about 20cm by 15cm, 3-5mm thick.
Place the ovals on the hot tray (in batches if needed) and bake 4-5 minutes until puffed and pale golden. Wrap each in a tea towel to stay soft.

name: Pitta pizzas

ingredients.raw_text: 6 slices salami, torn into small pieces

ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Heat the grill. Spread each pitta with tomato puree, sprinkle with mixed herbs, and top with tomato slices, salami, and cheese. Grill until the cheese is golden and bubbling.

name: Lime & chilli pitta crisps
steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Tear the pittas into crisp-sized pieces and brush with oil.
Sprinkle with lime zest and chilli flakes.
Bake 10 minutes until crisp, sprinkling with salt if desired.

name: Fluffy pittas

ingredients.raw_text: 500g strong white bread flour, plus extra for dusting
1 tbsp olive oil, plus a little extra for proving and brushing

steps: A few hours ahead, combine the flour, yeast, sugar, thyme, and salt. Add the milk, oil, and warm water, mixing to a dough. Knead 8-10 minutes (or 5 in a mixer). Prove in an oiled, covered bowl 1 hour until nearly doubled (longer if chilled).

Divide into eight pieces and roll each to a 20cm circle. Brush with oil and cook on a hot barbecue or in a hot pan 2-3 minutes per side until puffed and blistered. Wrap in foil to keep warm as you go.

name: Fast feta & aubergine pizzas

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Toast the pittas until lightly golden. Drain the aubergines, reserving some oil, and blend half to a smooth paste.
Spread the paste over the pittas, top with the remaining aubergine slices, and scatter with feta. Finish with mint and a drizzle of the reserved oil.

name: Chicken pitta pockets with hummus drizzle

ingredients.raw_text: a finger length of cucumber, cut into strips
2 wholemeal pitta breads, cut in half and split open

steps: Combine the chicken, cucumber, carrot, and cress. Mix the hummus with the yogurt. Lightly toast and halve the pittas, then split open.
Fill with the chicken mixture and top with the hummus drizzle.

name: Egg & veggie pittas

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tsp agave nectar or golden caster sugar
½ small red onion, very finely sliced

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Season and oil the aubergine slices on a tray and bake 15 minutes. Flip, spread with harissa, and bake 5 minutes more.
Meanwhile, simmer the eggs 10 minutes, cool under cold water, and peel.
Combine the vinegar and agave or sugar with seasoning, then toss with the beetroot, carrot, and onion. Separately combine the yogurt, dill, and garlic with seasoning. Toast and split the pittas.
Slice the eggs and fill the pittas with the aubergine, egg, and some beetroot salad. Spoon in the yogurt, serving any extras on the side.

name: Mozzarella, ham & pesto pizzas

ingredients.food_name: Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Heat the grill to high and warm the pittas briefly while slicing the mozzarella.

Flip the pittas, spread each with pesto, and top with a mozzarella slice, then the ham. Tear the remaining mozzarella over and grind over pepper. Grill 3-4 minutes more until melted and lightly golden.

name: Falafel burgers

ingredients.raw_text: handful of flat-leaf parsley  or curly parsley
½ tsp harissa paste  or chilli powder

ingredients.food_name: Green asparagus salad (cooked) with vinegar marinade

steps: Pat the drained chickpeas dry. Blend with the onion, garlic, parsley, cumin, coriander, harissa, flour, and salt until fairly smooth. Shape into four patties.
Heat the oil and fry the patties 3 minutes per side until lightly golden. Serve with toasted pitta, tomato salsa, and green salad.

name: Pea pakora pockets

ingredients.raw_text: small bunch mint, half roughly chopped
6 white or wholemeal pitta breads, halved
½ iceberg lettuce, shredded, to serve

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Boil the potatoes 8 minutes, adding the peas near the end. Drain, mash the potato with a third of the peas, curry powder, and seasoning over low heat to dry it out, then stir in the remaining peas.
Shape into about 16 oval pieces and bake on a lined tray 20 minutes until golden and crisp-edged.
Combine the yogurt with chopped mint. Warm the halved pittas and toss the remaining mint with shredded lettuce. Serve the hot pakoras with the extras for stuffing into the pittas.

name: Honey & almond layer cake

ingredients.raw_text: 280g unsalted butter, softened, plus extra for greasing
3 medium eggs, plus 4 medium egg yolks (reserve the whites for the buttercream)

steps: For the sponges, set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two tins. Beat the butter, sugar, and most of the honey until pale and fluffy. Add the eggs and yolks one at a time. Combine the flour, almonds, baking powder, and salt separately. Alternate adding the flour mixture and the vanilla-milk into the butter mixture, beating until just combined. Divide between the tins and bake 45-55 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool 10 minutes in the tins, then cool completely on a rack.
For the honey syrup, warm the remaining honey with water until dissolved; set aside.

For the candied almonds, heat sugar and water to 119C. Meanwhile, toast the almonds on a tray 10-12 minutes until golden. Stir the warm almonds into the syrup until it turns white and crunchy, then spread out and chop half.

For the buttercream, beat the butter until fluffy. Whisk the egg whites and sugar over simmering water until dissolved, then whisk off the heat 10 minutes until stiff and cool. Beat in the butter gradually until smooth, then beat in the honey. Transfer to a piping bag.

Level and split each sponge into two layers. Stack with buttercream between layers, brushing each with honey syrup. Cover with a thin layer of buttercream, pipe decoration on top, and finish with the candied almonds.

name: Number birthday cake

ingredients.raw_text: 350g unsalted butter, softened, plus extra for the tin
mini meringues, biscuits, macarons and raspberries (or any toppings you like)

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and butter a rectangular tin. Beat the butter and sugar 5 minutes until pale. Add the eggs one at a time. Sift in the flour and baking powder, add the lemon zest and milk, and fold in. Spoon into the tin and bake 35-40 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool 10 minutes in the tin, then cool completely on a rack.

Halve the cooled cake horizontally. Combine sugar and lemon juice and brush over the cut sides. For the buttercream, beat the butter, gradually add icing sugar and a splash of water, and beat until pale. Spread jam on the base layer, top with a third of the buttercream, and sandwich with the second layer. Cut into your desired shape, then pipe the remaining buttercream on top and decorate; keeps chilled up to three days.

name: Rainbow layer cake

ingredients.raw_text: 340g unsalted butter,  softened, plus extra for the tins
food colouring gels (for Pride, the colours are red, orange, yellow, green, dark blue, purple, light pink, baby blue, brown and black)

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5) and line three tins. Beat the butter and sugar until smooth. Gradually beat in the eggs, then the vanilla. Fold in the flour and baking powder, then loosen with milk.
Divide between the tins and bake 20 minutes until golden and a skewer comes out clean. Cool 10 minutes in the tins, then cool completely on a rack.

For the Swiss meringue buttercream, whisk the egg whites and sugar over simmering water 3-4 minutes until dissolved. Whisk 10 minutes until thick meringue forms, then gradually whisk in the softened butter until fluffy. Mix in the vanilla.

Layer the sponges with buttercream and either caramel or lemon curd in the middle layers, not spreading to the edges. Apply a thin crumb coat, chill 45 minutes, then apply a full coat and chill again.

Divide the remaining buttercream into small bowls, coloring most of them differently. Pipe alternating colored blobs down the side of the cake, smearing each away from you, then repeat in sequence to build a swirl pattern.
Continue the technique on top of the cake, smearing toward the center, until fully covered. Slice and serve; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Rainbow zebra cake

ingredients.raw_text: 375g salted butter, softened, plus extra for the tin
blue and pink food colouring (see tip, below)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line three tins.

Beat the butter and sugar 5-8 minutes until fluffy. Whisk in the eggs one at a time. Fold in the flour, baking powder, and milk. Divide into three bowls, coloring one blue and one pink, leaving one plain.

Spoon alternating dollops of the three colors into each tin, layering as you go without waiting for spreading, then gently tap to even out. Bake 25-30 minutes until firm. Turn out top-side down onto racks immediately and cool completely.
For the icing, beat the butter, icing sugar, and vanilla seeds until fluffy, then beat in the cream cheese briefly.

Stack the sponges with buttercream between layers, then cover the top and sides with a smooth layer, leaving some 'naked' patches. Top with sprinkles and candles if desired; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Chocolate & raspberry birthday layer cake

ingredients.raw_text: 225ml sunflower oil, plus extra for the tins

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two springform tins. Whisk the oil, sugar, eggs, and milk. Sift in the flour, cocoa, and bicarbonate of soda separately, then gradually combine with the wet ingredients.
Divide between the tins and bake 35-40 minutes until risen and springy. Cool 10 minutes in the tins, then cool completely on a rack.
Combine the jam and defrosted raspberries. Whip the cream with sugar to soft peaks, then fold in half the raspberry mixture for a ripple effect.

Spread most of the remaining raspberry mixture over one cake, top with half the cream, and smooth. Add the second cake, swirl over the remaining cream, and swirl in the last of the raspberry mixture; keeps chilled up to two days.

name: Super-easy birthday cake
steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two tins. Beat the butter and sugar, then add the eggs one at a time. Fold in the flour, milk, and vanilla until smooth.
Divide into two bowls, sifting cocoa into one. Pour each into a tin and bake 20-25 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool 5 minutes, then cool completely on a rack.

For the icing, beat the butter and gradually beat in the icing sugar until smooth and creamy. Tint with pink coloring. Sandwich the cakes with icing and spread the rest on top; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Rainbow zebra cake

ingredients.raw_text: 375g salted butter, softened, plus extra for the tin
blue and pink food colouring (see tip, below)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line three tins.

Beat the butter and sugar 5-8 minutes until fluffy. Whisk in the eggs one at a time. Fold in the flour, baking powder, and milk. Divide into three bowls, coloring one blue and one pink, leaving one plain.

Spoon alternating dollops of the three colors into each tin, layering as you go without waiting for spreading, then gently tap to even out. Bake 25-30 minutes until firm. Turn out top-side down onto racks immediately and cool completely.
For the icing, beat the butter, icing sugar, and vanilla seeds until fluffy, then beat in the cream cheese briefly.

Stack the sponges with buttercream between layers, then cover the top and sides with a smooth layer, leaving some 'naked' patches. Top with sprinkles and candles if desired; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Easy lemon layer cake

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tsp vanilla bean paste or extract
3 lemons, zest of 2 pared into strips, juiced (you’ll need 60ml)
1 tsp vanilla bean paste or extract

ingredients.food_name: Soft cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two sandwich tins. Beat the butter and sugar 3 minutes until fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well. Fold in the flour and baking powder, then the yogurt, vanilla, and lemon zest. Divide between the tins and bake 30-35 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
For the drizzle, dissolve the sugar in lemon juice and water over heat, add the zest, and simmer 2-3 minutes until softened and syrupy. Lift out the zest and set the syrup aside.
Cool the sponges 10 minutes in the tins, then pour over the warm drizzle syrup. Cool completely.
For the icing, beat the butter and icing sugar 4-5 minutes until smooth. Add the vanilla and soft cheese, beating 4 minutes more until thick (it will loosen then thicken as you beat).

Pipe icing around the edge of one sponge, fill the center, and chill 45 minutes to 1 hour until set. Top with the second sponge, pipe blobs of icing around the edge, and fill the gaps with the candied lemon zest.

name: Mini victoria sponge cakes

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp  icing sugar, plus extra for dusting
185g  jam of your choice (strawberry or raspberry works well)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a muffin tin. Beat the butter and sugar until pale and fluffy, then whisk in the eggs one at a time.

Add the milk, vanilla, flour, and salt, and beat until smooth. Divide between the cases and bake 17-20 minutes until golden and a skewer comes out clean. Cool 10 minutes in the tin, then cool completely (freezes up to three months at this stage).
Remove the cases and halve the sponges through the middle.
Whip the cream and icing sugar to soft peaks. Spread jam on the base of each sponge, top with cream, and sandwich with the tops. Dust with icing sugar; best eaten the same day.

name: Blackberry & orange cake

ingredients.raw_text: 225g unsalted butter, softened, plus extra for the tins
2 oranges, zested (reserve the oranges for the drizzle, below)
3 oranges, juiced (use the zested oranges, above, you’ll need about 150ml), 1 zested
1 orange, zested, plus extra zest to serve

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two tins. Beat the butter and sugar until pale and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time. Fold in the vanilla, flour, and baking powder until smooth, then gently fold in the orange zest and blackberries.
Divide between the tins and bake 35-40 minutes until a skewer comes out clean, covering with foil after 30 minutes if browning too fast.
For the drizzle, simmer the orange juice, zest, and sugar 4-5 minutes to a syrup.
Brush the warm syrup over both sponges while still warm. Cool completely in the tins.
For the buttercream, beat the butter and icing sugar until fluffy, then whisk in the orange zest and milk. Pipe into a bag. Loosen the marmalade and transfer some to a separate piping bag.

Pipe buttercream dots around the edge and center of one sponge, fill the gaps with marmalade, then top with the second sponge. Repeat the piping and marmalade on top, finishing with blackberries and extra zest.

name: Citrus, almond & yogurt cake

ingredients.raw_text: 165g butter, plus extra for the tin

steps: Melt the butter and cool slightly. Butter and line a springform tin. Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4).

Combine the sugar, flour, and almonds. Whisk the eggs and yogurt into the cooled butter, then combine with the dry ingredients. Zest the lemon and orange over the bowl and stir until no streaks remain. Pour into the tin and bake 40 minutes.

Slice a few pieces from the zested lemon and orange, then juice the rest into a pan with extra sugar and the slices. Simmer 5-10 minutes until syrupy and the slices soften. Cool, then dry the slices on parchment.
Check the cake with a skewer, baking longer if needed. Cool 5 minutes in the tin, then spoon over the citrus syrup. Cool 10 minutes more before removing from the tin (freezes well wrapped).
Combine yogurt and icing sugar for a thick icing. Spoon onto the cake, letting it drip over the edge. Scatter with flaked almonds and the candied fruit slices; keeps airtight up to five days.

name: Easy pound cake

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp vanilla paste / ½ vanilla pod seeds
1 tbsp vanilla paste / ½ vanilla pod seeds

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a loaf tin. Beat all the ingredients except the icing together until smooth. Pour into the tin and level, then bake 40-45 minutes.
Cool, then combine the icing sugar and vanilla with water gradually to a thick icing. Spread over the cooled cake, letting it drip down the sides, and let set before slicing.

name: Easy chocolate fudge cake

ingredients.raw_text: 150ml sunflower oil, plus extra for the tin
2½ tbsp milk (a little more if needed)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two sandwich tins. Sift the flour, cocoa, and bicarbonate of soda together with the sugar.
Make a well and add the golden syrup, eggs, oil, and milk; beat until smooth.
Divide between the tins and bake 25-30 minutes until risen and firm. Cool 10 minutes before turning out.
For the icing, beat the butter until soft, gradually beat in the icing sugar and cocoa, then add milk until fluffy and spreadable.
Sandwich the cakes together and cover the top and sides with the remaining icing.

name: Easy chocolate cake

ingredients.raw_text: 200g unsalted butter, softened plus extra for the tins

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5) and line two tins.
Beat the sugar, butter, eggs, flour, cocoa, baking powder, vanilla, milk, and salt together until pale.
Divide between the tins and bake 20 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
Cool 10 minutes in the tins, then cool completely on a rack.
For the buttercream, melt the chocolate and cool slightly.
Mash the butter and icing sugar together, switching to a spoon or beaters.
Sift in the cocoa and salt, add the melted chocolate and milk, and mix smooth.
Sandwich the cakes with half the buttercream and spread the rest on top; decorate with chocolate shards if desired.
For the shards, melt dark chocolate and pour onto a lined tray.
Melt milk and white chocolate separately and drizzle over before the dark chocolate sets.
Level by shaking the tray, let set, then break into shards.

name: Marble bundt cake

ingredients.raw_text: 180g butter, very soft, plus extra for the tin

steps: Butter and line a bundt tin. Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Beat the egg yolks and sugar until pale, then beat in the butter and milk or cream.
Separately whisk the egg whites to soft peaks, add vanilla sugar, and whisk stiff. Beat the flour and baking powder into the yolk mixture, then fold in the whites.
Pour half the batter into the tin. Fold the cocoa into the remaining batter and pour over. Swirl with a spoon handle. Bake about 1 hour until a skewer comes out clean.
Cool briefly in the tin, then turn out and cool completely on a rack. Dust with icing sugar.

name: Sachertorte
steps: Melt the chocolate over simmering water or in the microwave; cool slightly. Whisk the egg whites and salt to soft peaks, then gradually add sugar to stiff peaks.

Line a springform tin. Set the oven to 170C (150 fan/gas 3). Beat the butter and remaining sugar until light and creamy. Gradually beat in the egg yolks and cooled chocolate. Fold in a third of the egg whites, then sift and fold in the flour in two batches, followed by the remaining whites.
Pour into the tin and bake about 1 hour until risen and springy. Cool completely in the tin on a rack.

Warm the jam and liqueur or juice, then strain. Remove the cake from the tin, halve horizontally, spread half the jam mixture on the base, and top with the other half. Spread a thin layer of remaining jam over the whole cake and let dry.

For the glaze, boil water and sugar until dissolved, cool slightly, stir in some cream. Heat the remaining cream separately, melt in the butter, then gradually add the chocolate and the sugar syrup. Pour over the cake on a rack over a tray, tilting gently to coat evenly without smoothing.
For the stripes, melt the milk chocolate, pipe parallel lines onto the wet glaze, then drag a spoon tip alternately through the lines to create a pattern. Let dry.

name: Chocolate Swiss roll

ingredients.raw_text: 75g  golden caster sugar, plus extra for scattering
50g  icing sugar, sifted, plus extra to serve

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7) and line a swiss roll tin. Sift the flour and cocoa together.
Whisk the eggs and sugar 5 minutes until pale and thick. Gently fold in the flour mixture until no streaks remain.

Spread into the tin and bake 9-10 minutes until springy. Meanwhile, prepare a sugared sheet of parchment. Invert the baked sponge onto it, peel off the top parchment, score a line near one short edge, and roll into a spiral with the parchment inside. Cool completely.

Whisk the filling ingredients until stiff. Unroll the sponge, spread with the filling (leaving the scored edge clear), and re-roll using the parchment. Transfer to a plate and dust with icing sugar. Serve immediately or chill up to 24 hours.

name: Chocolate & raspberry birthday layer cake

ingredients.raw_text: 225ml sunflower oil, plus extra for the tins

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two springform tins. Whisk the oil, sugar, eggs, and milk. Sift in the flour, cocoa, and bicarbonate of soda separately, then gradually combine with the wet ingredients.
Divide between the tins and bake 35-40 minutes until risen and springy. Cool 10 minutes in the tins, then cool completely on a rack.
Combine the jam and defrosted raspberries. Whip the cream with sugar to soft peaks, then fold in half the raspberry mixture for a ripple effect.

Spread most of the remaining raspberry mixture over one cake, top with half the cream, and smooth. Add the second cake, swirl over the remaining cream, and swirl in the last of the raspberry mixture; keeps chilled up to two days.

name: Chocolate caramel cake

ingredients.raw_text: 5 tbsp dulce de leche or caramel sauce
25g caramel flavoured chocolate, finely chopped (we used Lindt)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two tins. Beat the butter and sugar until pale. Add the eggs one at a time, then the baking powder, cocoa, and a third of the flour. Stir in half the soured cream, then alternate adding the remaining flour and cream until just combined. Spoon into the tins and level.
Bake 30 minutes until firm and a skewer comes out clean. Cool 5 minutes in the tins, then cool completely on a rack.

Whip the cream with dulce de leche to soft peaks. Layer one cake with half the cream, drizzle with more dulce de leche, top with the second cake, and spread the remaining cream on top. Sprinkle with chocolate and chill until serving.

name: Easy chocolate brownie cake

ingredients.raw_text: 175g unsalted butter, plus extra for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a cake tin.
Melt most of the chocolate with the butter and sugar in a pan over gentle heat; cool.
Whisk the egg yolks into the mixture, then add the flour, nuts, and remaining chocolate.
Whisk the egg whites to soft peaks and fold in thoroughly but gently.
Pour into the tin and bake 35-40 minutes until crusty on top. Cool, then remove from the tin. Dust with icing sugar; serve warm with custard or ice cream, or cold with cream.

name: Quick peach Melba crumbles
steps: Heat the tinned peaches with their syrup and the butter until simmering. Add the raspberries and cook 1-2 minutes more until hot.
Meanwhile, whisk the cream with the icing sugar to soft peaks.
Spoon the fruit into four dishes and top each with granola and a dollop of cream.

name: Quick sticky toffee puddings

ingredients.raw_text: small knob of butter, for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Combine the muffins with the sultanas, divide into buttered ramekins, cover with foil, and bake 8 minutes until warmed through.
Meanwhile, gently heat the sugar, butter, and cream until the sugar dissolves. Pour over the pudding and serve warm with ice cream.

name: Easy strawberry mousse
steps: Set aside four whole strawberries for garnish; hull and chop the rest. Blend with the sugar until smooth.

Whip the cream to stiff peaks. Reserve some strawberry puree, then fold the rest into the cream until evenly pink. Spoon the reserved puree into glasses, top with the mousse, and chill at least 1 hour (keeps up to a day). Halve the reserved strawberries to decorate before serving.

name: Easy microwave brownies
steps: Melt the butter in a microwavable dish in 20-second bursts, then swirl to coat the dish and tip into a mixing bowl.
Whisk in the sugar until creamy and fully combined.
Combine the vanilla, milk, and beaten eggs, then whisk gradually into the butter mixture.
Sift in the flour and cocoa, folding gently, then stir in the chocolate chips.
Pour into the dish and smooth. Microwave 5 minutes until risen and slightly firm (a soft center will continue cooking as it cools).
Cool on a rack at least 20 minutes before cutting into 8 squares. Keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Microwave mug cake

ingredients.raw_text: 3 tbsp vegetable oil or sunflower oil
a few drops of vanilla essence or other essence (orange or peppermint work well)
2 tbsp chocolate chips, nuts, or raisins etc (optional)

steps: Combine the flour, sugar, and cocoa powder in a large mug.
Mix in the egg as thoroughly as possible.
Add the milk, oil, and vanilla, mixing until smooth, then stir in any chocolate chips, nuts, or raisins.
Microwave on high 1.5-2 minutes until risen and firm to the touch.

name: Microwave banana pudding

ingredients.raw_text: 100g butter, softened, plus extra for greasing
icing sugar, toffee sauce and ice cream, to serve, if you like

steps: Melt the butter in a baking dish in the microwave. Mash in most of the banana, then add the sugar, flour, cinnamon, eggs, and milk, mixing well.
Slice the remaining banana on top and microwave on high 8 minutes until risen and cooked through. Serve warm, dusted with icing sugar and toffee sauce, with ice cream if desired.

name: 5-minute mocha pots

ingredients.raw_text: 200g milk or dark chocolate with coffee, broken into chunks
ingredients.food_name: Milk drink 3.5 % fat, with coffee, sweetened

steps: Melt the chocolate in the microwave or over simmering water; cool slightly.
Whip the cream with the vanilla to soft peaks, then fold in the cooled chocolate.
Divide into bowls and top with a dollop of creme fraiche just before serving (or chill first and add the creme fraiche later).

name: Vegan mug cake

ingredients.raw_text: 3 tbsp dairy-free milk, we used oat milk
coconut cream or dairy-free ice cream

steps: Combine the milk with the lemon zest and juice in a mug and let sit 2-3 minutes until it looks slightly curdled. Stir in the oil, flour, sugar, and bicarbonate of soda until smooth.
Drop in the raspberries and microwave on high 1.5 minutes until puffed and cooked through.
Serve with coconut cream or dairy-free ice cream.

name: Raspberry fool with whisky & toasted oats
steps: Whisk the cream with the sugar to soft peaks, then fold in the whisky.
Layer the cream in glasses with the toasted oats and halved raspberries. Finish with grated white chocolate.

name: Lemon curd & yogurt fool

ingredients.raw_text: 300g jar  lemon curd  (we used Duchy Originals Traditional Lemon Curd)

steps: Fold the lemon curd and yogurt together for a marbled effect. Divide into glasses and chill.
Crush the raspberries lightly with icing sugar and spoon over the chilled dessert with the juices.

name: After-dinner mint cream

ingredients.food_name: Mascarpone min. 80 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 2-3 tbsp cream sherry (we used Harvey's Bristol Cream)
200g thin after-dinner  mints, plus 6 extra to serve

steps: Whisk the mascarpone, cream, sugar, and sherry until it holds its shape. Break the mints into pieces and fold in. Spoon into small glasses and chill. Top each with one more mint before serving.

name: No-bake chocolate tart

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp icing sugar, plus extra for dusting

steps: Crush the biscuits in a bag with a rolling pin. Melt the butter with the syrup or honey and stir in the biscuits. Press into a tart tin and chill.

Melt the chocolate bars in the microwave, stirring, then mix in the vanilla and sift in the icing sugar. Whip the cream to soft peaks and fold into the chocolate. Pour into the base and chill at least 2 hours, up to 2 days.
To serve, loosen the edges, slide onto a plate, and let sit at room temperature 30 minutes for easier release. Top with creme fraiche and raspberries, dust with icing sugar, and slice thinly.

name: Gregg's tangy lemon tart

ingredients.raw_text: 500g plain flour, plus extra for dusting
juice 2-3 lemon (about 100ml/3.5fl oz) and 2 tbsp lemon zest

steps: Combine the flour and icing sugar, rub in the butter to crumbs, then mix in the egg yolks, adding water if needed. Shape into a ball, halve (freeze half), flatten, wrap, and chill at least 30 minutes. Meanwhile, beat the filling ingredients (except zest) together, sieve, and stir in the zest.
Roll the pastry to line a tart tin, trim, prick the base, and chill 30 minutes.

Set the oven to 160C (140C fan/gas 3). Blind bake with foil and rice or beans 10 minutes, remove the foil, and bake 20 minutes more. Pour in the filling and bake 30-35 minutes until just set. Cool, then remove from the tin and serve at room temperature or chilled.

name: Raspberry bakewell slice

ingredients.raw_text: 200g butter, very soft, plus a little extra for the tin

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a traybake tin. Roll the pastry to line it, prick, and chill 20 minutes.
Bake 8-10 minutes until just cooked. Cool briefly, reduce the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Dot with jam and scatter with raspberries.

Beat all the sponge ingredients until well mixed and spoon over the raspberry layer, smoothing evenly. Scatter with almonds and bake 35-40 minutes until golden and firm. Cool completely in the tin (freezes up to 3 months).
To serve, thaw at room temperature 4 hours if frozen, reheat gently, then brush with warmed apricot jam.

name: Clementine custard tarts

ingredients.raw_text: 6 clementines, 3 zested, all juiced (you’ll need about 240ml)
2 eggs, plus 3 egg yolks (freeze the extra whites to make meringues)

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Simmer the clementine juice until reduced to about 50ml, up to 20 minutes; cool.
Unroll the pastry and scatter with most of the clementine zest. Re-roll into a spiral, then slice into 12 rounds. Roll each to a 10cm circle and line a muffin tin. Chill until needed.

Whisk the eggs, yolks, cornflour, sugar, reduced juice, and remaining zest together. Gradually whisk in the milk, then warm over low-medium heat, stirring, until thickened, 8-10 minutes. Cool briefly in a jug, whisking occasionally to prevent a skin.
Fill the pastry cases with custard and bake 18-22 minutes until golden and the custard is set. Cool completely in the tin. Dust with icing sugar to serve.

name: Custard tart
steps: Rub the butter into the flour with lemon zest and salt to crumbs. Add sugar, egg, and milk, bringing together into a dough (can be made 2 days ahead).
Roll out and line a tart tin, leaving overhang. Chill 30 minutes.
Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Blind bake with beans 20 minutes, remove beans, and bake 20 minutes more until biscuity. Reduce the oven to 140C (120C fan/gas 1).
Boil the cream, milk, vanilla pod, lemon zest, and nutmeg. Beat the egg yolks and sugar until pale, then whisk in the hot liquid. Strain and settle, skimming off froth.
Pour into the tart case, grate over more nutmeg, and bake 40 minutes until just set with a slight wobble. Trim the pastry edges and cool completely before serving with more nutmeg.

name: Tarte tatin

ingredients.raw_text: 6 dessert apples (about 900g/2lb), such as Braeburn, Cox’s Orange Pippin or Adam’s Pearmain
85g unsalted butter (60g/21/4oz chilled and diced, 25g/1oz melted)
crème fraîche (full-fat please!) or high-quality vanilla ice cream, to serve

steps: Roll the pastry to a 24cm circle, prick with a fork, and freeze on a tray while preparing the apples.

Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Peel, quarter, and core the apples. Cook the sugar in an ovenproof pan over medium-high heat 5-7 minutes to a dark amber caramel, then off the heat stir in the diced cold butter.
Arrange the apple quarters tightly around the edge of the pan, then fill the center, pressing to avoid gaps. Brush with melted butter.
Bake 30 minutes, then top with the frozen pastry disc, tucking the edges down and piercing a few steam holes. Bake 40-45 minutes more until golden and crisp.
Cool 1 hour, run a knife around the edge, and invert onto a deep serving plate. Serve with creme fraiche or vanilla ice cream.

name: Flat apple & vanilla tart

ingredients.raw_text: 5 large eating apples  - Cox's, russets or Elstar
3 tsp vanilla sugar or 1 tsp vanilla extract

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Roll the pastry to a 35cm round and transfer to a lined tray.
Toss the sliced apples in lemon juice and spread over the pastry, leaving a 2cm border, curling the edges up slightly.
Dot with butter and sprinkle with vanilla and sugar. Bake 15-20 minutes until the apples are tender and pastry crisp.
Brush the warmed conserve over the apples and pastry edge. Serve hot with vanilla ice cream or creme fraiche.

name: Next-level egg custard tart

ingredients.raw_text: 200g plain flour, plus extra for dusting
grating of nutmeg (at least  half a nutmeg)

steps: Melt the butter until nut-brown, then strain off the fat (discarding the sediment) and cool until solid. Combine the flour, icing sugar, and salt, cut in the hardened fat, and rub to crumbs. Mix in the egg yolk and water, flatten into a disc, wrap, and chill at least 30 minutes (can be made up to two days ahead; regular butter also works).
Roll out and line a tart tin, pressing in with excess pastry and leaving overhang. Chill at least 30 minutes or freeze 10 minutes; reserve trimmings.
Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Blind bake with parchment and beans 15 minutes, then remove and bake 15-20 minutes more until biscuity.
For the filling, boil the cream and vanilla. Stir (don't whisk) the egg yolks and sugar together, then slowly stir in the hot cream. Strain into a jug, skimming off froth.

Trim the case and patch any cracks with reserved pastry. Reduce the oven to 140C (120C fan/gas 1). With the tin half in the oven, pour in the filling to the top, grate over nutmeg, then slide fully in and bake 40 minutes until just set with a slight wobble. Cool completely in the tin before slicing.

name: Chocolate, hazelnut & salted caramel tart

ingredients.raw_text: 50g blanched hazelnut, toasted and roughly chopped

steps: Grind the hazelnuts finely in a processor. Add the flour, icing sugar, and butter, pulsing to crumbs. Add the egg yolk and water, pulsing to a dough. Flatten, wrap, and chill 30 minutes.
Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Roll out and line a deep tart tin, trim, prick, and blind bake with beans 20 minutes, then remove beans and bake 5-10 minutes more until light golden. Cool.

For the caramel, dissolve the sugar in water, then cook to an amber caramel. Reduce heat, stir in the butter, cream, and golden syrup until smooth. Add salt, cool slightly, then spread over the tart base. Scatter with chopped hazelnuts.

Melt the chocolate and butter over simmering water; cool slightly. Whisk the eggs, yolk, and sugar 6 minutes until thick and pale. Fold in the chocolate and cocoa, pour into the case, and bake 20-25 minutes until set with a crust. Cool to room temperature before slicing.

name: Peach & almond tart

ingredients.raw_text: 175g plain flour, plus extra for rolling
85g cold unsalted butter, cut into small pieces

steps: Rub the flour and butter to crumbs, mix in the sugar, then add the egg yolks with water until the dough comes together. Wrap and chill 30 minutes.
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Roll out and line a tart tin, patching any rips. Blind bake with beans 10 minutes, remove beans, prick the base, and bake 10-15 minutes more until biscuity.

Beat the sugar and butter to a pale paste, beat in the eggs, then the almonds and flour. Spoon into the case, arrange the peach slices on top, and bake 30-40 minutes until golden. Cool, then brush with warmed jam. Serve with creme fraiche.

name: Chocolate & date tart

ingredients.raw_text: 320g shortcrust pastry sheet (ensure vegan if needed)

steps: Sift cocoa and icing sugar over the unrolled pastry, fold in half, and chill 15 minutes. Roll to a larger rectangle to line a tart tin, leaving overhang. Chill 30 minutes. Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6).
Blind bake with beans 15-20 minutes, remove beans, and bake 15 minutes more until cooked through. Cool in the tin, then trim.
For the caramel, warm the coconut milk, pour some over the dates, soak 5 minutes, then blend with vanilla and salt until smooth. Spread over the tart base and chill 30 minutes to set.

Heat the remaining coconut milk until steaming, pour over the chocolate and coconut oil, and stir until combined. Spread over the caramel, sprinkle with salt, and chill about an hour to set. Bring to room temperature before slicing.

name: Custard tart with nutmeg pastry
steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Roll the pastry to 1cm thick, grate over nutmeg, fold in half, and re-roll to line a tin with overhang. Chill 10 minutes. Blind bake with beans 20 minutes, remove beans, and bake 15 minutes more until golden. Trim the edges.

Reduce the oven to 150C (130C fan/gas 2). Whisk the eggs and sugar. Boil the cream, milk, and vanilla, then whisk into the eggs. Strain into a jug. Pour the custard into the pastry case on the oven rack, filling to the top. Grate over nutmeg and bake 1 hour until set with a slight tremor in the center. Cool completely before slicing.

name: Blueberry & almond tart

ingredients.raw_text: 23-24cm cooked shortcrust pastry  tart case (see 'goes well with')

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Beat the butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Stir in the almonds, egg, and almond extract.
Stir in half the blueberries and spoon into the pastry case. Smooth, scatter with the remaining blueberries, and bake 45-50 minutes until crisp and golden.
Cool 10 minutes in the tin, then lift out. Dust with icing sugar and serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Classic bread & butter pudding

ingredients.raw_text: 175g caster sugar, plus extra to top the pudding

steps: Boil the cream. Whisk the egg yolks and sugar until fluffy. Pour the hot cream over, whisking constantly, and set aside.

Butter the bread and cut into triangles. Layer in a dish buttered-side up, scattering raisins and nutmeg between the bottom layers. Pour over the custard, pressing gently to soak, and rest at least 20 minutes (can be prepped ahead and chilled).
Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Place the dish in a water bath and bake 20-25 minutes until just set.
Sprinkle with more sugar and caramelize under a hot grill or with a blowtorch. Rest 5 minutes and serve with ice cream.

name: Lemon curd, mascarpone & passion fruit tart

ingredients.food_name: Mascarpone min. 80 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Unroll the pastry onto a lined tray, score a border, and brush with egg.
Bake 15-20 minutes until golden and crisp. Press down the center and cool completely.
Whisk the mascarpone, cream, and some lemon curd until soft and spoonable, then fold in more curd and passion fruit seeds.
Spoon onto the pastry center and top with more passion fruit seeds and chopped pistachios.

name: Strawberry shortcake slice
steps: Line a tin with cling film, leaving overhang. Whip most of the cream with vanilla, orange blossom water, and icing sugar until thick and billowy.
Layer biscuits, cream, and strawberry slices in the tin, repeating and finishing on biscuits. Press down firmly. Cover and chill overnight.

To serve, flip out onto a plate and remove the film. Whip the remaining cream with icing sugar and dollop on top. Finish with the remaining strawberries and a dusting of icing sugar, plus biscuit crumbs between the rows.

name: Peanut butter & jam pudding

ingredients.raw_text: vanilla ice cream or custard, to serve

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Heat the berries with jam until saucy. Combine some of the liquid with the cornflour, then stir back into the pan and simmer 2 minutes. Pour into a baking dish.

Beat the butter and sugar 5 minutes until fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, then the vanilla, flour, and peanut butter. Loosen with milk. Spoon over the berries, smooth, and scatter with nuts. Bake 45-50 minutes. Cool 10 minutes before serving warm with ice cream or custard.

name: Chocolate brownie cake
steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a cake tin.
Melt the butter, both sugars, chocolate, and golden syrup together until smooth.
Remove from heat.
Whisk the eggs until frothy, then combine with the vanilla, flour, baking powder, and cocoa into the chocolate mixture.
Pour into the tin and bake 25-30 minutes.
Cool 20-30 minutes before cutting into wedges.
Serve with cream or ice cream and fresh fruit.

name: Easy chocolate cake

ingredients.raw_text: 200g unsalted butter, softened plus extra for the tins
