steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5) and line two tins.
Beat the sugar, butter, eggs, flour, cocoa, baking powder, vanilla, milk, and salt together until pale.
Divide between the tins and bake 20 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
Cool 10 minutes in the tins, then cool completely on a rack.
For the buttercream, melt the chocolate and cool slightly.
Mash the butter and icing sugar together, switching to a spoon or beaters.
Sift in the cocoa and salt, add the melted chocolate and milk, and mix smooth.
Sandwich the cakes with half the buttercream and spread the rest on top; decorate with chocolate shards if desired.
For the shards, melt dark chocolate and pour onto a lined tray.
Melt milk and white chocolate separately and drizzle over before the dark chocolate sets.
Level by shaking the tray, let set, then break into shards.

name: Marble bundt cake

ingredients.raw_text: 180g butter, very soft, plus extra for the tin

steps: Butter and line a bundt tin. Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Beat the egg yolks and sugar until pale, then beat in the butter and milk or cream.
Separately whisk the egg whites to soft peaks, add vanilla sugar, and whisk stiff. Beat the flour and baking powder into the yolk mixture, then fold in the whites.
Pour half the batter into the tin. Fold the cocoa into the remaining batter and pour over. Swirl with a spoon handle. Bake about 1 hour until a skewer comes out clean.
Cool briefly in the tin, then turn out and cool completely on a rack. Dust with icing sugar.

name: Sachertorte
steps: Melt the chocolate over simmering water or in the microwave; cool slightly. Whisk the egg whites and salt to soft peaks, then gradually add sugar to stiff peaks.

Line a springform tin. Set the oven to 170C (150 fan/gas 3). Beat the butter and remaining sugar until light and creamy. Gradually beat in the egg yolks and cooled chocolate. Fold in a third of the egg whites, then sift and fold in the flour in two batches, followed by the remaining whites.
Pour into the tin and bake about 1 hour until risen and springy. Cool completely in the tin on a rack.

Warm the jam and liqueur or juice, then strain. Remove the cake from the tin, halve horizontally, spread half the jam mixture on the base, and top with the other half. Spread a thin layer of remaining jam over the whole cake and let dry.

For the glaze, boil water and sugar until dissolved, cool slightly, stir in some cream. Heat the remaining cream separately, melt in the butter, then gradually add the chocolate and the sugar syrup. Pour over the cake on a rack over a tray, tilting gently to coat evenly without smoothing.
For the stripes, melt the milk chocolate, pipe parallel lines onto the wet glaze, then drag a spoon tip alternately through the lines to create a pattern. Let dry.

name: Chocolate Swiss roll

ingredients.raw_text: 75g  golden caster sugar, plus extra for scattering
50g  icing sugar, sifted, plus extra to serve

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7) and line a swiss roll tin. Sift the flour and cocoa together.
Whisk the eggs and sugar 5 minutes until pale and thick. Gently fold in the flour mixture until no streaks remain.

Spread into the tin and bake 9-10 minutes until springy. Meanwhile, prepare a sugared sheet of parchment. Invert the baked sponge onto it, peel off the top parchment, score a line near one short edge, and roll into a spiral with the parchment inside. Cool completely.

Whisk the filling ingredients until stiff. Unroll the sponge, spread with the filling (leaving the scored edge clear), and re-roll using the parchment. Transfer to a plate and dust with icing sugar. Serve immediately or chill up to 24 hours.

name: Chocolate & raspberry birthday layer cake

ingredients.raw_text: 225ml sunflower oil, plus extra for the tins

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two springform tins. Whisk the oil, sugar, eggs, and milk. Sift in the flour, cocoa, and bicarbonate of soda separately, then gradually combine with the wet ingredients.
Divide between the tins and bake 35-40 minutes until risen and springy. Cool 10 minutes in the tins, then cool completely on a rack.
Combine the jam and defrosted raspberries. Whip the cream with sugar to soft peaks, then fold in half the raspberry mixture for a ripple effect.

Spread most of the remaining raspberry mixture over one cake, top with half the cream, and smooth. Add the second cake, swirl over the remaining cream, and swirl in the last of the raspberry mixture; keeps chilled up to two days.

name: Chocolate caramel cake

ingredients.raw_text: 5 tbsp dulce de leche or caramel sauce
25g caramel flavoured chocolate, finely chopped (we used Lindt)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two tins. Beat the butter and sugar until pale. Add the eggs one at a time, then the baking powder, cocoa, and a third of the flour. Stir in half the soured cream, then alternate adding the remaining flour and cream until just combined. Spoon into the tins and level.
Bake 30 minutes until firm and a skewer comes out clean. Cool 5 minutes in the tins, then cool completely on a rack.

Whip the cream with dulce de leche to soft peaks. Layer one cake with half the cream, drizzle with more dulce de leche, top with the second cake, and spread the remaining cream on top. Sprinkle with chocolate and chill until serving.

name: Easy chocolate brownie cake

ingredients.raw_text: 175g unsalted butter, plus extra for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a cake tin.
Melt most of the chocolate with the butter and sugar in a pan over gentle heat; cool.
Whisk the egg yolks into the mixture, then add the flour, nuts, and remaining chocolate.
Whisk the egg whites to soft peaks and fold in thoroughly but gently.
Pour into the tin and bake 35-40 minutes until crusty on top. Cool, then remove from the tin. Dust with icing sugar; serve warm with custard or ice cream, or cold with cream.

name: Chocolate cornflake cakes

ingredients.raw_text: 100g milk or dark chocolate, broken into chunks

steps: Measure out the butter, chocolate, and golden syrup into a pan or microwave bowl. Put the cornflakes in a separate large bowl.
Melt the butter, chocolate, and syrup gently, then cool slightly before pouring over the cornflakes.
Stir gently to coat, then spoon into 12 cupcake cases on a tray. Chill to set.

name: Number birthday cake

ingredients.raw_text: 350g unsalted butter, softened, plus extra for the tin
mini meringues, biscuits, macarons and raspberries (or any toppings you like)

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and butter a rectangular tin. Beat the butter and sugar 5 minutes until pale. Add the eggs one at a time. Sift in the flour and baking powder, add the lemon zest and milk, and fold in. Spoon into the tin and bake 35-40 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool 10 minutes in the tin, then cool completely on a rack.

Halve the cooled cake horizontally. Combine sugar and lemon juice and brush over the cut sides. For the buttercream, beat the butter, gradually add icing sugar and a splash of water, and beat until pale. Spread jam on the base layer, top with a third of the buttercream, and sandwich with the second layer. Cut into your desired shape, then pipe the remaining buttercream on top and decorate; keeps chilled up to three days.

name: Chocolate brownie cake
steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a cake tin.
Melt the butter, both sugars, chocolate, and golden syrup together until smooth.
Remove from heat.
Whisk the eggs until frothy, then combine with the vanilla, flour, baking powder, and cocoa into the chocolate mixture.
Pour into the tin and bake 25-30 minutes.
Cool 20-30 minutes before cutting into wedges.
Serve with cream or ice cream and fresh fruit.

name: Raspberry ripple cake
steps: Grease a deep round tin and line the base and sides with parchment. Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Beat the sugar and butter until creamy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well. Stir in the vanilla, then the flour and baking powder, then loosen with milk. Gently stir in half the raspberries.

Bake 25-30 minutes until nearly cooked through. Scatter marshmallows over the top, leaving a border, and tuck in the remaining raspberries. Return to the oven 10 minutes more until fully cooked and the marshmallows are gooey and lightly browned. Rest 5 minutes before removing from the tin and cutting.

name: Father's Day cupcakes

ingredients.raw_text: 140g self-raising flour  (swap 1 tbsp of flour for cocoa powder)

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5) and line a muffin tin. Combine the yogurt, eggs, and vanilla in a jug. Combine the dry ingredients and salt in a bowl and make a well.

Add the yogurt mixture and melted butter, folding quickly without overworking. Divide into the cases and bake 18-20 minutes until golden and springy. Cool briefly, then transfer to a rack; keeps airtight up to 3 days or freezes well.

For the frosting, melt the chocolate and cool. Beat the butter and icing sugar until creamy, then beat in the chocolate. Chill up to a month; bring to room temperature before spreading over the cakes and topping with chocolate buttons.

name: Sticky upside-down banana cake

ingredients.raw_text: 80g unsalted butter, plus extra for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a deep round tin.
Melt the butter and sugar together for the topping, then stir in the vanilla. Pour into the tin, and arrange the halved bananas cut-side down on top.

Beat all the batter ingredients with salt until smooth. Pour over the bananas and level. Bake 55 minutes to 1 hour until a skewer comes out clean, covering with foil after 45 minutes if browning too fast. Cool 10 minutes in the tin before turning out. Best warm, but good at room temperature; eat within 2 days.

name: Giant cookie cake

ingredients.food_name: Chocolate white, filled with coconut chips

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and grease a springform tin. Beat the butter and sugar until pale and fluffy, then mix in the egg, extra yolk, and vanilla.
Combine the flour, cornflour, and baking powder, and fold in until just combined (the mixture will be thick). Stir in the chocolate chips.
Spoon into the tin and bake 20-25 minutes until light golden. Cool 5 minutes and serve warm with ice cream.

name: Blueberry lemon cake with coconut crumble topping
steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a tin. Beat most of the butter with most of the sugar and the lemon zest until fluffy. Gradually beat in the eggs, adding a spoonful of flour if curdling. Fold in the flour and a third of the blueberries, spoon into the tin, level, and scatter with another third of the blueberries. Bake 20 minutes until set on the surface.
Melt the remaining butter and stir in the coconut, remaining sugar, and eggs. Warm the lemon curd until pourable.

Scatter the remaining blueberries over the cake, drizzle with lemon curd, and crumble over the coconut mixture. Bake 20-25 minutes more until the coconut is golden. Cool in the tin, then cut into squares.

name: Peach melba pop pies

ingredients.raw_text: 410g can peach slices in fruit juice, drained and chopped

steps: Set aside some raspberries; combine the rest with the peaches. Mix the cornflour and honey into a paste, pour over the fruit, and combine.

Cut rectangles from the pastry sheets. Fold each in half to crease, open, and spoon filling onto one side, leaving a border. Brush the edges with egg, fold over to seal, crimp with a fork, brush with more egg, and poke steam holes. Chill 30 minutes to 24 hours, or freeze up to 2 months.

Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Bake 20 minutes until golden and bubbling (5 minutes more from frozen). Cool at least 20 minutes. Crush the reserved raspberries with icing sugar for a thick icing, spread thinly over the pies, and top with freeze-dried raspberries or sprinkles.

name: Mixed fruit tart

ingredients.raw_text: 220g plain flour, plus extra for dusting

ingredients.food_name: Mascarpone min. 80 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 300g fruit (we used cherries, redcurrants and various berries), chopped if necessary

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Pulse the flour, sugar, and salt in a processor, add the butter and pulse to crumbs, then add the egg yolk and water, pulsing until it starts to come together. Bring together by hand, wrap, and chill 1 hour (up to 3 days ahead).

Roll out and line a tart tin, trim, and chill 30 minutes. Prick the base, line with parchment and beans, and bake 20 minutes. Remove the parchment and bake 12-14 minutes more until golden. Cool completely on a rack.
Whisk the mascarpone, cream, vanilla, and icing sugar to soft peaks. Spread into the pastry case and decorate with fruit; best eaten the same day.

name: Easy apple pie

ingredients.raw_text: crème fraîche, vanilla ice cream or natural yogurt, to serve

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Rub the butter into the sifted flour until crumbly.
Gradually add cold water to bind into a dough, then wrap and chill while preparing the apples, ideally 30 minutes.
Core and chunk the apples evenly. Add to the pie dish with honey, cinnamon, mixed spice, and a little water.
Roll out the pastry to cover the dish, lay over the apples, trim the excess (reroll for decoration if desired), and seal the edges.
Cut a small vent in the top and brush with beaten egg.
Bake 20-30 minutes until the pastry is golden and sandy and the filling bubbles. Serve warm with creme fraiche, ice cream, or yogurt.

name: Mince & pea pies

ingredients.raw_text: Tenderstem broccoli or other greens, to serve

steps: Brown the mince in oil, breaking up lumps; set aside. Cook the onion, carrots, and celery in the remaining oil over low heat until softened. Stir in the tomato puree and crumbled stock cube, return the mince, and cook a minute. Add water, cover, and simmer 20 minutes, then uncover and simmer until slightly thickened. Stir in the peas and cool 10 minutes.

Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Divide the mince between pie dishes, brush the rims with egg. Cut and roll the pastry to fit, top the pies, seal the edges with a fork or crimp, and brush with egg.
Poke a steam hole, decorate with pastry off-cuts if desired, and bake 25-30 minutes until golden and risen. Cool 5-10 minutes before serving.

name: Crumbly chicken & mixed vegetable pie

ingredients.raw_text: 40g plain flour, plus extra for dusting
200g mix of sweetcorn, peas, chopped, peppers, broccoli, carrots or other veg
1 egg, beaten or milk, for glazing

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Finely chop the onion and cook in butter 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Slice and add the mushrooms.
Once nearly cooked, stir in the flour to make a roux and crumble in the stock cube. Cook, stirring, 2 minutes to remove the raw flour taste.
Gradually add the warm milk, stirring throughout. Season with salt, pepper, nutmeg, and mustard powder, add the bay leaf, and bring to a boil, stirring to avoid lumps.
Thin with more milk or water if needed, bring back to a boil, then reduce to very low heat. Chop the cooked chicken and add to the sauce with the vegetables; pour into a pie dish.
Roll out the pastry on a floured surface and lift over the dish, trimming the excess. Crimp the edge decoratively.
Brush with beaten egg or milk, cut a steam vent, decorate with pastry scraps if desired, and bake 25 minutes until golden brown.

name: Cheese & spinach pasties

ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter
Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a tray. Pat the spinach dry, then combine with the cheeses and nutmeg in a bowl.

Roll the pastry into a rectangle and cut six circles. Brush the edges with water, spoon the spinach mixture into the center of each, fold and seal. Brush with more water and poke holes with a fork. Bake 15-20 minutes until golden; serve warm or pack cooled for lunch.

name: Tomato tart

ingredients.food_name: Soft cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: anchovies or olives to decorate, if you like

ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Combine the soft cheese and mustard.
Thickly slice the tomatoes.
Roll the pastry into a rectangle on a floured surface and transfer to a baking sheet.
Trim uneven edges and mark a border around the edge.
Spread the cheese mixture inside the border, top with the tomato slices, and add anchovies or olives if using.
Grate over the Parmesan and bake 30 minutes until the pastry is golden and tomatoes cooked.

name: Little jam tarts

ingredients.raw_text: 20 tsp jam (we used apricot, blackcurrant and strawberry)

steps: Roll the pastry thin and cut 20 circles to line mini muffin tins.
Prick with a fork and spoon jam into each, decorating with pastry cutouts if desired.
Bake at 200C (180C fan/gas 6) for 12-15 minutes until golden.

name: Strawberry tartlets

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp strawberry jam, without any whole strawberries in it

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Roll the pastry and cut four circles to line a tin.
Line with parchment and baking rice, bake 25 minutes, then remove the rice and paper and cool.
Whip the cream until thickening, then stir in the lemon curd.
Hull and slice the strawberries.
Spoon the cream into the pastry cases and arrange the strawberries on top.
Warm the jam with water and brush over the strawberries.

name: Easy sausage rolls

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp apple sauce, pickle or chutney
2 tsp sesame seeds or nigella seeds (optional)

steps: Roll the pastry into a rectangle, trim, and halve lengthways. Spread each strip thinly with apple sauce, pickle, or chutney, leaving a border.

Combine the sausage meat with cold water. Divide in two and shape into cylinders. Place each on a pastry strip, brush the border and top with egg, fold the pastry over to enclose, and seal with a fork. Cut into short lengths and arrange on a lined tray. Chill 20 minutes (can be made a day ahead or frozen up to a month).
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Brush with remaining egg, sprinkle with seeds if using, and bake 30-35 minutes until deep golden. Cool 10 minutes on a rack.

name: Tuna & sweetcorn slice

ingredients.raw_text: 185g can tuna in spring water, drained and flaked

ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: a few  chives, snipped to 1cm lengths

steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Pinch up the pastry edges to form a border on a baking sheet, prick the center, and bake 10-15 minutes.
Meanwhile, combine the tuna and sweetcorn; season.

Press down the puffed center, spread with creme fraiche, top with the tuna mixture, and sprinkle with cheese. Bake 10-15 minutes more until golden and cooked through. Sprinkle with chives and cut into quarters.

name: Easy volcano cake

ingredients.raw_text: 100g apricot glaze or sieved apricot jam, warmed
about 250g assorted chocolate honeycomb chunks, chocolate raisins, Maltesers, caramel popcorn etc, plus plastic dinosaurs, cake sparklers or fountains etc, to decorate

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Grease and line a round cake tin plus two ovenproof bowls.
Beat the butter, most of the sugar, eggs, flour, and milk until light and creamy. Spread just under half into the cake tin.
Color a portion of the remaining mixture deep red. Dollop spoonfuls of red into the tin batter and swirl with a spoon handle for a marbled effect.
Distribute the remaining plain and red batter randomly between the two bowls, swirling similarly.
Bake the small bowl about 30 minutes, the medium bowl about 35, and the tin 50-60 minutes, until firm and a skewer comes out clean.

Trim any peaks once cool. Cut notches in the round cake for an uneven base. Glaze with apricot jam and stack the bowl cakes on top, rounded-side up, brushing each layer with glaze. Hollow a small mouth shape in the top cake and glaze.
Roll out marzipan to drape over the stack in a rough volcano shape.
Melt chocolate and paint over the volcano, attaching chocolate chunks or popcorn. Add decorations and drizzle with remaining chocolate.
For the lava, melt the remaining sugar in a pan until light brown, stir in red coloring, then drizzle shapes onto an oiled tray and cool.
Once set, place the lava shapes in the volcano's mouth; add sparklers or candles for effect.

name: Super-easy birthday cake
steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two tins. Beat the butter and sugar, then add the eggs one at a time. Fold in the flour, milk, and vanilla until smooth.
Divide into two bowls, sifting cocoa into one. Pour each into a tin and bake 20-25 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool 5 minutes, then cool completely on a rack.

For the icing, beat the butter and gradually beat in the icing sugar until smooth and creamy. Tint with pink coloring. Sandwich the cakes with icing and spread the rest on top; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Chocolate sandwich cookie ice cream cupcakes

ingredients.raw_text: 20g crème-filled chocolate sandwich cookies, plus 8 whole cookies for decorating

steps: Line eight muffin holes with cases. Crush the cookies to fine crumbs and combine most of them with melted butter. Press into the base of each case.
Whisk the cream, condensed milk, and some crushed cookies until just thickening. Spoon over the bases and freeze at least 2 hours or overnight until solid (keeps frozen up to two days).
Whip the cream and icing sugar to a pipeable consistency and pipe onto each cupcake. Top with a whole cookie and cookie crumbs; serve immediately.

name: Easy sausage rolls

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp apple sauce, pickle or chutney
2 tsp sesame seeds or nigella seeds (optional)

steps: Roll the pastry into a rectangle, trim, and halve lengthways. Spread each strip thinly with apple sauce, pickle, or chutney, leaving a border.

Combine the sausage meat with cold water. Divide in two and shape into cylinders. Place each on a pastry strip, brush the border and top with egg, fold the pastry over to enclose, and seal with a fork. Cut into short lengths and arrange on a lined tray. Chill 20 minutes (can be made a day ahead or frozen up to a month).
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Brush with remaining egg, sprinkle with seeds if using, and bake 30-35 minutes until deep golden. Cool 10 minutes on a rack.

name: Number birthday cake

ingredients.raw_text: 350g unsalted butter, softened, plus extra for the tin
mini meringues, biscuits, macarons and raspberries (or any toppings you like)

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and butter a rectangular tin. Beat the butter and sugar 5 minutes until pale. Add the eggs one at a time. Sift in the flour and baking powder, add the lemon zest and milk, and fold in. Spoon into the tin and bake 35-40 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool 10 minutes in the tin, then cool completely on a rack.

Halve the cooled cake horizontally. Combine sugar and lemon juice and brush over the cut sides. For the buttercream, beat the butter, gradually add icing sugar and a splash of water, and beat until pale. Spread jam on the base layer, top with a third of the buttercream, and sandwich with the second layer. Cut into your desired shape, then pipe the remaining buttercream on top and decorate; keeps chilled up to three days.

name: Cheese & onion pasties

ingredients.raw_text: 150g mature cheddar (or a mixture of strong hard cheeses), grated
ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 250g plain flour, plus extra for dusting

steps: For the pastry, grate the butter into the flour, add the cayenne, mustard powder, half the egg, and salt, and bring together by hand, adding water if dry. Knead briefly, flatten into a disc, wrap, and chill.
Cook the onions and thyme in oil 8-10 minutes until softened and caramelizing; cool. Mix in the cheese, mustard, and spring onions.
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Roll the pastry thin and cut into circles, re-rolling scraps for about 10-12 total.
Spoon filling into the center of each circle, brush the edges with egg, fold into a half-moon, and crimp to seal. Brush with egg; freezes up to two months.
Bake 20-25 minutes (or 40-45 from frozen) until deep golden. Cool at least 10 minutes before serving warm, or cool completely for a picnic.

name: Peach Melba squares

ingredients.raw_text: 2 just-ripe peaches, stoned, halved, then each half cut into 3

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a tin. Melt the butter, cool 5 minutes, then beat in the sugar, vanilla, and eggs. Stir in the flour, almonds, and salt.

Spread into the tin, lay the peach slices evenly on top, scatter with raspberries and almonds, and bake 1 hour to 1 hour 10 minutes, covering with foil after 40 minutes. Cool 20 minutes in the tin, then transfer to a rack. Dust with icing sugar once cold and cut into squares.

name: Triple cheese & onion muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 150ml sunflower oil, plus extra for the tin
284ml carton buttermilk, made up to 350ml with milk

ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter
Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 200g full-fat soft cheese, gently diced into 2cm cubes
ingredients.food_name: Soft cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and oil a muffin tin. Whisk the oil, egg, and buttermilk. Combine the flour, salt, and mustard powder, then stir in the cheddar, spring onions, chives, and half the Parmesan.
Fold the wet ingredients into the dry gently, mixing in the soft cheese cubes at the end. Divide between the holes, top with remaining Parmesan, and bake 25 minutes.

name: Smoked salmon & poppy seed palmiers
steps: Unroll the pastry and brush one side with egg. Sprinkle with poppy seeds and flip. Spread with cream cheese, top with smoked salmon and chives. Mark a center line without cutting through, then roll each end tightly toward the middle. Slice into rounds and place cut-side up on a lined tray. Chill (or freeze up to 2 months).
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Bake 20-25 minutes until puffed and golden (add 5 minutes from frozen). Cool on a rack before packing.

name: Brie, courgette & red pepper muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 2 small or 1 large courgette, cut into small cubes
1 tbsp fresh oregano leaves or 1 tsp dried
2 red peppers, skinned (see tip, below) and cut into bite-sized pieces

ingredients.food_name: Chester (Cheddar) cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: 100g brie or camembert (check packaging for a vegetarian-friendly brand)
ingredients.food_name: Brie cheese min. 60 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5) and line 10 muffin holes. Cook the courgettes in butter about 5 minutes until softened.
Combine the flour, baking powder, oregano, and seasoning. Make a well, add the eggs, milk, and oil, stirring to draw in the flour, and beat 1 minute to a smooth batter.

Stir in the courgettes, peppers, most of the cheddar, and the brie or camembert. Divide between the cases, top with remaining cheddar, and bake 25-30 minutes until firm and golden. Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Homemade soft pretzels

ingredients.raw_text: 3 tbsp bicarbonate of soda, baked (see below)
1 large egg, lightly beaten, for glazing

steps: Combine the flour, yeast, sugar, and salt. Mix the warm water with the butter, add to the dry ingredients, and combine to a rough dough.
Knead 10-15 minutes until smooth and elastic. Prove covered in an oiled bowl about 1 hour until doubled.
Knock back and divide into eight pieces, rolling each into a 60cm rope.
Shape into a U, cross the ends over.
Fold the ends back and press into the curve to form a pretzel shape. Repeat with the rest. Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6).
Arrange on a lightly oiled lined tray, cover, and rest about 20 minutes until puffy.
Bring water to a boil with baked bicarbonate of soda, reduce to a simmer, and poach each pretzel 20 seconds per side, flipping with a slotted spoon as they float.
Return to the tray, brush with egg, and sprinkle with sea salt.
Bake 20-25 minutes until deep brown. Cool 10 minutes on the tray, then transfer to a rack; best fresh, freezes up to a month.

name: Cheese & pesto whirls

ingredients.raw_text: 450g strong white bread flour, plus a little for dusting
2 tbsp olive oil, plus a drizzle
240g tub semi-dried tomatoes, drained and roughly chopped
100g grated mozzarella (ready-grated is best for this, as it is drier than fresh)
ingredients.food_name: Mozzarella min. 45 % fat in dry matter
Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Combine the flour, yeast, sugar, and salt. Add warm water and oil, mixing to a dough. Knead 10 minutes by hand (or 5 in a mixer) until soft and elastic. Prove in an oiled, covered bowl 1-3 hours until doubled.
Line a tray. Knock back the dough and roll to a large rectangle. Spread with pesto, scatter with tomatoes, both cheeses, and basil, then roll up into a log from a long side.

Cut into 12 pieces and arrange cut-side up on the tray in a grid, tucking the loose ends toward the center so they don't unravel. Cover loosely and prove 30 minutes to 1 hour until doubled. Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6).
Bake 35-40 minutes until golden and cooked through. Cool at least 10 minutes before serving.

name: Gruyère & onion tear & share

ingredients.raw_text: 500g strong bread flour, plus extra for dusting
2 tbsp olive oil, plus 2 tsp extra

ingredients.food_name: Gruyère cheese min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Combine the flour, yeast, sugar, and salt. Gradually mix in warm water to a soft, not-too-sticky dough, adjusting with flour or water as needed. Knead 10 minutes until smooth and springy. Prove covered in a floured bowl 45 minutes to 1 hour until doubled.
Meanwhile, cook the onions in oil and butter 10-15 minutes until golden and soft. Cool, then stir in the cheese and thyme; season.

Knock back the dough and divide into 12 balls. Roll each to about 10cm, spoon in the onion filling, and seal by gathering the edges. Arrange seam-side down on an oiled tray, cover, and prove 30 minutes more.

Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Uncover, drizzle with remaining oil, and spoon over leftover onion mixture. Bake 10 minutes, then reduce to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and bake 10 minutes more until golden and crisp underneath. Cool before serving.

name: Blood orange & poppy drizzle muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 5 tbsp light rapeseed or sunflower oil, plus a little extra for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and grease a muffin tin. Combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, and poppy seeds. Separately whisk the oil, milk, egg, and zest and juice of one orange.
Quickly and lightly combine the wet and dry ingredients (the batter should stay lumpy). Divide into the tin and bake 10-12 minutes until risen and browned.
Meanwhile, combine the remaining zest with granulated sugar; reserve the remaining orange juice.
Cool the muffins 2 minutes, transfer to a rack, then quickly combine the juice with the sugar mixture and spoon over each muffin. Let set 5 minutes before eating.

name: Vegan cupcakes with banana & peanut butter

ingredients.raw_text: 2 large or 3 small ripe bananas, mashed

steps: Set the oven to 170C (150C fan/gas 3.5) and line muffin tins. Combine the flour, sugar, salt, and bicarbonate of soda. Separately combine the mayonnaise, mashed banana, and vanilla. Fold the wet into the dry until just combined. Divide into cases and bake 20 minutes.
Sprinkle chocolate chips over the warm cupcakes without touching, so they melt and reset.
Beat the margarine and icing sugar, add the vegan milk, and mix until smooth, then beat 3 minutes more. Stir in the peanut butter. Pipe or spread over the cooled cakes; keeps airtight up to 2 days.

name: Microwave mug cake

ingredients.raw_text: 3 tbsp vegetable oil or sunflower oil
a few drops of vanilla essence or other essence (orange or peppermint work well)
2 tbsp chocolate chips, nuts, or raisins etc (optional)

steps: Combine the flour, sugar, and cocoa powder in a large mug.
Mix in the egg as thoroughly as possible.
Add the milk, oil, and vanilla, mixing until smooth, then stir in any chocolate chips, nuts, or raisins.
Microwave on high 1.5-2 minutes until risen and firm to the touch.

name: Raspberry bakewell cake
steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a deep round tin. Blend the almonds, butter, sugar, flour, eggs, and vanilla until combined.

Spread half the mixture in the tin, scatter with raspberries, then dollop over the remaining mixture and roughly spread. Scatter with flaked almonds and bake 50 minutes until golden. Cool, remove from the tin, and dust with icing sugar.

name: Easy chocolate cupcakes

ingredients.raw_text: 300g dark chocolate, broken into chunks - don't use one with a high cocoa content
200g light muscovado sugar, plus 3 tbsp extra
150ml sunflower oil, plus a little extra for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a 10-hole muffin tin. Pulse the chocolate to small pieces. Combine the flour, sugar, cocoa, oil, soured cream, eggs, vanilla, and water, whisking until smooth, then stir in most of the chocolate pieces. Divide between the cases and bake 20 minutes until a skewer comes out clean (avoiding chocolate chunks). Cool on a rack.
For the icing, heat the remaining chocolate pieces, soured cream, and sugar gently until melted and smooth. Chill until firm enough to swirl on top of the cooled muffins.

name: Choc-cherry muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 100g bar  white chocolate, cut into chunks
100g bar  dark chocolate, cut into chunks

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a muffin tin. Sift the flour and bicarbonate of soda, then stir in the cherries, chocolate, and sugar. Add the beaten eggs, yogurt, and butter, mixing without overworking (lumps are fine).
Fill the cases and bake 20-25 minutes until risen and golden. Cool on a rack — best served slightly warm.

name: Easy microwave brownies
steps: Melt the butter in a microwavable dish in 20-second bursts, then swirl to coat the dish and tip into a mixing bowl.
Whisk in the sugar until creamy and fully combined.
Combine the vanilla, milk, and beaten eggs, then whisk gradually into the butter mixture.
Sift in the flour and cocoa, folding gently, then stir in the chocolate chips.
Pour into the dish and smooth. Microwave 5 minutes until risen and slightly firm (a soft center will continue cooking as it cools).
Cool on a rack at least 20 minutes before cutting into 8 squares. Keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Microwave chocolate cake

ingredients.raw_text: 100ml sunflower oil, plus extra for pan

steps: Grease a microwaveable cake pan and line the base with parchment.
Combine the sugar, flour, cocoa, and baking powder. Whisk the oil, eggs, vanilla, and hot water together separately.
Combine the wet and dry ingredients to a lump-free batter. Pour into the pan, tap to release bubbles, and cover with cling film.
Microwave on full power 7 minutes; check with a skewer for doneness. Rest 5 minutes, then uncover and turn out onto a rack.
For the ganache, melt the chocolate on half power, stirring every 30 seconds, then mix in the cream until smooth and glossy.
Once the cake is cool, spread with the ganache and scatter with sprinkles; keeps airtight up to 3 days.

name: Basic muffin recipe

ingredients.raw_text: 400g self-raising flour (or same quantity plain flour and 3 tsp baking powder)
100g chocolate chips or dried fruit such as sultanas or dried cherries (optional)

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line two muffin trays. Beat the eggs with an electric mixer 1 minute.
Add the oil and milk and beat until combined, then add the sugar and whisk to a smooth batter.
Sift in the flour and salt (or flour and baking powder) and mix until just smooth — avoid overmixing.
Fold in the chocolate chips or dried fruit if using.
Fill the cases two-thirds full and bake 20-25 minutes until risen and a skewer comes out clean, swapping shelves halfway if needed.
Cool briefly in the tin, then transfer to a rack.

name: Banoffee muffins with cream & salted caramel

ingredients.raw_text: 100g unsalted butter, melted, plus extra for the tin

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a muffin tin. Mash most of the bananas and stir in some yogurt. Separately combine the flours, baking powder, cardamom, and salt.

Whisk the eggs and sugar until pale and frothy. Slowly add the melted butter, then the vanilla. Alternately fold in the flour mixture and the banana-yogurt mixture until combined. Fold in the walnuts briefly. Divide into cases and bake 25-30 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
Meanwhile, heat the butter, cream, and sugar for the caramel until simmering; remove from heat and season with salt to taste.
Cool the muffins on a rack. Whip the cream, fold in the remaining yogurt, and slice the last banana. Top the muffins with cream, banana slices, and a drizzle of salted caramel.

name: Banana & blueberry muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 50g porridge oats, plus 1 tbsp for topping
2 medium bananas, the riper the better

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a muffin tin. Combine the flour and bicarbonate of soda in a bowl with most of the sugar and half the oats, reserving 1 tbsp sugar. Make a well. Mash the bananas and stir in the buttermilk, oil, and egg whites.

Pour the wet mixture into the well and stir briefly (a lumpy batter is fine — don't overmix). Fold in the blueberries with one more stir. Divide between the cases, top with the reserved oats and sugar, and bake 18-20 minutes until risen and dark golden. Cool 5 minutes before transferring to a rack.

name: Healthy banana muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 50g rolled oats, plus extra for sprinkling
2 tbsp mixed seed, we used pumpkin, sunflower and flaxseed

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a 12-hole muffin tin. Whisk together the eggs, yogurt, oil, apple sauce, banana, honey, and vanilla in a jug. Combine the remaining dry ingredients (except seeds) with a pinch of salt in a large bowl.

Fold the wet mixture into the dry until just combined — don't overmix. Divide between the cases, top with extra oats and seeds, and bake 25-30 minutes until risen and a skewer comes out clean. Cool on a rack; keeps airtight up to 3 days.

name: Toddler recipe: Mini egg & veg muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 1 small carrot, cut into small dice
½ small courgette, cut into small dice

ingredients.food_name: Feta min. 45 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and grease six muffin holes. Microwave the carrot, courgette, and peas with water, covered, 2 minutes until tender; drain.
Beat the eggs and stir in the vegetables and feta. Pour into the holes and bake 15 minutes until set. Rest briefly before removing with a knife.

name: Doughnut muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 140g golden caster sugar, plus 200g extra for dusting
140g butter, melted, plus extra for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5) and grease a muffin tin. Combine most of the sugar with the flour and bicarbonate of soda. Whisk the yogurt, eggs, and vanilla, then fold into the dry ingredients with the melted butter.
Divide two-thirds of the batter between the holes, add a teaspoon of jam to each center, then top with the remaining batter. Bake 16-18 minutes until risen and springy.
Cool 5 minutes, then roll each warm muffin in the reserved sugar.

name: Simnel muffins

ingredients.raw_text: grated zest and juice 1 medium orange

steps: Combine the fruit with zest and juice and microwave 2 minutes (or soak 1 hour). Line 12 muffin holes.
Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Beat the butter, sugar, eggs, flour, spices, and milk until fluffy, then stir in the soaked fruit.
Half-fill the cases. Shape marzipan into 12 flattened discs and place one in each case, then top with the remaining batter. Bake 25-30 minutes until risen and firm. Cool.
Combine icing sugar and orange juice into a thick icing, drizzle over, and top with a cluster of eggs. Best eaten within a day.

name: Choc-cherry muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 100g bar  white chocolate, cut into chunks
100g bar  dark chocolate, cut into chunks

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a muffin tin. Sift the flour and bicarbonate of soda, then stir in the cherries, chocolate, and sugar. Add the beaten eggs, yogurt, and butter, mixing without overworking (lumps are fine).
Fill the cases and bake 20-25 minutes until risen and golden. Cool on a rack — best served slightly warm.

name: Breakfast muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 100g apple sauce or pureed apples (find with the baby food)
50g rolled oats, plus extra for sprinkling
2 tbsp mixed seed (we used pumpkin, sunflower and flaxseed)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a 12-hole muffin tin. In a jug, whisk together the eggs, yogurt, oil, apple sauce, banana, honey, and vanilla. In a separate large bowl, combine the remaining dry ingredients (except the seeds) with a pinch of salt.

Fold the wet mixture into the dry until just combined - avoid overmixing, or the muffins will turn dense. Divide among the cases, then top with the reserved oats and seeds. Bake 25-30 minutes until risen and golden, and a skewer comes out clean. Cool on a wire rack. Keeps in a sealed container up to 3 days.

name: Summer fruit crumble muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 150g berries  of your choice (we used blueberries and raspberries) 2 peaches or nectarines, stoned and cut into small pieces

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a muffin tin. Beat most of the butter and the sugar until fluffy. Add the eggs, beat, then mix in the yogurt, vanilla, and milk. Combine most of the flour with the baking powder, bicarbonate of soda, and salt, then stir into the wet ingredients.
Spoon batter into each case, press in raspberries, blackberries, or chopped peaches (four cases each), then top with the remaining batter and matching fruit.
Rub the remaining flour and butter to small clumps, stir in the demerara sugar, and sprinkle over each muffin. Bake 22-25 minutes until risen and a skewer comes out clean.
Cool 10 minutes in the tin, then transfer to a rack; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Chocolate muffins with hot chocolate custard

ingredients.raw_text: 2 tbsp sunflower oil, plus extra for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 160C (140C fan/gas 3) and oil six muffin holes. Sift the cocoa with the remaining dry ingredients and make a well.

Whisk the milk, egg, and oil, pour into the well, and stir to a batter. Spoon into the holes and bake 15 minutes until risen and firm. Warm the custard, stir in chopped chocolate until smooth, and pour over the puddings in bowls.

name: Maple-glazed blueberry muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 50g maple syrup, plus 11/2 tbsp for the topping
175g blueberries, plus extra for the top

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a muffin tin. Combine the flour, baking powder, bicarbonate of soda, sugar, and salt. Whisk the butter, buttermilk, eggs, maple syrup, and vanilla, then combine with the dry ingredients and most of the blueberries, mixing briefly.
Divide between the cases and bake 22-25 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool on a rack.
Combine the remaining maple syrup, icing sugar, and salt. Brush over the warm muffins and top with the remaining blueberries. Best within a day or two.

name: Blood orange & poppy drizzle muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 5 tbsp light rapeseed or sunflower oil, plus a little extra for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and grease a muffin tin. Combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, and poppy seeds. Separately whisk the oil, milk, egg, and zest and juice of one orange.
Quickly and lightly combine the wet and dry ingredients (the batter should stay lumpy). Divide into the tin and bake 10-12 minutes until risen and browned.
Meanwhile, combine the remaining zest with granulated sugar; reserve the remaining orange juice.
Cool the muffins 2 minutes, transfer to a rack, then quickly combine the juice with the sugar mixture and spoon over each muffin. Let set 5 minutes before eating.

name: Slow cooker shepherd's pie

ingredients.raw_text: 650g potatoes, peeled and cut into chunks
250g sweet potatoes, peeled and cut into chunks

steps: Switch on the slow cooker if needed. Heat the oil and cook the onions and thyme 2-3 minutes. Add the carrots and cook until browning. Stir in the mince and cook until no longer pink. Stir in the flour, cooking briefly, then the tomato puree and lentils, seasoning with pepper and Worcestershire sauce, adding water if too dry. Transfer to the slow cooker.
Boil both types of potato 12-13 minutes until tender, drain, and mash with creme fraiche. Spoon over the mince and cook on low 5 hours until bubbling at the sides. Crisp under the grill if desired.

name: Puff pastry cinnamon rolls
steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a tray. Combine the cinnamon and sugar. Unroll the pastry and sprinkle the mixture over, leaving a border on one short side. Brush the border with egg.

Roll from the unmarked short side into a tight log, seal against the egg-washed border, and slice into 12 rounds. Arrange touching on the tray, brush with the remaining egg, and bake 15-18 minutes until golden and risen. Cool 10-15 minutes on the tray.
Combine the icing sugar with water to a thick, runny icing and drizzle over the warm rolls; serve immediately.

name: Smoked salmon & poppy seed palmiers
steps: Unroll the pastry and brush one side with egg. Sprinkle with poppy seeds and flip. Spread with cream cheese, top with smoked salmon and chives. Mark a center line without cutting through, then roll each end tightly toward the middle. Slice into rounds and place cut-side up on a lined tray. Chill (or freeze up to 2 months).
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Bake 20-25 minutes until puffed and golden (add 5 minutes from frozen). Cool on a rack before packing.

name: Almond & raspberry cruffins

ingredients.raw_text: 225g strong white flour, sifted, plus extra for shaping
140g cold unsalted butter, chopped into sugar-cube-sized pieces
a few tbsp seedless raspberry jam

steps: Dissolve the yeast in warm water. Combine cold water with lemon juice separately. Mix the flour, sugar, and salt, then toss in the butter cubes to coat. Add the yeast water and lemon water, working quickly with a knife to a rough dough with visible butter lumps.
Shape into a rectangle on a floured surface without over-kneading, wrap, and chill 15 minutes in the freezer.
Roll into a long rectangle about three times as long as wide, keeping edges straight.

Fold into thirds like a letter, turn so the opening faces right, and press the edges. Repeat this roll-and-fold three more times, chilling briefly if it becomes greasy or springy. Wrap and chill at least 1 hour.
For the filling, beat all ingredients together with a pinch of salt; set aside.

Halve the dough, keeping one half chilled. Roll the other to a rectangle and cut into three strips, folding each in half lengthways. Add a spoonful of filling at one end and roll into a spiral. Place cut-edge up in a muffin tin. Repeat for six cruffins; cover and chill (can be overnight).

Prove at cool room temperature 2 hours until filling the tin. Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Bake 20-25 minutes until deep golden. Cool slightly, then pipe jam into the center of each and dust with icing sugar; best eaten the day of baking.

name: Tomato galette

ingredients.raw_text: 500g tomatoes (use a mixture of sizes and colours), cut into ½cm pieces
50g gruyère or vegetarian alternative, coarsely grated
ingredients.food_name: Vegetarian cold cuts alternative, with egg

ingredients.raw_text: 225g plain flour, plus extra for dusting
2 eggs, plus extra beaten egg for brushing

steps: Salt the tomatoes in a colander over a bowl and let sit 1 hour to draw out moisture. Meanwhile, blend the flour, butter, and salt to fine crumbs in a processor. Add beaten egg and pulse to a soft dough, adding water if dry. Shape into a disc, wrap, and chill 20 minutes.
Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Combine the drained tomatoes with the cheese, garlic, herbs, and pepper.
Roll the pastry to a 30cm circle. Pile the tomato mixture in the center, leaving a border. Brush the border with egg and fold up over the filling, overlapping loosely.
Brush with more egg and bake 40-50 minutes until golden and the tomatoes are tender.

name: Smoked salmon, leek & dill tart
steps: Cook the leeks in the oil or butter with a pinch of salt 10 minutes, stirring, until softened. Cool a few minutes.
Meanwhile, whisk the creme fraiche, eggs, black pepper, lemon zest, salt, and half the dill.
Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Unroll the pastry into a tin so it comes up the sides, trimming excess if desired.
Spread the cooled leeks over the pastry, top with the smoked salmon, and pour over the egg mixture. Season and bake 25-30 minutes until set and golden.
Cool 10 minutes before serving, scattered with the remaining dill. Keeps chilled up to two days; serve hot or cold.

name: Roasted tomato, basil & parmesan quiche

ingredients.food_name: Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

ingredients.raw_text: handful basil  leaves, shredded, plus a few small ones left whole for scattering
280g plain flour, plus extra for dusting

steps: Rub the flour and butter to crumbs, add cold water, and bring together into a ball (use immediately or chill up to 2 days, or freeze up to a month).
Roll the pastry to line a tart tin with overhang, pressing into the corners. Chill 20 minutes. Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6).
Toss the tomatoes with oil and seasoning in a small tin and roast on a low shelf.
Prick the pastry base, line with parchment and beans, and blind bake 20 minutes, then remove and bake 5-10 minutes more until biscuity.
Remove the tart case and tomatoes together from the oven.

Meanwhile, beat the eggs, gradually add the cream, stir in the basil, and season. Once the case is ready, scatter half the cheese over the base, add the tomatoes, pour over the egg mixture, and top with the remaining cheese. Bake 20-25 minutes until set and golden. Cool in the case, trim the pastry, remove from the tin, and finish with more basil.

name: Beetroot & shallot tatins
steps: Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7) and grease two sandwich tins. Cook the shallots gently in oil until golden and soft, adding water and covering if they color too fast. Set aside and wipe the pan.
Melt the butter with the nuts, garlic, rosemary, maple syrup, and vinegar; season and bubble until syrupy. Return the shallots, coating them. Divide between the tins, alternating with the beetroot.

Roll the pastry into two pieces just larger than the tins, lay over the vegetables, tuck down the sides, trim, and pierce a steam hole. Bake 15-20 minutes until golden. Cool briefly in the tins, then turn out and top with rocket.

name: Roasted ratatouille & goat’s cheese tart

ingredients.raw_text: 1 yellow  pepper, deseeded and cut into strips
½ small bunch basil, leaves only, plus a few extra leaves to serve

ingredients.food_name: Semi-hard cheese made from goat's milk, min. 50 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Toss the aubergine, courgette, pepper, and onion with garlic, oil, and seasoning, and roast 20 minutes on trays. Add the tomatoes and roast 15-20 minutes more until tender and caramelizing.
Meanwhile, roll the pastry to line a tart tin and chill 30 minutes.
Reduce the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Blind bake with beans 15 minutes, remove beans, and bake 10-15 minutes more until light golden.

Reduce the oven to 160C (140C fan/gas 3). Mash roasted garlic with salt into a paste, whisk in the cream, then the eggs; season. Toss basil through the cooled vegetables, spread two-thirds in the tart case, crumble over most of the goat's cheese, and pour over the cream mixture. Top with remaining vegetables and cheese. Bake 40-50 minutes until just set with a slight wobble. Serve warm or cold with extra basil and pine nuts.

name: Smoked trout tartlets
steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Cut circles from the pastry and line a muffin tin. Chill while making the filling.
Combine the remaining ingredients except the trout in a jug, seasoning lightly (the trout is salty) with black pepper.
Divide the trout between the cases, then pour over the egg mixture, stirring between pours so the chives and zest don't settle.
Bake 18-20 minutes until the pastry is golden and the filling puffed (it will sink slightly). Serve warm.

name: Courgette, ham & ricotta tarts

ingredients.food_name: Ricotta min. 45 % fat in dry matter
Parmesan cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Roll the pastry into a square and quarter it. Prick with a fork and bake on a tray 15 minutes until puffed and golden.
Meanwhile, fry the courgettes in oil until soft and golden, adding garlic for the final minute. Combine the cream, ricotta, egg, and Parmesan.
Flatten the puffed centers, spread with the ricotta mixture leaving a border, top with ham, tomatoes, and courgette, season, and bake 15 minutes more until risen and golden. Serve with salad.

name: Easter fudge
steps: Line a square tin. Combine all ingredients except the mini eggs in a pan over low heat, stirring until the sugar melts. Simmer 10-15 minutes, scraping the base, until it reaches 115C (or forms a soft ball when dropped in cold water).
Cool 5 minutes off the heat, then beat 5 minutes with a wooden spoon.
Chop most of the mini eggs and stir in. Spread into the tin, scatter with remaining eggs, and let set at room temperature. Turn out and cut into 20 squares.

name: Chocolate fudge icing

ingredients.raw_text: 100g milk chocolate, chopped into small pieces

steps: Melt the chocolate over simmering water or in the microwave, stirring occasionally.
Cool slightly. Mash the butter and icing sugar together, then whisk until smooth.
Sift in the cocoa, add the melted chocolate and milk, and mix smooth. Use immediately; keeps refrigerated up to a week, brought to room temperature and beaten well before use.

name: Chocolate & orange fudge squares

ingredients.raw_text: 200g plain chocolate, broken into cubes (we used Bournville)
200g tub soft cheese (I used Philadelphia Extra Light)
ingredients.food_name: Soft cheese min. 30 % fat in dry matter

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a tin. Melt the chocolate, sugar, and butter together gently, stirring; cool 10 minutes. Beat in the egg yolks, flour, vanilla, and half the orange zest.

Whisk the egg whites to stiff peaks, fold a quarter into the chocolate mixture to loosen, then fold in the rest. Pour into the tin and bake 25 minutes until risen and just firm. Cool in the tin, then cut into squares (freezes up to a month).
Beat the cheese, vanilla, sugar, and remaining zest until smooth and spread over each square just before serving.

name: Instant frozen berry yogurt
steps: Blend the berries, yogurt, and honey or agave 20 seconds until it reaches an ice-cream-like texture. Scoop into bowls and serve.

name: Oaty hazelnut cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 50g butter, plus a little for greasing
1 dessert apple, unpeeled and coarsely grated (you need 85g)
40g unblanched hazelnuts, cut into chunky slices

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and grease a tray. Melt the butter and syrup, add the apple, and cook, stirring, 6-7 minutes until softened. Stir in the cinnamon and raisins.
Combine the oats, flour, and hazelnuts, add the apple mixture and egg, and beat well.
Spoon into nine mounds on the tray, pressing into discs, and bake 18-20 minutes until golden. Cool on a rack; keeps airtight three days or frozen six weeks.

name: Healthy pumpkin muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 200g coarsely grated pumpkin or butternut squash

ingredients.food_name: Oat flakes unsweetened, with yogurt 3.5 % fat and mixed fruit

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and lightly oil a muffin tin. Microwave the pumpkin, partially covered, 5 minutes, then mash with the spices; cool.

Beat in the yogurt, eggs, oat milk, and maple syrup, then fold in the flour, raisins, and baking powder, loosening with a little more milk if needed. Divide into cases, top with pecans, and bake 20-25 minutes until risen and firm. Cool; keeps chilled up to three days.

name: Breakfast muffins

ingredients.raw_text: 100g apple sauce or pureed apples (find with the baby food)
50g rolled oats, plus extra for sprinkling
2 tbsp mixed seed (we used pumpkin, sunflower and flaxseed)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a 12-hole muffin tin. In a jug, whisk together the eggs, yogurt, oil, apple sauce, banana, honey, and vanilla. In a separate large bowl, combine the remaining dry ingredients (except the seeds) with a pinch of salt.

Fold the wet mixture into the dry until just combined - avoid overmixing, or the muffins will turn dense. Divide among the cases, then top with the reserved oats and seeds. Bake 25-30 minutes until risen and golden, and a skewer comes out clean. Cool on a wire rack. Keeps in a sealed container up to 3 days.

name: Berry yogurt pots

ingredients.raw_text: 160g blueberries, raspberries or cherries (or a combination)
1 tbsp pumpkin seeds, sunflower seeds or flaked almonds

steps: Spoon the yogurt into glasses. Combine your choice of fruit, seeds or nuts with mint if using, and pile on top.

name: Almond, raisin & popcorn trail mix
steps: Heat the oil, add the corn, cover, and cook 2-3 minutes until popped. Cool, then mix in the raisins, almonds, and puffed wheat. Store in an airtight container.

name: Avocado & strawberry smoothie

ingredients.raw_text: ½ avocado, stoned, peeled and cut into chunks
lemon or  lime juice, to taste

steps: Blend all the ingredients until smooth, thinning with a little water if too thick.

name: Minty pineapple smoothie

ingredients.raw_text: 200g pineapple, peeled, cored and cut into chunks

steps: Blend all the ingredients with water until smooth, adding more water (up to 400ml) to reach the right consistency.

name: Energy balls with dates
steps: Blend the apricots, dates, and cherries to a fine paste. Work in the coconut oil by hand. Shape into walnut-sized balls and roll in sesame seeds. Store airtight.

name: Healthy chocolate milk
steps: Blend all the ingredients together until smooth and pour into a glass to serve.

name: Fruity fondue

ingredients.raw_text: 650g mixed fruits, such as strawberries, pineapple chunks, grapes, mango chunks,  melon  chunks
150g pot fat-free yogurt, use your favourite

steps: Thread the fruit onto skewers. Melt the chocolate gently in the microwave. Serve the skewers with the yogurt and melted chocolate for dipping.

name: Avocado & strawberry ices

ingredients.raw_text: 1 avocado, stoned, peeled and roughly chopped

steps: Blend the strawberries (reserving a few), avocado, vinegar, and vanilla until as smooth as possible. Add maple syrup only if needed for sweetness.
Divide into containers, add a reserved strawberry to each, cover, and freeze. Let soften 5-10 minutes before eating.

name: Sachertorte
steps: Melt the chocolate over simmering water or in the microwave; cool slightly. Whisk the egg whites and salt to soft peaks, then gradually add sugar to stiff peaks.

Line a springform tin. Set the oven to 170C (150 fan/gas 3). Beat the butter and remaining sugar until light and creamy. Gradually beat in the egg yolks and cooled chocolate. Fold in a third of the egg whites, then sift and fold in the flour in two batches, followed by the remaining whites.
Pour into the tin and bake about 1 hour until risen and springy. Cool completely in the tin on a rack.

Warm the jam and liqueur or juice, then strain. Remove the cake from the tin, halve horizontally, spread half the jam mixture on the base, and top with the other half. Spread a thin layer of remaining jam over the whole cake and let dry.

For the glaze, boil water and sugar until dissolved, cool slightly, stir in some cream. Heat the remaining cream separately, melt in the butter, then gradually add the chocolate and the sugar syrup. Pour over the cake on a rack over a tray, tilting gently to coat evenly without smoothing.
For the stripes, melt the milk chocolate, pipe parallel lines onto the wet glaze, then drag a spoon tip alternately through the lines to create a pattern. Let dry.

name: Homemade hot chocolate

ingredients.raw_text: 25g dark or plain  chocolate, finely chopped, plus a grating to serve
1 tbsp  whipped cream or squirty cream

steps: Heat the milk, cocoa, sugar, and chocolate together, stirring, until steaming and melted.
Pour into a mug, top with a float of cream, and grate over extra chocolate.

name: Chocolate cornflake cakes

ingredients.raw_text: 100g milk or dark chocolate, broken into chunks

steps: Measure out the butter, chocolate, and golden syrup into a pan or microwave bowl. Put the cornflakes in a separate large bowl.
Melt the butter, chocolate, and syrup gently, then cool slightly before pouring over the cornflakes.
Stir gently to coat, then spoon into 12 cupcake cases on a tray. Chill to set.

name: Chocolate-dipped strawberries

ingredients.raw_text: 30g white and milk  chocolate, roughy chopped (optional)

steps: Melt the dark chocolate over simmering water or in short microwave bursts, stirring until smooth. Transfer to a deep bowl.
Dip the strawberries by their tops into the chocolate, tap off excess, and set on parchment to firm up. Save any extra melted chocolate on parchment to reuse.
For decoration, melt the white and milk chocolate and drizzle lightly over the dipped strawberries.

name: Chocolate rice crispy cakes

ingredients.raw_text: sprinkles, mini marshmallows, nuts, Smarties, dried fruit or white chocolate buttons

steps: Melt the chocolate with the butter and golden syrup in the microwave or over simmering water until smooth. Off the heat, stir in the puffed rice until fully coated.
Divide between cupcake cases (set in a muffin tin to hold shape) and let set, or chill 1 hour to speed it up.
Drizzle with extra melted chocolate and decorate with sweets or nuts while still tacky. Keeps airtight up to five days.

name: Easy chocolate mousse

ingredients.raw_text: 150g 70% dark chocolate, plus extra to serve
4 tbsp crème fraîche and grated chocolate, to serve

steps: Melt the chocolate in the microwave, stirring often. Whisk the egg whites to soft peaks, then add sugar and whisk to stiff peaks.

Beat a spoonful of egg white into the warm chocolate to loosen it, working quickly, then gently fold in the rest. Spoon into glasses and chill 2-3 hours. Serve with creme fraiche and chocolate shavings.

name: Sticky chocolate cake

ingredients.raw_text: 100g dark chocolate chips or a bar

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a cake tin. Melt the butter and chocolate together gently, stirring.
Reserve some marmalade. Off the heat, stir the rest of the marmalade and the sugar into the chocolate mixture, then beat in the eggs. Fold in the flour in batches. Pour into the tin and smooth.
Bake 45-55 minutes, rotating halfway and covering with foil after 30 minutes if browning too fast, until firm in the center with a few crumbs on a skewer.
Cool 10 minutes, then turn out onto a rack. Warm the reserved marmalade and brush over the cake to glaze. Cool before serving.

name: Easy chocolate truffles
steps: Chop the chocolate into a bowl. Heat the cream and butter until simmering, then pour over the chocolate and stir until smooth. Mix in any flavorings if desired. Cool and chill at least 4 hours.
Scoop or roll the mixture into balls using a melon baller, oiled hands, or a piping bag.
Roll immediately in toppings such as chopped nuts, coconut, or cocoa, or dip in melted chocolate using a fork, then chill on parchment.
Package in small boxes for gifting; keeps refrigerated up to three days or frozen up to a month.

name: Hot chocolate stirrers

ingredients.raw_text: 300g dark, milk or white chocolate (or a mixture), broken into squares
selection of chocolate buttons, hundreds and thousands, mini fudge pieces or chopped peppermint candy canes

steps: Poke a small hole in the center of each cupcake case with a pencil.
Brush a thin layer of oil inside each fromage frais pot.
Melt the chocolate in short microwave bursts or over simmering water, melting different types separately if using more than one.
Pour the melted chocolate into the pots, insert a lolly stick in the center of each, and sprinkle with decorations.
Cover each pot with a cupcake case so the stick pokes through the hole, and chill overnight to set.
The next day, remove the chocolates from the pots, discard the cases, and wrap each in cellophane with a tag reading to stir into hot milk.

name: Fudgy brownies

ingredients.raw_text: 170g unsalted butter, plus extra for the tin
100g mixture of milk chocolate chunks, pretzels and nuts

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a square tin. Melt the butter and chocolate together gently until smooth; cool slightly.
Whisk the sugar and eggs until doubled in volume and a ribbon trail holds. Fold in the cooled chocolate mixture gently.

Sift in the cocoa, flour, and salt, then fold in most of the milk chocolate, pretzels, and nuts, reserving some for the top. Pour into the tin, scatter with the reserved toppings, and bake 25 minutes until just set with a shiny, wobbly top. Cool completely before slicing.

name: Salted caramel brownies

ingredients.raw_text: 200g unsalted butter, plus a little extra for greasing
1 tsp flaky sea salt, plus a little extra for the top

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a square tin.
Melt the butter with both chocolates in a pan, then remove from heat.
Mix some of the caramel with sea salt to loosen it.
Beat the remaining caramel with the sugar and eggs until even, then whisk in the chocolate mixture.
Sift the flour, cocoa, and salt together and fold in until smooth.
Pour half the batter into the tin and level.
Spoon half the salted caramel on top in stripes.
Top with the remaining batter, smoothing carefully, then stripe with the rest of the caramel and feather with a skewer.
Sprinkle with sea salt and bake 25-30 minutes until risen with a firm, slightly jiggly crust.
Cool completely in the tin before cutting into squares.

name: Easy microwave brownies
steps: Melt the butter in a microwavable dish in 20-second bursts, then swirl to coat the dish and tip into a mixing bowl.
Whisk in the sugar until creamy and fully combined.
Combine the vanilla, milk, and beaten eggs, then whisk gradually into the butter mixture.
Sift in the flour and cocoa, folding gently, then stir in the chocolate chips.
Pour into the dish and smooth. Microwave 5 minutes until risen and slightly firm (a soft center will continue cooking as it cools).
Cool on a rack at least 20 minutes before cutting into 8 squares. Keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Hazelnut brownies

ingredients.raw_text: box of 16 hazelnut chocolate wafer balls
250g pack salted butter, plus extra for greasing

steps: Freeze the unwrapped chocolates on a tray. Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a square tin.
Melt the butter, sugars, and cocoa together gently, stirring; cool 5 minutes.
Beat in the eggs one at a time until smooth and glossy, then stir in the flour, hazelnuts, and liqueur. Pour into the tin and bake 35 minutes.

Mark the top into 16 squares without cutting through, press a dent into each, and add a frozen chocolate to each. Bake 3 minutes more, then cool completely before cutting into squares; keeps airtight three days.

name: Vegan brownies

ingredients.raw_text: 80g vegan margarine, plus extra for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 170C (150C fan/gas 3.5) and line a square tin. Combine the flaxseed with water and set aside at least 5 minutes.
Melt most of the chocolate with the coffee and margarine and water over low heat; cool slightly.

Combine the flour, almonds, cocoa, baking powder, and salt. Whisk the sugar into the melted chocolate mixture until smooth and glossy. Stir in the flaxseed mixture, vanilla, and remaining chocolate, then the flour mixture. Spoon into the tin.
Bake 35-45 minutes until a skewer comes out with moist crumbs. Cool completely in the tin before cutting; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Chai caramel chocolate brownies

ingredients.raw_text: 200g salted butter, plus extra for the tin

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line a square tin. Melt the butter and chocolate together; cool slightly.
Combine the chai caramel ingredients until loosened.

Whisk the eggs and sugar 5 minutes until pale and thick. Fold in the cooled chocolate mixture, then the flour and cocoa until no streaks remain. Pour into the tin, drizzle with the chai caramel, and swirl with a teaspoon.
Bake 25-30 minutes until set at the edges with a slight wobble in the middle (bake longer if very wobbly). Cool completely before cutting into 16 squares; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Pitta bread

ingredients.raw_text: 500g strong white bread flour, plus extra for dusting

steps: Combine the yeast with warm water and let sit 5 minutes until bubbly. Add the flour, salt, and oil, bringing together into a soft dough.
Knead 5-10 minutes with minimal extra flour until smooth and elastic. Prove in an oiled, covered bowl about 1 hour until doubled.
Set the oven as high as possible with a tray inside to heat. Divide the dough into eight, flatten each, then roll into ovals about 20cm by 15cm, 3-5mm thick.
Place the ovals on the hot tray (in batches if needed) and bake 4-5 minutes until puffed and pale golden. Wrap each in a tea towel to stay soft.

name: Crusty bread

ingredients.raw_text: 500g strong white bread flour, plus extra for dusting
10g fine sea salt, sea salt flakes or table salt

steps: Combine the flour, yeast, and water into a rough dough, ensuring all flour is incorporated. Cover and rest 30 minutes.

Work in the salt by stretching the dough repeatedly. Turn out and stretch-and-fold for about 5 minutes until shiny and springy. Shape into a ball, cover, and prove at room temperature 1 hour until doubled (or overnight chilled for deeper flavor).
Knock back, rest 5 minutes, shape into a tight round, and place domed-side down in a lined proving basket. Prove 40 minutes to 1 hour at room temperature, or 2-3 hours chilled, until doubled.

Heat a covered casserole in the oven at 230C (210C fan/gas 8). Flip the dough onto parchment, slash the top, and lower into the hot casserole using the parchment. Cover and bake 25 minutes, then uncover and bake 5-10 minutes more until deeply golden and hollow-sounding. Cool at least 40 minutes before slicing; keeps airtight up to two days.

name: Simit bread

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp pomegranate molasses or molasses syrup

steps: Combine the flour, salt, and sugar. Add the yeast and warm water, mix, and knead a few minutes to bring together, then knead 10-12 minutes more until stretchy. Prove covered in a bowl 1-2 hours until doubled.
Meanwhile, toast the sesame seeds until golden. Combine the molasses (or pomegranate molasses) with water in a shallow bowl.

Divide the dough into four pieces and roll each into a 45cm rope. Fold back and twist the strands, then loop into a ring, sealing the ends. Dip each in the molasses water, then the sesame seeds, coating well. Transfer to a tray, cover, and prove 45 minutes to 1 hour. Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6).
Bake 15-18 minutes until golden. Cool, then wrap to keep fresh; can be made a day ahead and warmed gently.

name: Seeded flatbreads

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp kalonji seeds (also called black onion seeds or nigella seeds)

steps: Combine the yeast with warm water and sugar, and let sit a few minutes. Combine the flours and salt, make a well, add the yeast mixture and warm water, and mix to a dough. Knead 5-10 minutes until smooth. Prove covered 1 hour until doubled.
Knock back the dough and knead in the seeds. Divide into 12 pieces and roll each thinly. Cook in a hot dry pan 2 minutes per side until bubbly. Wrap in foil; keeps up to a day, reheat in a warm oven.

name: Focaccia

ingredients.raw_text: 500g strong bread flour, plus extra for dusting
5 tbsp olive oil, plus extra for the tin and to serve
¼ small bunch of rosemary, sprigs picked

steps: Mix the yeast into one side of the flour and the salt into the other, then combine (this keeps the salt from killing the yeast).
Make a well, add oil and warm water gradually to a slightly sticky dough. Knead 5-10 minutes on a floured surface until smooth. Prove covered 1 hour until doubled.
Oil a shallow tin and stretch the dough to fill it. Cover and prove 35-45 minutes more.
Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Dimple the dough with your fingers. Combine oil, water, and flaky salt, drizzle over, and push rosemary sprigs into the dimples.
Bake 20 minutes until golden. Drizzle with more oil while hot, then cut into squares and serve warm or cold.

name: Potato & turmeric focaccia

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tsp fresh yeast, or ½ tsp fast-action dried yeast
2 tbsp olive oil, plus extra to serve
1 rosemary sprig, leaves picked and chopped, to serve

steps: Dissolve the yeast in cold water and add the oil. Mix in the flour and salt to a dough by hand. Cover and leave in the fridge or a cool place overnight.
The next day, turn onto an oiled tray and stretch into a rectangle, then fold in half; rotate 90 degrees and repeat. Rest 30 minutes, then repeat the stretch-fold-rest twice more.
Set the oven to 240C (220C fan/gas 9). Gently stretch the dough to fill the tray, resting if it springs back.

Layer the potato slices over the dough, overlapping. Brush with oil mixed with turmeric, sprinkle with salt, and bake on the top shelf 25-30 minutes. Drizzle with more oil and scatter with rosemary once baked.

name: Tortillas

ingredients.raw_text: 250g plain flour, plus a little more for dusting

steps: Combine the flour, oil, and salt with warm water, kneading to bring together. Knead on a floured surface about 5 minutes until smooth. Cover and rest at least 15 minutes.
Divide into six pieces and roll each thinly into a 20cm round.
Cook in a hot dry pan 1-2 minutes per side until golden and toasted. Keep warm wrapped in foil and a tea towel; keeps two days wrapped or three months frozen.

name: Vegan brownies

ingredients.raw_text: 80g vegan margarine, plus extra for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 170C (150C fan/gas 3.5) and line a square tin. Combine the flaxseed with water and set aside at least 5 minutes.
Melt most of the chocolate with the coffee and margarine and water over low heat; cool slightly.

Combine the flour, almonds, cocoa, baking powder, and salt. Whisk the sugar into the melted chocolate mixture until smooth and glossy. Stir in the flaxseed mixture, vanilla, and remaining chocolate, then the flour mixture. Spoon into the tin.
Bake 35-45 minutes until a skewer comes out with moist crumbs. Cool completely in the tin before cutting; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Vegan chocolate ice cream

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp vanilla extract or 1 heaped tsp vanilla bean paste

steps: Combine most of the coconut milk with the sugar, cocoa, chocolate, salt, and vanilla in a pan; heat gently 10 minutes until dissolved and melted.
Combine the cornflour with the reserved coconut milk, stir into the hot mixture, and cook 5-10 minutes, stirring, until it thickens to a pourable custard. Strain, cover, and cool to room temperature.

Churn in an ice cream maker 20-30 minutes to soft-scoop consistency, then freeze in a sealed container up to three months. Without a machine, freeze in a shallow dish, stirring every 20 minutes for 3 hours until soft-set, then transfer to a container. Soften 10 minutes before scooping.

name: Chocolate & date tart

ingredients.raw_text: 320g shortcrust pastry sheet (ensure vegan if needed)

steps: Sift cocoa and icing sugar over the unrolled pastry, fold in half, and chill 15 minutes. Roll to a larger rectangle to line a tart tin, leaving overhang. Chill 30 minutes. Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6).
Blind bake with beans 15-20 minutes, remove beans, and bake 15 minutes more until cooked through. Cool in the tin, then trim.
For the caramel, warm the coconut milk, pour some over the dates, soak 5 minutes, then blend with vanilla and salt until smooth. Spread over the tart base and chill 30 minutes to set.

Heat the remaining coconut milk until steaming, pour over the chocolate and coconut oil, and stir until combined. Spread over the caramel, sprinkle with salt, and chill about an hour to set. Bring to room temperature before slicing.

name: Vegan millionaire's shortbread
steps: Line a square tin. Blitz the cashews and oats to crumbs in a processor, add the dates and coconut oil, and blend again. Press into the tin as an even base layer. Chill.

For the filling, simmer the dates, almond milk, maple syrup, and coconut oil with salt 2-3 minutes until the dates soften. Cool slightly, then blend smooth with vanilla, adjusting salt to taste. Spread over the base and chill.

Melt the coconut oil, whisk in the cocoa and maple syrup until smooth, cool 10 minutes, then pour over the caramel layer. Chill at least 3 hours until firmly set. Cut into squares; keeps refrigerated up to a week.

name: Vegan chocolate banana ice cream
steps: Blend the frozen banana with the cocoa powder until smooth. Eat right away.

name: Vegan sponge cake

ingredients.raw_text: 150g dairy-free spread, plus extra for the tins
ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

ingredients.raw_text: 300ml dairy-free milk, we used oat milk

ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

ingredients.raw_text: 200g icing sugar, plus extra for dusting

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Line two sandwich tins with parchment and grease with dairy-free spread.

Combine the dairy-free milk with the vinegar and let sit a few minutes until slightly curdled. Combine half the vanilla seeds with the remaining cake ingredients in a bowl, then add the milk mixture and beat until smooth.
Divide between the tins and bake 30-35 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tins, then turn out onto racks.
For the buttercream, beat the dairy-free spread, icing sugar, and remaining vanilla seeds until pale and fluffy (mix by hand if the spread is very soft, or add extra milk if too firm).
Spread jam on one sponge, top with buttercream, and sandwich with the second sponge. Dust with icing sugar before slicing.

name: Vegan cupcakes with banana & peanut butter

ingredients.raw_text: 2 large or 3 small ripe bananas, mashed

steps: Set the oven to 170C (150C fan/gas 3.5) and line muffin tins. Combine the flour, sugar, salt, and bicarbonate of soda. Separately combine the mayonnaise, mashed banana, and vanilla. Fold the wet into the dry until just combined. Divide into cases and bake 20 minutes.
Sprinkle chocolate chips over the warm cupcakes without touching, so they melt and reset.
Beat the margarine and icing sugar, add the vegan milk, and mix until smooth, then beat 3 minutes more. Stir in the peanut butter. Pipe or spread over the cooled cakes; keeps airtight up to 2 days.

name: Vegan sponge cake

ingredients.raw_text: 150g dairy-free spread, plus extra for the tins
