ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

ingredients.raw_text: 300ml dairy-free milk, we used oat milk

ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

ingredients.raw_text: 200g icing sugar, plus extra for dusting

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Line two sandwich tins with parchment and grease with dairy-free spread.

Combine the dairy-free milk with the vinegar and let sit a few minutes until slightly curdled. Combine half the vanilla seeds with the remaining cake ingredients in a bowl, then add the milk mixture and beat until smooth.
Divide between the tins and bake 30-35 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tins, then turn out onto racks.
For the buttercream, beat the dairy-free spread, icing sugar, and remaining vanilla seeds until pale and fluffy (mix by hand if the spread is very soft, or add extra milk if too firm).
Spread jam on one sponge, top with buttercream, and sandwich with the second sponge. Dust with icing sugar before slicing.

name: Vegan brownies

ingredients.raw_text: 80g vegan margarine, plus extra for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 170C (150C fan/gas 3.5) and line a square tin. Combine the flaxseed with water and set aside at least 5 minutes.
Melt most of the chocolate with the coffee and margarine and water over low heat; cool slightly.

Combine the flour, almonds, cocoa, baking powder, and salt. Whisk the sugar into the melted chocolate mixture until smooth and glossy. Stir in the flaxseed mixture, vanilla, and remaining chocolate, then the flour mixture. Spoon into the tin.
Bake 35-45 minutes until a skewer comes out with moist crumbs. Cool completely in the tin before cutting; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Vegan lemon cake

ingredients.raw_text: 100ml vegetable oil, plus extra for the tin

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a loaf tin. Combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, and lemon zest. Add the oil, lemon juice, and water, mixing until smooth.
Pour into the tin and bake 30 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tin 10 minutes, then transfer to a rack to cool fully.
For the icing, sift the icing sugar and mix in just enough lemon juice for a thick pourable icing, then drizzle over the loaf.

name: Vegan mug cake

ingredients.raw_text: 3 tbsp dairy-free milk, we used oat milk
coconut cream or dairy-free ice cream

steps: Combine the milk with the lemon zest and juice in a mug and let sit 2-3 minutes until it looks slightly curdled. Stir in the oil, flour, sugar, and bicarbonate of soda until smooth.
Drop in the raspberries and microwave on high 1.5 minutes until puffed and cooked through.
Serve with coconut cream or dairy-free ice cream.

name: Vegan cupcakes with banana & peanut butter

ingredients.raw_text: 2 large or 3 small ripe bananas, mashed

steps: Set the oven to 170C (150C fan/gas 3.5) and line muffin tins. Combine the flour, sugar, salt, and bicarbonate of soda. Separately combine the mayonnaise, mashed banana, and vanilla. Fold the wet into the dry until just combined. Divide into cases and bake 20 minutes.
Sprinkle chocolate chips over the warm cupcakes without touching, so they melt and reset.
Beat the margarine and icing sugar, add the vegan milk, and mix until smooth, then beat 3 minutes more. Stir in the peanut butter. Pipe or spread over the cooled cakes; keeps airtight up to 2 days.

name: Chocolate orange cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 175g dark milk or milk chocolate, chopped
5 strips sugared candied orange peel, each cut into 5 pieces

steps: Beat the butter until fluffy, then beat in the icing sugar gradually with the orange zest and juice. Beat in the flour and cocoa gradually, then mix in the chocolate, orange peel, and milk.
Divide in two, shape each into a log on wrap, and chill until firm, about 2 hours.

Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two trays. Slice the logs into thin rounds, spacing on the trays to allow spreading. Top each with a piece of candied orange. Bake 10-15 minutes until puffed. Cool briefly on the trays before transferring to a rack; keeps airtight two to three days.

name: Easiest ever biscuits

ingredients.raw_text: 400g plain flour, plus extra for dusting

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a tray. Beat the butter until soft, then beat in the sugar, egg, and vanilla, and finally the flour to form a dough, adding more flour if sticky.
Roll pieces of dough on a floured surface (or mat) to about the thickness of two coins. Cut shapes with a cutter, press toys gently into the surface for a pattern, and re-roll scraps.
Transfer to the tray and bake 8-10 minutes until edges are just browned. Cool 5 minutes before serving; keeps three days in a tin.

name: Peanut butter cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 200g peanut butter (crunchy or smooth is fine)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two trays.
Combine the peanut butter, sugar, and salt. Add the egg and mix to a dough.
Portion into small balls, space apart on the trays, and flatten with a fork. Can be frozen up to 2 months; bake from frozen adding 1-2 minutes.
Bake 12 minutes until golden at the edges and pale in the center. Cool 10 minutes on the trays, then transfer to a rack; keeps three days.

name: Jammy coconut mallows

ingredients.raw_text: 18 large pink and white marshmallows, cut in half across the middle

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Beat the butter, sugar, egg, vanilla, and salt until smooth, then fold in the flour and coconut to a dough.
Shape into a round on a floured surface and roll to coin thickness. Cut rounds with a cutter, transfer to trays, and bake 14 minutes until light golden. Cool briefly, then transfer to a rack.

Flip half the biscuits and top each with jam and a marshmallow piece. Bake 2.5 minutes until just melted, then quickly sandwich with the remaining biscuits, pressing to stick. Cool 10 minutes, then dip the edges in jam and roll in coconut; keeps airtight up to 3 days.

name: Rainbow cookies
steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Combine the butter, sugars, egg yolks, and vanilla until creamy, then mix in the flour in two batches and stir in the orange zest. Roll into about 22 balls and bake on trays 15 minutes until golden; cool.
Combine icing sugar with enough orange juice for a thick, runny icing. Dip each cooled cookie half into the icing, then straight into sprinkles, and dry on a rack.

name: Edible name place biscuits
steps: Combine icing sugar, peppermint extract, and water to a thick icing. Spread over cookies and pipe a border with sweets and sprinkles. Let set 10 minutes.
Write names in the center of each with an icing pen once set, then wrap in cellophane with ribbon.

name: Easy chocolate chip cookies
steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two trays. Cream the butter and sugars until light and fluffy, then beat in the egg and vanilla. Stir in the flour, bicarbonate of soda, chocolate, and salt.

Scoop large spoonfuls onto the trays, spaced well apart. Bake 10-12 minutes until firm at the edges but soft in the middle. Cool briefly on the tray before eating warm, or cool completely on a rack; keeps airtight three days.

name: Chocolate & hazelnut thumbprint cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tsp coconut oil (or use any flavourless oil)

steps: Line a tray. Pulse the hazelnuts finely in a processor. Add the flours, sugar, and salt, and pulse to combine. Add the butter and pulse until just coming together, then knead by hand until smooth.
Roll into 25 small balls, place on the tray, and press a thumbprint into each. Chill 30 minutes. Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4).
Bake 15-20 minutes until light golden. Melt the chocolate and oil over simmering water, then spoon a little into each thumbprint and let set.

name: Double-dipped shortbread cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tbsp milk, plus extra if needed

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Beat the butter and icing sugar until fluffy. Beat in the vanilla and flour (it will stiffen). Add milk and beat until soft and cohesive.
Pipe swirls onto a lined tray (or roll into balls if too stiff to pipe), spacing well apart. Bake 15 minutes until lightly golden, then cool on a rack.
Melt the white and milk chocolate separately. Dip each cookie into both, milk at one end and white at the other, letting excess drip off before setting on the rack; keeps airtight up to four days.

name: Festive cookies for the freezer
steps: Beat the butter and sugar until fluffy. Add the condensed milk and vanilla, beating 1 minute. Combine the flour, baking powder, and cinnamon, then stir into the wet mixture to a dough. Knead in the cranberries and white chocolate.
Divide in two, roll each into a log, wrap in parchment, tie the ends, and freeze at least 24 hours (up to three months). Keep frozen until ready to bake, packing with ice for transport.
To bake: set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Slice into rounds, space on a tray, and bake 12 minutes until golden at the edges. Cool 5 minutes, then transfer to a rack.

name: Christmas crinkle cookies
steps: Combine the cocoa, sugar, and oil. Whisk in the eggs one at a time.
Combine the flour, baking powder, orange zest, spices, and salt separately, then mix into the cocoa mixture to a soft dough. Chill 1 hour if too soft.
Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Roll heaped teaspoons into balls and coat in icing sugar. Space apart on lined trays.
Bake 10 minutes, then cool on a rack — they'll firm up but stay fudgy inside; keeps airtight up to four days.

name: Chilli chocolate cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 1 ½ tsp cayenne pepper (less or more to taste)
100g bar dark chilli chocolate, roughly chopped

steps: Beat the butter and sugars, gradually mixing in the vanilla and eggs. Combine the flour, cocoa, salt, baking powder, and cayenne separately, then mix in and fold through the chocolate.

Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Space walnut-sized spoonfuls on lined trays. Bake in batches 10-12 minutes to your preferred gooeyness. Cool 1 minute on the tray before transferring to a plate; serve with vanilla ice cream.

name: Peanut butter cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 200g peanut butter (crunchy or smooth is fine)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two trays.
Combine the peanut butter, sugar, and salt. Add the egg and mix to a dough.
Portion into small balls, space apart on the trays, and flatten with a fork. Can be frozen up to 2 months; bake from frozen adding 1-2 minutes.
Bake 12 minutes until golden at the edges and pale in the center. Cool 10 minutes on the trays, then transfer to a rack; keeps three days.

name: Double choc peanut butter cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 100g unsalted butter, softened at room temperature
200g milk chocolate, 150g chopped into chunks and 50g melted for drizzling
75g peanut butter (crunchy or smooth is fine)
handful of salted peanuts, roughly chopped

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two trays. Beat the butter and sugar until fluffy, whisk in the egg, then beat in the flour, cocoa, salt, and chocolate chunks. Swirl the peanut butter through with a spoon.

Scoop into 12 large cookies across the trays, spacing well apart. Bake 9-10 minutes — they'll look underbaked but firm up as they cool. Drizzle with melted milk chocolate and top with chopped peanuts and flaky salt. Serve with milk.

name: Oaty hazelnut cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 50g butter, plus a little for greasing
1 dessert apple, unpeeled and coarsely grated (you need 85g)
40g unblanched hazelnuts, cut into chunky slices

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and grease a tray. Melt the butter and syrup, add the apple, and cook, stirring, 6-7 minutes until softened. Stir in the cinnamon and raisins.
Combine the oats, flour, and hazelnuts, add the apple mixture and egg, and beat well.
Spoon into nine mounds on the tray, pressing into discs, and bake 18-20 minutes until golden. Cool on a rack; keeps airtight three days or frozen six weeks.

name: Easy chocolate chip cookies
steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two trays. Cream the butter and sugars until light and fluffy, then beat in the egg and vanilla. Stir in the flour, bicarbonate of soda, chocolate, and salt.

Scoop large spoonfuls onto the trays, spaced well apart. Bake 10-12 minutes until firm at the edges but soft in the middle. Cool briefly on the tray before eating warm, or cool completely on a rack; keeps airtight three days.

name: Basic cookies

ingredients.food_name: Chocolate white, filled with coconut chips

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Cream the butter until soft, then beat in the sugar until fluffy. Sift in the flour with any optional extras, and bring together by hand into a dough (can be frozen at this point).
Roll into walnut-sized balls, space apart on a tray, and flatten slightly. Bake 10-12 minutes until golden and firm on top. Cool on a rack about 15 minutes before serving.

name: Cookie dough pizza
steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Beat the butter and sugars until combined, then beat in the eggs and vanilla.
Fold in the flour and salt, then scatter in the chocolate chips and stir through.

Shape into one large round cookie on a lined tray (chill 30 minutes if too soft). Bake 25 minutes until golden at the edges and slightly wobbly in the middle. Cool briefly, then top with melted chocolate, chocolate buttons, marshmallows, and strawberries. Slice and serve warm.

name: Chocolate & hazelnut thumbprint cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 1 tsp coconut oil (or use any flavourless oil)

steps: Line a tray. Pulse the hazelnuts finely in a processor. Add the flours, sugar, and salt, and pulse to combine. Add the butter and pulse until just coming together, then knead by hand until smooth.
Roll into 25 small balls, place on the tray, and press a thumbprint into each. Chill 30 minutes. Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4).
Bake 15-20 minutes until light golden. Melt the chocolate and oil over simmering water, then spoon a little into each thumbprint and let set.

name: Shortbread biscuits

ingredients.raw_text: 150g plain flour, plus extra for dusting
50g caster sugar, plus 1 tbsp for sprinkling

steps: Set the oven to 170C (150C fan/gas 3). Combine the flour, butter, and sugar by hand to crumbs, then bring together into a dough.
Roll to about half a centimeter thick, cut into fingers, and place on a lined tray. Mark with a fork and sprinkle with extra sugar.
Chill 20 minutes, then bake 15-20 minutes until golden. Cool 10 minutes on the tray.

name: Simple jammy biscuits
steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Rub the flour, sugar, and butter to crumbs (or use a processor). Add enough egg to bring together into a stiff dough.
Shape into a tube, slice into rounds, and space on a tray to allow for spreading.
Make an indent in each with a spoon handle and add a teaspoon of jam. Bake 10-15 minutes until slightly risen and golden. Cool on a rack.

name: Ricciarelli biscuits

ingredients.raw_text: 2 egg whites (use the yolks in another recipe, such as fried rice)
200g icing sugar, plus extra for dusting

steps: Whisk the egg whites to stiff peaks, then whisk in the almond extract and lemon zest.
Sift the icing sugar, ground almonds, and salt together, then fold into the egg whites to a dough. Cover and chill at least 4 hours, ideally overnight.

Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Turn the dough onto a surface dusted with icing sugar and roll into a 40cm log. Slice into rounds, shape into ovals, and dust with more icing sugar. Arrange on a lined tray.
Bake 20-23 minutes until just turning brown (they'll firm as they cool). Cool completely on the tray; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Forgotten cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 150g  dark  chocolate  (at least 70% cocoa solids), roughly chopped

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two trays with foil. Whisk the egg whites with salt to stiff peaks, then gradually whisk in the sugar to a thick, glossy meringue. Fold in the pecans, chocolate, and vanilla.
Spoon heaped teaspoons onto the trays, spaced apart. Place in the oven, turn it off, and leave the cookies at least 3 hours or overnight until the oven is cold.
Peel from the foil and store airtight; keeps 3-4 days.

name: Basic cookies

ingredients.food_name: Chocolate white, filled with coconut chips

steps: Set the oven to 190C (170C fan/gas 5). Cream the butter until soft, then beat in the sugar until fluffy. Sift in the flour with any optional extras, and bring together by hand into a dough (can be frozen at this point).
Roll into walnut-sized balls, space apart on a tray, and flatten slightly. Bake 10-12 minutes until golden and firm on top. Cool on a rack about 15 minutes before serving.

name: Peanut butter cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 200g peanut butter (crunchy or smooth is fine)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two trays.
Combine the peanut butter, sugar, and salt. Add the egg and mix to a dough.
Portion into small balls, space apart on the trays, and flatten with a fork. Can be frozen up to 2 months; bake from frozen adding 1-2 minutes.
Bake 12 minutes until golden at the edges and pale in the center. Cool 10 minutes on the trays, then transfer to a rack; keeps three days.

name: Easiest ever biscuits

ingredients.raw_text: 400g plain flour, plus extra for dusting

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a tray. Beat the butter until soft, then beat in the sugar, egg, and vanilla, and finally the flour to form a dough, adding more flour if sticky.
Roll pieces of dough on a floured surface (or mat) to about the thickness of two coins. Cut shapes with a cutter, press toys gently into the surface for a pattern, and re-roll scraps.
Transfer to the tray and bake 8-10 minutes until edges are just browned. Cool 5 minutes before serving; keeps three days in a tin.

name: Double chocolate shortbreads
steps: Combine the butter and sugar, then stir in the flour, cocoa, and chocolate chips, using your hands if needed. Halve and roll each portion into a log, wrap, and chill at least 1 hour (or freeze up to a month).
Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Slice into rounds, arrange on a lined tray, and bake 10-12 minutes. Cool on the tray.

name: Sugar-dusted vanilla shortbread

ingredients.raw_text: 200g chilled salted butter, plus a little more for the sheets

steps: Pulse the flour and cubed butter in a processor to crumbs. Add the sugar, vanilla, and egg yolks, and pulse to a smooth dough.
Roll into a log about 25cm long on a floured surface, wrap, and chill at least 1 hour (freezes up to 6 weeks; thaw 1 hour before slicing).
Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and grease two trays. Slice into rounds and arrange with space to spread.
Bake 20 minutes until pale golden at the edges, then cool on a rack. Dust with icing sugar; keeps airtight up to a week.

name: Vanilla shortbread dough
steps: Pulse the flour and cubed butter in a processor to crumbs.
Add the sugar, vanilla, and egg yolks, and pulse to a dough.
Roll into a log using parchment, twisting the ends, and chill 3 hours or overnight.
Set the oven to 180C (160C fan). Slice into rounds and space on a tray.
Bake 15-20 minutes until golden. Cool briefly on the tray before transferring to a rack.

name: Coconut & chocolate macaroons

ingredients.raw_text: 200g coarsely grated fresh coconut  (about 1 coconut in total)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Whisk the egg whites to stiff peaks, gradually adding sugar until thick and glossy. Sift in the flour and fold in with the coconut.
Shape spoonfuls with a cutter onto lined trays (in batches if needed). Bake 15-18 minutes until golden at the edges. Cool, then transfer to a rack.
Melt the chocolate and cool slightly. Coat the flat side of each macaroon and chill to set; keeps airtight two days.

name: Shortbread recipe
steps: Blend the butter and most of the sugar in a processor until smooth.
Add both flours and salt, and blend until combined.
Press the mixture evenly into a lined tray with your hands, smoothing with cling film. Chill uncooked at least 30 minutes, up to 2 days.
Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Uncover and mark all over with a fork.
Sprinkle with the remaining sugar and bake 20-25 minutes.
Cool in the tin, then cut into 24 thin slices; keeps airtight up to a week.

name: Anzac biscuits

ingredients.raw_text: 100g butter, plus extra butter for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Combine the oats, coconut, flour, and sugar. Melt the butter and stir in the golden syrup. Dissolve the bicarbonate of soda in boiling water and stir into the butter mixture.
Make a well in the dry ingredients and pour in the butter mixture, stirring gently to combine.
Drop spoonfuls onto buttered trays, spaced apart. Bake in batches 8-10 minutes until golden. Cool on a rack.

name: Fruity flapjack cookies
steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Toast the oats on a tray 20 minutes, turning once, until lightly browned; cool. Beat the butter and sugar 2 minutes until fluffy. Add the egg, golden syrup, and vanilla, beating well. Stir in the flour, dried fruit, coconut, cinnamon, toasted oats, and salt to a thick dough. Use immediately or chill up to two days.

Line two trays and arrange six spoonfuls of dough on each, well spaced. Bake 15 minutes (18-20 if chilled) until spread and browned at the edges but soft in the middle. Cool 5 minutes on the tray, then transfer to a rack; keeps airtight up to five days.

name: Freezer biscuits

ingredients.raw_text: 50g chopped nuts such as pecan, hazelnuts or almonds

steps: Beat the softened butter with the sugar until combined. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then the vanilla and salt. Stir in the flour and oats, along with any extra flavorings.
Spread half the mixture along the center of a sheet of parchment, roll into a tight log using the paper, and twist the ends. Freeze up to 3 months.

To bake, set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Slice off rounds from the frozen log (dip the knife in hot water if needed), spacing on a tray to allow for spreading. Bake 15 minutes until golden. Cool at least 5 minutes before eating.

name: Oatmeal raisin cookies
steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two trays. Soak the raisins in boiling water 20 minutes until plump; drain, reserving the liquid.
Combine the oil and sugar, gradually beat in the egg, reserved liquid, cinnamon, and vanilla. Sift in the flour, bicarbonate of soda, and salt, then add the oats. Mix in the raisins.
Drop heaped spoonfuls onto the trays, spaced apart. Bake 12-15 minutes until golden. Cool 10 minutes on the trays, then transfer to a rack; keeps airtight up to 3 days.

name: Vegan banana bread

ingredients.raw_text: 75ml vegetable oil or sunflower oil, plus extra for the tin
225g plain flour (or use self-raising flour and reduce the baking powder to 2 heaped tsp)

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Mash the bananas and combine with the oil and sugar.
Add the flour, baking powder, and cinnamon or mixed spice, combining well. Fold in dried fruit or nuts if using.
Bake in a lined loaf tin 20 minutes, covering with foil if browning too fast.
Bake 20 minutes more until a skewer comes out clean.
Cool briefly before slicing; even better the day after baking.

name: Vegan lemon cake

ingredients.raw_text: 100ml vegetable oil, plus extra for the tin

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6) and line a loaf tin. Combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, and lemon zest. Add the oil, lemon juice, and water, mixing until smooth.
Pour into the tin and bake 30 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tin 10 minutes, then transfer to a rack to cool fully.
For the icing, sift the icing sugar and mix in just enough lemon juice for a thick pourable icing, then drizzle over the loaf.

name: Vegan sponge cake

ingredients.raw_text: 150g dairy-free spread, plus extra for the tins
ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

ingredients.raw_text: 300ml dairy-free milk, we used oat milk

ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

ingredients.raw_text: 200g icing sugar, plus extra for dusting

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Line two sandwich tins with parchment and grease with dairy-free spread.

Combine the dairy-free milk with the vinegar and let sit a few minutes until slightly curdled. Combine half the vanilla seeds with the remaining cake ingredients in a bowl, then add the milk mixture and beat until smooth.
Divide between the tins and bake 30-35 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tins, then turn out onto racks.
For the buttercream, beat the dairy-free spread, icing sugar, and remaining vanilla seeds until pale and fluffy (mix by hand if the spread is very soft, or add extra milk if too firm).
Spread jam on one sponge, top with buttercream, and sandwich with the second sponge. Dust with icing sugar before slicing.

name: Vegan cupcakes with banana & peanut butter

ingredients.raw_text: 2 large or 3 small ripe bananas, mashed

steps: Set the oven to 170C (150C fan/gas 3.5) and line muffin tins. Combine the flour, sugar, salt, and bicarbonate of soda. Separately combine the mayonnaise, mashed banana, and vanilla. Fold the wet into the dry until just combined. Divide into cases and bake 20 minutes.
Sprinkle chocolate chips over the warm cupcakes without touching, so they melt and reset.
Beat the margarine and icing sugar, add the vegan milk, and mix until smooth, then beat 3 minutes more. Stir in the peanut butter. Pipe or spread over the cooled cakes; keeps airtight up to 2 days.

name: Vegan millionaire's shortbread
steps: Line a square tin. Blitz the cashews and oats to crumbs in a processor, add the dates and coconut oil, and blend again. Press into the tin as an even base layer. Chill.

For the filling, simmer the dates, almond milk, maple syrup, and coconut oil with salt 2-3 minutes until the dates soften. Cool slightly, then blend smooth with vanilla, adjusting salt to taste. Spread over the base and chill.

Melt the coconut oil, whisk in the cocoa and maple syrup until smooth, cool 10 minutes, then pour over the caramel layer. Chill at least 3 hours until firmly set. Cut into squares; keeps refrigerated up to a week.

name: Vegan brownies

ingredients.raw_text: 80g vegan margarine, plus extra for greasing

steps: Set the oven to 170C (150C fan/gas 3.5) and line a square tin. Combine the flaxseed with water and set aside at least 5 minutes.
Melt most of the chocolate with the coffee and margarine and water over low heat; cool slightly.

Combine the flour, almonds, cocoa, baking powder, and salt. Whisk the sugar into the melted chocolate mixture until smooth and glossy. Stir in the flaxseed mixture, vanilla, and remaining chocolate, then the flour mixture. Spoon into the tin.
Bake 35-45 minutes until a skewer comes out with moist crumbs. Cool completely in the tin before cutting; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Vegan flapjacks

ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

ingredients.raw_text: 75g dried fruit (such as raisins, dried cranberries, chopped apricots)

steps: Set the oven to 160C (140C fan/gas 4) and line a square tin.
Melt the dairy-free spread, sugar, and syrup together. Off the heat, stir in the oats, nuts, and dried fruit. Press into the tin firmly.
Bake 30-35 minutes until lightly golden and crisp at the edges. Cool in the tin before slicing; keeps airtight up to three days.

name: Vegan hot cross buns

ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

steps: Warm the almond milk, add the dairy-free spread, and let melt off the heat; cool to hand temperature.
Combine the flour, yeast, sugar, salt, and spices. Make a well, add the milk mixture, and combine to a sticky dough.
Knead 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic. Shape into a ball, place in an oiled bowl, cover, and prove 1 hour until doubled.
Flatten the dough, scatter with orange zest, sultanas, and mixed peel, and knead to distribute. Reshape, cover, and prove 1 hour more.
Divide into 12 pieces, roll into balls, and arrange spaced apart on a lined tray. Cover and prove 45 minutes.
Set the oven to 220C (200C fan/gas 7). Mix flour with water to a pipeable paste and pipe crosses on the buns. Bake 15-20 minutes until deep golden.
Warm and strain the jam, then brush over the warm buns. Cool slightly before eating.

name: Peanut butter cookies

ingredients.raw_text: 200g peanut butter (crunchy or smooth is fine)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and line two trays.
Combine the peanut butter, sugar, and salt. Add the egg and mix to a dough.
Portion into small balls, space apart on the trays, and flatten with a fork. Can be frozen up to 2 months; bake from frozen adding 1-2 minutes.
Bake 12 minutes until golden at the edges and pale in the center. Cool 10 minutes on the trays, then transfer to a rack; keeps three days.

name: Gluten-free bread

ingredients.raw_text: 284ml buttermilk (or the same amount of whole milk with a squeeze of lemon juice)

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4). Combine the flour, salt, and yeast. Whisk the buttermilk, eggs, and oil separately, then combine into a sticky dough.

Grease a loaf tin or flour a tray. Shape the dough into a log for the tin or a ball for a cob, scoring the top if free-form. Cover loosely and prove somewhere warm 1 hour until risen by about a third.
Bake 50-60 minutes until golden and risen. Cool on a rack at least 20 minutes before slicing.

ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

ingredients.raw_text: 75g dried fruit (such as raisins, dried cranberries, chopped apricots)

steps: Set the oven to 160C (140C fan/gas 4) and line a square tin.

name: Vegan banana bread

ingredients.raw_text: 75ml vegetable oil or sunflower oil, plus extra for the tin
225g plain flour (or use self-raising flour and reduce the baking powder to 2 heaped tsp)

steps: Set the oven to 200C (180C fan/gas 6). Mash the bananas and combine with the oil and sugar.
Add the flour, baking powder, and cinnamon or mixed spice, combining well. Fold in dried fruit or nuts if using.
Bake in a lined loaf tin 20 minutes, covering with foil if browning too fast.
Bake 20 minutes more until a skewer comes out clean.
Cool briefly before slicing; even better the day after baking.

name: Vegan sponge cake

ingredients.raw_text: 150g dairy-free spread, plus extra for the tins
ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

ingredients.raw_text: 300ml dairy-free milk, we used oat milk

ingredients.food_name: Margarine made from sunflower oil, fortified with vitamin D

ingredients.raw_text: 200g icing sugar, plus extra for dusting

name: Blueberry & coconut cake

ingredients.raw_text: 250ml rice bran oil, plus extra for the tin
140g fresh or frozen blueberries, plus extra to serve

steps: Set the oven to 180C (160C fan/gas 4) and grease a ring tin. Whisk the oil, eggs, sugar, and vanilla. Combine the flour and coconut, then alternately fold this and the soya milk into the wet mixture, starting and ending with the flour.
Spoon a quarter of the batter into the tin. Fold the blueberries into the rest and spoon in. Bake 1-1.25 hours until a skewer comes out clean, covering with foil if browning too fast.
Cool 10 minutes in the tin, then turn out to cool completely. Fill the center with extra blueberries and dust with icing sugar.
